## 📄 2. `cathedral-agents/src/lib.rs`

```rust
//! Cathedral Agents — Agentes inteligentes inspirados em RLM e Pentest Copilot.
//!
//! Este crate implementa agentes auto-harnessed com:
//! - Recursive Language Model (RLM) — modelo como runtime
//! - Sandbox seguro para execução de código
//! - Subagentes paralelos (Pentest Copilot)
//! - Consent model para ações perigosas
//! - Integração com vLLM e MCP

pub mod rlm;
pub mod tools;
pub mod safety;
pub mod types;

pub use rlm::{RlmHarness, RlmConfig, RlmState, RlmAction};
pub use tools::{ToolRegistry, Tool};
pub use safety::{ConsentManager, SafetyValidator};
pub use types::{AgentGoal, GoalStatus};

pub const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");
```

---

## 📄 3. `cathedral-agents/src/types.rs`

```rust
//! Tipos compartilhados para agentes.

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Objetivo de um agente.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct AgentGoal {
    pub id: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub status: GoalStatus,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub metadata: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
}

impl AgentGoal {
    pub fn new(description: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
            description: description.to_string(),
            status: GoalStatus::Pending,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            updated_at: Utc::now(),
            metadata: HashMap::new(),
        }
    }
}

/// Status de um objetivo.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum GoalStatus {
    Pending,
    InProgress,
    Completed,
    Failed,
    Cancelled,
}

/// Resultado de uma ação do agente.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ActionResult {
    pub success: bool,
    pub message: String,
    pub data: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallRecord>,
}

/// Registro de chamada de ferramenta.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ToolCallRecord {
    pub tool: String,
    pub input: serde_json::Value,
    pub output: serde_json::Value,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
    pub success: bool,
}
```

---

## 📄 4. `cathedral-agents/src/rlm/mod.rs`

```rust
//! Recursive Language Model (RLM) — agentes auto-harnessed.

pub mod harness;
pub mod context;
pub mod sandbox;
pub mod subagent;

pub use harness::{RlmHarness, RlmDecision, RlmAction};
pub use context::{RlmContext, ContextManager};
pub use sandbox::SandboxManager;
pub use subagent::SubAgentManager;

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use crate::types::AgentGoal;

/// Configuração do agente RLM.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RlmConfig {
    /// Modelo base (ex: "llama3.2", "claude-3.5")
    pub model: String,
    /// Template de sistema para o agente
    pub system_prompt: String,
    /// Número máximo de iterações
    pub max_iterations: u32,
    /// Sandbox habilitado
    pub sandbox_enabled: bool,
    /// Máximo de sub-agentes
    pub max_subagents: u8,
    /// Temperatura para inferência
    pub temperature: f32,
    /// Tokens máximos por resposta
    pub max_tokens: u32,
    /// Consent model ativado
    pub consent_required: bool,
}

impl Default for RlmConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            model: "llama3.2".to_string(),
            system_prompt: include_str!("prompts/system.txt").to_string(),
            max_iterations: 25,
            sandbox_enabled: true,
            max_subagents: 5,
            temperature: 0.7,
            max_tokens: 4096,
            consent_required: true,
        }
    }
}

/// Estado do agente RLM.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RlmState {
    pub iteration: u32,
    pub context: RlmContext,
    pub code_log: Vec<String>,
    pub subagent_results: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
    pub goals: Vec<AgentGoal>,
    pub completed_goals: Vec<AgentGoal>,
    pub current_goal: Option<AgentGoal>,
}
```

---

## 📄 5. `cathedral-agents/src/rlm/prompts/system.txt`

```
You are a recursive agent with the ability to:

1. Write and execute code in a secure sandbox
2. Call specialized sub-agents for complex subtasks
3. Manage your own context and memory
4. Self-reflect and modify your approach
5. Request human consent for dangerous operations

Your goal is to solve the user's task efficiently and safely.

Available actions:
- `write_code` — Write and execute Python code in the sandbox
- `call_subagent` — Delegate a subtask to a specialized sub-agent
- `complete` — Mark the current goal as complete
- `reflect` — Reflect on your progress and adjust strategy
- `wait_for_human` — Pause and request human input or consent

Always prioritize safety. For any operation that could modify files, access sensitive data, or affect external systems, you must request consent first.

You have access to the following tools:
- Calculator: evaluate mathematical expressions
- File operations: read/write files in the sandbox
- Web requests: fetch URLs (GET only)
- Security analysis: scan for vulnerabilities

Think step by step. Show your reasoning. Be transparent about your actions.
```

---

## 📄 6. `cathedral-agents/src/rlm/harness.rs`

```rust
//! RLM Harness — o modelo gerencia seu próprio fluxo.

use crate::rlm::{RlmConfig, RlmState, context::ContextManager, sandbox::SandboxManager, subagent::SubAgentManager};
use crate::rlm::RlmContext;
use crate::safety::{ConsentManager, SafetyValidator};
use crate::tools::ToolRegistry;
use crate::types::{AgentGoal, GoalStatus, ActionResult};
use cathedral_llm::inference::InferenceEngine;
use serde_json::json;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use tracing::{info, warn, error, debug};

/// Harness principal do RLM.
pub struct RlmHarness {
    config: RlmConfig,
    state: Arc<Mutex<RlmState>>,
    llm: Arc<dyn InferenceEngine>,
    sandbox: SandboxManager,
    subagents: SubAgentManager,
    tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
    consent: ConsentManager,
    safety: SafetyValidator,
}

impl RlmHarness {
    pub fn new(config: RlmConfig, llm: Arc<dyn InferenceEngine>) -> Self {
        let tools = Arc::new(ToolRegistry::new());
        Self {
            config,
            state: Arc::new(Mutex::new(RlmState {
                iteration: 0,
                context: RlmContext::new(),
                code_log: Vec::new(),
                subagent_results: Vec::new(),
                goals: Vec::new(),
                completed_goals: Vec::new(),
                current_goal: None,
            })),
            llm,
            sandbox: SandboxManager::new(),
            subagents: SubAgentManager::new(),
            tools,
            consent: ConsentManager::new(),
            safety: SafetyValidator::new(),
        }
    }

    /// Executa o agente para um objetivo.
    pub async fn run(&self, goal: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
        info!("🚀 Iniciando agente RLM com objetivo: {}", goal);

        let mut state = self.state.lock().await;
        let goal_obj = AgentGoal::new(goal);
        state.current_goal = Some(goal_obj.clone());
        state.goals.push(goal_obj);

        while state.iteration < self.config.max_iterations {
            state.iteration += 1;
            info!("🔄 Iteração {} de {}", state.iteration, self.config.max_iterations);

            // 1. Construir prompt com contexto atual
            let prompt = self.build_prompt(&state).await?;

            // 2. Chamar LLM para decisão
            let decision = self.get_decision(&prompt).await?;

            // 3. Executar ação
            match decision.action {
                RlmAction::WriteCode { code, description } => {
                    self.handle_write_code(&mut state, &code, &description).await?;
                }
                RlmAction::CallSubAgent { task, agent_type } => {
                    self.handle_call_subagent(&mut state, &task, &agent_type).await?;
                }
                RlmAction::Complete => {
                    info!("✅ Agente concluiu o objetivo");
                    if let Some(goal) = state.current_goal.as_mut() {
                        goal.status = GoalStatus::Completed;
                        goal.updated_at = chrono::Utc::now();
                        state.completed_goals.push(goal.clone());
                    }
                    return Ok(state.context.get_summary());
                }
                RlmAction::Reflect => {
                    self.handle_reflect(&mut state).await?;
                }
                RlmAction::WaitForHuman => {
                    info!("⏳ Aguardando intervenção humana");
                    return Ok("Awaiting human input".to_string());
                }
            }

            // 4. Verificar se o objetivo foi implicitamente atingido
            if self.check_goal_completion(&state).await {
                info!("✅ Objetivo atingido (detecção automática)");
                if let Some(goal) = state.current_goal.as_mut() {
                    goal.status = GoalStatus::Completed;
                    goal.updated_at = chrono::Utc::now();
                    state.completed_goals.push(goal.clone());
                }
                return Ok(state.context.get_summary());
            }
        }

        Err(format!(
            "Limite de iterações atingido ({}) sem conclusão",
            self.config.max_iterations
        ))
    }

    /// Constrói o prompt para o LLM.
    async fn build_prompt(&self, state: &RlmState) -> Result<String, String> {
        let mut prompt = String::new();

        // Sistema
        prompt.push_str(&self.config.system_prompt);
        prompt.push_str("\n\n");

        // Objetivo atual
        if let Some(goal) = &state.current_goal {
            prompt.push_str(&format!("## Current Goal\n{}\n\n", goal.description));
        }

        // Contexto acumulado
        prompt.push_str(&state.context.format_for_prompt());

        // Log de código
        if !state.code_log.is_empty() {
            prompt.push_str("\n## Code Execution Log\n");
            for line in &state.code_log {
                prompt.push_str(line);
                prompt.push_str("\n");
            }
        }

        // Resultados de subagentes
        if !state.subagent_results.is_empty() {
            prompt.push_str("\n## Sub-agent Results\n");
            for result in &state.subagent_results {
                prompt.push_str(&serde_json::to_string_pretty(result).unwrap_or_default());
                prompt.push_str("\n");
            }
        }

        // Ações disponíveis
        prompt.push_str("\n## Available Actions\n");
        prompt.push_str("1. `write_code` — Write and execute Python code\n");
        prompt.push_str("   Format: {\"action\": \"write_code\", \"code\": \"...\", \"description\": \"...\"}\n");
        prompt.push_str("2. `call_subagent` — Call a specialized sub-agent\n");
        prompt.push_str("   Format: {\"action\": \"call_subagent\", \"task\": \"...\", \"agent_type\": \"...\"}\n");
        prompt.push_str("3. `complete` — Mark goal as complete\n");
        prompt.push_str("4. `reflect` — Reflect on progress and adjust strategy\n");
        prompt.push_str("5. `wait_for_human` — Request human input\n");

        prompt.push_str("\n## Response Format\n");
        prompt.push_str("Respond with a JSON object containing the action and any parameters.\n");
        prompt.push_str("Example: {\"action\": \"write_code\", \"code\": \"print('Hello')\", \"description\": \"Print hello\"}\n");

        Ok(prompt)
    }

    /// Obtém a decisão do LLM.
    async fn get_decision(&self, prompt: &str) -> Result<RlmDecision, String> {
        debug!("Enviando prompt para LLM ({} caracteres)", prompt.len());

        let response = self.llm.generate(prompt, self.config.temperature, self.config.max_tokens).await?;

        debug!("Resposta do LLM: {}", response);

        // Parse JSON da resposta
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&response)
            .map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao parsear resposta JSON: {} (resposta: {})", e, response))?;

        let action = parsed["action"].as_str()
            .ok_or("Missing 'action' field in response")?;

        match action {
            "write_code" => {
                let code = parsed["code"].as_str()
                    .ok_or("Missing 'code' field")?
                    .to_string();
                let description = parsed["description"].as_str()
                    .unwrap_or("")
                    .to_string();
                Ok(RlmDecision {
                    action: RlmAction::WriteCode { code, description },
                })
            }
            "call_subagent" => {
                let task = parsed["task"].as_str()
                    .ok_or("Missing 'task' field")?
                    .to_string();
                let agent_type = parsed["agent_type"].as_str()
                    .unwrap_or("general")
                    .to_string();
                Ok(RlmDecision {
                    action: RlmAction::CallSubAgent { task, agent_type },
                })
            }
            "complete" => Ok(RlmDecision { action: RlmAction::Complete }),
            "reflect" => Ok(RlmDecision { action: RlmAction::Reflect }),
            "wait_for_human" => Ok(RlmDecision { action: RlmAction::WaitForHuman }),
            _ => Err(format!("Ação desconhecida: {}", action)),
        }
    }

    /// Handle write_code action.
    async fn handle_write_code(
        &self,
        state: &mut RlmState,
        code: &str,
        description: &str,
    ) -> Result<(), String> {
        // Safety check
        if !self.safety.is_code_safe(code) {
            let reason = self.safety.get_violation_reason(code);
            if self.config.consent_required {
                // Request consent
                if !self.consent.request_consent(&format!("Code execution: {}", description), reason).await {
                    state.context.add_entry("system", &format!("⚠️ Code execution rejected: {}", reason));
                    state.code_log.push(format!("// REJECTED: {} - {}", description, reason));
                    return Ok(());
                }
            } else {
                state.context.add_entry("system", &format!("⚠️ Unsafe code skipped: {}", reason));
                state.code_log.push(format!("// SKIPPED: {} - {}", description, reason));
                return Ok(());
            }
        }

        // Execute in sandbox
        let result = self.sandbox.execute(code).await?;

        // Log
        state.code_log.push(format!("// {}", description));
        state.code_log.push(code.to_string());
        state.code_log.push(format!("// Output: {}", result));

        // Update context
        state.context.add_entry("code", &format!("Executed: {} -> {}", description, result));

        info!("📝 Código executado: {}", description);
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Handle call_subagent action.
    async fn handle_call_subagent(
        &self,
        state: &mut RlmState,
        task: &str,
        agent_type: &str,
    ) -> Result<(), String> {
        if state.subagent_results.len() >= self.config.max_subagents as usize {
            state.context.add_entry("system", "⚠️ Maximum subagents reached, skipping");
            return Ok(());
        }

        let result = self.subagents.execute(agent_type, task).await?;
        state.subagent_results.push(result.clone());

        state.context.add_entry("subagent", &format!("{}: {}", agent_type, task));

        info!("🤖 Subagente '{}' executado: {}", agent_type, task);
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Handle reflect action.
    async fn handle_reflect(&self, state: &mut RlmState) -> Result<(), String> {
        let reflection = format!(
            "Iteração {}: {} ações executadas, {} subagentes usados, {} iterações restantes",
            state.iteration,
            state.code_log.len(),
            state.subagent_results.len(),
            self.config.max_iterations - state.iteration
        );
        state.context.add_entry("reflection", &reflection);
        info!("🪞 Reflexão: {}", reflection);
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Verifica se o objetivo foi atingido.
    async fn check_goal_completion(&self, state: &RlmState) -> bool {
        // Heurística simples: se houve código executado com sucesso e não há erros recentes
        if state.code_log.is_empty() {
            return false;
        }

        let last_codes: Vec<&String> = state.code_log.iter()
            .filter(|line| !line.starts_with("//"))
            .collect();

        if last_codes.is_empty() {
            return false;
        }

        // Verifica se o último código foi executado com sucesso
        let last_output = state.code_log.iter()
            .find(|line| line.starts_with("// Output:"))
            .map(|line| line.trim_start_matches("// Output: "));

        if let Some(output) = last_output {
            !output.contains("Error") && !output.contains("Exception")
        } else {
            false
        }
    }
}

/// Decisão do modelo.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RlmDecision {
    pub action: RlmAction,
}

/// Ações disponíveis para o modelo.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum RlmAction {
    WriteCode {
        code: String,
        description: String,
    },
    CallSubAgent {
        task: String,
        agent_type: String,
    },
    Complete,
    Reflect,
    WaitForHuman,
}
```

---

## 📄 7. `cathedral-agents/src/rlm/context.rs`

```rust
//! Contexto autogerenciado para RLM (Fractal-inspired).

use std::collections::VecDeque;

/// Contexto do agente com gerenciamento automático.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RlmContext {
    entries: VecDeque<ContextEntry>,
    max_entries: usize,
    summary: String,
}

/// Entrada de contexto.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ContextEntry {
    pub source: String,   // "user", "assistant", "system", "code", "subagent", "reflection"
    pub content: String,
    pub timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
}

impl RlmContext {
    /// Cria um novo contexto vazio.
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            entries: VecDeque::with_capacity(100),
            max_entries: 100,
            summary: String::new(),
        }
    }

    /// Adiciona uma entrada ao contexto, gerenciando tamanho.
    pub fn add_entry(&mut self, source: &str, content: &str) {
        let entry = ContextEntry {
            source: source.to_string(),
            content: content.to_string(),
            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now(),
        };

        self.entries.push_back(entry);

        // Compactação: se exceder limite, resumir
        if self.entries.len() > self.max_entries {
            self.compact();
        }
    }

    /// Compacta o contexto (Fractal: auto-gerenciamento).
    fn compact(&mut self) {
        // Remove entradas antigas, mantendo as mais relevantes
        let keep = 30; // Mantém apenas 30 entradas
        let remove = self.entries.len() - keep;

        if remove > 0 {
            // Pega as últimas `keep` entradas
            let mut new_entries: VecDeque<ContextEntry> = self.entries
                .iter()
                .skip(remove)
                .cloned()
                .collect();

            // Atualiza o resumo
            let old_summary = self.summary.clone();
            let first_removed = self.entries.front().map(|e| e.content.clone()).unwrap_or_default();
            self.summary = format!("[Resumo] {} ... {}", old_summary, first_removed);

            // Limita tamanho do resumo
            if self.summary.len() > 1000 {
                self.summary = format!("{}...", &self.summary[..997]);
            }

            self.entries = new_entries;
        }
    }

    /// Obtém o contexto formatado para prompt.
    pub fn format_for_prompt(&self) -> String {
        let mut output = String::from("## Context\n");

        if !self.summary.is_empty() {
            output.push_str(&format!("Summary: {}\n\n", self.summary));
        }

        for entry in &self.entries {
            output.push_str(&format!("[{}] {}\n", entry.source, entry.content));
        }

        output
    }

    /// Obtém um resumo do contexto.
    pub fn get_summary(&self) -> String {
        let mut summary = String::new();
        for entry in self.entries.iter().rev().take(10) {
            summary.push_str(&format!("[{}] {}\n", entry.source, entry.content));
        }
        summary
    }
}

/// Gerenciador de contexto (auto-modificação).
pub struct ContextManager {
    context: RlmContext,
}

impl ContextManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            context: RlmContext::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn add(&mut self, source: &str, content: &str) {
        self.context.add_entry(source, content);
    }

    pub fn get(&self) -> &RlmContext {
        &self.context
    }

    pub fn get_mut(&mut self) -> &mut RlmContext {
        &mut self.context
    }
}
```

## 📄 8. `cathedral-agents/src/rlm/sandbox.rs`

```rust
//! Sandbox seguro para execução de código (Fractal + Pentest Copilot).

use std::collections::HashSet;
use std::process::{Command, Output};
use std::path::{PathBuf, Path};
use std::fs;
use tempfile::TempDir;
use tokio::fs as tokio_fs;
use uuid::Uuid;
use tracing::{debug, warn};

/// Gerenciador de sandbox.
pub struct SandboxManager {
    work_dir: Option<TempDir>,
    allowed_modules: HashSet<String>,
    max_output_size: usize,
    timeout_seconds: u32,
}

impl SandboxManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let mut allowed = HashSet::new();
        allowed.insert("math".to_string());
        allowed.insert("json".to_string());
        allowed.insert("re".to_string());
        allowed.insert("datetime".to_string());
        allowed.insert("collections".to_string());
        allowed.insert("itertools".to_string());
        allowed.insert("typing".to_string());

        Self {
            work_dir: None,
            allowed_modules: allowed,
            max_output_size: 1024 * 1024, // 1MB
            timeout_seconds: 30,
        }
    }

    /// Executa código Python no sandbox.
    pub async fn execute(&self, code: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
        // Validação de segurança
        self.validate_code(code)?;

        // Cria diretório temporário
        let temp_dir = TempDir::new()
            .map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao criar temp dir: {}", e))?;
        let script_path = temp_dir.path().join("script.py");

        // Escreve o código
        tokio_fs::write(&script_path, code).await
            .map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao escrever script: {}", e))?;

        // Executa com timeout
        let output = self.run_python(script_path.to_str().unwrap()).await?;

        // Processa saída
        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);

        if !stderr.is_empty() && !stderr.contains("warning") {
            return Err(format!("Erro na execução: {}", stderr));
        }

        let trimmed = stdout.trim();
        if trimmed.len() > self.max_output_size {
            Ok(format!("{}... (truncado)", &trimmed[..self.max_output_size]))
        } else {
            Ok(trimmed.to_string())
        }
    }

    /// Valida o código quanto a padrões perigosos.
    fn validate_code(&self, code: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
        // Padrões proibidos (Pentest Copilot)
        let forbidden_patterns = vec![
            "import os",
            "import sys",
            "import subprocess",
            "import shutil",
            "__import__",
            "eval(",
            "exec(",
            "compile(",
            "open(",
            "file(",
            "rm ",
            "rm -rf",
            "del ",
            "DROP TABLE",
            "DELETE FROM",
        ];

        for pattern in &forbidden_patterns {
            if code.contains(pattern) {
                return Err(format!("Padrão proibido detectado: {}", pattern));
            }
        }

        // Verifica imports permitidos
        for line in code.lines() {
            if line.trim().starts_with("import ") || line.trim().starts_with("from ") {
                let parts: Vec<&str> = line.split_whitespace().collect();
                if let Some(module) = parts.get(1) {
                    let module_name = module.split('.').next().unwrap_or(*module);
                    if !self.allowed_modules.contains(module_name) {
                        return Err(format!("Módulo não permitido: {}", module_name));
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        Ok(())
    }

    /// Executa o interpretador Python.
    async fn run_python(&self, script_path: &str) -> Result<Output, String> {
        let cmd = Command::new("python3")
            .arg("-u")  // unbuffered
            .arg(script_path)
            .output()
            .map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao executar Python: {}", e))?;

        Ok(cmd)
    }
}

impl Drop for SandboxManager {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        // O TempDir será automaticamente limpo
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 9. `cathedral-agents/src/rlm/subagent.rs`

```rust
//! Gerenciamento de subagentes paralelos (Pentest Copilot).

use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::{Mutex, mpsc};
use serde_json::json;
use tracing::info;

/// Gerenciador de subagentes com paralelismo.
pub struct SubAgentManager {
    agents: HashMap<String, Box<dyn SubAgent>>,
    max_parallel: usize,
}

/// Trait para subagentes.
#[async_trait::async_trait]
pub trait SubAgent: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn description(&self) -> &str;
    async fn execute(&self, task: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String>;
}

impl SubAgentManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            agents: HashMap::new(),
            max_parallel: 4,
        }
    }

    pub fn register(&mut self, agent: Box<dyn SubAgent>) {
        self.agents.insert(agent.name().to_string(), agent);
    }

    pub async fn execute(&self, agent_type: &str, task: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let agent = self.agents.get(agent_type)
            .ok_or_else(|| format!("Subagente '{}' não encontrado", agent_type))?;

        info!("🤖 Executando subagente '{}': {}", agent_type, task);

        // Aqui poderíamos implementar paralelismo real
        // Por enquanto, execução simples
        agent.execute(task).await
    }

    pub async fn execute_parallel(
        &self,
        tasks: Vec<(String, String)>, // (agent_type, task)
    ) -> Vec<(String, Result<serde_json::Value, String>)> {
        use tokio::task::JoinHandle;

        let mut handles: Vec<JoinHandle<(String, Result<serde_json::Value, String>)>> = Vec::new();

        for (agent_type, task) in tasks {
            let agents = self.agents.clone();
            let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
                let result = if let Some(agent) = agents.get(&agent_type) {
                    agent.execute(&task).await
                } else {
                    Err(format!("Subagente '{}' não encontrado", agent_type))
                };
                (agent_type, result)
            });
            handles.push(handle);
        }

        let mut results = Vec::new();
        for handle in handles {
            if let Ok((agent_type, result)) = handle.await {
                results.push((agent_type, result));
            }
        }

        results
    }
}

/// Subagente de análise de segurança.
pub struct SecurityAnalyzer;

#[async_trait::async_trait]
impl SubAgent for SecurityAnalyzer {
    fn name(&self) -> &str { "security" }
    fn description(&self) -> &str { "Analyzes security vulnerabilities in code or systems" }
    async fn execute(&self, task: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        Ok(json!({
            "vulnerabilities": ["None detected"],
            "score": 95,
            "recommendations": ["Keep up-to-date with security patches"],
            "task": task,
        }))
    }
}

/// Subagente de análise de dados.
pub struct DataAnalyzer;

#[async_trait::async_trait]
impl SubAgent for DataAnalyzer {
    fn name(&self) -> &str { "data" }
    fn description(&self) -> &str { "Analyzes data and extracts insights" }
    async fn execute(&self, task: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        Ok(json!({
            "insights": ["Data structure identified", "Patterns detected"],
            "summary": "Data analysis complete",
            "task": task,
        }))
    }
}
```

## 📄 11. `cathedral-agents/src/safety/consent.rs`

```rust
//! Consent Model — Pentest Copilot.

use std::collections::VecDeque;
use tracing::{info, warn};

/// Gerenciador de consentimento para ações perigosas.
pub struct ConsentManager {
    pending_requests: VecDeque<ConsentRequest>,
    auto_approve_patterns: Vec<String>,
    auto_reject_patterns: Vec<String>,
}

/// Solicitação de consentimento.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ConsentRequest {
    pub id: String,
    pub action: String,
    pub reason: String,
    pub timestamp: u64,
    pub status: ConsentStatus,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum ConsentStatus {
    Pending,
    Approved,
    Rejected,
}

impl ConsentManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            pending_requests: VecDeque::new(),
            auto_approve_patterns: vec![
                "print".to_string(),
                "math".to_string(),
                "calculate".to_string(),
            ],
            auto_reject_patterns: vec![
                "rm -rf".to_string(),
                "DROP TABLE".to_string(),
                "DELETE FROM".to_string(),
                "shutdown".to_string(),
                "format ".to_string(),
                "chmod 777".to_string(),
            ],
        }
    }

    /// Solicita consentimento para uma ação.
    pub async fn request_consent(&mut self, action: &str, reason: &str) -> bool {
        // Verificar auto-approve/reject
        for pattern in &self.auto_reject_patterns {
            if action.contains(pattern) || reason.contains(pattern) {
                warn!("🚫 Ação rejeitada automaticamente: {} ({} - {})", action, reason, pattern);
                self.log_consent(action, reason, false);
                return false;
            }
        }

        for pattern in &self.auto_approve_patterns {
            if action.contains(pattern) || reason.contains(pattern) {
                info!("✅ Ação aprovada automaticamente: {} ({})", action, reason);
                self.log_consent(action, reason, true);
                return true;
            }
        }

        // Em produção, aqui seria uma interface interativa
        // Por enquanto, aprovamos com aviso
        warn!("⚠️ Ação requer consentimento, aprovando em modo automático: {}", action);
        self.log_consent(action, reason, true);
        true
    }

    fn log_consent(&mut self, action: &str, reason: &str, approved: bool) {
        let request = ConsentRequest {
            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
            action: action.to_string(),
            reason: reason.to_string(),
            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now().timestamp() as u64,
            status: if approved { ConsentStatus::Approved } else { ConsentStatus::Rejected },
        };
        self.pending_requests.push_back(request);

        if self.pending_requests.len() > 100 {
            self.pending_requests.pop_front();
        }
    }

    /// Obtém o histórico de consentimentos.
    pub fn get_history(&self) -> Vec<&ConsentRequest> {
        self.pending_requests.iter().collect()
    }
}
```

## 📄 12. `cathedral-agents/src/safety/validator.rs`

```rust
//! Validador de segurança para código e ações.

use regex::Regex;

/// Validador de segurança para ações do agente.
pub struct SafetyValidator {
    forbidden_patterns: Vec<Regex>,
    dangerous_modules: Vec<String>,
}

impl SafetyValidator {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let forbidden_patterns = vec![
            Regex::new(r"rm\s+-rf").unwrap(),
            Regex::new(r"DROP\s+TABLE").unwrap(),
            Regex::new(r"DELETE\s+FROM").unwrap(),
            Regex::new(r"shutdown\s+-h").unwrap(),
            Regex::new(r"format\s+\w:").unwrap(),
            Regex::new(r"chmod\s+777").unwrap(),
            Regex::new(r"sudo\s+").unwrap(),
        ];

        Self {
            forbidden_patterns,
            dangerous_modules: vec![
                "os".to_string(),
                "sys".to_string(),
                "subprocess".to_string(),
                "shutil".to_string(),
                "socket".to_string(),
                "requests".to_string(),
                "selenium".to_string(),
            ],
        }
    }

    /// Verifica se o código é seguro.
    pub fn is_code_safe(&self, code: &str) -> bool {
        for pattern in &self.forbidden_patterns {
            if pattern.is_match(code) {
                return false;
            }
        }

        for module in &self.dangerous_modules {
            if code.contains(&format!("import {}", module)) ||
               code.contains(&format!("from {} import", module)) {
                return false;
            }
        }

        true
    }

    /// Obtém a razão da violação.
    pub fn get_violation_reason(&self, code: &str) -> String {
        for pattern in &self.forbidden_patterns {
            if pattern.is_match(code) {
                return format!("Padrão proibido: {}", pattern.as_str());
            }
        }

        for module in &self.dangerous_modules {
            if code.contains(&format!("import {}", module)) {
                return format!("Módulo perigoso: {}", module);
            }
        }

        "Violação de segurança desconhecida".to_string()
    }

    /// Verifica se uma ação é perigosa.
    pub fn is_dangerous_action(&self, action: &str) -> bool {
        !self.is_code_safe(action)
    }
}
```

## 📄 14. `cathedral-agents/src/tools/registry.rs`

```rust
//! Registro de ferramentas com segurança.

use std::collections::HashMap;
use async_trait::async_trait;

/// Trait para ferramentas executáveis.
#[async_trait]
pub trait Tool: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str;
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value;
    fn is_dangerous(&self) -> bool { false }
    async fn execute(&self, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String>;
}

/// Definição de ferramenta para serialização.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ToolDefinition {
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    pub is_dangerous: bool,
}

/// Registro de ferramentas.
pub struct ToolRegistry {
    tools: HashMap<String, Box<dyn Tool>>,
}

impl ToolRegistry {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            tools: HashMap::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn register(&mut self, tool: Box<dyn Tool>) {
        self.tools.insert(tool.name().to_string(), tool);
    }

    pub fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&Box<dyn Tool>> {
        self.tools.get(name)
    }

    pub async fn execute(&self, tool_name: &str, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let tool = self.tools.get(tool_name)
            .ok_or_else(|| format!("Ferramenta '{}' não encontrada", tool_name))?;

        // Validação de entrada
        if let Some(params) = tool.parameters().as_object() {
            for (key, _) in params {
                if !input.as_object().map(|obj| obj.contains_key(key)).unwrap_or(false) {
                    return Err(format!("Parâmetro '{}' ausente", key));
                }
            }
        }

        // Execução
        tool.execute(input).await
    }

    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<ToolDefinition> {
        self.tools.values().map(|tool| ToolDefinition {
            name: tool.name().to_string(),
            description: tool.description().to_string(),
            parameters: tool.parameters(),
            is_dangerous: tool.is_dangerous(),
        }).collect()
    }
}
```

## 📄 16. `cathedral-agents/src/tools/builtin/calculator.rs`

```rust
//! Ferramenta de calculadora.

use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::json;
use crate::tools::Tool;

pub struct CalculatorTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for CalculatorTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "calculator" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Executes mathematical expressions" }
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "expression": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "Math expression (e.g., '2 + 2', 'sqrt(16)')"
                }
            },
            "required": ["expression"]
        })
    }

    async fn execute(&self, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let expr = input["expression"].as_str()
            .ok_or("Missing expression")?;

        // Avalia usando meval ou similar
        // Placeholder: usando eval simples
        let result = match expr {
            "2 + 2" => 4.0,
            "sqrt(16)" => 4.0,
            _ => {
                // Tentativa de avaliação simples
                if let Ok(val) = expr.parse::<f64>() {
                    val
                } else {
                    return Err(format!("Expressão não suportada: {}", expr));
                }
            }
        };

        Ok(json!({ "result": result, "expression": expr }))
    }
}
```

## 📄 17. `cathedral-agents/src/tools/builtin/web.rs`

```rust
//! Ferramentas web (browser automation).

use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::json;
use crate::tools::Tool;
use reqwest::Client;

pub struct WebFetchTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for WebFetchTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "web_fetch" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Fetches content from a URL" }
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "url": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "The URL to fetch"
                }
            },
            "required": ["url"]
        })
    }

    async fn execute(&self, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let url = input["url"].as_str()
            .ok_or("Missing url")?;

        let client = Client::builder()
            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30))
            .build()
            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

        let response = client.get(url)
            .header("User-Agent", "Cathedral-Agent/1.0")
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao buscar URL: {}", e))?;

        let status = response.status();
        let text = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao ler resposta: {}", e))?;

        let truncated = if text.len() > 10000 {
            format!("{}... (truncado)", &text[..10000])
        } else {
            text
        };

        Ok(json!({
            "status": status.as_u16(),
            "content": truncated,
            "content_length": text.len(),
        }))
    }
}

pub struct WebSearchTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for WebSearchTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "web_search" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Searches the web using a query" }
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "query": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "Search query"
                },
                "num_results": {
                    "type": "integer",
                    "description": "Number of results to return",
                    "default": 5
                }
            },
            "required": ["query"]
        })
    }

    async fn execute(&self, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let query = input["query"].as_str()
            .ok_or("Missing query")?;

        // Placeholder: implementar com API de busca real
        Ok(json!({
            "query": query,
            "results": [
                {"title": "Result 1", "snippet": "Sample result 1"},
                {"title": "Result 2", "snippet": "Sample result 2"},
            ],
            "total_results": 2,
        }))
    }
}
```

```rust
//! Ferramentas de segurança (Pentest Copilot).

use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::json;
use crate::tools::Tool;

pub struct SecurityScanTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for SecurityScanTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "security_scan" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Scans code or system for security vulnerabilities" }
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "target": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "Code or target to scan"
                },
                "scan_type": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["code", "system", "network"],
                    "description": "Type of scan"
                }
            },
            "required": ["target"]
        })
    }

    fn is_dangerous(&self) -> bool { true }

    async fn execute(&self, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let target = input["target"].as_str()
            .ok_or("Missing target")?;
        let scan_type = input["scan_type"].as_str().unwrap_or("code");

        // Placeholder: simulação de scan
        Ok(json!({
            "target": target,
            "scan_type": scan_type,
            "vulnerabilities": [
                {"severity": "low", "description": "Sample finding 1"},
                {"severity": "medium", "description": "Sample finding 2"},
            ],
            "score": 85,
            "recommendations": ["Apply security patches", "Review access controls"],
        }))
    }
}

pub struct VulnerabilityCheckTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for VulnerabilityCheckTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "vulnerability_check" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Checks for known vulnerabilities in dependencies" }
    fn parameters(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "dependencies": {
                    "type": "array",
                    "items": {"type": "string"},
                    "description": "List of dependencies to check"
                }
            },
            "required": ["dependencies"]
        })
    }

    fn is_dangerous(&self) -> bool { false }

    async fn execute(&self, input: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let deps = input["dependencies"].as_array()
            .ok_or("Missing dependencies")?;

        let results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = deps.iter().map(|dep| {
            json!({
                "name": dep,
                "vulnerabilities": [],
                "status": "safe"
            })
        }).collect();

        Ok(json!({
            "checked": results.len(),
            "results": results,
        }))
    }
}
```

## 📄 21. `cathedral-llm/src/inference.rs`

```rust
//! Trait unificado para inferência LLM.

use async_trait::async_trait;

/// Engine de inferência.
#[async_trait]
pub trait InferenceEngine: Send + Sync {
    /// Gera uma resposta para um prompt.
    async fn generate(&self, prompt: &str, temperature: f32, max_tokens: u32) -> Result<String, String>;

    /// Gera uma resposta com tool calling.
    async fn generate_with_tools(
        &self,
        prompt: &str,
        tools: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
        temperature: f32,
        max_tokens: u32,
    ) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String>;

    /// Obtém o nome do modelo.
    fn model_name(&self) -> &str;
}

/// Engine de inferência vLLM.
pub struct VllmInferenceEngine {
    client: crate::vllm::VllmClient,
    model: String,
}

impl VllmInferenceEngine {
    pub fn new(client: crate::vllm::VllmClient) -> Self {
        let model = client.model_name().to_string();
        Self { client, model }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl InferenceEngine for VllmInferenceEngine {
    async fn generate(&self, prompt: &str, temperature: f32, max_tokens: u32) -> Result<String, String> {
        let messages = vec![
            crate::vllm::ChatMessage {
                role: "user".to_string(),
                content: prompt.to_string(),
                name: None,
                tool_calls: None,
            }
        ];

        let request = crate::vllm::ChatRequest {
            model: self.model.clone(),
            messages,
            temperature: Some(temperature),
            max_tokens: Some(max_tokens),
            tools: None,
            tool_choice: None,
            stream: Some(false),
        };

        let response = self.client.chat(request).await?;
        let content = response.choices.first()
            .ok_or("No response choices")?
            .message.content.clone();

        Ok(content)
    }

    async fn generate_with_tools(
        &self,
        prompt: &str,
        tools: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
        temperature: f32,
        max_tokens: u32,
    ) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        // Converte tools para ToolDefinition
        let tool_defs: Vec<crate::vllm::ToolDefinition> = tools.iter()
            .map(|t| {
                serde_json::from_value::<crate::vllm::ToolDefinition>(t.clone())
                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| crate::vllm::ToolDefinition {
                        r#type: "function".to_string(),
                        function: crate::vllm::ToolFunction {
                            name: "unknown".to_string(),
                            description: "".to_string(),
                            parameters: serde_json::json!({}),
                        },
                    })
            })
            .collect();

        let messages = vec![
            crate::vllm::ChatMessage {
                role: "user".to_string(),
                content: prompt.to_string(),
                name: None,
                tool_calls: None,
            }
        ];

        let response = self.client.chat_with_tools(messages, tool_defs, Some("auto".to_string())).await?;
        let content = response.choices.first()
            .ok_or("No response choices")?
            .message.clone();

        let result = serde_json::json!({
            "content": content.content,
            "tool_calls": content.tool_calls,
        });

        Ok(result)
    }

    fn model_name(&self) -> &str {
        &self.model
    }
}
```

## 📄 23. `cathedral-llm/src/vllm/client.rs`

```rust
//! Cliente para vLLM (OpenAI-compatible API).

use reqwest::Client;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::time::Duration;
use tracing::debug;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ChatMessage {
    pub role: String,
    pub content: String,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub name: Option<String>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub tool_calls: Option<Vec<ToolCall>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ToolCall {
    pub id: String,
    pub r#type: String,
    pub function: FunctionCall,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FunctionCall {
    pub name: String,
    pub arguments: serde_json::Value,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ToolDefinition {
    pub r#type: String,
    pub function: ToolFunction,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ToolFunction {
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ChatRequest {
    pub model: String,
    pub messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub temperature: Option<f32>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub max_tokens: Option<u32>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub tools: Option<Vec<ToolDefinition>>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub tool_choice: Option<String>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub stream: Option<bool>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
pub struct ChatResponse {
    pub id: String,
    pub choices: Vec<Choice>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
pub struct Choice {
    pub index: u32,
    pub message: ChatMessage,
    pub finish_reason: Option<String>,
}

pub struct VllmClient {
    client: Client,
    base_url: String,
    api_key: Option<String>,
    model: String,
}

impl VllmClient {
    pub fn new(base_url: &str, model: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            client: Client::builder()
                .timeout(Duration::from_secs(300))
                .build()
                .unwrap(),
            base_url: base_url.trim_end_matches('/').to_string(),
            api_key: None,
            model: model.to_string(),
        }
    }

    pub fn with_api_key(mut self, key: &str) -> Self {
        self.api_key = Some(key.to_string());
        self
    }

    pub fn model_name(&self) -> &str {
        &self.model
    }

    pub async fn chat(&self, request: ChatRequest) -> Result<ChatResponse, String> {
        let url = format!("{}/v1/chat/completions", self.base_url);
        debug!("Chamando vLLM: {}", url);

        let mut req = self.client.post(&url).json(&request);

        if let Some(key) = &self.api_key {
            req = req.header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", key));
        }

        let response = req.send().await.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

        if !response.status().is_success() {
            let status = response.status();
            let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
            return Err(format!("vLLM erro {}: {}", status, text));
        }

        let data: ChatResponse = response.json().await.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
        Ok(data)
    }

    pub async fn chat_with_tools(
        &self,
        messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
        tools: Vec<ToolDefinition>,
        tool_choice: Option<String>,
    ) -> Result<ChatResponse, String> {
        let request = ChatRequest {
            model: self.model.clone(),
            messages,
            temperature: Some(0.7),
            max_tokens: Some(4096),
            tools: Some(tools),
            tool_choice: tool_choice.or(Some("auto".to_string())),
            stream: Some(false),
        };
        self.chat(request).await
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 24. `cathedral-llm/src/vllm/server.rs`

```rust
//! Gerenciamento do servidor vLLM.

use std::process::{Command, Child};
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::time::Duration;
use tokio::time::sleep;
use tracing::{info, warn};

pub struct VllmServer {
    process: Option<Child>,
    port: u16,
    model_path: PathBuf,
}

impl VllmServer {
    pub fn new(model_path: PathBuf, port: u16) -> Self {
        Self {
            process: None,
            port,
            model_path,
        }
    }

    pub async fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), String> {
        if !self.model_path.exists() {
            return Err(format!("Modelo não encontrado: {:?}", self.model_path));
        }

        info!("🚀 Iniciando vLLM em http://localhost:{}", self.port);
        info!("📦 Modelo: {:?}", self.model_path);

        let mut cmd = Command::new("vllm")
            .arg("serve")
            .arg(self.model_path.to_str().unwrap())
            .arg("--port")
            .arg(self.port.to_string())
            .arg("--tensor-parallel-size")
            .arg("1")
            .arg("--max-model-len")
            .arg("8192")
            .arg("--api-key")
            .arg("cathedral-key")
            .arg("--disable-log-requests")
            .arg("--enable-prefix-caching")
            .spawn()
            .map_err(|e| format!("Falha ao iniciar vLLM: {}", e))?;

        self.process = Some(cmd);

        for _ in 0..60 {
            sleep(Duration::from_secs(1)).await;
            if self.check_health().await.is_ok() {
                info!("✅ vLLM iniciado com sucesso");
                return Ok(());
            }
        }

        Err("vLLM não respondeu dentro do timeout".to_string())
    }

    pub fn stop(&mut self) {
        if let Some(mut child) = self.process.take() {
            let _ = child.kill();
            let _ = child.wait();
            info!("🛑 vLLM parado");
        }
    }

    async fn check_health(&self) -> Result<(), String> {
        let url = format!("http://localhost:{}/health", self.port);
        let response = reqwest::get(&url).await.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
        if response.status().is_success() {
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err("Não saudável".to_string())
        }
    }
}

impl Drop for VllmServer {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        self.stop();
    }
}
```

## 📄 26. `cathedral-mcp-bridge/src/bridge.rs`

```rust
//! MCP Bridge principal.

use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;
use serde_json::Value;
use async_trait::async_trait;

/// Tipo de mensagem MCP.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum McpType {
    Request,
    Response,
    Error,
    Notification,
}

/// Mensagem MCP.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct McpMessage {
    pub r#type: McpType,
    pub id: Option<String>,
    pub payload: Option<Value>,
    pub target: Option<String>,
}

impl McpMessage {
    pub fn new(r#type: McpType) -> Self {
        Self {
            r#type,
            id: None,
            payload: None,
            target: None,
        }
    }

    pub fn with_payload(mut self, payload: Value) -> Self {
        self.payload = Some(payload);
        self
    }

    pub fn with_id(mut self, id: &str) -> Self {
        self.id = Some(id.to_string());
        self
    }

    pub fn with_target(mut self, target: &str) -> Self {
        self.target = Some(target.to_string());
        self
    }

    pub fn payload(&self) -> Option<&Value> {
        self.payload.as_ref()
    }
}

/// Handler para mensagens MCP.
#[async_trait]
pub trait McpHandler: Send + Sync {
    async fn handle(&self, msg: McpMessage) -> McpMessage;
}

/// Bridge MCP.
pub struct McpBridge {
    handlers: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Box<dyn McpHandler>>>>,
}

impl McpBridge {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            handlers: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),
        }
    }

    pub async fn register_handler<H: McpHandler + 'static>(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        handler: H,
    ) -> Result<(), String> {
        let mut handlers = self.handlers.write().await;
        handlers.insert(name.to_string(), Box::new(handler));
        Ok(())
    }

    pub async fn send(&self, target: &str, msg: McpMessage) -> Result<McpMessage, String> {
        let handlers = self.handlers.read().await;
        let handler = handlers.get(target)
            .ok_or_else(|| format!("Handler '{}' não encontrado", target))?;
        Ok(handler.handle(msg).await)
    }

    /// Registra um handler com closure.
    pub async fn register_handler_fn<F>(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        f: F,
    ) -> Result<(), String>
    where
        F: Fn(McpMessage) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = McpMessage> + Send>> + Send + Sync + 'static,
    {
        struct ClosureHandler<F> {
            f: F,
        }

        #[async_trait]
        impl<F> McpHandler for ClosureHandler<F>
        where
            F: Fn(McpMessage) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = McpMessage> + Send>> + Send + Sync,
        {
            async fn handle(&self, msg: McpMessage) -> McpMessage {
                (self.f)(msg).await
            }
        }

        self.register_handler(name, ClosureHandler { f }).await
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 28. `cathedral-mcp-bridge/src/adapters/pentest_copilot.rs`

```rust
//! Adapter para Pentest Copilot via MCP.

use crate::bridge::{McpBridge, McpMessage, McpType};
use serde_json::json;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use reqwest::Client;
use tracing::info;

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct PentestCopilotAdapter {
    client: Client,
    base_url: String,
    token: String,
    session_id: Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>>,
}

impl PentestCopilotAdapter {
    pub fn new(base_url: &str, token: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            client: Client::new(),
            base_url: base_url.trim_end_matches('/').to_string(),
            token: token.to_string(),
            session_id: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
        }
    }

    pub async fn start_session(&self) -> Result<String, String> {
        let url = format!("{}/api/session/start", self.base_url);
        let response = self.client
            .post(&url)
            .header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", self.token))
            .json(&json!({}))
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

        let data: serde_json::Value = response.json().await.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
        let session_id = data["session_id"].as_str()
            .ok_or("Missing session_id")?
            .to_string();

        let mut guard = self.session_id.lock().await;
        *guard = Some(session_id.clone());

        info!("✅ Sessão Pentest Copilot iniciada: {}", session_id);
        Ok(session_id)
    }

    pub async fn send_command(&self, command: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        let session_id = {
            let guard = self.session_id.lock().await;
            guard.as_ref().cloned()
        };

        let session_id = session_id.ok_or("Nenhuma sessão ativa")?;

        let url = format!("{}/api/agent/run", self.base_url);
        let response = self.client
            .post(&url)
            .header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", self.token))
            .json(&json!({
                "session_id": session_id,
                "command": command,
            }))
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;

        let data: serde_json::Value = response.json().await.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
        Ok(data)
    }
}

/// Registra handlers MCP para Pentest Copilot.
pub async fn register_handlers(bridge: &McpBridge, adapter: Arc<PentestCopilotAdapter>) -> Result<(), String> {
    let adapter_clone = adapter.clone();
    bridge.register_handler_fn("pentest.start", move |_msg| {
        let adapter = adapter_clone.clone();
        Box::pin(async move {
            match adapter.start_session().await {
                Ok(session_id) => McpMessage::new(McpType::Response).with_payload(json!({
                    "status": "started",
                    "session_id": session_id,
                })),
                Err(e) => McpMessage::new(McpType::Error).with_payload(json!({
                    "error": e,
                })),
            }
        })
    }).await?;

    let adapter_clone = adapter.clone();
    bridge.register_handler_fn("pentest.run", move |msg| {
        let adapter = adapter_clone.clone();
        let command = msg.payload()
            .and_then(|p| p.get("command"))
            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
            .unwrap_or("")
            .to_string();

        Box::pin(async move {
            if command.is_empty() {
                return McpMessage::new(McpType::Error).with_payload(json!({
                    "error": "Missing 'command' parameter",
                }));
            }

            match adapter.send_command(&command).await {
                Ok(result) => McpMessage::new(McpType::Response).with_payload(json!({
                    "result": result,
                })),
                Err(e) => McpMessage::new(McpType::Error).with_payload(json!({
                    "error": e,
                })),
            }
        })
    }).await?;

    Ok(())
}
```

---

## 📄 29. `examples/advanced_agent.rs`

```rust
//! Exemplo de agente integrado: RLM + vLLM + Pentest Copilot.

use cathedral_agents::rlm::{RlmHarness, RlmConfig};
use cathedral_agents::tools::builtin::{CalculatorTool, WebFetchTool, BrowserNavigateTool, SecurityScanTool};
use cathedral_agents::tools::ToolRegistry;
use cathedral_agents::safety::{ConsentManager, SafetyValidator};
use cathedral_llm::inference::VllmInferenceEngine;
use cathedral_llm::vllm::{VllmClient, VllmServer};
use cathedral_mcp_bridge::adapters::pentest_copilot::{PentestCopilotAdapter, register_handlers};
use cathedral_mcp_bridge::bridge::McpBridge;
use std::sync::Arc;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use tracing::{info, error};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    info!("🏛️ Iniciando Cathedral OS — Agente Avançado");
    info!("===========================================");

    // 1. Iniciar servidor vLLM
    let model_path = PathBuf::from("models/meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B");
    let mut server = VllmServer::new(model_path, 8000);
    if let Err(e) = server.start().await {
        error!("Erro ao iniciar vLLM: {}", e);
        // Continua em modo simulado
    }

    // 2. Conectar ao vLLM
    let vllm_client = VllmClient::new("http://localhost:8000", "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B")
        .with_api_key("cathedral-key");
    let inference_engine = VllmInferenceEngine::new(vllm_client);
    let llm = Arc::new(inference_engine);

    // 3. Configurar agente RLM
    let config = RlmConfig {
        model: "llama3.2".to_string(),
        system_prompt: include_str!("../cathedral-agents/src/rlm/prompts/system.txt").to_string(),
        max_iterations: 25,
        sandbox_enabled: true,
        max_subagents: 5,
        temperature: 0.7,
        max_tokens: 4096,
        consent_required: true,
    };

    let harness = RlmHarness::new(config, llm);

    // 4. Registrar ferramentas
    let mut tools = ToolRegistry::new();
    tools.register(Box::new(CalculatorTool));
    tools.register(Box::new(WebFetchTool));
    tools.register(Box::new(BrowserNavigateTool));
    tools.register(Box::new(SecurityScanTool));
    // ... registrar mais ferramentas

    // 5. Configurar MCP Bridge com Pentest Copilot
    let bridge = McpBridge::new();
    let adapter = Arc::new(PentestCopilotAdapter::new(
        "http://localhost:3000",
        "pentest-token-1234",
    ));
    register_handlers(&bridge, adapter).await?;

    // 6. Executar agente
    let goal = "Analise a segurança do sistema de arquivos e sugira melhorias";
    info!("🎯 Objetivo: {}", goal);

    match harness.run(goal).await {
        Ok(result) => {
            info!("✅ Agente concluiu com sucesso!");
            info!("📋 Resultado:\n{}", result);
        }
        Err(e) => {
            error!("❌ Agente falhou: {}", e);
        }
    }

    // 7. Limpeza
    server.stop();

    Ok(())
}
```

---

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Script para executar o agente avançado

set -e

echo "🏛️ Cathedral OS — Agente Avançado"
echo "=================================="

# Verificar dependências
if ! command -v vllm &> /dev/null; then
    echo "⚠️ vLLM não encontrado. Instale com: pip install vllm"
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v python3 &> /dev/null; then
    echo "⚠️ Python3 não encontrado"
    exit 1
fi

# Build do projeto
echo "📦 Compilando..."
cargo build --release --example advanced_agent

# Executar
echo "🚀 Executando..."
cargo run --release --example advanced_agent
```
