## 🔄 1. `examples/multi_agent_consensus.rs`

**Propósito:** Demonstra como 3 agentes usam TPM para chegar a um consenso sobre um valor compartilhado, sem líder central.

```rust
//! Multi-agent consensus using Tree Parity Machines.
//!
//! This example demonstrates how multiple agents can synchronize their
//! internal states using TPMs without revealing sensitive information.
//!
//! Run: cargo run --example multi_agent_consensus

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use rand::Rng;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use tracing::{info, debug};

/// A simplified agent with TPM consensus capability.
struct ConsensusAgent {
    id: usize,
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    consensus_state: Option<Vec<u8>>,
}

impl ConsensusAgent {
    fn new(id: usize, hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        Self {
            id,
            tpm,
            consensus_state: None,
        }
    }

    /// Propose a value by broadcasting a public output.
    fn propose(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    /// Update the TPM based on public input and target output.
    fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target: i32) {
        self.tpm.update(input, target);
    }

    /// Check if synchronized with another agent.
    fn is_synchronized(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.tpm.is_synchronized(&other.tpm)
    }

    /// Derive consensus key from TPM weights.
    fn derive_key(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
        TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32)
    }

    /// Reset consensus state.
    fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.consensus_state = None;
    }
}

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    println!("🏛️ Cathedral OS — Multi-Agent Consensus with TPM");
    println!("================================================");

    // Configuration
    const NUM_AGENTS: usize = 3;
    const HIDDEN: usize = 3;
    const INPUT_SIZE: usize = 20;
    const WEIGHT_RANGE: i32 = 3;
    const MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 100;

    // 1. Create agents with different initial TPM weights
    let mut agents: Vec<ConsensusAgent> = (0..NUM_AGENTS)
        .map(|id| ConsensusAgent::new(id, HIDDEN, INPUT_SIZE, WEIGHT_RANGE))
        .collect();

    // 2. Generate public inputs (shared by all agents)
    let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
    let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..MAX_ITERATIONS)
        .map(|_| (0..INPUT_SIZE).map(|_| rng.gen_range(-1..=1)).collect())
        .collect();

    // 3. Display initial weights divergence
    println!("\n📊 Initial state:");
    for (i, agent) in agents.iter().enumerate() {
        let flat = agent.tpm.flatten_weights();
        println!("   Agent {}: {} weights (sample: {:?})", i, flat.len(), &flat[..5]);
    }

    // 4. Pairwise synchronization (simplified multi-party protocol)
    println!("\n🔄 Synchronizing agents...");
    let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(MAX_ITERATIONS, 0);

    // We'll synchronize all agents to agent 0 (but in a real scenario, there's no leader)
    // Here we just demonstrate that all converge to the same state.
    let target_agent = 0;
    for i in 1..NUM_AGENTS {
        let mut agent_a = agents[target_agent].tpm.clone();
        let mut agent_b = agents[i].tpm.clone();

        let result = synchronizer.synchronize(&mut agent_a, &mut agent_b, &inputs);
        match result {
            SyncResult::Success => {
                info!("Agent {} synchronized with agent {}", i, target_agent);
                // Apply synchronized TPM back to agent i
                agents[i].tpm = agent_b;
            }
            _ => {
                eprintln!("Agent {} failed to synchronize with agent {}", i, target_agent);
            }
        }
    }

    // 5. Verify all agents are synchronized
    println!("\n✅ Consensus result:");
    let mut all_synced = true;
    for i in 1..NUM_AGENTS {
        let synced = agents[0].is_synchronized(&agents[i]);
        println!("   Agent 0 ↔ Agent {}: {}", i, if synced { "✅ Synced" } else { "❌ Diverged" });
        if !synced {
            all_synced = false;
        }
    }

    if all_synced {
        println!("\n🎉 All agents reached consensus!");

        // Derive a shared key from agent 0
        let key = agents[0].derive_key();
        println!("🔑 Shared key: {:x?}", &key[..8]);

        // All agents can now derive the same key
        for i in 1..NUM_AGENTS {
            let key_i = agents[i].derive_key();
            assert_eq!(key, key_i, "Key mismatch between agent 0 and {}", i);
        }
        println!("✅ All agents derived identical keys!");

        // Store consensus state in each agent
        for agent in &mut agents {
            agent.consensus_state = Some(key.clone());
        }
    } else {
        println!("\n⚠️ Consensus not fully reached. Try increasing iterations.");
    }

    // 6. Demonstrate that agents can continue to update their TPMs independently
    // without breaking consensus (they remain synchronized)
    println!("\n🔄 Agents processing independent observations...");
    let new_input = inputs.last().cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| vec![0; INPUT_SIZE]);

    for (i, agent) in agents.iter_mut().enumerate() {
        let output = agent.propose(&new_input);
        agent.update(&new_input, output);
        println!("   Agent {} updated with output: {}", i, output);
    }

    // Verify they remain synchronized
    let still_synced = agents[0].is_synchronized(&agents[1]);
    println!("   Agents still synchronized: {}", still_synced);

    if still_synced {
        let new_key = agents[0].derive_key();
        println!("🔑 New shared key (after update): {:x?}", &new_key[..8]);
    }

    println!("\n🏛️ Multi-agent consensus demonstration complete.");
    Ok(())
}
```

---

## 🔒 2. `examples/fwupd_agent.rs`

**Propósito:** Demonstra um agente que monitora dispositivos via fwupd e toma decisões baseadas no estado de firmware.

```rust
//! Agent with fwupd hardware security monitoring.
//!
//! This example shows how an agent can query device firmware status
//! and take actions (e.g., trigger updates) based on security policies.
//!
//! Run: cargo run --example fwupd_agent

use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdClient, FwupdPermissionManager, FirmwareAction};
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use tracing::{info, warn, error};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    println!("🏛️ Cathedral OS — fwupd Security Agent");
    println!("=======================================");

    // 1. Connect to fwupd daemon
    let client = match FwupdClient::new().await {
        Ok(c) => Arc::new(Mutex::new(c)),
        Err(e) => {
            eprintln!("❌ Failed to connect to fwupd: {}", e);
            eprintln!("   Make sure fwupd is running: systemctl status fwupd");
            return Ok(());
        }
    };

    // 2. Set up permission manager
    let permission_manager = Arc::new(FwupdPermissionManager::new());

    // Allow updates for specific device types (example)
    // In a real scenario, this would be configurable via policy.
    permission_manager.allow_action(FirmwareAction::Update).await;

    // 3. List all devices
    println!("\n📟 Scanning devices...");
    let devices = {
        let c = client.lock().await;
        c.list_devices().await?
    };

    if devices.is_empty() {
        println!("   No devices found.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    println!("   Found {} devices:", devices.len());
    for device in &devices {
        let status = if device.update_available {
            "⚠️ UPDATE AVAILABLE"
        } else {
            "✅ Up to date"
        };
        println!(
            "   - {} ({}): v{} [{}]",
            device.name, device.id, device.version, status
        );
    }

    // 4. Identify devices that need updates
    let devices_needing_update: Vec<_> = devices
        .iter()
        .filter(|d| d.update_available)
        .collect();

    if devices_needing_update.is_empty() {
        println!("\n✅ All devices are up to date.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    println!("\n🔍 Devices requiring updates:");
    for device in &devices_needing_update {
        println!(
            "   - {}: current {} → available {}",
            device.name,
            device.version,
            device.update_version.as_deref().unwrap_or("unknown")
        );
    }

    // 5. Check permissions and apply updates (if allowed)
    let mut update_results = Vec::new();

    for device in devices_needing_update {
        let device_id = device.id.clone();
        let device_name = device.name.clone();

        // Check if update is allowed by policy
        let allowed = permission_manager
            .is_allowed(&device_id, FirmwareAction::Update)
            .await;

        if !allowed {
            warn!("⚠️ Update not allowed for {} (policy)", device_name);
            update_results.push((device_name, "Blocked by policy".to_string()));
            continue;
        }

        // Check if the device is critical (e.g., firmware version < some threshold)
        let current_version = &device.version;
        let critical = current_version.starts_with('0') || current_version.starts_with('1');

        if critical {
            warn!("🚨 Critical device {} has outdated firmware!", device_name);
            // In a real scenario, we would escalate or require manual approval
            update_results.push((device_name, "Critical - manual approval required".to_string()));
            continue;
        }

        // Perform update
        info!("🔄 Updating {}...", device_name);
        let result = {
            let mut c = client.lock().await;
            c.update_device(&device_id, 0).await
        };

        match result {
            Ok(_) => {
                info!("✅ Update successful for {}", device_name);
                update_results.push((device_name, "Success".to_string()));
            }
            Err(e) => {
                error!("❌ Update failed for {}: {}", device_name, e);
                update_results.push((device_name, format!("Failed: {}", e)));
            }
        }
    }

    // 6. Summary
    println!("\n📊 Update Summary:");
    for (name, status) in update_results {
        println!("   - {}: {}", name, status);
    }

    // 7. Audit trail (simulated)
    println!("\n📜 Audit Trail:");
    println!("   Agent DID: did:cathedral:fwupd-agent");
    println!("   Timestamp: {}", chrono::Utc::now());
    println!("   Devices audited: {}", devices.len());
    println!("   Updates applied: {}", update_results.iter().filter(|(_, s)| s == "Success").count());

    // 8. Save audit to WormGraph (placeholder)
    // In a real implementation, you would call wormgraph.record_event()
    println!("   Audit saved to WormGraph (simulated).");

    println!("\n🏛️ fwupd agent completed.");
    Ok(())
}
```

---

## 🛡️ 3. `examples/secure_cluster.rs`

**Propósito:** Demonstra um cluster de agentes que combina:
- Consenso TPM para chave compartilhada
- fwupd para verificação de hardware
- Autenticação multifatorial para ações críticas

```rust
//! Secure cluster with consensus + fwupd + multifactor authentication.
//!
//! This example demonstrates a cluster of agents working together securely:
//! - TPM consensus for shared state
//! - fwupd for hardware security checks
//! - Multifactor authentication for sensitive operations
//!
//! Run: cargo run --example secure_cluster

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer};
use cathedral_identity::{AuthManager, AuthFactors, ContextData, Did};
use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdClient, FwupdPermissionManager, FirmwareAction};
use chrono::Utc;
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::{Mutex, RwLock};
use tracing::{info, warn, error};

/// A secure agent in the cluster.
struct SecureAgent {
    id: usize,
    did: Did,
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    consensus_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    auth_manager: AuthManager,
    /// Hardware security status (from fwupd)
    hardware_secure: bool,
}

impl SecureAgent {
    fn new(id: usize, hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let did = Did::from_string(&format!("did:cathedral:agent:{}", id));
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        Self {
            id,
            did,
            tpm,
            consensus_key: None,
            auth_manager: AuthManager::new(),
            hardware_secure: false,
        }
    }

    fn propose(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target: i32) {
        self.tpm.update(input, target);
    }

    fn is_synchronized(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.tpm.is_synchronized(&other.tpm)
    }

    fn derive_key(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
        TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32)
    }

    fn set_consensus_key(&mut self, key: Vec<u8>) {
        self.consensus_key = Some(key);
    }

    fn set_hardware_secure(&mut self, secure: bool) {
        self.hardware_secure = secure;
    }

    /// Authenticate a multifactor credential for this agent.
    fn authenticate(&mut self, factors: AuthFactors) -> String {
        self.auth_manager.issue_credential(&factors).token
    }

    /// Verify a one-time credential.
    fn verify_credential(&mut self, token: &str, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        self.auth_manager.validate_and_consume(token, factors)
    }

    /// Execute a sensitive action (requires consensus key + multifactor + hardware security).
    async fn execute_sensitive_action(
        &mut self,
        action: &str,
        params: serde_json::Value,
        credential_token: &str,
        factors: &AuthFactors,
    ) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        // 1. Verify consensus key is present
        if self.consensus_key.is_none() {
            return Err("No consensus key established".to_string());
        }

        // 2. Verify multifactor credential
        if !self.verify_credential(credential_token, factors) {
            return Err("Multifactor authentication failed".to_string());
        }

        // 3. Verify hardware security
        if !self.hardware_secure {
            return Err("Hardware not secure (firmware outdated or tampered)".to_string());
        }

        // 4. Execute action (simulated)
        info!("🔒 Agent {} executing sensitive action: {}", self.id, action);
        Ok(serde_json::json!({
            "status": "success",
            "action": action,
            "agent": self.id,
            "timestamp": Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
            "params": params,
        }))
    }
}

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    println!("🏛️ Cathedral OS — Secure Cluster with TPM + fwupd + Multi-Factor");
    println!("================================================================");

    // 1. Create cluster of 3 agents
    const NUM_AGENTS: usize = 3;
    let mut agents: Vec<SecureAgent> = (0..NUM_AGENTS)
        .map(|i| SecureAgent::new(i, 3, 20, 3))
        .collect();

    // 2. Establish consensus key using TPM (simplified)
    println!("\n🔑 Establishing consensus key via TPM...");
    let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
    let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..200)
        .map(|_| (0..20).map(|_| rand::random::<i32>() % 3 - 1).collect())
        .collect();

    // Synchronize all agents to agent 0
    let target_agent = 0;
    for i in 1..NUM_AGENTS {
        let mut tpm_a = agents[target_agent].tpm.clone();
        let mut tpm_b = agents[i].tpm.clone();
        let result = synchronizer.synchronize(&mut tpm_a, &mut tpm_b, &inputs);
        if result == cathedral_consensus_tpm::SyncResult::Success {
            agents[i].tpm = tpm_b;
            info!("Agent {} synchronized with agent {}", i, target_agent);
        } else {
            eprintln!("Agent {} failed to synchronize", i);
        }
    }

    // Verify all agents are synchronized and derive key
    let mut all_synced = true;
    for i in 1..NUM_AGENTS {
        if !agents[0].is_synchronized(&agents[i]) {
            all_synced = false;
            warn!("Agent {} not synchronized", i);
        }
    }

    if all_synced {
        let key = agents[0].derive_key();
        for agent in &mut agents {
            agent.set_consensus_key(key.clone());
        }
        println!("✅ Consensus key established: {:x?}", &key[..8]);
    } else {
        println!("⚠️ Consensus not fully reached. Exiting.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    // 3. Hardware security check via fwupd
    println!("\n🔍 Checking hardware security via fwupd...");
    let fwupd_client = match FwupdClient::new().await {
        Ok(c) => Arc::new(Mutex::new(c)),
        Err(e) => {
            println!("⚠️ fwupd not available: {}", e);
            println!("   Simulating hardware security (fallback).");
            // In simulation, mark some agents secure and some not
            for (i, agent) in agents.iter_mut().enumerate() {
                agent.set_hardware_secure(i % 2 == 0);
            }
            // Continue with simulated security
            return run_security_simulation(agents).await;
        }
    };

    // Query devices and assess security
    let devices = {
        let c = fwupd_client.lock().await;
        c.list_devices().await?
    };

    println!("   Found {} devices.", devices.len());

    // Simple security policy: if any critical device has updates available, mark insecure
    let has_critical_update = devices.iter().any(|d| d.update_available && d.vendor.contains("Intel"));
    if has_critical_update {
        println!("⚠️ Critical firmware updates available. Cluster not fully secure.");
        for agent in &mut agents {
            agent.set_hardware_secure(false);
        }
    } else {
        println!("✅ No critical firmware updates. Cluster hardware secure.");
        for agent in &mut agents {
            agent.set_hardware_secure(true);
        }
    }

    // 4. Multifactor authentication for each agent
    println!("\n🔐 Multifactor authentication...");
    let mut credentials = HashMap::new();

    for agent in &mut agents {
        let context = ContextData {
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
            location: Some("Cluster-01".to_string()),
            device_id: Some(format!("agent-{}", agent.id)),
            ip_address: Some("10.0.0.1".to_string()),
        };

        let factors = AuthFactors {
            did: agent.did.clone(),
            behavior_signature: vec![agent.id as u8, 0xAA, 0xBB, 0xCC],
            context: context.clone(),
            hardware_proof: if agent.hardware_secure {
                Some(vec![0xDE, 0xAD, 0xBE, 0xEF])
            } else {
                None
            },
        };

        let token = agent.authenticate(factors.clone());
        credentials.insert(agent.id, (token, factors));
        println!("   Agent {} credential issued: {}", agent.id, &token[..8]);
    }

    // 5. Execute a sensitive action that requires consensus + multifactor + hardware
    println!("\n⚡ Executing sensitive cluster action...");
    let action = "deploy_asset";

    for (i, agent) in agents.iter_mut().enumerate() {
        let (token, factors) = credentials.get(&i).unwrap().clone();

        let params = serde_json::json!({
            "asset": "BTC",
            "amount": "0.001",
            "target_cluster": "cluster-02",
        });

        match agent.execute_sensitive_action(action, params, &token, &factors).await {
            Ok(result) => {
                println!("✅ Agent {}: {}", i, result);
            }
            Err(e) => {
                error!("❌ Agent {} failed: {}", i, e);
            }
        }
    }

    // 6. Attempt action without hardware security (should fail for agents without it)
    println!("\n🔓 Attempting action without hardware security...");
    for agent in &mut agents {
        if !agent.hardware_secure {
            let (token, factors) = credentials.get(&agent.id).unwrap().clone();
            let params = serde_json::json!({ "action": "unauthorized" });
            match agent.execute_sensitive_action("illegal_action", params, &token, &factors).await {
                Ok(_) => println!("⚠️ Agent {} succeeded despite hardware insecurity (bug)", agent.id),
                Err(e) => println!("✅ Agent {} blocked: {}", agent.id, e),
            }
        }
    }

    println!("\n🏛️ Secure cluster demonstration complete.");
    Ok(())
}

/// Run security simulation when fwupd is unavailable.
async fn run_security_simulation(mut agents: Vec<SecureAgent>) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    println!("\n🔍 Running security simulation (fwupd fallback)...");
    // Simulate hardware security check: even-numbered agents are secure
    for (i, agent) in agents.iter_mut().enumerate() {
        let secure = i % 2 == 0;
        agent.set_hardware_secure(secure);
        println!("   Agent {} secure: {}", i, secure);
    }

    // Continue with the rest of the example (multifactor, actions)
    // We'll reuse the same logic as above by refactoring.
    // For brevity, we just print a message and exit.
    println!("✅ Simulation complete. Hardware security status set.");
    Ok(())
}
```

## 🔧 2. INTEGRAÇÃO DO `FwupdTool` NO `UniversalModel`

### `src/universal_model.rs` (atualizado)

```rust
//! Main UniversalModel with all subsystems integrated.
//!
//! Includes:
//! - Neural Engine (llama-cpp-2)
//! - Tools: Calculator, Hashtree, Memory, Action, **Fwupd**
//! - TPM-based consensus and key reconciliation
//! - Multifactor authentication

use crate::config::UniversalConfig;
use crate::error::Result;
use crate::memory::LongTermMemory;
use crate::neural::NeuralEngine;
use crate::pipeline::UniversalPipeline;
use crate::reasoning::CausalReasoner;
use crate::tools::{
    ActionTool, CalculatorTool, HashtreeTool, MemoryTool, ToolRegistry,
};
use crate::types::*;
use cathedral_identity::{Did, KeyReconciliation, AuthManager, AuthFactors, ContextData};
use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdClient, FwupdTool, FwupdPermissionManager};
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::TreeParityMachine;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;
use tracing::info;

pub struct UniversalModel {
    pipeline: Arc<UniversalPipeline>,
    reasoner: Arc<RwLock<CausalReasoner>>,
    state: Arc<RwLock<ModelState>>,
    agent_did: Did,
    // Novos componentes
    key_reconciliation: Arc<RwLock<KeyReconciliation>>,
    auth_manager: Arc<RwLock<AuthManager>>,
    fwupd_tool: Arc<FwupdTool>,
}

impl UniversalModel {
    pub async fn new(config: UniversalConfig, agent_did: Did) -> Result<Self> {
        info!("🚀 Initializing Universal Model with full security stack");

        // 1. Neural Engine
        NeuralEngine::init(&config.model_path, 4096).await?;

        // 2. Memory
        let memory = Arc::new(RwLock::new(
            LongTermMemory::new(config.memory_config.clone(), agent_did.clone()).await?
        ));

        // 3. State
        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(ModelState::default()));

        // 4. Key Reconciliation (TPM)
        let key_reconciliation = Arc::new(RwLock::new(
            KeyReconciliation::new(3, 200, 3)
        ));

        // 5. Authentication Manager (multifactor)
        let auth_manager = Arc::new(RwLock::new(AuthManager::new()));

        // 6. Fwupd Client & Tool
        let fwupd_client = match FwupdClient::new().await {
            Ok(c) => Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(c)),
            Err(e) => {
                info!("⚠️ fwupd not available: {}", e);
                // Criar cliente mock para fallback
                // Em produção, seria melhor falhar ou usar fallback
                Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(
                    FwupdClient::new().await.expect("fwupd required")
                ))
            }
        };
        let permission_manager = Arc::new(FwupdPermissionManager::new());
        let fwupd_tool = Arc::new(
            FwupdTool::new(fwupd_client)
                .with_permission_manager(permission_manager)
        );

        // 7. Tools Registry
        let mut registry = ToolRegistry::new();
        registry.register(CalculatorTool);
        registry.register(MemoryTool::new(memory.clone()));
        registry.register(HashtreeTool);

        // Action tool with callback
        let state_clone = state.clone();
        let action_callback = move |atype: ActionType, target: &str, params: Value| -> Result<Value> {
            info!("Action requested: {:?} -> {}", atype, target);
            // Atualiza estado
            if let Ok(mut s) = state_clone.try_write() {
                s.beliefs.insert("last_action".to_string(), serde_json::json!({
                    "type": format!("{:?}", atype),
                    "target": target,
                    "params": params,
                }));
            }
            Ok(serde_json::json!({
                "status": "queued",
                "action": format!("{:?}", atype),
                "target": target,
            }))
        };
        registry.register(ActionTool::new(action_callback));

        // 8. Fwupd Tool (segurança de hardware)
        registry.register(fwupd_tool.clone());

        let tools = Arc::new(RwLock::new(registry));

        // 9. Pipeline
        let pipeline = Arc::new(UniversalPipeline::new(tools, memory, state.clone()).await);

        // 10. Reasoner
        let reasoner = Arc::new(RwLock::new(CausalReasoner::default()));

        Ok(Self {
            pipeline,
            reasoner,
            state,
            agent_did,
            key_reconciliation,
            auth_manager,
            fwupd_tool,
        })
    }

    /// Process input with optional sampling override.
    pub async fn process(&self, input: UniversalInput) -> Result<UniversalOutput> {
        self.pipeline.process(input, None).await
    }

    /// Process input with custom sampling.
    pub async fn process_with_sampling(
        &self,
        input: UniversalInput,
        sampling: crate::neural::config::SamplingConfig,
    ) -> Result<UniversalOutput> {
        self.pipeline.process(input, Some(sampling)).await
    }

    /// Get current state.
    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ModelState {
        self.state.read().await.clone()
    }

    /// Get agent DID.
    pub fn agent_did(&self) -> &Did {
        &self.agent_did
    }

    // --- Novas funcionalidades de segurança ---

    /// Establish a shared key with a peer using TPM.
    pub async fn establish_shared_key(
        &self,
        peer_did: &Did,
        peer_reconciliation: &mut KeyReconciliation,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
        let mut self_rec = self.key_reconciliation.write().await;
        let key = self_rec.reconcile(peer_reconciliation, inputs).await
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(key)
    }

    /// Issue a one-time credential for multifactor authentication.
    pub async fn issue_credential(
        &self,
        behavior_signature: Vec<u8>,
        context: ContextData,
        hardware_proof: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> String {
        let factors = AuthFactors {
            did: self.agent_did.clone(),
            behavior_signature,
            context,
            hardware_proof,
        };
        let mut auth = self.auth_manager.write().await;
        let cred = auth.issue_credential(&factors);
        cred.token
    }

    /// Validate and consume a one-time credential.
    pub async fn validate_credential(
        &self,
        token: &str,
        behavior_signature: Vec<u8>,
        context: ContextData,
        hardware_proof: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> bool {
        let factors = AuthFactors {
            did: self.agent_did.clone(),
            behavior_signature,
            context,
            hardware_proof,
        };
        let mut auth = self.auth_manager.write().await;
        auth.validate_and_consume(token, &factors)
    }

    /// Get the fwupd tool for hardware security operations.
    pub fn fwupd_tool(&self) -> &FwupdTool {
        &self.fwupd_tool
    }

    /// Perform a security audit of all devices via fwupd.
    pub async fn audit_hardware_security(&self) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
        let result = self.fwupd_tool.execute(serde_json::json!({
            "action": "list_devices"
        })).await?;
        Ok(result)
    }

    /// Update firmware for a specific device.
    pub async fn update_device_firmware(&self, device_id: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
        let result = self.fwupd_tool.execute(serde_json::json!({
            "action": "update_device",
            "device_id": device_id,
            "actor_did": self.agent_did.to_string(),
        })).await?;
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## 🧪 3. EXEMPLO COMPLETO DE USO

### `examples/secure_agent.rs`

```rust
//! Exemplo completo de um agente Cathedral OS usando:
//! - TPM para reconciliação de chaves
//! - fwupd para verificação de firmware
//! - Autenticação multifatorial

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::TreeParityMachine;
use cathedral_identity::{
    Did, KeyReconciliation, AuthManager, AuthFactors, ContextData,
};
use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::FwupdClient;
use cathedral_agents::agent::Agent;
use chrono::Utc;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // Inicializa logging
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    println!("🏛️ Cathedral OS — Agente Soberano com Segurança Quântica");

    // 1. Cria identidades (DIDs) para Alice e Bob
    let alice_did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:alice");
    let bob_did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:bob");

    // 2. Inicializa reconciliação de chaves (TPM)
    let mut alice_rec = KeyReconciliation::new(3, 100, 3);
    let mut bob_rec = KeyReconciliation::new(3, 100, 3);

    // 3. Gerar inputs públicos para sincronização
    let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..200)
        .map(|_| (0..100).map(|_| rand::random::<i32>() % 3 - 1).collect())
        .collect();

    // 4. Alice e Bob estabelecem chave compartilhada via TPM
    println!("🔑 Estabelecendo chave compartilhada via TPM...");
    let shared_key = alice_rec.reconcile(&mut bob_rec, &inputs).await?;
    println!("✅ Chave compartilhada: {:x?}", &shared_key[..8]);

    // 5. Configuração do fwupd para verificação de hardware
    println!("🔍 Verificando firmware de dispositivos...");
    let fwupd_client = match FwupdClient::new().await {
        Ok(c) => Arc::new(Mutex::new(c)),
        Err(e) => {
            println!("⚠️ fwupd não disponível: {}", e);
            println!("   Pulando verificação de firmware...");
            // Continua sem fwupd
            return Ok(());
        }
    };

    // 6. Lista dispositivos via fwupd
    let client = fwupd_client.lock().await;
    let devices = client.list_devices().await?;
    println!("📟 Dispositivos encontrados:");
    for device in devices {
        println!("   - {} (v{}) - {}",
            device.name,
            device.version,
            if device.update_available { "⚠️ Atualização disponível" } else { "✅ Atualizado" }
        );
    }
    drop(client);

    // 7. Autenticação multifatorial de Alice
    println!("🔐 Autenticando Alice com múltiplos fatores...");
    let auth_manager = AuthManager::new();

    let behavior_signature = vec![1, 2, 3, 4]; // Simulação
    let context = ContextData {
        timestamp: Utc::now(),
        location: Some("São Paulo, SP".to_string()),
        device_id: Some("device-001".to_string()),
        ip_address: Some("192.168.1.100".to_string()),
    };
    let hardware_proof = Some(vec![0xde, 0xad, 0xbe, 0xef]); // Simulação

    let factors = AuthFactors {
        did: alice_did.clone(),
        behavior_signature: behavior_signature.clone(),
        context: context.clone(),
        hardware_proof: hardware_proof.clone(),
    };

    let credential = auth_manager.issue_credential(&factors);
    println!("✅ Credencial emitida: {}", credential.token);

    // 8. Validação da credencial
    println!("🔄 Validando credencial...");
    let valid = auth_manager.validate_and_consume(&credential.token, &factors);
    if valid {
        println!("✅ Credencial validada com sucesso! (one-time)");
    } else {
        println!("❌ Validação falhou");
    }

    // 9. Tentativa de reutilização da mesma credencial (deve falhar)
    let reused = auth_manager.validate_and_consume(&credential.token, &factors);
    println!("🔁 Reutilização da credencial: {}", if reused { "✅ OK" } else { "❌ Bloqueada (one-time)" });

    // 10. Exemplo de uso combinado: agente com todos os componentes
    println!("🤖 Criando agente soberano...");
    let agent = Agent::new(
        alice_did.clone(),
        shared_key,
        auth_manager,
    );

    // 11. Simulação de ação do agente
    println!("⚡ Agente executando ação...");
    let result = agent.execute_action(
        "transfer_asset",
        serde_json::json!({
            "asset": "BTC",
            "amount": "0.001",
            "to": bob_did.to_string(),
        }),
    ).await?;
    println!("✅ Resultado: {}", result);

    println!("🏛️ Agente concluiu operação com segurança quântica!");
    Ok(())
}

/// Estrutura simplificada de um agente para demonstração.
pub struct Agent {
    did: Did,
    shared_key: Vec<u8>,
    auth_manager: AuthManager,
}

impl Agent {
    pub fn new(did: Did, shared_key: Vec<u8>, auth_manager: AuthManager) -> Self {
        Self { did, shared_key, auth_manager }
    }

    pub async fn execute_action(&self, action: &str, params: serde_json::Value) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Simula execução de ação com autenticação
        let context = ContextData {
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
            location: Some("Sao Paulo".to_string()),
            device_id: Some("device-001".to_string()),
            ip_address: Some("192.168.1.100".to_string()),
        };
        let factors = AuthFactors {
            did: self.did.clone(),
            behavior_signature: vec![1, 2, 3, 4],
            context,
            hardware_proof: None,
        };
        let cred = self.auth_manager.issue_credential(&factors);
        // Valida a credencial antes de executar
        if self.auth_manager.validate_and_consume(&cred.token, &factors) {
            Ok(format!("Ação '{}' executada com sucesso!", action))
        } else {
            Err("Falha na autenticação multifatorial".into())
        }
    }
}
```

## 🔑 1. `cathedral-identity` — Módulo KeyReconciliation + erro + multifactor

### `src/key_reconciliation.rs`

```rust
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::{Did, IdentityError};

pub struct KeyReconciliation {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
}

impl KeyReconciliation {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer }
    }

    pub fn public_output(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    pub fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target: i32) {
        self.tpm.update(input, target)
    }

    pub async fn reconcile(
        &mut self,
        peer: &mut Self,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, IdentityError> {
        let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, &mut peer.tpm, inputs);
        match result {
            SyncResult::Success => Ok(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32)),
            _ => Err(IdentityError::ReconciliationFailed),
        }
    }
}
```

### `src/error.rs` — adição

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum IdentityError {
    // ... variantes existentes ...
    #[error("Key reconciliation failed")]
    ReconciliationFailed,
    #[error("Invalid TPM input: {0}")]
    InvalidTpmInput(String),
}
```

### `src/multifactor.rs`

```rust
use crate::Did;
use blake3;
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc, Duration};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::collections::HashMap;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct AuthFactors {
    pub did: Did,
    pub behavior_signature: Vec<u8>,
    pub context: ContextData,
    pub hardware_proof: Option<Vec<u8>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ContextData {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub location: Option<String>,
    pub device_id: Option<String>,
    pub ip_address: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OneTimeCredential {
    pub token: String,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub factors_hash: String,
}

impl OneTimeCredential {
    fn normalize_timestamp(dt: &DateTime<Utc>) -> DateTime<Utc> {
        let ts = dt.timestamp();
        let rounded = (ts / 60) * 60;
        DateTime::from_timestamp(rounded, 0).unwrap_or(*dt)
    }

    pub fn new(factors: &AuthFactors, validity_seconds: i64) -> Self {
        let normalized_ts = Self::normalize_timestamp(&factors.context.timestamp);
        let combined = format!(
            "{:?}{:?}{:?}",
            factors.did.to_string(),
            hex::encode(&factors.behavior_signature),
            normalized_ts,
        );
        let hash = blake3::hash(combined.as_bytes());
        let token = hex::encode(hash.as_bytes());
        let now = Utc::now();
        Self {
            token,
            created_at: now,
            expires_at: now + Duration::seconds(validity_seconds),
            factors_hash: hex::encode(hash.as_bytes()),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool { Utc::now() < self.expires_at }

    pub fn verify(&self, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        let normalized_ts = Self::normalize_timestamp(&factors.context.timestamp);
        let combined = format!(
            "{:?}{:?}{:?}",
            factors.did.to_string(),
            hex::encode(&factors.behavior_signature),
            normalized_ts,
        );
        let hash = blake3::hash(combined.as_bytes());
        let token = hex::encode(hash.as_bytes());
        token == self.token && self.is_valid()
    }
}

pub struct AuthManager {
    credentials: HashMap<String, OneTimeCredential>,
}

impl AuthManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self { Self { credentials: HashMap::new() } }

    pub fn issue_credential(&mut self, factors: &AuthFactors) -> OneTimeCredential {
        let cred = OneTimeCredential::new(factors, 60);
        self.credentials.insert(cred.token.clone(), cred.clone());
        cred
    }

    pub fn validate_and_consume(&mut self, token: &str, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        if let Some(cred) = self.credentials.remove(token) {
            cred.verify(factors)
        } else {
            false
        }
    }
}
```

## 🤖 2. `cathedral-agents` — TpmConsensus + ConsensusError

### `src/consensus.rs`

```rust
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::error::ConsensusError;
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct TpmConsensus {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
    peers: HashMap<String, Vec<i32>>,
}

impl TpmConsensus {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer, peers: HashMap::new() }
    }

    pub fn propose_state(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    pub fn register_peer_output(&mut self, peer_id: &str, input: &[i32], output: i32) {
        self.peers.insert(peer_id.to_string(), input.to_vec());
    }

    pub async fn reach_consensus(
        &mut self,
        peer_instances: &mut [TreeParityMachine],
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ConsensusError> {
        for (i, peer_tpm) in peer_instances.iter_mut().enumerate() {
            let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, peer_tpm, inputs);
            if result != SyncResult::Success {
                return Err(ConsensusError::SynchronizationFailed(i));
            }
        }
        Ok(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32))
    }
}
```

### `src/error.rs`

```rust
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum ConsensusError {
    #[error("Synchronization failed with peer {0}")]
    SynchronizationFailed(usize),
    #[error("Timeout during consensus")]
    Timeout,
    #[error("Invalid peer TPM architecture")]
    InvalidArchitecture,
}
```

## 📡 3. `cathedral-nostr` — ReconciliableEvent

### `src/reconciliation.rs`

```rust
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ReconciliableEvent {
    pub id: String,
    pub pubkey: String,
    pub created_at: u64,
    pub kind: u64,
    pub tags: Vec<Vec<String>>,
    pub content: String,
    pub sig: String,
    pub tpm_output: i32,
    pub tpm_input: Vec<i32>,
}

impl ReconciliableEvent {
    pub fn verify_tpm(&self, tpm: &TreeParityMachine) -> bool {
        tpm.output(&self.tpm_input) == self.tpm_output
    }

    pub fn reconcile_events(
        event_a: &Self,
        event_b: &Self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
    ) -> bool {
        if event_a.tpm_input != event_b.tpm_input {
            return false;
        }
        let sync = TpmSynchronizer::new(100, 0);
        let result = sync.synchronize(tpm_a, tpm_b, &[event_a.tpm_input.clone()]);
        result == SyncResult::Success
    }
}
```

## 🤝 4. `cathedral-mcp-bridge` — FastHandshake

### `src/handshake/types.rs`

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum HandshakeState {
    Idle,
    Initiated,
    Responded,
    Synchronized,
    Complete,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum HandshakeMessage {
    Initiate { hidden: usize, input_size: usize, seed: u64 },
    Response { input: Vec<i32>, output: i32 },
    Acknowledge { confirmation: Vec<u8> },
    Complete,
}
```

### `src/handshake/mod.rs`

```rust
mod types;
pub use types::{HandshakeState, HandshakeMessage};

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use blake3;

pub struct FastHandshake {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
    peer_tpm: Option<TreeParityMachine>,
    state: HandshakeState,
}

impl FastHandshake {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(50, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer, peer_tpm: None, state: HandshakeState::Idle }
    }

    pub fn initiate(&mut self) -> HandshakeMessage {
        self.state = HandshakeState::Initiated;
        HandshakeMessage::Initiate {
            hidden: self.tpm.hidden,
            input_size: self.tpm.input_size,
            seed: rand::random(),
        }
    }

    pub fn process(&mut self, msg: HandshakeMessage) -> Result<HandshakeMessage, BridgeError> {
        match msg {
            HandshakeMessage::Initiate { hidden, input_size, .. } => {
                if hidden != self.tpm.hidden || input_size != self.tpm.input_size {
                    return Err(BridgeError::HandshakeMismatch);
                }
                self.peer_tpm = Some(TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, self.tpm.weight_range));
                let input = self.tpm.random_input();
                let output = self.tpm.output(&input);
                self.state = HandshakeState::Responded;
                Ok(HandshakeMessage::Response { input, output })
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Response { input, output } => {
                if self.state != HandshakeState::Initiated {
                    return Err(BridgeError::InvalidHandshakeState);
                }
                let mut peer_tpm = self.peer_tpm.take().ok_or(BridgeError::PeerTpmMissing)?;
                peer_tpm.update(&input, output);
                let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, &mut peer_tpm, &[input]);
                if result == SyncResult::Success {
                    self.state = HandshakeState::Synchronized;
                    let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                    let confirmation = blake3::hash(&key).as_bytes().to_vec();
                    Ok(HandshakeMessage::Acknowledge { confirmation })
                } else {
                    Err(BridgeError::HandshakeFailed)
                }
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Acknowledge { confirmation } => {
                if self.state != HandshakeState::Responded {
                    return Err(BridgeError::InvalidHandshakeState);
                }
                let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                let expected = blake3::hash(&key).as_bytes().to_vec();
                if confirmation == expected {
                    self.state = HandshakeState::Complete;
                    Ok(HandshakeMessage::Complete)
                } else {
                    Err(BridgeError::HandshakeFailed)
                }
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Complete => {
                self.state = HandshakeState::Complete;
                Ok(HandshakeMessage::Complete)
            }
        }
    }

    pub fn shared_key(&self) -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
        if self.state == HandshakeState::Complete {
            Some(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

### `src/error.rs` — extensão

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum BridgeError {
    // ... existing variants ...
    #[error("Handshake mismatch: incompatible TPM architecture")]
    HandshakeMismatch,
    #[error("Invalid handshake state")]
    InvalidHandshakeState,
    #[error("Peer TPM missing")]
    PeerTpmMissing,
    #[error("Handshake failed")]
    HandshakeFailed,
}
```

```rust
pub enum SyncResult {

    Failed,
    Partial,

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum TpmError {
    #[error("Architecture mismatch: hidden={expected} vs {actual}")]
    ArchitectureMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },
    #[error("Synchronization failed after {0} iterations")]
    SyncFailed(usize),
    #[error("Invalid input size: expected {expected}, got {actual}")]
    InvalidInputSize { expected: usize, actual: usize },
}
```

### `src/tpm.rs`

```rust
use rand::Rng;
use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
use rand::SeedableRng;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::fmt;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TreeParityMachine {
    pub weights: Vec<Vec<i32>>,
    pub hidden: usize,
    pub input_size: usize,
    pub weight_range: i32,
}

impl TreeParityMachine {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        Self::with_seed(hidden, input_size, weight_range, rand::random())
    }

    pub fn with_seed(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32, seed: u64) -> Self {
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed);
        let weights = (0..hidden)
            .map(|_| (0..input_size).map(|_| rng.gen_range(-weight_range..=weight_range)).collect())
            .collect();
        Self { weights, hidden, input_size, weight_range }
    }

    pub fn random_input(&self) -> Vec<i32> {
        Self::random_input_with_seed(self.input_size, rand::random())
    }

    pub fn random_input_with_seed(size: usize, seed: u64) -> Vec<i32> {
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed);
        (0..size).map(|_| rng.gen_range(-1..=1)).collect()
    }

    pub fn hidden_output(&self, input: &[i32], neuron: usize) -> i32 {
        let sum: i32 = self.weights[neuron].iter().zip(input.iter()).map(|(&w, &x)| w * x).sum();
        if sum > 0 { 1 } else if sum < 0 { -1 } else { 0 }
    }

    pub fn output(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        let product: i32 = (0..self.hidden).map(|i| self.hidden_output(input, i)).product();
        if product > 0 { 1 } else if product < 0 { -1 } else { 0 }
    }

    pub fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target_output: i32) {
        let tau = self.output(input);
        if tau != target_output { return; }
        for i in 0..self.hidden {
            let sigma = self.hidden_output(input, i);
            if sigma == tau {
                for j in 0..self.input_size {
                    let update = self.weights[i][j] + input[j] * tau;
                    self.weights[i][j] = update.clamp(-self.weight_range, self.weight_range);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    pub fn distance(&self, other: &Self) -> usize {
        assert_eq!(self.hidden, other.hidden);
        assert_eq!(self.input_size, other.input_size);
        self.weights.iter().zip(&other.weights).map(|(a, b)| a.iter().zip(b).filter(|(x, y)| x != y).count()).sum()
    }

    pub fn is_synchronized(&self, other: &Self) -> bool { self.distance(other) == 0 }

    pub fn flatten_weights(&self) -> Vec<i32> {
        self.weights.iter().flat_map(|row| row.iter().cloned()).collect()
    }
}

impl fmt::Display for TreeParityMachine {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "TPM(h={}, n={}, range={})", self.hidden, self.input_size, self.weight_range)
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    const SEED: u64 = 42;

    #[test]
    fn test_tpm_creation_with_seed() {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::with_seed(3, 10, 3, SEED);
        assert_eq!(tpm.hidden, 3);
        assert_eq!(tpm.input_size, 10);
        assert_eq!(tpm.weights.len(), 3);
        assert_eq!(tpm.weights[0].len(), 10);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_tpm_output_deterministic() {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::with_seed(3, 10, 3, SEED);
        let input = TreeParityMachine::random_input_with_seed(10, SEED + 1);
        let output1 = tpm.output(&input);
        let output2 = tpm.output(&input);
        assert_eq!(output1, output2);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_tpm_update() {
        let mut tpm = TreeParityMachine::with_seed(3, 10, 3, SEED);
        let input = TreeParityMachine::random_input_with_seed(10, SEED + 2);
        let tau = tpm.output(&input);
        let weights_before = tpm.weights.clone();
        tpm.update(&input, -tau);
        assert_eq!(tpm.weights, weights_before);
        tpm.update(&input, tau);
        // Weights may have changed, but we can't assert equality because update may or may not happen.
        // At least it should not panic.
    }
}
```

### `src/synchronizer.rs`

```rust
use crate::{TreeParityMachine, SyncResult, TpmError};
use blake3;
use rand::Rng;
use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
use rand::SeedableRng;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct TpmSynchronizer {
    pub max_iterations: usize,
    pub target_distance: usize,
}

impl TpmSynchronizer {
    pub fn new(max_iterations: usize, target_distance: usize) -> Self {
        Self { max_iterations, target_distance }
    }

    pub fn synchronize(
        &self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> SyncResult {
        if tpm_a.hidden != tpm_b.hidden || tpm_a.input_size != tpm_b.input_size {
            return SyncResult::Failed;
        }
        for (iteration, input) in inputs.iter().enumerate() {
            if iteration >= self.max_iterations {
                debug!("Synchronization: max iterations reached");
                return SyncResult::Failed;
            }
            let tau_a = tpm_a.output(input);
            let tau_b = tpm_b.output(input);
            if tau_a == tau_b {
                tpm_a.update(input, tau_a);
                tpm_b.update(input, tau_b);
            }
            let distance = tpm_a.distance(tpm_b);
            debug!("Iteration {}: distance = {} (target {})", iteration, distance, self.target_distance);
            if distance <= self.target_distance {
                return SyncResult::Success;
            }
        }
        SyncResult::Failed
    }

    pub fn synchronize_random(
        &self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        seed: u64,
    ) -> SyncResult {
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed);
        let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..self.max_iterations)
            .map(|_| (0..tpm_a.input_size).map(|_| rng.gen_range(-1..=1)).collect())
            .collect();
        self.synchronize(tpm_a, tpm_b, &inputs)
    }

    pub fn derive_key(tpm: &TreeParityMachine, length: usize) -> Vec<u8> {
        let weights = tpm.flatten_weights();
        let hash = blake3::hash(&weights.iter().map(|&w| w as u8).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        let mut key = hash.as_bytes().to_vec();
        while key.len() < length {
            let next_hash = blake3::hash(&key);
            key.extend_from_slice(next_hash.as_bytes());
        }
        key.truncate(length);
        key
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    const SEED: u64 = 12345;

    #[test]
    fn test_synchronization_deterministic() {
        let mut alice = TreeParityMachine::with_seed(3, 10, 3, SEED);
        let mut bob = TreeParityMachine::with_seed(3, 10, 3, SEED + 100);
        let sync = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        let result = sync.synchronize_random(&mut alice, &mut bob, SEED + 200);
        assert_eq!(result, SyncResult::Success);
        assert!(alice.is_synchronized(&bob));
        let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&alice, 32);
        assert_eq!(key.len(), 32);
    }
}
```

## 🔑 2. `cathedral-identity` — Extensão KeyReconciliation

### `src/key_reconciliation.rs`

```rust
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::{Did, IdentityError};

pub struct KeyReconciliation {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
}

impl KeyReconciliation {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer }
    }

    pub fn public_output(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    pub fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target: i32) {
        self.tpm.update(input, target);
    }

    pub async fn reconcile(
        &mut self,
        peer: &mut Self,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, IdentityError> {
        let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, &mut peer.tpm, inputs);
        match result {
            SyncResult::Success => Ok(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32)),
            _ => Err(IdentityError::ReconciliationFailed),
        }
    }

    pub fn tpm_state(&self) -> &TreeParityMachine {
        &self.tpm
    }
}
```

### `src/error.rs` (extensão)

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum IdentityError {
    // ... existing variants ...
    #[error("Key reconciliation failed")]
    ReconciliationFailed,
    #[error("Invalid TPM input: {0}")]
    InvalidTpmInput(String),
}
```

## 🤖 3. `cathedral-agents` — Consenso TPM

### `src/consensus.rs`

```rust
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::error::ConsensusError;
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct TpmConsensus {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
    peers: HashMap<String, Vec<i32>>,
}

impl TpmConsensus {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer, peers: HashMap::new() }
    }

    pub fn propose_state(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    pub fn register_peer_output(&mut self, peer_id: &str, input: &[i32], output: i32) {
        self.peers.insert(peer_id.to_string(), input.to_vec());
    }

    pub async fn reach_consensus(
        &mut self,
        peer_instances: &mut [TreeParityMachine],
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ConsensusError> {
        for (i, peer_tpm) in peer_instances.iter_mut().enumerate() {
            let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, peer_tpm, inputs);
            if result != SyncResult::Success {
                return Err(ConsensusError::SynchronizationFailed(i));
            }
        }
        Ok(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32))
    }
}
```

### `src/error.rs`

```rust
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum ConsensusError {
    #[error("Synchronization failed with peer {0}")]
    SynchronizationFailed(usize),
    #[error("Timeout during consensus")]
    Timeout,
    #[error("Invalid peer TPM architecture")]
    InvalidArchitecture,
}
```

## 📡 4. `cathedral-nostr` — Mensagens Reconciliadas

### `src/reconciliation.rs`

```rust
use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ReconciliableEvent {
    pub id: String,
    pub pubkey: String,
    pub created_at: u64,
    pub kind: u64,
    pub tags: Vec<Vec<String>>,
    pub content: String,
    pub sig: String,
    pub tpm_output: i32,
    pub tpm_input: Vec<i32>,
}

impl ReconciliableEvent {
    pub fn verify_tpm(&self, tpm: &TreeParityMachine) -> bool {
        tpm.output(&self.tpm_input) == self.tpm_output
    }

    pub fn reconcile_events(
        event_a: &Self,
        event_b: &Self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
    ) -> bool {
        if event_a.tpm_input != event_b.tpm_input {
            return false;
        }
        let sync = TpmSynchronizer::new(100, 0);
        let result = sync.synchronize(tpm_a, tpm_b, &[event_a.tpm_input.clone()]);
        result == SyncResult::Success
    }
}
```

## 🔗 5. Autenticação Multifatorial

### `src/multifactor.rs`

```rust
use crate::Did;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use blake3;
use std::collections::HashMap;
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc, Duration};

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct AuthFactors {
    pub did: Did,
    pub behavior_signature: Vec<u8>,
    pub context: ContextData,
    pub hardware_proof: Option<Vec<u8>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ContextData {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub location: Option<String>,
    pub device_id: Option<String>,
    pub ip_address: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OneTimeCredential {
    pub token: String,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub factors_hash: String,
}

impl OneTimeCredential {
    /// Normaliza timestamp para o minuto mais próximo (evita variações de ms)
    fn normalize_timestamp(dt: &DateTime<Utc>) -> DateTime<Utc> {
        let timestamp = dt.timestamp();
        let rounded = (timestamp / 60) * 60;
        DateTime::from_timestamp(rounded, 0).unwrap_or(*dt)
    }

    pub fn new(factors: &AuthFactors, validity_seconds: i64) -> Self {
        let normalized_ts = Self::normalize_timestamp(&factors.context.timestamp);
        let combined = format!(
            "{:?}{:?}{:?}",
            factors.did.to_string(),
            hex::encode(&factors.behavior_signature),
            normalized_ts,
        );
        let hash = blake3::hash(combined.as_bytes());
        let token = hex::encode(hash.as_bytes());
        let now = Utc::now();
        Self {
            token,
            created_at: now,
            expires_at: now + Duration::seconds(validity_seconds),
            factors_hash: hex::encode(hash.as_bytes()),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {
        Utc::now() < self.expires_at
    }

    pub fn verify(&self, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        let normalized_ts = Self::normalize_timestamp(&factors.context.timestamp);
        let combined = format!(
            "{:?}{:?}{:?}",
            factors.did.to_string(),
            hex::encode(&factors.behavior_signature),
            normalized_ts,
        );
        let hash = blake3::hash(combined.as_bytes());
        let token = hex::encode(hash.as_bytes());
        token == self.token && self.is_valid()
    }
}

pub struct AuthManager {
    credentials: HashMap<String, OneTimeCredential>,
}

impl AuthManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { credentials: HashMap::new() }
    }

    pub fn issue_credential(&mut self, factors: &AuthFactors) -> OneTimeCredential {
        let cred = OneTimeCredential::new(factors, 60);
        self.credentials.insert(cred.token.clone(), cred.clone());
        cred
    }

    pub fn validate_and_consume(&mut self, token: &str, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        if let Some(cred) = self.credentials.remove(token) {
            return cred.verify(factors);
        }
        false
    }
}
```

## 🤝 6. Handshake Rápido no MCP Bridge

### `src/handshake/types.rs`

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum HandshakeState {
    Idle,
    Initiated,
    Responded,
    Synchronized,
    Complete,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum HandshakeMessage {
    Initiate { hidden: usize, input_size: usize, seed: u64 },
    Response { input: Vec<i32>, output: i32 },
    Acknowledge { confirmation: Vec<u8> },
    Complete,
}
```

### `src/handshake/mod.rs`

```rust
mod types;
pub use types::{HandshakeState, HandshakeMessage};

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use rand::Rng;
use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
use rand::SeedableRng;

pub struct FastHandshake {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
    peer_tpm: Option<TreeParityMachine>,
    state: HandshakeState,
}

impl FastHandshake {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(50, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer, peer_tpm: None, state: HandshakeState::Idle }
    }

    pub fn initiate(&mut self) -> HandshakeMessage {
        self.state = HandshakeState::Initiated;
        HandshakeMessage::Initiate {
            hidden: self.tpm.hidden,
            input_size: self.tpm.input_size,
            seed: rand::random(),
        }
    }

    pub fn process(&mut self, msg: HandshakeMessage) -> Result<HandshakeMessage, BridgeError> {
        match msg {
            HandshakeMessage::Initiate { hidden, input_size, .. } => {
                if hidden != self.tpm.hidden || input_size != self.tpm.input_size {
                    return Err(BridgeError::HandshakeMismatch);
                }
                self.peer_tpm = Some(TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, self.tpm.weight_range));
                let input = self.tpm.random_input();
                let output = self.tpm.output(&input);
                self.state = HandshakeState::Responded;
                Ok(HandshakeMessage::Response { input, output })
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Response { input, output } => {
                if self.state != HandshakeState::Initiated {
                    return Err(BridgeError::InvalidHandshakeState);
                }
                let mut peer_tpm = self.peer_tpm.take().ok_or(BridgeError::PeerTpmMissing)?;
                peer_tpm.update(&input, output);
                let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, &mut peer_tpm, &[input]);
                if result == SyncResult::Success {
                    self.state = HandshakeState::Synchronized;
                    let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                    let confirmation = blake3::hash(&key).as_bytes().to_vec();
                    Ok(HandshakeMessage::Acknowledge { confirmation })
                } else {
                    Err(BridgeError::HandshakeFailed)
                }
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Acknowledge { confirmation } => {
                if self.state != HandshakeState::Responded {
                    return Err(BridgeError::InvalidHandshakeState);
                }
                let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                let expected = blake3::hash(&key).as_bytes().to_vec();
                if confirmation == expected {
                    self.state = HandshakeState::Complete;
                    Ok(HandshakeMessage::Complete)
                } else {
                    Err(BridgeError::HandshakeFailed)
                }
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Complete => {
                self.state = HandshakeState::Complete;
                Ok(HandshakeMessage::Complete)
            }
        }
    }

    pub fn shared_key(&self) -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
        if self.state == HandshakeState::Complete {
            Some(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

### `src/error.rs` (extensão)

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum BridgeError {
    // ... existing variants ...
    #[error("Handshake mismatch: incompatible TPM architecture")]
    HandshakeMismatch,
    #[error("Invalid handshake state")]
    InvalidHandshakeState,
    #[error("Peer TPM missing")]
    PeerTpmMissing,
    #[error("Handshake failed")]
    HandshakeFailed,
}
```

## 🧪 TESTE DE INTEGRAÇÃO (COM SEED FIXA)

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_fast_handshake_deterministic() {
        let mut alice = FastHandshake::new(3, 10, 3);
        let mut bob = FastHandshake::new(3, 10, 3);

        // Override random seed for reproducibility (would need to inject RNG)
        // In practice, we mock the random inputs.

        let msg1 = alice.initiate();
        let msg2 = bob.process(msg1).unwrap();
        let msg3 = alice.process(msg2).unwrap();
        let msg4 = bob.process(msg3).unwrap();

        assert_eq!(alice.state, HandshakeState::Complete);
        assert_eq!(bob.state, HandshakeState::Complete);

        let key_a = alice.shared_key().unwrap();
        let key_b = bob.shared_key().unwrap();
        assert_eq!(key_a, key_b);
    }
}
```

## 📦 1. `cathedral-consensus-tpm` — Sincronização TPM

**Propósito:** Implementação da Tree Parity Machine (TPM) para sincronização descentralizada entre agentes, gerando chaves compartilhadas sem trocar informações sensíveis.

### 1.1. `Cargo.toml`

```toml
[package]
name = "cathedral-consensus-tpm"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Cathedral OS Architects <arkhe@cathedral-os.org>"]
description = "Tree Parity Machine for decentralized synchronization and key reconciliation"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/cathedral-os/cathedral-consensus-tpm"

[dependencies]
rand = "0.8"
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
```
