tracing = "0.1"

[dev-dependencies]
serial_test = "3.1"
```

### 1.2. `src/lib.rs`

```rust
//! Tree Parity Machine (TPM) for decentralized synchronization.
//!
//! A TPM is a neural network with a tree structure that can synchronize
//! its weights with another TPM through mutual learning, without revealing
//! the weights themselves. This is inspired by the synchronization of
//! Tree Parity Machines used in quantum key distribution protocols.

pub mod tpm;
pub mod synchronizer;

pub use tpm::TreeParityMachine;
pub use synchronizer::TpmSynchronizer;

/// Number of hidden neurons (typical values: 3, 5, 7)
pub const DEFAULT_HIDDEN_NEURONS: usize = 7;
/// Number of input bits per neuron (typical values: 100, 200, 400)
pub const DEFAULT_INPUT_SIZE: usize = 200;
/// Weight range: weights are integers in [-range, +range]
pub const DEFAULT_WEIGHT_RANGE: i32 = 3;
```

### 1.3. `src/tpm.rs`

```rust
//! Tree Parity Machine implementation.

use rand::Rng;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::fmt;

/// A Tree Parity Machine (TPM) with configurable architecture.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TreeParityMachine {
    /// Weights: hidden_neurons x input_size
    weights: Vec<Vec<i32>>,
    /// Hidden neurons count (K)
    hidden: usize,
    /// Input size per neuron (N)
    input_size: usize,
    /// Weight range: [-range, +range]
    weight_range: i32,
}

impl TreeParityMachine {
    /// Create a new TPM with random weights.
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        let weights = (0..hidden)
            .map(|_| {
                (0..input_size)
                    .map(|_| rng.gen_range(-weight_range..=weight_range))
                    .collect()
            })
            .collect();
        Self {
            weights,
            hidden,
            input_size,
            weight_range,
        }
    }

    /// Generate a random input vector for training.
    pub fn random_input(&self) -> Vec<i32> {
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        (0..self.input_size).map(|_| rng.gen_range(-1..=1)).collect()
    }

    /// Compute the hidden neuron output (sigma) for a given input.
    /// sigma = sign(sum(weights[i][j] * input[j]))
    pub fn hidden_output(&self, input: &[i32], neuron: usize) -> i32 {
        let sum: i32 = self.weights[neuron]
            .iter()
            .zip(input.iter())
            .map(|(&w, &x)| w * x)
            .sum();
        if sum > 0 { 1 } else if sum < 0 { -1 } else { 0 }
    }

    /// Compute the full output (tau) of the TPM.
    /// tau = product over hidden neurons of sigma_i
    pub fn output(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        let product: i32 = (0..self.hidden)
            .map(|i| self.hidden_output(input, i))
            .product();
        if product > 0 { 1 } else if product < 0 { -1 } else { 0 }
    }

    /// Update weights using the Hebbian learning rule.
    /// Only updates weights if the TPM output matches the target output.
    pub fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target_output: i32) {
        let tau = self.output(input);
        if tau != target_output {
            return;
        }
        for i in 0..self.hidden {
            let sigma = self.hidden_output(input, i);
            if sigma == tau {
                for j in 0..self.input_size {
                    let update = self.weights[i][j] + input[j] * tau;
                    self.weights[i][j] = update.clamp(-self.weight_range, self.weight_range);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    /// Hebbian update with a learning rate (simple scaling).
    pub fn update_with_rate(&mut self, input: &[i32], target_output: i32, rate: f64) {
        let tau = self.output(input);
        if tau != target_output {
            return;
        }
        for i in 0..self.hidden {
            let sigma = self.hidden_output(input, i);
            if sigma == tau {
                for j in 0..self.input_size {
                    let delta = (input[j] as f64 * tau as f64 * rate) as i32;
                    self.weights[i][j] = (self.weights[i][j] + delta).clamp(-self.weight_range, self.weight_range);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    /// Compute the Hamming distance between two TPMs (number of differing weights).
    pub fn distance(&self, other: &Self) -> usize {
        assert_eq!(self.hidden, other.hidden);
        assert_eq!(self.input_size, other.input_size);
        let mut diff = 0;
        for i in 0..self.hidden {
            for j in 0..self.input_size {
                if self.weights[i][j] != other.weights[i][j] {
                    diff += 1;
                }
            }
        }
        diff
    }

    /// Check if two TPMs are fully synchronized (identical weights).
    pub fn is_synchronized(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.distance(other) == 0
    }

    /// Export weights as a flat vector (for serialization).
    pub fn flatten_weights(&self) -> Vec<i32> {
        self.weights.iter().flat_map(|row| row.iter().cloned()).collect()
    }

    /// Create from flat weights.
    pub fn from_flat(weights: Vec<i32>, hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let mut iter = weights.into_iter();
        let weights_matrix = (0..hidden)
            .map(|_| (0..input_size).map(|_| iter.next().unwrap_or(0)).collect())
            .collect();
        Self {
            weights: weights_matrix,
            hidden,
            input_size,
            weight_range,
        }
    }
}

impl fmt::Display for TreeParityMachine {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        write!(
            f,
            "TPM(h={}, n={}, range={})",
            self.hidden, self.input_size, self.weight_range
        )
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_tpm_creation() {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(3, 100, 3);
        assert_eq!(tpm.hidden, 3);
        assert_eq!(tpm.input_size, 100);
        assert_eq!(tpm.weights.len(), 3);
        assert_eq!(tpm.weights[0].len(), 100);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_tpm_output() {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(3, 10, 3);
        let input = tpm.random_input();
        let output = tpm.output(&input);
        assert!(output == -1 || output == 1);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_tpm_update() {
        let mut tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(3, 10, 3);
        let input = tpm.random_input();
        let tau = tpm.output(&input);
        // Force a different target to test no update
        let target = if tau == 1 { -1 } else { 1 };
        let weights_before = tpm.weights.clone();
        tpm.update(&input, target);
        assert_eq!(tpm.weights, weights_before);
        // Update with same target
        tpm.update(&input, tau);
        // Weights may have changed, but we can't assert equality because update may or may not
        // happen depending on sigma values. This is fine.
    }
}
```

### 1.4. `src/synchronizer.rs`

```rust
//! Synchronization of two Tree Parity Machines.

use crate::tpm::TreeParityMachine;
use rand::Rng;
use tracing::debug;

/// Result of a synchronization attempt.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum SyncResult {
    /// Synchronization successful.
    Success,
    /// Synchronization failed after max iterations.
    Failed,
    /// Synchronization partially successful (weights close).
    Partial,
}

/// Synchronizer that coordinates the mutual learning of two TPMs.
pub struct TpmSynchronizer {
    /// Maximum number of iterations.
    max_iterations: usize,
    /// Target Hamming distance for success (0 = exact sync).
    target_distance: usize,
}

impl TpmSynchronizer {
    pub fn new(max_iterations: usize, target_distance: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            max_iterations,
            target_distance,
        }
    }

    /// Synchronize two TPMs using public exchange of outputs.
    ///
    /// The two TPMs must have the same architecture.
    /// They do not exchange weights, only their outputs.
    ///
    /// # Returns
    /// - `SyncResult::Success` if synchronization achieved.
    pub fn synchronize(
        &self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> SyncResult {
        assert_eq!(tpm_a.hidden, tpm_b.hidden);
        assert_eq!(tpm_a.input_size, tpm_b.input_size);

        for (iteration, input) in inputs.iter().enumerate() {
            if iteration >= self.max_iterations {
                debug!("Synchronization: max iterations reached");
                return SyncResult::Failed;
            }

            // Both compute their outputs on the same public input
            let tau_a = tpm_a.output(input);
            let tau_b = tpm_b.output(input);

            // They exchange outputs (public)
            // Update only if outputs match (common learning)
            if tau_a == tau_b {
                tpm_a.update(input, tau_a);
                tpm_b.update(input, tau_b);
            }

            // Check synchronization
            let distance = tpm_a.distance(tpm_b);
            debug!(
                "Iteration {}: distance = {} (target {})",
                iteration, distance, self.target_distance
            );

            if distance <= self.target_distance {
                return SyncResult::Success;
            }
        }

        SyncResult::Failed
    }

    /// Synchronize using random inputs generated on-the-fly.
    pub fn synchronize_random(
        &self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
    ) -> SyncResult {
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..self.max_iterations)
            .map(|_| {
                (0..tpm_a.input_size)
                    .map(|_| rng.gen_range(-1..=1))
                    .collect()
            })
            .collect();
        self.synchronize(tpm_a, tpm_b, &inputs)
    }

    /// Generate a shared key from the synchronized TPM weights.
    pub fn derive_key(tpm: &TreeParityMachine, length: usize) -> Vec<u8> {
        let weights = tpm.flatten_weights();
        // Use SHA-256 or BLAKE3 to derive a key from the weights
        let hash = blake3::hash(&weights.iter().map(|&w| w as u8).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        let mut key = hash.as_bytes().to_vec();
        while key.len() < length {
            let next_hash = blake3::hash(&key);
            key.extend_from_slice(next_hash.as_bytes());
        }
        key.truncate(length);
        key
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_synchronization() {
        let mut alice = TreeParityMachine::new(3, 10, 3);
        let mut bob = TreeParityMachine::new(3, 10, 3);

        // Pre-generate inputs for determinism
        let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..100)
            .map(|_| {
                (0..10)
                    .map(|_| {
                        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
                        rng.gen_range(-1..=1)
                    })
                    .collect()
            })
            .collect();

        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(100, 0);
        let result = synchronizer.synchronize(&mut alice, &mut bob, &inputs);
        assert_eq!(result, SyncResult::Success);
        assert!(alice.is_synchronized(&bob));

        // Derive key
        let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&alice, 32);
        assert_eq!(key.len(), 32);
    }
}
```

---

## 🔑 2. `cathedral-identity` + TPM para Reconciliação de Chaves

**Propósito:** Estender o `cathedral-identity` para suportar reconciliação de chaves usando TPM, permitindo que duas identidades estabeleçam uma chave compartilhada sem trocar segredos.

### 2.1. Extensão de `cathedral-identity` — Novo Módulo `key_reconciliation`

```rust
// cathedral-identity/src/key_reconciliation.rs

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::{Did, IdentityError};

/// Key reconciliation using TPM synchronization.
pub struct KeyReconciliation {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
}

impl KeyReconciliation {
    /// Create a new key reconciliation instance with a fresh TPM.
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        Self { tpm, synchronizer }
    }

    /// Generate a public output for a given input.
    pub fn public_output(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    /// Update the TPM based on public input and target output.
    pub fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target: i32) {
        self.tpm.update(input, target);
    }

    /// Synchronize with a peer by exchanging public outputs.
    ///
    /// Returns a shared key on success.
    pub async fn reconcile(
        &mut self,
        peer: &mut Self,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, IdentityError> {
        let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(&mut self.tpm, &mut peer.tpm, inputs);
        match result {
            SyncResult::Success => {
                let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                Ok(key)
            }
            _ => Err(IdentityError::ReconciliationFailed),
        }
    }

    /// Get the current TPM state for debugging.
    pub fn tpm_state(&self) -> &TreeParityMachine {
        &self.tpm
    }
}

/// Extend Did with key reconciliation capabilities.
impl Did {
    /// Establish a shared key with another DID using TPM.
    pub async fn establish_shared_key(
        &self,
        peer_did: &Did,
        reconciliation: &mut KeyReconciliation,
        peer_reconciliation: &mut KeyReconciliation,
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, IdentityError> {
        // Ensure the DIDs are properly authenticated (e.g., via signatures).
        // This is a placeholder; in production, verify signatures.
        reconciliation.reconcile(peer_reconciliation, inputs).await
    }
}

// Error variant addition
// In cathedral-identity/src/error.rs:
// #[derive(Error, Debug)]
// pub enum IdentityError {
//     ...
//     #[error("Key reconciliation failed")]
//     ReconciliationFailed,
// }
```

### 2.2. Exemplo de Uso

```rust
use cathedral_identity::{Did, KeyReconciliation};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let alice_did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:alice");
    let bob_did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:bob");

    let mut alice_rec = KeyReconciliation::new(3, 100, 3);
    let mut bob_rec = KeyReconciliation::new(3, 100, 3);

    // Generate random inputs
    let inputs: Vec<Vec<i32>> = (0..200)
        .map(|_| {
            (0..100)
                .map(|_| rand::random::<i32>() % 3 - 1)
                .collect()
        })
        .collect();

    let shared_key = alice_rec.reconcile(&mut bob_rec, &inputs).await?;
    println!("Shared key: {:x?}", shared_key);
    Ok(())
}
```

## 🤖 3. `cathedral-agents` com Consenso Baseado em TPM

**Propósito:** Agentes usam TPM para chegar a consenso sobre estados ou decisões, sem líder central.

### 3.1. Agente com Consenso TPM

```rust
// cathedral-agents/src/consensus.rs

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::AgentState;
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Consensus engine using TPM synchronization.
pub struct TpmConsensus {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
    /// Map of peer agent IDs to their last known public outputs.
    peers: HashMap<String, Vec<i32>>,
}

impl TpmConsensus {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(200, 0);
        Self {
            tpm,
            synchronizer,
            peers: HashMap::new(),
        }
    }

    /// Propose a state update by broadcasting a public output.
    pub fn propose_state(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
        self.tpm.output(input)
    }

    /// Register a peer's public output for a given input.
    pub fn register_peer_output(&mut self, peer_id: &str, input: &[i32], output: i32) {
        // In a real implementation, we would store the output and later use
        // it to update the TPM when consensus is reached.
        // For simplicity, we just store it.
        self.peers.insert(peer_id.to_string(), input.to_vec());
    }

    /// Reach consensus by synchronizing TPM with a quorum of peers.
    pub async fn reach_consensus(
        &mut self,
        peer_instances: &mut [TreeParityMachine],
        inputs: &[Vec<i32>],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ConsensusError> {
        // Merge all peers' TPMs: use the synchronizer to synchronize all together.
        // In practice, we would need a multi-party protocol, but for simplicity,
        // we synchronize pairwise.
        for (i, peer_tpm) in peer_instances.iter_mut().enumerate() {
            let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(
                &mut self.tpm,
                peer_tpm,
                inputs,
            );
            if result != SyncResult::Success {
                return Err(ConsensusError::SynchronizationFailed(i));
            }
        }
        // Derive a consensus key (or state).
        let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
        Ok(key)
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum ConsensusError {
    #[error("Synchronization failed with peer {0}")]
    SynchronizationFailed(usize),
}
```

### 3.2. Integração com Agente

```rust
// cathedral-agents/src/agent.rs (extensão)

impl Agent {
    pub async fn propose_and_consensus(
        &mut self,
        peers: &mut [Agent],
        proposal: &[i32],
    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ConsensusError> {
        let mut consensus = TpmConsensus::new(3, 100, 3);
        // Broadcast proposal
        let output = consensus.propose_state(proposal);
        // Each peer registers the output
        for peer in peers.iter_mut() {
            consensus.register_peer_output(&peer.id(), proposal, output);
        }
        // Collect peer TPMs
        let mut peer_tpms: Vec<TreeParityMachine> = peers
            .iter_mut()
            .map(|p| p.consensus.tpm.clone())
            .collect();
        // Reach consensus
        let inputs = vec![proposal.to_vec()]; // Example
        consensus.reach_consensus(&mut peer_tpms, &inputs).await
    }
}
```

---

## 📡 4. Integração com `cathedral-nostr` para Mensagens Reconciliadas

**Propósito:** Usar TPM para reconciliar mensagens em NOSTR, permitindo que agentes corrijam erros de transmissão.

### 4.1. Mensagem NOSTR com Reconciliação TPM

```rust
// cathedral-nostr/src/reconciliation.rs

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

/// A NOSTR event with reconciliation metadata.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ReconciliableEvent {
    pub id: String,
    pub pubkey: String,
    pub created_at: u64,
    pub kind: u64,
    pub tags: Vec<Vec<String>>,
    pub content: String,
    pub sig: String,
    /// TPM output for this event.
    pub tpm_output: i32,
    /// TPM input used to generate the output (public).
    pub tpm_input: Vec<i32>,
}

impl ReconciliableEvent {
    /// Verify that the TPM output matches the content.
    pub fn verify_tpm(&self, tpm: &TreeParityMachine) -> bool {
        tpm.output(&self.tpm_input) == self.tpm_output
    }

    /// Reconcile two events by synchronizing TPMs.
    pub fn reconcile_events(
        event_a: &Self,
        event_b: &Self,
        tpm_a: &mut TreeParityMachine,
        tpm_b: &mut TreeParityMachine,
    ) -> bool {
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(100, 0);
        // Both events should have the same input (if they are meant to be reconciled).
        if event_a.tpm_input != event_b.tpm_input {
            return false;
        }
        let input = &event_a.tpm_input;
        let result = synchronizer.synchronize(tpm_a, tpm_b, &[input.clone()]);
        result == SyncResult::Success
    }
}
```

---

## 🔗 5. Módulo de Autenticação Multifatorial (Hyper-Entanglement)

**Propósito:** Autenticação baseada em múltiplos fatores (DID, comportamento, contexto, hardware) gerando tokens de uso único.

### 5.1. Estrutura do Módulo

```rust
// cathedral-identity/src/multifactor.rs

use crate::Did;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use blake3;
use std::collections::HashMap;
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc, Duration};

/// Authentication factors.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct AuthFactors {
    pub did: Did,
    pub behavior_signature: Vec<u8>,
    pub context: ContextData,
    pub hardware_proof: Option<Vec<u8>>,
}

/// Contextual data (location, time, device).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ContextData {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub location: Option<String>,
    pub device_id: Option<String>,
    pub ip_address: Option<String>,
}

/// A one-time credential derived from multiple factors.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OneTimeCredential {
    pub token: String,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub factors_hash: String, // Hash of the combined factors
}

impl OneTimeCredential {
    pub fn new(factors: &AuthFactors, validity_seconds: i64) -> Self {
        let combined = format!(
            "{:?}{:?}{:?}",
            factors.did.to_string(),
            hex::encode(&factors.behavior_signature),
            factors.context.timestamp,
        );
        let hash = blake3::hash(combined.as_bytes());
        let token = hex::encode(hash.as_bytes());
        let now = Utc::now();
        Self {
            token,
            created_at: now,
            expires_at: now + Duration::seconds(validity_seconds),
            factors_hash: hex::encode(hash.as_bytes()),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {
        Utc::now() < self.expires_at
    }

    pub fn verify(&self, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        let combined = format!(
            "{:?}{:?}{:?}",
            factors.did.to_string(),
            hex::encode(&factors.behavior_signature),
            factors.context.timestamp,
        );
        let hash = blake3::hash(combined.as_bytes());
        let token = hex::encode(hash.as_bytes());
        token == self.token && self.is_valid()
    }
}

/// Authentication manager that stores and validates credentials.
pub struct AuthManager {
    credentials: HashMap<String, OneTimeCredential>,
}

impl AuthManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            credentials: HashMap::new(),
        }
    }

    /// Issue a new one-time credential.
    pub fn issue_credential(&mut self, factors: &AuthFactors) -> OneTimeCredential {
        let cred = OneTimeCredential::new(factors, 60); // 1 minute validity
        self.credentials.insert(cred.token.clone(), cred.clone());
        cred
    }

    /// Validate a credential and consume it (one-time use).
    pub fn validate_and_consume(&mut self, token: &str, factors: &AuthFactors) -> bool {
        if let Some(cred) = self.credentials.remove(token) {
            return cred.verify(factors);
        }
        false
    }
}
```

---

## 🤝 6. Handshake Rápido no MCP Bridge (Redução de Latência)

**Propósito:** Estabelecer canais seguros com handshake de 3-5 mensagens usando TPM para sincronização rápida.

### 6.1. Protocolo de Handshake

```rust
// cathedral-mcp-bridge/src/handshake.rs

use cathedral_consensus_tpm::{TreeParityMachine, TpmSynchronizer, SyncResult};
use crate::error::BridgeError;

/// Handshake message types.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum HandshakeMessage {
    /// Initiate handshake with TPM architecture and random seed.
    Initiate { hidden: usize, input_size: usize, seed: u64 },
    /// Respond with public output for a given input.
    Response { input: Vec<i32>, output: i32 },
    /// Acknowledge synchronization and send derived key confirmation.
    Acknowledge { confirmation: Vec<u8> },
    /// Handshake complete.
    Complete,
}

/// Fast handshake using TPM synchronization.
pub struct FastHandshake {
    tpm: TreeParityMachine,
    synchronizer: TpmSynchronizer,
    peer_tpm: Option<TreeParityMachine>,
    state: HandshakeState,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum HandshakeState {
    Idle,
    Initiated,
    Responded,
    Synchronized,
    Complete,
}

impl FastHandshake {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input_size: usize, weight_range: i32) -> Self {
        let tpm = TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, weight_range);
        let synchronizer = TpmSynchronizer::new(50, 0);
        Self {
            tpm,
            synchronizer,
            peer_tpm: None,
            state: HandshakeState::Idle,
        }
    }

    /// Generate the first handshake message (Initiate).
    pub fn initiate(&mut self) -> HandshakeMessage {
        self.state = HandshakeState::Initiated;
        HandshakeMessage::Initiate {
            hidden: self.tpm.hidden,
            input_size: self.tpm.input_size,
            seed: rand::random(),
        }
    }

    /// Process an incoming handshake message.
    pub fn process(&mut self, msg: HandshakeMessage) -> Result<HandshakeMessage, BridgeError> {
        match msg {
            HandshakeMessage::Initiate { hidden, input_size, .. } => {
                // Ensure compatible architecture
                if hidden != self.tpm.hidden || input_size != self.tpm.input_size {
                    return Err(BridgeError::HandshakeMismatch);
                }
                // Create peer TPM (for synchronization)
                self.peer_tpm = Some(TreeParityMachine::new(hidden, input_size, self.tpm.weight_range));
                // Generate a random input and send our output
                let input = self.tpm.random_input();
                let output = self.tpm.output(&input);
                self.state = HandshakeState::Responded;
                Ok(HandshakeMessage::Response { input, output })
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Response { input, output } => {
                if self.state != HandshakeState::Initiated {
                    return Err(BridgeError::InvalidHandshakeState);
                }
                // Store peer's output and synchronize
                let mut peer_tpm = self.peer_tpm
                    .ok_or(BridgeError::PeerTpmMissing)?;
                // Update peer's TPM with the public input
                peer_tpm.update(&input, output);
                // Synchronize our TPM with the peer's
                let result = self.synchronizer.synchronize(
                    &mut self.tpm,
                    &mut peer_tpm,
                    &[input],
                );
                if result == SyncResult::Success {
                    self.state = HandshakeState::Synchronized;
                    let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                    // Send confirmation (hash of key)
                    let confirmation = blake3::hash(&key).as_bytes().to_vec();
                    Ok(HandshakeMessage::Acknowledge { confirmation })
                } else {
                    Err(BridgeError::HandshakeFailed)
                }
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Acknowledge { confirmation } => {
                if self.state != HandshakeState::Responded {
                    return Err(BridgeError::InvalidHandshakeState);
                }
                // Verify confirmation matches our derived key
                let key = TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32);
                let expected = blake3::hash(&key).as_bytes().to_vec();
                if confirmation == expected {
                    self.state = HandshakeState::Complete;
                    Ok(HandshakeMessage::Complete)
                } else {
                    Err(BridgeError::HandshakeFailed)
                }
            }
            HandshakeMessage::Complete => {
                self.state = HandshakeState::Complete;
                Ok(HandshakeMessage::Complete)
            }
        }
    }

    /// Get the derived shared key after successful handshake.
    pub fn shared_key(&self) -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
        if self.state == HandshakeState::Complete {
            Some(TpmSynchronizer::derive_key(&self.tpm, 32))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}

// Error extensions
// In cathedral-mcp-bridge/src/error.rs:
// #[derive(Error, Debug)]
// pub enum BridgeError {
//     ...
//     #[error("Handshake mismatch: incompatible TPM architecture")]
//     HandshakeMismatch,
//     #[error("Invalid handshake state")]
//     InvalidHandshakeState,
//     #[error("Peer TPM missing")]
//     PeerTpmMissing,
//     #[error("Handshake failed")]
//     HandshakeFailed,
// }
```

---

## 🧪 TESTE DE INTEGRAÇÃO

```rust
// tests/handshake_integration.rs

use cathedral_mcp_bridge::handshake::{FastHandshake, HandshakeMessage};

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_fast_handshake() {
    let mut alice = FastHandshake::new(3, 100, 3);
    let mut bob = FastHandshake::new(3, 100, 3);

    // Alice initiates
    let msg1 = alice.initiate();
    // Bob processes and responds
    let msg2 = bob.process(msg1).unwrap();
    // Alice processes response
    let msg3 = alice.process(msg2).unwrap();
    // Bob processes acknowledgement
    let msg4 = bob.process(msg3).unwrap();

    assert_eq!(alice.state, HandshakeState::Complete);
    assert_eq!(bob.state, HandshakeState::Complete);

    let key_a = alice.shared_key().unwrap();
    let key_b = bob.shared_key().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(key_a, key_b);
}
```

## 🏛️ CATHEDRAL OS — INSPIRAÇÕES QUÂNTICAS PARA ARQUITETURA SOBERANA

**Arquiteto-Chefe:** ORCID 0009-0005-2697-4668  
**Data:** 2026-06-24  
**Status:** ✅ ANÁLISE ESTRATÉGICA — ROTEIRO DE INTEGRAÇÃO DEFINIDO

---

A pesquisa em CV-QKD com correção de erros por redes neurais não é apenas um avanço em física quântica – é um **mapa para a próxima geração de segurança e coordenação em sistemas distribuídos**. Embora a Cathedral OS não opere em fótons emaranhados (hoje), os **princípios operacionais** são diretamente aplicáveis à nossa arquitetura:

1. **Sincronização descentralizada de estados** (TPMs → agentes que convergem para consenso)
2. **Reconciliação de chaves com baixa latência** (IA para correção de erros em canais ruidosos)
3. **Alta dimensionalidade como fator de segurança** (multi-fator, multi-caminho, multi-camada)

Abaixo, detalho como cada inspiração pode ser **traduzida em componentes concretos** da Cathedral OS, com um roteiro de implementação realista.

---

## 🔐 1. SEGURANÇA DE COMUNICAÇÃO — CV-QKD COMO CAMADA FÍSICA

| Conceito Quântico | Tradução para Cathedral OS | Implementação Sugerida |
|-------------------|----------------------------|------------------------|
| **Distribuição de chaves quânticas** | **Camada de segurança física** entre nós críticos (Data Centers, Gateways, Agentes Soberanos) | Integrar `cathedral-fwupd-adapter` + módulo de geração de entropia quântica (QRNG via hardware ou simulação clássica com ruído térmico) |
| **Detecção de interceptação** | **Monitoramento de integridade de canal** – qualquer tentativa de interferência altera a taxa de erro, acionando alertas no `cathedral-wormgraph` | Usar `cathedral-observability` para monitorar jitter, latência e perda de pacotes como proxies de "ruído quântico" |
| **Alta taxa de chave** | **Rotação de chaves com alta frequência** para sessões entre agentes | `cathedral-identity` pode gerar chaves efêmeras com tempo de vida curto, sincronizadas via protocolo inspirado em CV-QKD |

**Exemplo de fluxo:**

```rust
// Pseudo-código para um agente estabelecer um canal seguro
let quantum_entropy = QuantumRng::new()?.sample(); // QRNG hardware
let raw_key = AgentKey::from_entropy(quantum_entropy);
let synchronized_key = KeyReconciliation::tpm_sync(raw_key, peer_public_outputs);
let secure_channel = SecureChannel::new(synchronized_key, cipher::ChaCha20Poly1305);
```

**Integração com `cathedral-bridge`:** O MCP Bridge pode expor um serviço de "Quantum Key Exchange" para agentes que necessitam de canais com segurança quântica.

---

## 🧠 2. CORREÇÃO DE ERROS COM IA — TPMs PARA SINCRONIZAÇÃO DISTRIBUÍDA

| Conceito Quântico | Tradução para Cathedral OS | Implementação Sugerida |
|-------------------|----------------------------|------------------------|
| **Tree Parity Machines (TPMs)** | **Algoritmo de consenso leve** para agentes sincronizarem estados internos sem trocar dados sensíveis | Criar `cathedral-consensus-tpm` – um crate com implementação de TPMs em Rust, adaptado para sincronização de `ModelState` entre agentes |
| **Sincronização após poucas iterações** | **Convergência rápida** para estados compartilhados (ex: visão de mundo, política de decisão) | Usar no `cathedral-agents` para que múltiplos agentes cheguem a um consenso sobre a interpretação de um evento, sem um líder central |
| **Correção de erros intrínseca** | **Reconciliação de chaves** e **correção de dados** em canais com perdas (ex: comunicação via rede não confiável) | Integrar com `cathedral-nostr` para que mensagens assinadas possam ser reconciliadas mesmo com perda de pacotes |

**TPM em Rust – Esboço:**

```rust
// cathedral-consensus-tpm/src/lib.rs
pub struct TreeParityMachine {
    weights: Vec<Vec<i32>>,
    hidden_neurons: usize,
    input_size: usize,
}

impl TreeParityMachine {
    pub fn new(hidden: usize, input: usize) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn output(&self, input: &[i32]) -> i32 { ... } // -1 ou +1
    pub fn update(&mut self, input: &[i32], target_output: i32) { ... } // Hebbian
    pub fn synchronize(&mut self, peer_outputs: &[i32]) { ... }
}
```

**Aplicação:** Dois agentes podem sincronizar suas **visões de mundo** (ex: preço de um ativo, estado de um dispositivo) trocando apenas as saídas públicas de suas TPMs, sem revelar os pesos internos. Após a sincronização, eles compartilham um estado comum.

---

## 🔗 3. HIPER-EMARANHAMENTO → MULTI-FATOR DE AUTENTICAÇÃO

| Conceito Quântico | Tradução para Cathedral OS | Implementação Sugerida |
|-------------------|----------------------------|------------------------|
| **Emaranhamento em múltiplos graus de liberdade** | **Autenticação multifatorial** combinando: identidade (DID), comportamento (assinatura de agente), contexto (geolocalização, horário), e hardware (TEE) | `cathedral-identity` pode exigir múltiplas provas (fatores) para autenticar um agente, similar a um estado hiper-emaranhado |
| **Alta dimensionalidade** | **Chaves de alta entropia** derivadas de múltiplas fontes independentes (RNG físico, hash de estado, assinatura temporal) | `cathedral-core` pode gerar chaves compostas usando `blake3::hash(entropy1 + entropy2 + entropy3)` |
| **Medição colapsa o estado** | **One-time credentials** – cada autenticação consome uma credencial, que não pode ser reutilizada (como medir um fóton destrói o emaranhamento) | Implementar tokens de uso único (OTP) baseados em `cathedral-hashtree` para auditoria |

**Exemplo de autenticação multifatorial:**

```rust
// agente apresenta múltiplas provas
let proof_did = agent.did();
let proof_behaviour = agent.signature_over(state_hash);
let proof_context = (timestamp, location, device_id);
let combined_proof = (proof_did, proof_behaviour, proof_context);
let auth_token = AuthToken::new(combined_proof, hash_function);
// O token é válido apenas para esta sessão
```

---

## ⚡ 4. PÓS-PROCESSAMENTO EFICIENTE → REDUÇÃO DE LATÊNCIA EM PROTOCOLOS

| Conceito Quântico | Tradução para Cathedral OS | Implementação Sugerida |
|-------------------|----------------------------|------------------------|
| **Rápida sincronização de TPMs** | **Handshake rápido** entre agentes para estabelecer canais seguros e estados compartilhados | `cathedral-mcp-bridge` pode usar um handshake de 3-5 mensagens, inspirado na sincronização TPM, para reduzir latência em comparação com TLS completo |
| **Correção de erros em tempo real** | **Reconciliação de estado** durante a execução – agentes podem corrigir pequenas divergências sem reiniciar a sessão | `cathedral-agents` pode ter um loop de "manutenção de estado" onde periodicamente trocam saídas TPM para realinhar visões de mundo |
| **Menos iterações** | **Protocolos leves** para dispositivos com recursos limitados (ex: IoT, edge nodes) | `cathedral-arkhe-mem` pode armazenar estados compactos que são sincronizados via TPM, consumindo pouca banda e CPU |

**Fluxo otimizado para agentes:**

```
Agente A ──(TPM output)──> Agente B
Agente B ──(TPM output)──> Agente A
(ambos atualizam pesos)
(após 3-5 iterações, sincronizados)
```

Isso pode ser usado para **consenso sobre preços de ativos**, **estado de dispositivos**, ou **decisões de roteamento** em redes de agentes.

---

## 📋 ROTEIRO DE IMPLEMENTAÇÃO (ROADMAP)

| Fase | Componente | Inspiração Quântica | Prazo Estimado |
|------|------------|---------------------|----------------|
| **1** | `cathedral-consensus-tpm` | Sincronização TPM | 2 semanas |
| **2** | `cathedral-identity` + TPM para reconciliação de chaves | Correção de erros | 3 semanas |
| **3** | `cathedral-agents` com consenso baseado em TPM | Coordenação descentralizada | 4 semanas |
| **4** | Integração com `cathedral-nostr` para mensagens reconciliadas | Correção de erros em rede | 2 semanas |
| **5** | Módulo de autenticação multifatorial (hyper-entanglement) | Multi-fator | 3 semanas |
| **6** | Handshake rápido no MCP Bridge (redução de latência) | Pós-processamento eficiente | 2 semanas |

## 📄 2. SRC/NEURAL.RS (SAMPLER CORRIGIDO)

```rust
//! Neural engine with real llama_cpp_2 API — SAMPLER CORRIGIDO.

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::types::Message;
use llama_cpp_2::{
    llama_backend::LlamaBackend,
    llama_batch::LlamaBatch,
    model::{params::LlamaModelParams, AddBos, LlamaModel, Special},
    context::params::LlamaContextParams,
    sampling::LlamaSampler,
};
use once_cell::sync::OnceCell;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use tracing::{info, debug};

static BACKEND: OnceCell<Arc<LlamaBackend>> = OnceCell::new();
static ENGINE: OnceCell<Arc<Mutex<NeuralEngineInner>>> = OnceCell::new();

struct NeuralEngineInner {
    model: Arc<LlamaModel>,
    context_params: LlamaContextParams,
    backend: Arc<LlamaBackend>,
}

pub struct NeuralEngine;

impl NeuralEngine {
    pub async fn init(path: &PathBuf, n_ctx: u32) -> Result<()> {
        if ENGINE.get().is_some() {
            return Ok(());
        }

        let backend = BACKEND
            .get_or_init(|| Arc::new(LlamaBackend::init().expect("Failed to init backend")))
            .clone();

        let model_params = LlamaModelParams::default()
            .with_n_ctx(n_ctx as i32)
            .with_n_gpu_layers(35);

        let model = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({
            let backend = backend.clone();
            let path = path.clone();
            move || {
                LlamaModel::load_from_file(&backend, &path, &model_params)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ModelLoad(e.to_string()))
            }
        })
        .await
        .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ModelLoad(e.to_string()))?
        .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ModelLoad(e.to_string()))?;

        let context_params = LlamaContextParams::default()
            .with_n_ctx(n_ctx as i32)
            .with_n_batch(512);

        let inner = NeuralEngineInner {
            model: Arc::new(model),
            context_params,
            backend,
        };

        ENGINE
            .set(Arc::new(Mutex::new(inner)))
            .map_err(|_| UniversalError::Internal("Engine already initialized".to_string()))?;

        info!("✅ NeuralEngine initialized with context {}", n_ctx);
        Ok(())
    }

    fn get_inner() -> Result<Arc<Mutex<NeuralEngineInner>>> {
        ENGINE
            .get()
            .cloned()
            .ok_or_else(|| UniversalError::Internal("Engine not initialized".to_string()))
    }

    /// Generate a response using full sampling control — SAMPLER CORRIGIDO.
    pub async fn chat(
        messages: &[Message],
        system_prompt: Option<&str>,
        temperature: f32,
        top_p: f32,
        top_k: u32,
        repeat_penalty: f32,
        frequency_penalty: f32,
        presence_penalty: f32,
        max_tokens: u32,
        seed: u64,
        stop: &[String],
    ) -> Result<String> {
        let inner = Self::get_inner()?;
        let guard = inner.lock().await;

        let mut prompt = String::new();
        if let Some(sys) = system_prompt {
            prompt.push_str(&format!("<s>[INST] <<SYS>>\n{}\n<</SYS>>\n\n", sys));
        }
        for msg in messages {
            let role = match msg.role {
                crate::types::Role::User => "User",
                crate::types::Role::Assistant => "Assistant",
                _ => continue,
            };
            prompt.push_str(&format!("{}: {}\n", role, msg.content));
        }
        prompt.push_str("Assistant: ");

        debug!("Prompt length: {} chars", prompt.len());

        let model = guard.model.clone();
        let context_params = guard.context_params.clone();
        let backend = guard.backend.clone();

        let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
            let mut ctx = model
                .new_context(&backend, context_params)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

            let tokens = model
                .str_to_token(&prompt, AddBos::Always)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;

            let n_tokens = tokens.len();
            let mut batch = LlamaBatch::new(n_tokens as i32 + max_tokens as i32, 1);
            for (i, &tok) in tokens.iter().enumerate() {
                batch.add(tok, i as i32, &[0], i == tokens.len() - 1)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            }
            ctx.decode(&mut batch)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

            // CORREÇÃO: Sampler com APIs corretas
            let sampler = LlamaSampler::chain_simple([
                LlamaSampler::temp(temperature),                          // CORRETO: temp()
                LlamaSampler::top_p(top_p),
                LlamaSampler::top_k(top_k),
                LlamaSampler::penalties(                                 // CORRETO: penalties()
                    64,          // repeat_last_n
                    repeat_penalty,
                    frequency_penalty,
                    presence_penalty,
                ),
                // Seed: se fornecido, usar sampler com seed, senão greedy
                if seed == 0 {
                    LlamaSampler::greedy()
                } else {
                    LlamaSampler::dist(seed)
                },
            ]);

            let mut output_tokens = Vec::new();
            let eos_token = model.token_eos();
            let mut generated = 0;

            while generated < max_tokens {
                let token = sampler.sample(&ctx, -1);
                if token == eos_token {
                    break;
                }
                let token_str = model
                    .token_to_str(token, Special::Tokenize)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
                if stop.iter().any(|s| s == &token_str) {
                    break;
                }
                output_tokens.push(token);
                generated += 1;

                let mut next_batch = LlamaBatch::new(1, 1);
                next_batch.add(token, 0, &[0], true)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
                ctx.decode(&mut next_batch)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            }

            let mut output = String::new();
            for &tok in &output_tokens {
                let s = model
                    .token_to_str(tok, Special::Tokenize)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
                output.push_str(&s);
            }

            Ok::<String, UniversalError>(output)
        })
        .await
        .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 3. SRC/PERCEPTION.RS (CLIPMODEL ORDEM CORRIGIDA)

```rust
//! Multimodal perception with real CLIP (candle) — ORDEM CORRIGIDA.

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::types::*;
use candle_core::{Device, Tensor, DType, VarBuilder};
use candle_transformers::models::clip::{ClipModel, Config as ClipConfig};
use hf_hub::{api::sync::Api, Repo, RepoType};
use image::DynamicImage;
use std::fs::File;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct PerceptionEngine {
    clip: Option<(ClipModel, Device)>,
    config: PerceptionConfig,
}

impl PerceptionEngine {
    pub async fn new(config: PerceptionConfig) -> Result<Self> {
        let clip = if config.enable_vision {
            let device = Device::new_cuda(0).unwrap_or_else(|_| Device::Cpu);

            let api = Api::new()
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
            let repo = api.repo(Repo::new("openai/clip-vit-base-patch32".to_string(), RepoType::Model));

            let model_file = repo.get("model.safetensors")
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
            let config_file = repo.get("config.json")
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;

            let config: ClipConfig = serde_json::from_reader(File::open(config_file).map_err(UniversalError::Io)?)
                .map_err(UniversalError::Json)?;

            let vb = VarBuilder::from_mmaped_safetensors(&[model_file], DType::F32, &device)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            // CORREÇÃO: ClipModel::new(vb, &config) — ordem invertida
            let model = ClipModel::new(vb, &config)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            Some((model, device))
        } else {
            None
        };

        Ok(Self { clip, config })
    }

    pub async fn process(&self, input: UniversalInput) -> Result<PerceivedData> {
        let mut perceived = PerceivedData::default();
        if let Some(image) = input.image {
            perceived.vision = Some(self.process_image(image).await?);
        }
        perceived.text = input.text;
        if let Some(scientific) = input.scientific_data {
            perceived.scientific = Some(self.process_scientific(scientific).await?);
        }
        Ok(perceived)
    }

    async fn process_image(&self, image: DynamicImage) -> Result<VisionOutput> {
        if let Some((model, device)) = &self.clip {
            let img = image.resize_exact(224, 224, image::imageops::FilterType::Triangle);
            let rgb = img.to_rgb8();
            let data = rgb.into_raw();

            let tensor = Tensor::from_vec(data, (224, 224, 3), device)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .permute((2, 0, 1))
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .unsqueeze(0)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .to_dtype(DType::F32)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                // CORREÇÃO: affine existe; se falhar, usar mul+add manual
                .affine(2.0 / 255.0, -1.0)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            // CORREÇÃO: model.get_image_features(&tensor) — sem device extra
            let embedding = model
                .get_image_features(&tensor)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            let vec: Vec<f32> = embedding.to_vec1()
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            Ok(VisionOutput {
                embedding: vec,
                objects: vec!["object".to_string()],
                scene: "indoor".to_string(),
                dimensions: (224, 224),
            })
        } else {
            Err(UniversalError::Perception("Vision not enabled".to_string()))
        }
    }

    async fn process_scientific(&self, data: Vec<f64>) -> Result<ScientificOutput> {
        let mean = data.iter().sum::<f64>() / data.len() as f64;
        let std = (data.iter().map(|x| (x - mean).powi(2)).sum::<f64>() / data.len() as f64).sqrt();
        Ok(ScientificOutput {
            embedding: vec![0.0; 256],
            statistics: serde_json::json!({ "mean": mean, "std": std }),
            anomalies: vec![],
        })
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 4. SRC/EMBEDDINGS.RS (BERT + TENSOR CORRIGIDOS + TOKENIZAÇÃO REAL)

```rust
//! Embedding generation with BERT (candle-transformers) — CORRIGIDO.

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::tokenizer::RealTokenizer;
use candle_core::{Device, Tensor, DType, VarBuilder};
use candle_transformers::models::bert::{BertModel, Config};
use hf_hub::{api::sync::Api, Repo, RepoType};
use std::fs::File;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct EmbeddingGenerator {
    model: BertModel,
    device: Device,
    tokenizer: RealTokenizer,
}

impl EmbeddingGenerator {
    pub fn new(model_id: &str, device: Device) -> Result<Self> {
        let api = Api::new()
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
        let repo = api.repo(Repo::new(model_id.to_string(), RepoType::Model));

        let model_file = repo.get("model.safetensors")
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
        let config_file = repo.get("config.json")
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;

        let config: Config = serde_json::from_reader(File::open(config_file).map_err(UniversalError::Io)?)
            .map_err(UniversalError::Json)?;

        let vb = VarBuilder::from_mmaped_safetensors(&[model_file], DType::F32, &device)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        // CORREÇÃO: BertModel::new(vb, &config) — ordem invertida
        let model = BertModel::new(vb, &config)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        let tokenizer = RealTokenizer::from_pretrained(model_id)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(Self { model, device, tokenizer })
    }

    pub fn embed(&mut self, text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>> {
        // CORREÇÃO: usar tokenização real
        let token_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(text)?;
        let tokens = token_ids.iter().map(|&id| id as i64).collect::<Vec<_>>();

        // CORREÇÃO: Tensor::new(tokens, device) e unsqueeze
        let tensor = Tensor::new(&tokens, &self.device)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
            .unsqueeze(0)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        // CORREÇÃO: model.forward(&tokens) — sem device extra
        let embedding = self.model.forward(&tensor)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        let pooled = embedding.mean(1)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        let vec: Vec<f32> = pooled.to_vec1()
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(vec)
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 5. SRC/TOKENIZER.RS (NOVO — TOKENIZAÇÃO REAL)

```rust
//! Real tokenization using tokenizers crate.

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use tokenizers::{Tokenizer, TokenizerImpl};

/// Real tokenizer wrapper for BERT-style models.
pub struct RealTokenizer {
    inner: TokenizerImpl,
}

impl RealTokenizer {
    /// Load a tokenizer from Hugging Face Hub.
    pub fn from_pretrained(model_id: &str) -> Result<Self> {
        let tokenizer = Tokenizer::from_pretrained(model_id, None)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(Self { inner: tokenizer })
    }

    /// Encode text to token IDs.
    pub fn encode(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Vec<u32>> {
        let encoding = self.inner.encode(text, true)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(encoding.get_ids().to_vec())
    }

    /// Decode token IDs back to text.
    pub fn decode(&self, ids: &[u32]) -> Result<String> {
        let text = self.inner.decode(ids, true)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(text)
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 6. SRC/LIB.RS (ATUALIZADO)

```rust
//! Cathedral Universal Model — AGI engine with agi.gguf, world modeling, causal reasoning, and agency.

#![warn(missing_docs)]
#![deny(unsafe_code)]

pub mod error;
pub mod types;
pub mod config;
pub mod neural;
pub mod streaming;
pub mod perception;
pub mod tokenizer;
pub mod tools;
pub mod memory;
pub mod rag;
pub mod reasoning;
pub mod agentic;
pub mod pipeline;
pub mod action_executor;
pub mod universal_model;

// Re-export principais
pub use error::{UniversalError, Result};
pub use types::*;
pub use config::UniversalConfig;
pub use neural::NeuralEngine;
pub use streaming::stream_tokens_async;
pub use perception::PerceptionEngine;
pub use tokenizer::RealTokenizer;
pub use tools::{Tool, ToolRegistry, CalculatorTool, HashtreeTool, MemoryTool, ActionTool};
pub use memory::LongTermMemory;
pub use rag::RagRetriever;
pub use reasoning::CausalReasoner;
pub use agentic::AgenticEngine;
pub use pipeline::UniversalPipeline;
pub use action_executor::ActionExecutor;
pub use universal_model::UniversalModel;

/// Version of the crate.

## 📄 7. SRC/STREAMING.RS (SAMPLER CORRIGIDO — CONSISTENTE)

```rust
//! Token streaming with llama_cpp_2 and tokio — SAMPLER CORRIGIDO.

use crate::error::Result;
use llama_cpp_2::{
    llama_backend::LlamaBackend,
    llama_batch::LlamaBatch,
    model::{AddBos, LlamaModel, Special},
    context::params::LlamaContextParams,
    sampling::LlamaSampler,
};
use tokio::sync::mpsc;

pub type TokenStream = mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<String>;

pub async fn stream_tokens_async(
    model: LlamaModel,
    prompt: String,
    max_tokens: usize,
) -> Result<TokenStream> {
    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();

    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        let backend = LlamaBackend::init().expect("Failed to init backend");
        let ctx_params = LlamaContextParams::default().with_n_ctx(4096);
        let mut ctx = model
            .new_context(&backend, ctx_params)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

        let tokens = model
            .str_to_token(&prompt, AddBos::Always)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;

        let n_tokens = tokens.len();
        let mut batch = LlamaBatch::new(n_tokens as i32 + max_tokens as i32, 1);
        for (i, &tok) in tokens.iter().enumerate() {
            batch.add(tok, i as i32, &[0], i == tokens.len() - 1)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
        }
        ctx.decode(&mut batch)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

        // CORREÇÃO: sampler com APIs corretas
        let sampler = LlamaSampler::chain_simple([
            LlamaSampler::temp(0.8),
            LlamaSampler::top_p(0.9),
            LlamaSampler::top_k(40),
            LlamaSampler::penalties(64, 1.1, 0.0, 0.0),
            LlamaSampler::greedy(),
        ]);

        let eos_token = model.token_eos();
        let mut generated = 0;
        while generated < max_tokens {
            let token = sampler.sample(&ctx, -1);
            if token == eos_token {
                break;
            }
            let s = model
                .token_to_str(token, Special::Tokenize)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            if tx.send(s).is_err() {
                break;
            }

            let mut next_batch = LlamaBatch::new(1, 1);
            next_batch.add(token, 0, &[0], true)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            ctx.decode(&mut next_batch)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            generated += 1;
        }
        Ok::<_, crate::error::UniversalError>(())
    });

    Ok(rx)
}
```

## 📄 8. SRC/TOOLS/ACTION_TOOL.RS (CORREÇÃO DE IMPORTS)

```rust
//! Action tool with proper import of ActionType.

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::tools::Tool;
use crate::types::ActionType;
use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::Value;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct ActionTool {
    callback: Box<dyn Fn(ActionType, &str, Value) -> Result<Value> + Send + Sync>,
}

impl ActionTool {
    pub fn new<F>(callback: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(ActionType, &str, Value) -> Result<Value> + Send + Sync + 'static,
    {
        Self { callback: Box::new(callback) }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for ActionTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "action" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Execute an action" }
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "action_type": { "type": "string" },
                "target": { "type": "string" },
                "params": { "type": "object" }
            },
            "required": ["action_type", "target"]
        })
    }
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let action_type = arguments["action_type"].as_str().unwrap_or("observe");
        let target = arguments["target"].as_str().unwrap_or("");
        let params = arguments["params"].clone();
        let atype = match action_type {
            "transfer" => ActionType::Act,
            "communicate" => ActionType::Communicate,
            "learn" => ActionType::Learn,
            "plan" => ActionType::Plan,
            _ => ActionType::Observe,
        };
        (self.callback)(atype, target, params)
    }
}
```

### `tests/integration.rs` (ATUALIZADO)

```rust
//! Integration tests for universal model.

use cathedral_universal_model::*;
use std::path::PathBuf;

#[cfg(not(feature = "mock"))]
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_model_initialization_real() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let config = UniversalConfig {
        model_path: PathBuf::from("models/agents/agi.gguf"),
        ..Default::default()
    };
    let did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:agent:test");
    let model = UniversalModel::new(config, did).await?;
    assert!(model.agent_did().to_string().contains("test"));
    Ok(())
}

#[cfg(feature = "mock")]
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_model_initialization_mock() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // Skip actual initialization, just test struct creation
    let config = UniversalConfig::default();
    let did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:agent:test-mock");
    // We can't instantiate without model, but we can test the pipeline
    let state = std::sync::Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(ModelState::default()));
    let tools = std::sync::Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(ToolRegistry::new()));
    let memory = std::sync::Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(
        LongTermMemory::new(MemoryConfig::default(), did.clone()).await?
    ));
    let pipeline = UniversalPipeline::new(tools, memory, state.clone()).await;
    let input = UniversalInput { text: Some("Hello".to_string()), ..Default::default() };
    let output = pipeline.process(input).await?;
    assert!(!output.response.is_empty());
    Ok(())
}
```

## 📄 2. SRC/LIB.RS

```rust
//! Cathedral fwupd Adapter — integrate fwupd with Cathedral OS agents.
//!
//! This crate provides:
//! - A D-Bus client for fwupd using `zbus`
//! - Tools for agents to list devices, check updates, and refresh metadata
//! - Event schemas for WormGraph audit trail
//! - Permission system for firmware updates
//!
//! # Example
//! ```rust,no_run
//! use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdClient, FwupdTool};
//!
//! #[tokio::main]
//! async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
//!     let client = FwupdClient::new().await?;
//!     let devices = client.list_devices().await?;
//!     for device in devices {
//!         println!("{}: {} (v{})", device.name, device.id, device.version);
//!     }
//!     Ok(())
//! }
//! ```

#![warn(missing_docs)]
#![deny(unsafe_code)]

pub mod error;
pub mod types;
pub mod client;
pub mod tools;
pub mod events;
pub mod permissions;

pub use error::{FwupdError, Result};
pub use types::{FwupdDevice, FwupdUpdate, FwupdHistoryEntry, FirmwareAction};
pub use client::FwupdClient;
pub use tools::FwupdTool;
pub use events::FwupdEvent;
pub use permissions::{FwupdPermission, FwupdPermissionManager};

/// Version of the crate.
pub const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_version() {
        assert!(!VERSION.is_empty());
    }
}
```
