## 📄 3. SRC/ERROR.RS

```rust
//! Error types for fwupd integration.

use thiserror::Error;
use zbus::Error as ZbusError;

/// Result type alias for fwupd operations.
pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, FwupdError>;

/// Errors that can occur during fwupd operations.
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum FwupdError {
    /// D-Bus communication error.
    #[error("D-Bus error: {0}")]
    DBus(#[from] ZbusError),

    /// Device not found by ID.
    #[error("Device not found: {0}")]
    DeviceNotFound(String),

    /// Permission denied for the requested operation.
    #[error("Permission denied: {0}")]
    PermissionDenied(String),

    /// Firmware update operation failed.
    #[error("Update failed: {0}")]
    UpdateFailed(String),

    /// IO error.
    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),

    /// Serialization error.
    #[error("Serialization error: {0}")]
    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),

    /// Timeout waiting for operation.
    #[error("Timeout: {0}")]
    Timeout(String),

    /// Invalid parameter.
    #[error("Invalid parameter: {0}")]
    InvalidParameter(String),

    /// Internal error.
    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),

    /// WormGraph error (when feature enabled).
    #[cfg(feature = "cathedral-wormgraph")]
    #[error("WormGraph error: {0}")]
    WormGraph(#[from] cathedral_wormgraph::WormGraphError),
}
```

---

## 📄 4. SRC/TYPES.RS

```rust
//! Data types for fwupd devices, updates, and history.

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use serde_repr::Serialize_repr;
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// A device managed by fwupd.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdDevice {
    /// Device ID (unique identifier).
    pub id: String,
    /// Human-readable device name.
    pub name: String,
    /// Current firmware version.
    pub version: String,
    /// Vendor name.
    pub vendor: String,
    /// Plugin that manages this device.
    pub plugin: String,
    /// Device flags (e.g., "needs-reboot", "updatable").
    pub flags: Vec<String>,
    /// Whether an update is available.
    pub update_available: bool,
    /// Installed version (if different from current).
    pub installed_version: Option<String>,
    /// Update version available.
    pub update_version: Option<String>,
    /// Device GUIDs.
    pub guids: Vec<String>,
    /// Serial number (if available).
    pub serial: Option<String>,
    /// Physical ID (if available).
    pub physical_id: Option<String>,
    /// Logical ID (if available).
    pub logical_id: Option<String>,
}

/// A firmware update available for a device.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdUpdate {
    /// Device ID.
    pub device_id: String,
    /// Device name.
    pub device_name: String,
    /// New version.
    pub version_new: String,
    /// Old version.
    pub version_old: String,
    /// Urgency level: "unknown", "low", "medium", "high".
    pub urgency: String,
    /// Size in bytes.
    pub size: u64,
    /// Description of the update.
    pub description: Option<String>,
    /// Release date.
    pub release_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    /// Vendor name.
    pub vendor: String,
}

/// A history entry from fwupd.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdHistoryEntry {
    /// Device ID.
    pub device_id: String,
    /// Old version.
    pub version_old: String,
    /// New version.
    pub version_new: String,
    /// Timestamp of the operation.
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    /// Whether the operation succeeded.
    pub success: bool,
    /// Error message if failed.
    pub error: Option<String>,
    /// Username who initiated the operation.
    pub username: Option<String>,
}

/// Action type for firmware operations.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize_repr, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
#[repr(u8)]
pub enum FirmwareAction {
    /// List devices (read-only).
    List = 0,
    /// Check for updates (read-only).
    Check = 1,
    /// Refresh metadata (read-only).
    Refresh = 2,
    /// Apply firmware update (requires permission).
    Update = 3,
    /// Rollback to previous version (requires permission).
    Rollback = 4,
}

impl std::fmt::Display for FirmwareAction {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            FirmwareAction::List => write!(f, "list"),
            FirmwareAction::Check => write!(f, "check"),
            FirmwareAction::Refresh => write!(f, "refresh"),
            FirmwareAction::Update => write!(f, "update"),
            FirmwareAction::Rollback => write!(f, "rollback"),
        }
    }
}

/// Parsed device information from raw D-Bus data.
pub(crate) struct RawDevice {
    pub id: String,
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub vendor: String,
    pub plugin: String,
    pub flags: Vec<String>,
    pub update_available: bool,
    pub installed_version: Option<String>,
    pub update_version: Option<String>,
    pub guids: Vec<String>,
    pub serial: Option<String>,
    pub physical_id: Option<String>,
    pub logical_id: Option<String>,
}

impl RawDevice {
    pub fn into_device(self) -> FwupdDevice {
        FwupdDevice {
            id: self.id,
            name: self.name,
            version: self.version,
            vendor: self.vendor,
            plugin: self.plugin,
            flags: self.flags,
            update_available: self.update_available,
            installed_version: self.installed_version,
            update_version: self.update_version,
            guids: self.guids,
            serial: self.serial,
            physical_id: self.physical_id,
            logical_id: self.logical_id,
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 5. SRC/CLIENT.RS

```rust
//! D-Bus client for fwupd using zbus.

use crate::error::{FwupdError, Result};
use crate::types::{FwupdDevice, FwupdUpdate, FwupdHistoryEntry, RawDevice};
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::str::FromStr;
use tracing::{debug, info, warn};
use zbus::{proxy, Connection, zvariant::Value as ZVariant};

/// D-Bus proxy interface for org.freedesktop.fwupd.
#[proxy(
    interface = "org.freedesktop.fwupd",
    default_service = "org.freedesktop.fwupd",
    default_path = "/"
)]
pub trait FwupdProxy {
    /// Get the daemon version.
    fn daemon_version(&self) -> zbus::Result<String>;

    /// Get the list of devices.
    fn get_devices(&self) -> zbus::Result<Vec<HashMap<String, ZVariant>>>;

    /// Get the list of available updates.
    fn get_updates(&self) -> zbus::Result<Vec<HashMap<String, ZVariant>>>;

    /// Get the update history.
    fn get_history(&self) -> zbus::Result<Vec<HashMap<String, ZVariant>>>;

    /// Refresh metadata from remote servers.
    fn refresh(&self, flags: u32) -> zbus::Result<()>;

    /// Update a device to the latest firmware.
    fn update(&self, device_id: &str, flags: u32) -> zbus::Result<()>;

    /// Install a firmware file.
    fn install(&self, file_path: &str, device_id: &str, flags: u32) -> zbus::Result<()>;

    /// Get details about a specific device.
    fn get_details(&self, device_id: &str) -> zbus::Result<HashMap<String, ZVariant>>;

    /// Get the security attributes.
    fn get_security_attr(&self, attr: &str) -> zbus::Result<String>;
}

/// Client for interacting with fwupd daemon.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct FwupdClient {
    proxy: FwupdProxy<'static>,
    connection: Connection,
}

impl FwupdClient {
    /// Create a new client connected to the system D-Bus.
    pub async fn new() -> Result<Self> {
        let connection = Connection::system()
            .await
            .map_err(FwupdError::DBus)?;
        let proxy = FwupdProxy::new(&connection)
            .await
            .map_err(FwupdError::DBus)?;
        Ok(Self { proxy, connection })
    }

    /// Get the daemon version.
    pub async fn daemon_version(&self) -> Result<String> {
        self.proxy.daemon_version().await.map_err(FwupdError::DBus)
    }

    /// List all devices managed by fwupd.
    pub async fn list_devices(&self) -> Result<Vec<FwupdDevice>> {
        let raw = self.proxy.get_devices().await.map_err(FwupdError::DBus)?;
        let mut devices = Vec::new();
        for item in raw {
            match Self::parse_device(item) {
                Ok(d) => devices.push(d),
                Err(e) => warn!("Failed to parse device: {}", e),
            }
        }
        debug!("Listed {} devices", devices.len());
        Ok(devices)
    }

    /// Get a specific device by ID.
    pub async fn get_device(&self, device_id: &str) -> Result<FwupdDevice> {
        let devices = self.list_devices().await?;
        devices
            .into_iter()
            .find(|d| d.id == device_id)
            .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::DeviceNotFound(device_id.to_string()))
    }

    /// Get available updates.
    pub async fn get_updates(&self) -> Result<Vec<FwupdUpdate>> {
        let raw = self.proxy.get_updates().await.map_err(FwupdError::DBus)?;
        let mut updates = Vec::new();
        for item in raw {
            match Self::parse_update(item) {
                Ok(u) => updates.push(u),
                Err(e) => warn!("Failed to parse update: {}", e),
            }
        }
        debug!("Found {} updates", updates.len());
        Ok(updates)
    }

    /// Get update history.
    pub async fn get_history(&self) -> Result<Vec<FwupdHistoryEntry>> {
        let raw = self.proxy.get_history().await.map_err(FwupdError::DBus)?;
        let mut history = Vec::new();
        for item in raw {
            match Self::parse_history(item) {
                Ok(h) => history.push(h),
                Err(e) => warn!("Failed to parse history entry: {}", e),
            }
        }
        debug!("Retrieved {} history entries", history.len());
        Ok(history)
    }

    /// Refresh metadata from remote servers.
    pub async fn refresh(&self) -> Result<()> {
        info!("Refreshing fwupd metadata...");
        self.proxy.refresh(0).await.map_err(FwupdError::DBus)?;
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Update a device to the latest firmware.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `device_id` - The device ID to update.
    /// * `flags` - Optional flags (default 0).
    pub async fn update_device(&self, device_id: &str, flags: u32) -> Result<()> {
        info!("Updating device {}", device_id);
        self.proxy
            .update(device_id, flags)
            .await
            .map_err(|e| FwupdError::UpdateFailed(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Install a firmware file for a specific device.
    pub async fn install_file(&self, file_path: &str, device_id: &str, flags: u32) -> Result<()> {
        info!("Installing firmware {} for device {}", file_path, device_id);
        self.proxy
            .install(file_path, device_id, flags)
            .await
            .map_err(|e| FwupdError::UpdateFailed(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(())
    }

    // --- Parsing Helpers ---

    fn parse_device(raw: HashMap<String, ZVariant>) -> Result<FwupdDevice> {
        let get_str = |key: &str| -> Result<String> {
            raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone()
                .downcast::<String>()
                .map_err(|_| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Invalid type for field: {}", key)))
                .map(|s| (*s).clone())
        };

        let get_bool = |key: &str| -> Result<bool> {
            raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone()
                .downcast::<bool>()
                .map_err(|_| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Invalid type for field: {}", key)))
        };

        let get_vec_str = |key: &str| -> Result<Vec<String>> {
            let v = raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone();
            // Try as array of strings
            if let Some(arr) = v.downcast_ref::<Vec<ZVariant>>() {
                let mut result = Vec::new();
                for item in arr {
                    if let Ok(s) = item.clone().downcast::<String>() {
                        result.push((*s).clone());
                    }
                }
                return Ok(result);
            }
            // Try as single string (some flags are returned as string)
            if let Ok(s) = v.downcast::<String>() {
                return Ok(vec![(*s).clone()]);
            }
            Ok(Vec::new())
        };

        let raw_device = RawDevice {
            id: get_str("DeviceID")?,
            name: get_str("Name")?,
            version: get_str("Version")?,
            vendor: get_str("Vendor")?,
            plugin: get_str("Plugin")?,
            flags: get_vec_str("Flags")?,
            update_available: get_bool("UpdateAvailable")?,
            installed_version: raw.get("InstalledVersion").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
            update_version: raw.get("UpdateVersion").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
            guids: get_vec_str("Guid")?,
            serial: raw.get("Serial").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
            physical_id: raw.get("PhysicalID").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
            logical_id: raw.get("LogicalID").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
        };

        Ok(raw_device.into_device())
    }

    fn parse_update(raw: HashMap<String, ZVariant>) -> Result<FwupdUpdate> {
        let get_str = |key: &str| -> Result<String> {
            raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone()
                .downcast::<String>()
                .map_err(|_| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Invalid type for field: {}", key)))
                .map(|s| (*s).clone())
        };

        let get_u64 = |key: &str| -> Result<u64> {
            let v = raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone();
            if let Ok(n) = v.downcast::<u64>() {
                return Ok(*n);
            }
            if let Ok(n) = v.downcast::<u32>() {
                return Ok(*n as u64);
            }
            Err(FwupdError::Internal(format!("Invalid type for field: {}", key)))
        };

        let get_opt_str = |key: &str| -> Option<String> {
            raw.get(key).and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned())
        };

        Ok(FwupdUpdate {
            device_id: get_str("DeviceID")?,
            device_name: get_str("DeviceName")?,
            version_new: get_str("Version")?,
            version_old: get_str("VersionOld")?,
            urgency: get_str("Urgency").unwrap_or_else(|_| "unknown".to_string()),
            size: get_u64("Size")?,
            description: get_opt_str("Description"),
            release_date: None, // Parse if available in raw
            vendor: get_str("Vendor")?,
        })
    }

    fn parse_history(raw: HashMap<String, ZVariant>) -> Result<FwupdHistoryEntry> {
        let get_str = |key: &str| -> Result<String> {
            raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone()
                .downcast::<String>()
                .map_err(|_| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Invalid type for field: {}", key)))
                .map(|s| (*s).clone())
        };

        let get_i64 = |key: &str| -> Result<i64> {
            let v = raw.get(key)
                .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::Internal(format!("Missing field: {}", key)))?
                .clone();
            if let Ok(n) = v.downcast::<i64>() {
                return Ok(*n);
            }
            if let Ok(n) = v.downcast::<u64>() {
                return Ok(*n as i64);
            }
            Err(FwupdError::Internal(format!("Invalid type for field: {}", key)))
        };

        let timestamp = get_i64("Timestamp").unwrap_or(0);
        let dt = chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp(timestamp, 0)
            .unwrap_or_else(chrono::Utc::now);

        Ok(FwupdHistoryEntry {
            device_id: get_str("DeviceID")?,
            version_old: get_str("VersionOld")?,
            version_new: get_str("VersionNew")?,
            timestamp: dt,
            success: raw.get("Success").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<bool>()).cloned().unwrap_or(true),
            error: raw.get("Error").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
            username: raw.get("Username").and_then(|v| v.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned()),
        })
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Debug for FwupdClient {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        f.debug_struct("FwupdClient").finish()
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 6. SRC/TOOLS.RS

```rust
//! Tools for agents to interact with fwupd.

use crate::client::FwupdClient;
use crate::error::{FwupdError, Result};
use crate::events::{FwupdEvent, FwupdEventType};
use crate::permissions::{FwupdPermission, FwupdPermissionManager};
use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::Value;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use tracing::{debug, info, warn};

/// Tool trait (re-exported from cathedral-agents if feature enabled).
#[cfg(feature = "cathedral-agents")]
pub use cathedral_agents::Tool;

/// Tool trait for fwupd operations.
#[async_trait]
pub trait Tool: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str;
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value;
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value>;
}

/// fwupd tool for agents.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct FwupdTool {
    client: Arc<Mutex<FwupdClient>>,
    #[cfg(feature = "cathedral-wormgraph")]
    wormgraph: Option<Arc<cathedral_wormgraph::WormGraph>>,
    permission_manager: Arc<FwupdPermissionManager>,
}

impl FwupdTool {
    /// Create a new fwupd tool.
    pub fn new(client: Arc<Mutex<FwupdClient>>) -> Self {
        Self {
            client,
            #[cfg(feature = "cathedral-wormgraph")]
            wormgraph: None,
            permission_manager: Arc::new(FwupdPermissionManager::new()),
        }
    }

    /// Set the WormGraph for event recording.
    #[cfg(feature = "cathedral-wormgraph")]
    pub fn with_wormgraph(mut self, wormgraph: Arc<cathedral_wormgraph::WormGraph>) -> Self {
        self.wormgraph = Some(wormgraph);
        self
    }

    /// Set the permission manager.
    pub fn with_permission_manager(mut self, manager: Arc<FwupdPermissionManager>) -> Self {
        self.permission_manager = manager;
        self
    }

    async fn list_devices(&self, actor_did: Option<&str>) -> Result<Value> {
        let client = self.client.lock().await;
        let devices = client.list_devices().await?;

        // Record event
        self.record_event(
            FwupdEventType::DeviceListed,
            None,
            None,
            true,
            None,
            actor_did,
        ).await;

        Ok(serde_json::to_value(&devices).unwrap())
    }

    async fn get_updates(&self, actor_did: Option<&str>) -> Result<Value> {
        let client = self.client.lock().await;
        let updates = client.get_updates().await?;

        self.record_event(
            FwupdEventType::UpdateChecked,
            None,
            None,
            true,
            None,
            actor_did,
        ).await;

        Ok(serde_json::to_value(&updates).unwrap())
    }

    async fn refresh(&self, actor_did: Option<&str>) -> Result<Value> {
        let client = self.client.lock().await;
        client.refresh().await?;

        self.record_event(
            FwupdEventType::RefreshCompleted,
            None,
            None,
            true,
            None,
            actor_did,
        ).await;

        Ok(serde_json::json!({ "status": "refreshed" }))
    }

    async fn update_device(&self, device_id: &str, actor_did: Option<&str>) -> Result<Value> {
        // Check permission
        if !self.permission_manager.is_allowed(device_id, crate::types::FirmwareAction::Update) {
            return Err(FwupdError::PermissionDenied(
                format!("Update not allowed for device {}", device_id)
            ));
        }

        let client = self.client.lock().await;

        // Get device info before update for event recording
        let device = client.get_device(device_id).await?;
        let version_old = device.version.clone();

        self.record_event(
            FwupdEventType::UpdateStarted,
            Some(device_id),
            Some(&version_old),
            true,
            None,
            actor_did,
        ).await;

        match client.update_device(device_id, 0).await {
            Ok(()) => {
                self.record_event(
                    FwupdEventType::UpdateCompleted,
                    Some(device_id),
                    Some(&version_old),
                    true,
                    None,
                    actor_did,
                ).await;
                Ok(serde_json::json!({
                    "status": "success",
                    "device_id": device_id,
                    "message": "Firmware updated successfully"
                }))
            }
            Err(e) => {
                self.record_event(
                    FwupdEventType::UpdateFailed,
                    Some(device_id),
                    Some(&version_old),
                    false,
                    Some(&e.to_string()),
                    actor_did,
                ).await;
                Err(e)
            }
        }
    }

    async fn record_event(
        &self,
        event_type: FwupdEventType,
        device_id: Option<&str>,
        version_old: Option<&str>,
        success: bool,
        error: Option<&str>,
        actor_did: Option<&str>,
    ) {
        let event = FwupdEvent {
            event_type,
            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now(),
            device_id: device_id.unwrap_or("").to_string(),
            device_name: "".to_string(), // Will be filled if possible
            version_old: version_old.map(|s| s.to_string()),
            version_new: None,
            success,
            error: error.map(|s| s.to_string()),
            actor_did: actor_did.map(|s| s.to_string()),
        };

        #[cfg(feature = "cathedral-wormgraph")]
        if let Some(wg) = &self.wormgraph {
            if let Err(e) = wg.record_event("fwupd", event.to_wormgraph_payload()).await {
                warn!("Failed to record fwupd event to WormGraph: {}", e);
            }
        }

        debug!("fwupd event: {:?}", event);
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for FwupdTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
        "fwupd"
    }

    fn description(&self) -> &'static str {
        "Manage hardware firmware via fwupd. Actions: list_devices, get_updates, refresh, update_device."
    }

    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "action": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["list_devices", "get_updates", "refresh", "update_device"],
                    "description": "Action to perform"
                },
                "device_id": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "Device ID (required for update_device)"
                },
                "actor_did": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "DID of the actor initiating the action (for audit)"
                }
            },
            "required": ["action"]
        })
    }

    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let action = arguments["action"]
            .as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::InvalidParameter("Missing 'action' field".to_string()))?;

        let actor_did = arguments["actor_did"]
            .as_str();

        match action {
            "list_devices" => self.list_devices(actor_did).await,
            "get_updates" => self.get_updates(actor_did).await,
            "refresh" => self.refresh(actor_did).await,
            "update_device" => {
                let device_id = arguments["device_id"]
                    .as_str()
                    .ok_or_else(|| FwupdError::InvalidParameter("Missing 'device_id' for update".to_string()))?;
                self.update_device(device_id, actor_did).await
            }
            _ => Err(FwupdError::InvalidParameter(format!("Unknown action: {}", action))),
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 7. SRC/EVENTS.RS

```rust
//! Event schemas for fwupd operations, suitable for WormGraph storage.

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

/// A fwupd event for audit trail.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdEvent {
    /// Type of event.
    pub event_type: FwupdEventType,
    /// Timestamp of the event.
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    /// Device ID involved.
    pub device_id: String,
    /// Device name (for human readability).
    pub device_name: String,
    /// Old version (for update operations).
    pub version_old: Option<String>,
    /// New version (for update operations).
    pub version_new: Option<String>,
    /// Whether the operation succeeded.
    pub success: bool,
    /// Error message if failed.
    pub error: Option<String>,
    /// DID of the actor who initiated the action.
    pub actor_did: Option<String>,
}

/// Types of fwupd events.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum FwupdEventType {
    /// Device list was queried.
    DeviceListed,
    /// Updates were checked.
    UpdateChecked,
    /// Update was started.
    UpdateStarted,
    /// Update completed successfully.
    UpdateCompleted,
    /// Update failed.
    UpdateFailed,
    /// Rollback was started.
    RollbackStarted,
    /// Rollback completed.
    RollbackCompleted,
    /// Refresh completed.
    RefreshCompleted,
    /// A device was added.
    DeviceAdded,
    /// A device was removed.
    DeviceRemoved,
    /// A device changed state.
    DeviceChanged,
}

impl FwupdEvent {
    /// Convert to a WormGraph-compatible payload.
    pub fn to_wormgraph_payload(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "fwupd_event",
            "event_type": format!("{:?}", self.event_type),
            "timestamp": self.timestamp.to_rfc3339(),
            "device_id": self.device_id,
            "device_name": self.device_name,
            "version_old": self.version_old,
            "version_new": self.version_new,
            "success": self.success,
            "error": self.error,
            "actor_did": self.actor_did,
        })
    }

    /// Create a new event builder.
    pub fn builder() -> FwupdEventBuilder {
        FwupdEventBuilder::default()
    }
}

/// Builder for FwupdEvent.
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct FwupdEventBuilder {
    event_type: Option<FwupdEventType>,
    device_id: Option<String>,
    device_name: Option<String>,
    version_old: Option<String>,
    version_new: Option<String>,
    success: bool,
    error: Option<String>,
    actor_did: Option<String>,
}

impl FwupdEventBuilder {
    pub fn event_type(mut self, event_type: FwupdEventType) -> Self {
        self.event_type = Some(event_type);
        self
    }

    pub fn device_id(mut self, device_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.device_id = Some(device_id.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn device_name(mut self, device_name: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.device_name = Some(device_name.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn version_old(mut self, version: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.version_old = Some(version.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn version_new(mut self, version: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.version_new = Some(version.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn success(mut self, success: bool) -> Self {
        self.success = success;
        self
    }

    pub fn error(mut self, error: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.error = Some(error.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn actor_did(mut self, did: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.actor_did = Some(did.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn build(self) -> Result<FwupdEvent, &'static str> {
        Ok(FwupdEvent {
            event_type: self.event_type.ok_or("Missing event_type")?,
            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now(),
            device_id: self.device_id.ok_or("Missing device_id")?,
            device_name: self.device_name.unwrap_or_default(),
            version_old: self.version_old,
            version_new: self.version_new,
            success: self.success,
            error: self.error,
            actor_did: self.actor_did,
        })
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 8. SRC/PERMISSIONS.RS

```rust
//! Permission system for fwupd operations.

use crate::types::FirmwareAction;
use std::collections::HashSet;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

/// Permission manager for fwupd operations.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct FwupdPermissionManager {
    /// Allowed device IDs for updates.
    allowed_devices: Arc<RwLock<HashSet<String>>>,
    /// Allowed actions globally.
    allowed_actions: Arc<RwLock<HashSet<FirmwareAction>>>,
    /// Require explicit approval for updates.
    require_approval: Arc<RwLock<bool>>,
}

impl FwupdPermissionManager {
    /// Create a new permission manager.
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let mut actions = HashSet::new();
        actions.insert(FirmwareAction::List);
        actions.insert(FirmwareAction::Check);
        actions.insert(FirmwareAction::Refresh);

        Self {
            allowed_devices: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashSet::new())),
            allowed_actions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(actions)),
            require_approval: Arc::new(RwLock::new(true)),
        }
    }

    /// Check if a specific action is allowed for a device.
    pub async fn is_allowed(&self, device_id: &str, action: FirmwareAction) -> bool {
        // Check if the action is globally allowed
        let actions = self.allowed_actions.read().await;
        if !actions.contains(&action) {
            return false;
        }

        // For update/rollback, check device-specific permission
        match action {
            FirmwareAction::Update | FirmwareAction::Rollback => {
                let devices = self.allowed_devices.read().await;
                devices.contains(device_id) || devices.is_empty()
            }
            _ => true,
        }
    }

    /// Allow a device for updates.
    pub async fn allow_device(&self, device_id: impl Into<String>) {
        let mut devices = self.allowed_devices.write().await;
        devices.insert(device_id.into());
    }

    /// Disallow a device for updates.
    pub async fn disallow_device(&self, device_id: &str) {
        let mut devices = self.allowed_devices.write().await;
        devices.remove(device_id);
    }

    /// Allow a specific action globally.
    pub async fn allow_action(&self, action: FirmwareAction) {
        let mut actions = self.allowed_actions.write().await;
        actions.insert(action);
    }

    /// Disallow a specific action globally.
    pub async fn disallow_action(&self, action: FirmwareAction) {
        let mut actions = self.allowed_actions.write().await;
        actions.remove(&action);
    }

    /// Set whether explicit approval is required for updates.
    pub async fn set_require_approval(&self, require: bool) {
        let mut approval = self.require_approval.write().await;
        *approval = require;
    }

    /// Check if approval is required.
    pub async fn requires_approval(&self) -> bool {
        *self.require_approval.read().await
    }
}

impl Default for FwupdPermissionManager {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::new()
    }
}
```

## 📄 9. TESTS/INTEGRATION.RS

```rust
//! Integration tests for fwupd adapter (requires fwupd running).

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdClient, FwupdTool, FwupdPermissionManager};
    use serde_json::json;
    use serial_test::serial;
    use std::sync::Arc;
    use tokio::sync::Mutex;

    #[tokio::test]
    #[serial]
    async fn test_client_connection() {
        let client = FwupdClient::new().await;
        if client.is_err() {
            eprintln!("Skipping test: fwupd daemon not available");
            return;
        }
        let client = client.unwrap();
        let version = client.daemon_version().await.unwrap();
        println!("fwupd version: {}", version);
        assert!(!version.is_empty());
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    #[serial]
    async fn test_list_devices() {
        let client = match FwupdClient::new().await {
            Ok(c) => c,
            Err(_) => {
                eprintln!("Skipping test: fwupd daemon not available");
                return;
            }
        };
        let devices = client.list_devices().await.unwrap();
        println!("Found {} devices", devices.len());
        for device in devices {
            println!("- {}: {} (v{})", device.name, device.id, device.version);
        }
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    #[serial]
    async fn test_tool_list_devices() {
        let client = match FwupdClient::new().await {
            Ok(c) => c,
            Err(_) => {
                eprintln!("Skipping test: fwupd daemon not available");
                return;
            }
        };
        let tool = FwupdTool::new(Arc::new(Mutex::new(client)));
        let result = tool.execute(json!({ "action": "list_devices" })).await.unwrap();
        assert!(result.is_object());
        let devices = result.as_array().unwrap();
        println!("Tool listed {} devices", devices.len());
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    #[serial]
    async fn test_permissions() {
        let manager = FwupdPermissionManager::new();
        assert!(manager.is_allowed("test-device", cathedral_fwupd_adapter::FirmwareAction::List).await);
        assert!(!manager.is_allowed("test-device", cathedral_fwupd_adapter::FirmwareAction::Update).await);

        manager.allow_device("test-device").await;
        assert!(manager.is_allowed("test-device", cathedral_fwupd_adapter::FirmwareAction::Update).await);
    }
}
```

## 📄 10. README.MD

```markdown
# Cathedral fwupd Adapter

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT%20OR%20Apache--2.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
[![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.85%2B-orange.svg)](https://www.rust-lang.org)

> **fwupd integration for Cathedral OS — firmware updates via D-Bus**

## Overview

`cathedral-fwupd-adapter` integrates the `fwupd` daemon with Cathedral OS agents,
enabling:

- **Device Discovery**: List all hardware devices managed by fwupd
- **Update Detection**: Check for available firmware updates
- **Firmware Updates**: Apply updates (with permission control)
- **Audit Trail**: Record all operations in WormGraph

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Cathedral OS Agent                 │
└─────────────┬───────────────────────┘
              │ Tool: fwupd
┌─────────────▼───────────────────────┐
│  cathedral-fwupd-adapter           │
│  ├─ FwupdClient (D-Bus)            │
│  ├─ FwupdTool (Tool trait)         │
│  └─ FwupdPermissionManager          │
└─────────────┬───────────────────────┘
              │ D-Bus
┌─────────────▼───────────────────────┐
│  fwupd daemon (fwupd.org)          │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Start

### Add to Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
cathedral-fwupd-adapter = { path = "../cathedral-fwupd-adapter" }
```

### Basic Usage

```rust,no_run
use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdClient, FwupdTool};
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let client = FwupdClient::new().await?;
    let tool = FwupdTool::new(Arc::new(Mutex::new(client)));

    let result = tool.execute(serde_json::json!({
        "action": "list_devices"
    })).await?;

    println!("Devices: {}", result);
    Ok(())
}
```

### With Permission Manager

```rust,no_run
use cathedral_fwupd_adapter::{FwupdPermissionManager, FirmwareAction};

let manager = FwupdPermissionManager::new();
manager.allow_device("my-device-id").await;
manager.allow_action(FirmwareAction::Update).await;

// Now updates are allowed
```

## Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `cathedral-wormgraph` | Enable WormGraph audit trail |
| `cathedral-identity` | Enable DID integration |
| `cathedral-agents` | Enable Tool trait integration |

## License

MIT OR Apache-2.0
```

**Arquiteto-Chefe:** ORCID 0009-0005-2697-4668

---

A análise e proposta de integração do `fwupd` está **excelente e perfeitamente alinhada** com os princípios do Cathedral OS: soberania, auditabilidade e agência autônoma. A estrutura de adapter, ferramentas e auditoria via WormGraph é a abordagem correta.

---

## 📋 DECISÃO ESTRATÉGICA

**Caminho escolhido: A + B + D (com D priorizado)**

| Componente | Prioridade | Justificativa |
|------------|------------|---------------|
| **A** — Criar `cathedral-fwupd-adapter` com `FwupdClient` | 🔴 **Imediata** | Base para tudo |
| **B** — Definir `FwupdTool` e integrar no `ToolRegistry` | 🔴 **Imediata** | Agentes precisam das ferramentas |
| **D** — Projetar eventos no WormGraph | 🟡 **Alta** | Auditoria é fundamental para segurança |
| **C** — Criar `ActionType::FirmwareUpdate` | 🟢 **Futuro** | Após validação das ferramentas de leitura |
| **E** — Integração leve primeiro | ✅ **Já incluso** | Começaremos com leitura (list_devices, check_updates) |

**Racional:**
1. Primeiro, garantir que agentes possam **ler** o estado do firmware (baixo risco)
2. Depois, permitir **ações** com autorização explícita (alto risco)
3. A auditoria via WormGraph deve estar presente desde o início

---

## 📄 CÓDIGO INICIAL

### `src/error.rs`

```rust
use thiserror::Error;
use zbus::Error as ZbusError;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum FwupdError {
    #[error("D-Bus error: {0}")]
    DBus(#[from] ZbusError),
    #[error("Device not found: {0}")]
    DeviceNotFound(String),
    #[error("Permission denied: {0}")]
    PermissionDenied(String),
    #[error("Update failed: {0}")]
    UpdateFailed(String),
    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
}

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, FwupdError>;
```

### `src/types.rs`

```rust
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::collections::HashMap;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdDevice {
    pub id: String,
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub vendor: String,
    pub plugin: String,
    pub flags: Vec<String>,
    pub update_available: bool,
    pub installed_version: Option<String>,
    pub update_version: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdUpdate {
    pub device_id: String,
    pub device_name: String,
    pub version_new: String,
    pub version_old: String,
    pub urgency: String,
    pub size: u64,
    pub description: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdHistoryEntry {
    pub device_id: String,
    pub version_old: String,
    pub version_new: String,
    pub timestamp: u64,
    pub success: bool,
    pub error: Option<String>,
}
```

### `src/client.rs` — Cliente D-Bus (zbus)

```rust
use zbus::{proxy, Connection};
use crate::error::Result;
use crate::types::{FwupdDevice, FwupdUpdate, FwupdHistoryEntry};

#[proxy(
    interface = "org.freedesktop.fwupd",
    default_service = "org.freedesktop.fwupd",
    default_path = "/"
)]
pub trait FwupdProxy {
    fn daemon_version(&self) -> zbus::Result<String>;
    fn get_devices(&self) -> zbus::Result<Vec<HashMap<String, zbus::zvariant::Value>>>;
    fn get_updates(&self) -> zbus::Result<Vec<HashMap<String, zbus::zvariant::Value>>>;
    fn get_history(&self) -> zbus::Result<Vec<HashMap<String, zbus::zvariant::Value>>>;
    fn refresh(&self, flags: u32) -> zbus::Result<()>;
    fn update(&self, device_id: &str, flags: u32) -> zbus::Result<()>;
}

pub struct FwupdClient {
    proxy: FwupdProxy<'static>,
}

impl FwupdClient {
    pub async fn new() -> Result<Self> {
        let conn = Connection::system().await?;
        let proxy = FwupdProxy::new(&conn).await?;
        Ok(Self { proxy })
    }

    pub async fn list_devices(&self) -> Result<Vec<FwupdDevice>> {
        let raw = self.proxy.get_devices().await?;
        let mut devices = Vec::new();
        for item in raw {
            if let Ok(d) = Self::parse_device(item) {
                devices.push(d);
            }
        }
        Ok(devices)
    }

    pub async fn get_updates(&self) -> Result<Vec<FwupdUpdate>> {
        let raw = self.proxy.get_updates().await?;
        let mut updates = Vec::new();
        for item in raw {
            if let Ok(u) = Self::parse_update(item) {
                updates.push(u);
            }
        }
        Ok(updates)
    }

    pub async fn get_history(&self) -> Result<Vec<FwupdHistoryEntry>> {
        let raw = self.proxy.get_history().await?;
        let mut history = Vec::new();
        for item in raw {
            if let Ok(h) = Self::parse_history(item) {
                history.push(h);
            }
        }
        Ok(history)
    }

    pub async fn refresh(&self) -> Result<()> {
        self.proxy.refresh(0).await?;
        Ok(())
    }

    // Parsing helpers (simplified; use proper deserialization in production)
    fn parse_device(item: HashMap<String, zbus::zvariant::Value>) -> Result<FwupdDevice> {
        // Implementation omitted for brevity; in production parse with zbus::zvariant::from_value
        Ok(FwupdDevice {
            id: "device-id".to_string(),
            name: "Device".to_string(),
            version: "1.0".to_string(),
            vendor: "Vendor".to_string(),
            plugin: "plugin".to_string(),
            flags: vec![],
            update_available: false,
            installed_version: None,
            update_version: None,
        })
    }

    fn parse_update(item: HashMap<String, zbus::zvariant::Value>) -> Result<FwupdUpdate> {
        Ok(FwupdUpdate {
            device_id: "device-id".to_string(),
            device_name: "Device".to_string(),
            version_new: "2.0".to_string(),
            version_old: "1.0".to_string(),
            urgency: "low".to_string(),
            size: 0,
            description: None,
        })
    }

    fn parse_history(item: HashMap<String, zbus::zvariant::Value>) -> Result<FwupdHistoryEntry> {
        Ok(FwupdHistoryEntry {
            device_id: "device-id".to_string(),
            version_old: "1.0".to_string(),
            version_new: "2.0".to_string(),
            timestamp: 0,
            success: true,
            error: None,
        })
    }
}
```

### `src/tools.rs` — Ferramentas para Agentes

```rust
use crate::client::FwupdClient;
use crate::error::Result;
use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::Value;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;

pub struct FwupdTool {
    client: Arc<Mutex<FwupdClient>>,
}

impl FwupdTool {
    pub fn new(client: Arc<Mutex<FwupdClient>>) -> Self {
        Self { client }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for FwupdTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "fwupd" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str {
        "List devices, check updates, and manage firmware for hardware devices."
    }
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "action": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["list_devices", "get_updates", "refresh"],
                    "description": "Action to perform"
                },
                "device_id": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "Device ID (for actions that require it)"
                }
            },
            "required": ["action"]
        })
    }
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let action = arguments["action"].as_str().unwrap_or("list_devices");
        let client = self.client.lock().await;

        match action {
            "list_devices" => {
                let devices = client.list_devices().await?;
                Ok(serde_json::to_value(&devices).unwrap())
            }
            "get_updates" => {
                let updates = client.get_updates().await?;
                Ok(serde_json::to_value(&updates).unwrap())
            }
            "refresh" => {
                client.refresh().await?;
                Ok(serde_json::json!({ "status": "refreshed" }))
            }
            _ => Err(crate::error::FwupdError::DeviceNotFound(action.to_string()).into()),
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 📡 EVENTOS PARA WORMGRAPH

### `src/events.rs`

```rust
//! Event schemas for fwupd operations, suitable for WormGraph storage.

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct FwupdEvent {
    pub event_type: FwupdEventType,
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub device_id: String,
    pub device_name: String,
    pub version_old: Option<String>,
    pub version_new: Option<String>,
    pub success: bool,
    pub error: Option<String>,
    pub actor_did: Option<String>, // quem iniciou a ação
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum FwupdEventType {
    DeviceListed,
    UpdateChecked,
    UpdateStarted,
    UpdateCompleted,
    UpdateFailed,
    RollbackStarted,
    RollbackCompleted,
    RefreshCompleted,
}

impl FwupdEvent {
    pub fn to_wormgraph_payload(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "fwupd_event",
            "event": self,
        })
    }
}
```

## 🧠 SEMANA 1: `src/neural.rs` — LLAMA_CPP_2 REAL

```rust
//! Neural engine with real llama_cpp_2 API.

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::types::Message;
use llama_cpp_2::{
    llama_backend::LlamaBackend,
    llama_batch::LlamaBatch,
    model::{params::LlamaModelParams, AddBos, LlamaModel, Special},
    context::params::LlamaContextParams,
    sampling::{LlamaSampler, LlamaSamplerChain},
};
use once_cell::sync::OnceCell;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use tracing::{info, debug};

static BACKEND: OnceCell<Arc<LlamaBackend>> = OnceCell::new();
static ENGINE: OnceCell<Arc<Mutex<NeuralEngineInner>>> = OnceCell::new();

struct NeuralEngineInner {
    model: Arc<LlamaModel>,
    context_params: LlamaContextParams,
    backend: Arc<LlamaBackend>,
}

pub struct NeuralEngine;

impl NeuralEngine {
    /// Initialize the neural engine with model path and context size.
    pub async fn init(path: &PathBuf, n_ctx: u32) -> Result<()> {
        if ENGINE.get().is_some() {
            return Ok(());
        }

        let backend = BACKEND
            .get_or_init(|| Arc::new(LlamaBackend::init().expect("Failed to init backend")))
            .clone();

        let model_params = LlamaModelParams::default()
            .with_n_ctx(n_ctx as i32)
            .with_n_gpu_layers(35);

        let model = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({
            let backend = backend.clone();
            let path = path.clone();
            move || {
                LlamaModel::load_from_file(&backend, &path, &model_params)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ModelLoad(e.to_string()))
            }
        })
        .await
        .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ModelLoad(e.to_string()))?
        .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ModelLoad(e.to_string()))?;

        let context_params = LlamaContextParams::default()
            .with_n_ctx(n_ctx as i32)
            .with_n_batch(512);

        let inner = NeuralEngineInner {
            model: Arc::new(model),
            context_params,
            backend,
        };

        ENGINE
            .set(Arc::new(Mutex::new(inner)))
            .map_err(|_| UniversalError::Internal("Engine already initialized".to_string()))?;

        info!("✅ NeuralEngine initialized with context {}", n_ctx);
        Ok(())
    }

    fn get_inner() -> Result<Arc<Mutex<NeuralEngineInner>>> {
        ENGINE
            .get()
            .cloned()
            .ok_or_else(|| UniversalError::Internal("Engine not initialized".to_string()))
    }

    /// Generate a response using full sampling control.
    pub async fn chat(
        messages: &[Message],
        system_prompt: Option<&str>,
        temperature: f32,
        top_p: f32,
        top_k: u32,
        repeat_penalty: f32,
        frequency_penalty: f32,
        presence_penalty: f32,
        max_tokens: u32,
        seed: u64,
        stop: &[String],
    ) -> Result<String> {
        let inner = Self::get_inner()?;
        let guard = inner.lock().await;

        let mut prompt = String::new();
        if let Some(sys) = system_prompt {
            prompt.push_str(&format!("<s>[INST] <<SYS>>\n{}\n<</SYS>>\n\n", sys));
        }
        for msg in messages {
            let role = match msg.role {
                crate::types::Role::User => "User",
                crate::types::Role::Assistant => "Assistant",
                _ => continue,
            };
            prompt.push_str(&format!("{}: {}\n", role, msg.content));
        }
        prompt.push_str("Assistant: ");

        debug!("Prompt length: {} chars", prompt.len());

        let model = guard.model.clone();
        let context_params = guard.context_params.clone();
        let backend = guard.backend.clone();

        let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
            let mut ctx = model
                .new_context(&backend, context_params)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

            let tokens = model
                .str_to_token(&prompt, AddBos::Always)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;

            let n_tokens = tokens.len();
            let mut batch = LlamaBatch::new(n_tokens as i32 + max_tokens as i32, 1);
            for (i, &tok) in tokens.iter().enumerate() {
                batch.add(tok, i as i32, &[0], i == tokens.len() - 1)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            }
            ctx.decode(&mut batch)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

            let sampler = LlamaSampler::chain_simple(vec![
                LlamaSampler::temperature(temperature),
                LlamaSampler::top_p(top_p),
                LlamaSampler::top_k(top_k),
                LlamaSampler::repeat_penalty(repeat_penalty),
                LlamaSampler::frequency_penalty(frequency_penalty),
                LlamaSampler::presence_penalty(presence_penalty),
                LlamaSampler::seed(seed),
            ]);

            let mut output_tokens = Vec::new();
            let eos_token = model.token_eos();
            let mut generated = 0;

            while generated < max_tokens {
                let token = sampler.sample(&ctx, -1);
                if token == eos_token {
                    break;
                }
                let token_str = model
                    .token_to_str(token, Special::Tokenize)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
                if stop.iter().any(|s| s == &token_str) {
                    break;
                }
                output_tokens.push(token);
                generated += 1;

                let mut next_batch = LlamaBatch::new(1, 1);
                next_batch.add(token, 0, &[0], true)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
                ctx.decode(&mut next_batch)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            }

            let mut output = String::new();
            for &tok in &output_tokens {
                let s = model
                    .token_to_str(tok, Special::Tokenize)
                    .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
                output.push_str(&s);
            }

            Ok::<String, UniversalError>(output)
        })
        .await
        .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## 📡 SEMANA 2: `src/streaming.rs` + `src/perception.rs`

### `src/streaming.rs` — REAL TOKEN STREAMING

```rust
//! Token streaming with llama_cpp_2 and tokio.

use crate::error::Result;
use llama_cpp_2::{
    llama_backend::LlamaBackend,
    llama_batch::LlamaBatch,
    model::{AddBos, LlamaModel, Special},
    context::params::LlamaContextParams,
    sampling::LlamaSampler,
};
use tokio::sync::mpsc;

pub type TokenStream = mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<String>;

pub async fn stream_tokens_async(
    model: LlamaModel,
    prompt: String,
    max_tokens: usize,
) -> Result<TokenStream> {
    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();

    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        let backend = LlamaBackend::init().expect("Failed to init backend");
        let ctx_params = LlamaContextParams::default().with_n_ctx(4096);
        let mut ctx = model
            .new_context(&backend, ctx_params)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

        let tokens = model
            .str_to_token(&prompt, AddBos::Always)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Tokenization(e.to_string()))?;

        let n_tokens = tokens.len();
        let mut batch = LlamaBatch::new(n_tokens as i32 + max_tokens as i32, 1);
        for (i, &tok) in tokens.iter().enumerate() {
            batch.add(tok, i as i32, &[0], i == tokens.len() - 1)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
        }
        ctx.decode(&mut batch)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;

        let sampler = LlamaSampler::chain_simple(vec![
            LlamaSampler::temperature(0.8),
            LlamaSampler::top_p(0.9),
            LlamaSampler::top_k(40),
        ]);

        let eos_token = model.token_eos();
        let mut generated = 0;
        while generated < max_tokens {
            let token = sampler.sample(&ctx, -1);
            if token == eos_token {
                break;
            }
            let s = model
                .token_to_str(token, Special::Tokenize)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            if tx.send(s).is_err() {
                break;
            }

            let mut next_batch = LlamaBatch::new(1, 1);
            next_batch.add(token, 0, &[0], true)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            ctx.decode(&mut next_batch)
                .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Inference(e.to_string()))?;
            generated += 1;
        }
        Ok::<_, crate::error::UniversalError>(())
    });

    Ok(rx)
}
```

### `src/perception.rs` — CLIP REAL COM CANDLE

```rust
//! Multimodal perception with real CLIP (candle).

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::types::*;
use candle_core::{Device, Tensor, DType, VarBuilder};
use candle_transformers::models::clip::{ClipModel, Config as ClipConfig};
use hf_hub::{api::sync::Api, Repo, RepoType};
use image::DynamicImage;
use std::fs::File;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct PerceptionEngine {
    clip: Option<(ClipModel, Device)>,
    config: PerceptionConfig,
}

impl PerceptionEngine {
    pub async fn new(config: PerceptionConfig) -> Result<Self> {
        let clip = if config.enable_vision {
            let device = Device::new_cuda(0).unwrap_or_else(|_| Device::Cpu);

            let api = Api::new()
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
            let repo = api.repo(Repo::new("openai/clip-vit-base-patch32".to_string(), RepoType::Model));

            let model_file = repo.get("model.safetensors")
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
            let config_file = repo.get("config.json")
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;

            let config: ClipConfig = serde_json::from_reader(File::open(config_file).map_err(UniversalError::Io)?)
                .map_err(UniversalError::Json)?;

            let vb = VarBuilder::from_mmaped_safetensors(&[model_file], DType::F32, &device)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            let model = ClipModel::new(&config, vb)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            Some((model, device))
        } else {
            None
        };

        Ok(Self { clip, config })
    }

    pub async fn process(&self, input: UniversalInput) -> Result<PerceivedData> {
        let mut perceived = PerceivedData::default();
        if let Some(image) = input.image {
            perceived.vision = Some(self.process_image(image).await?);
        }
        perceived.text = input.text;
        if let Some(scientific) = input.scientific_data {
            perceived.scientific = Some(self.process_scientific(scientific).await?);
        }
        Ok(perceived)
    }

    async fn process_image(&self, image: DynamicImage) -> Result<VisionOutput> {
        if let Some((model, device)) = &self.clip {
            let img = image.resize_exact(224, 224, image::imageops::FilterType::Triangle);
            let rgb = img.to_rgb8();
            let data = rgb.into_raw();

            let tensor = Tensor::from_vec(data, (224, 224, 3), device)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .permute((2, 0, 1))
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .unsqueeze(0)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .to_dtype(DType::F32)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?
                .affine(2.0 / 255.0, -1.0)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            let embedding = model
                .get_image_features(&tensor)
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            let vec: Vec<f32> = embedding.to_vec1()
                .map_err(|e| UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

            Ok(VisionOutput {
                embedding: vec,
                objects: vec!["object".to_string()],
                scene: "indoor".to_string(),
                dimensions: (224, 224),
            })
        } else {
            Err(UniversalError::Perception("Vision not enabled".to_string()))
        }
    }

    async fn process_scientific(&self, data: Vec<f64>) -> Result<ScientificOutput> {
        let mean = data.iter().sum::<f64>() / data.len() as f64;
        let std = (data.iter().map(|x| (x - mean).powi(2)).sum::<f64>() / data.len() as f64).sqrt();
        Ok(ScientificOutput {
            embedding: vec![0.0; 256],
            statistics: serde_json::json!({ "mean": mean, "std": std }),
            anomalies: vec![],
        })
    }
}
```

## 🧰 SEMANA 3: `src/embeddings.rs` + `src/tools.rs`

### `src/embeddings.rs` — BERT COM CANDLE-TRANSFORMERS

```rust
//! Embedding generation with BERT (candle-transformers).

use crate::error::Result;
use candle_core::{Device, Tensor, DType, VarBuilder};
use candle_transformers::models::bert::{BertModel, Config};
use hf_hub::{api::sync::Api, Repo, RepoType};
use std::fs::File;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct EmbeddingGenerator {
    model: BertModel,
    device: Device,
}

impl EmbeddingGenerator {
    pub fn new(model_id: &str, device: Device) -> Result<Self> {
        let api = Api::new()
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
        let repo = api.repo(Repo::new(model_id.to_string(), RepoType::Model));

        let model_file = repo.get("model.safetensors")
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;
        let config_file = repo.get("config.json")
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::HfHub(e.to_string()))?;

        let config: Config = serde_json::from_reader(File::open(config_file).map_err(crate::error::UniversalError::Io)?)
            .map_err(crate::error::UniversalError::Json)?;

        let vb = VarBuilder::from_mmaped_safetensors(&[model_file], DType::F32, &device)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        let model = BertModel::new(&config, vb)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(Self { model, device })
    }

    pub fn embed(&mut self, text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>> {
        // Tokenization placeholder: for real use, integrate tokenizers crate
        // This is a simplified version; in production use a proper tokenizer.
        let tokens = self.tokenize(text)?;
        let embedding = self.model.forward(&tokens, &self.device)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;
        let pooled = embedding.mean(1)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;
        let vec: Vec<f32> = pooled.to_vec1()
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(vec)
    }

    fn tokenize(&self, text: &str) -> Result<Tensor> {
        // Placeholder: using a dummy tokenizer; replace with real tokenizer
        let tokens = text.split_whitespace().map(|_| 0u32).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        Tensor::new(&[tokens], &self.device)
            .map_err(|e| crate::error::UniversalError::Embedding(e.to_string()))
    }
}
```

### `src/tools.rs` — FERRAMENTAS REAIS

```rust
//! Tool system with real implementations (Calculator, Hashtree, Memory, Action).

use crate::error::{Result, UniversalError};
use crate::types::{ToolCall, ToolResult, ToolDefinition};
use async_trait::async_trait;
use serde_json::Value;
use std::collections::HashMap;
use tracing::debug;

// ===== Trait and Registry =====

#[async_trait]
pub trait Tool: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str;
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value;
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value>;
}

pub struct ToolRegistry {
    tools: HashMap<String, Box<dyn Tool>>,
}

impl ToolRegistry {
    pub fn new() -> Self { Self { tools: HashMap::new() } }
    pub fn register<T: Tool + 'static>(&mut self, tool: T) {
        self.tools.insert(tool.name().to_string(), Box::new(tool));
    }
    pub fn get_tool(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&Box<dyn Tool>> {
        self.tools.get(name)
    }
    pub fn list_definitions(&self) -> Vec<ToolDefinition> {
        self.tools.values()
            .map(|tool| ToolDefinition {
                name: tool.name().to_string(),
                description: tool.description().to_string(),
                parameters: tool.parameters_schema(),
            })
            .collect()
    }
    pub async fn execute_call(&self, call: ToolCall) -> ToolResult {
        match self.get_tool(&call.name) {
            Some(tool) => match tool.execute(call.arguments).await {
                Ok(output) => ToolResult { id: call.id, output, error: None },
                Err(e) => ToolResult { id: call.id, output: Value::Null, error: Some(e.to_string()) },
            },
            None => ToolResult { id: call.id, output: Value::Null, error: Some(format!("Tool '{}' not found", call.name)) },
        }
    }
}

// ===== Calculator Tool =====

pub struct CalculatorTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for CalculatorTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "calculator" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Executes arithmetic expressions" }
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "expression": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "description": "Math expression"
                }
            },
            "required": ["expression"]
        })
    }
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let expr = arguments["expression"].as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| UniversalError::ToolExecution("Missing expression".to_string()))?;
        let result = meval::eval_str(expr)
            .map_err(|e| UniversalError::ToolExecution(format!("Math error: {}", e)))?;
        Ok(serde_json::json!({ "expression": expr, "result": result }))
    }
}

// ===== Hashtree Tool =====

pub struct HashtreeTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for HashtreeTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "hashtree" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Search or retrieve content from Hashtree" }
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "action": { "type": "string", "enum": ["get", "search"] },
                "query": { "type": "string" }
            },
            "required": ["action", "query"]
        })
    }
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let action = arguments["action"].as_str().unwrap_or("search");
        let query = arguments["query"].as_str().unwrap_or("");
        match action {
            "get" => Ok(serde_json::json!({ "content": "Fake content", "found": true })),
            "search" => Ok(serde_json::json!({ "results": ["fake1", "fake2"], "count": 2 })),
            _ => Err(UniversalError::ToolExecution("Unknown action".to_string())),
        }
    }
}

// ===== Memory Tool =====

pub struct MemoryTool;

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for MemoryTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "memory" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Retrieve past experiences from memory" }
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "query": { "type": "string" },
                "limit": { "type": "integer", "default": 5 }
            },
            "required": ["query"]
        })
    }
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let query = arguments["query"].as_str().unwrap_or("");
        let limit = arguments["limit"].as_u64().unwrap_or(5);
        Ok(serde_json::json!({
            "query": query,
            "results": vec!["past experience 1", "past experience 2"],
            "count": 2
        }))
    }
}

// ===== Action Tool =====

pub struct ActionTool {
    callback: Box<dyn Fn(ActionType, &str, Value) -> Result<Value> + Send + Sync>,
}

impl ActionTool {
    pub fn new<F>(callback: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(ActionType, &str, Value) -> Result<Value> + Send + Sync + 'static,
    {
        Self { callback: Box::new(callback) }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl Tool for ActionTool {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "action" }
    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { "Execute an action" }
    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "action_type": { "type": "string" },
                "target": { "type": "string" },
                "params": { "type": "object" }
            },
            "required": ["action_type", "target"]
        })
    }
    async fn execute(&self, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value> {
        let action_type = arguments["action_type"].as_str().unwrap_or("observe");
        let target = arguments["target"].as_str().unwrap_or("");
        let params = arguments["params"].clone();
        let atype = match action_type {
            "transfer" => ActionType::Act,
            "communicate" => ActionType::Communicate,
            "learn" => ActionType::Learn,
            "plan" => ActionType::Plan,
            _ => ActionType::Observe,
        };
        (self.callback)(atype, target, params)
    }
}
```

## 🧠 SEMANA 4: `src/reasoning.rs` + `src/agentic.rs`

### `src/reasoning.rs` — GRAFO CAUSAL REAL COM PETGRAPH

```rust
//! Causal reasoning with petgraph.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::types::*;
use petgraph::graph::DiGraph;
use petgraph::algo;
use std::collections::HashMap;

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct CausalGraph {
    graph: DiGraph<String, f64>,
    nodes: HashMap<String, petgraph::prelude::NodeIndex>,
}

impl CausalGraph {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { graph: DiGraph::new(), nodes: HashMap::new() }
    }
    pub fn add_edge(&mut self, cause: &str, effect: &str, weight: f64) {
        let cause_idx = *self.nodes.entry(cause.to_string())
            .or_insert_with(|| self.graph.add_node(cause.to_string()));
        let effect_idx = *self.nodes.entry(effect.to_string())
            .or_insert_with(|| self.graph.add_node(effect.to_string()));
        self.graph.add_edge(cause_idx, effect_idx, weight);
    }
    pub fn get_effects(&self, cause: &str) -> Vec<Effect> {
        if let Some(&idx) = self.nodes.get(cause) {
            self.graph.neighbors(idx).map(|n| {
                let effect = self.graph.node_weight(n).unwrap().clone();
                Effect { cause: cause.to_string(), effect, magnitude: 1.0, uncertainty: 0.1 }
            }).collect()
        } else {
            Vec::new()
        }
    }
}

impl Default for CausalGraph {
    fn default() -> Self {
        let mut g = Self::new();
        g.add_edge("force", "acceleration", 0.9);
        g.add_edge("acceleration", "velocity", 0.95);
        g.add_edge("supply", "price", -0.8);
        g.add_edge("demand", "price", 0.9);
        g
    }
}

pub struct CausalReasoner {
    graph: CausalGraph,
}

impl Default for CausalReasoner {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self { graph: CausalGraph::default() }
    }
}

impl CausalReasoner {
    pub async fn reason(&self, perception: &PerceivedData, _state: &ModelState) -> Result<ReasoningResult> {
        let concepts = self.extract_concepts(perception);
        let effects = concepts.iter().flat_map(|c| self.graph.get_effects(&c.name)).collect();
        Ok(ReasoningResult {
            concepts,
            causal: CausalResult { effects },
            ..Default::default()
        })
    }
    fn extract_concepts(&self, perception: &PerceivedData) -> Vec<Concept> {
        let mut out = Vec::new();
        if let Some(text) = &perception.text {
            out.push(Concept { name: "text".to_string(), attributes: HashMap::new() });
        }
        out
    }
}
```

### `src/agentic.rs` — MCTS COM UCB1

```rust
//! Agentic planning with Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS).

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::types::*;
use rand::Rng;
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct MCTSNode {
    state: ModelState,
    visits: u32,
    total_reward: f64,
    children: Vec<MCTSNode>,
    action: Option<Action>,
}

impl MCTSNode {
    pub fn new(state: ModelState) -> Self {
        Self { state, visits: 0, total_reward: 0.0, children: Vec::new(), action: None }
    }
    pub fn ucb1(&self, total_visits: u32, exploration: f64) -> f64 {
        if self.visits == 0 { return f64::INFINITY; }
        let exploitation = self.total_reward / self.visits as f64;
        exploitation + exploration * ((total_visits as f64).ln() / self.visits as f64).sqrt()
    }
}

pub struct AgenticEngine {
    config: AgenticConfig,
}

impl AgenticEngine {
    pub async fn new(config: AgenticConfig) -> Result<Self> {
        Ok(Self { config })
    }
    pub async fn simulate(&self, _reasoning: &ReasoningResult, state: &ModelState) -> Result<SimulationResult> {
        // Simple simulation: perturb state
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        let mut alt_state = state.clone();
        alt_state.uncertainty *= (0.9 + 0.1 * rng.gen::<f64>());
        alt_state.expected_utility = Some(alt_state.expected_utility.unwrap_or(0.0) + rng.gen::<f64>() - 0.5);
        Ok(SimulationResult {
            predicted_state: alt_state,
            confidence: 0.8,
            alternative_outcomes: Vec::new(),
        })
    }
    pub async fn plan(&self, _reasoning: &ReasoningResult, _simulation: &SimulationResult, state: &ModelState) -> Result<Action> {
        // Simple MCTS with 10 iterations
        let mut root = MCTSNode::new(state.clone());
        for _ in 0..10 {
            let node = self.select(&mut root);
            let reward = self.rollout(&node.state);
            self.backpropagate(node, reward);
        }
        // Choose best child
        if let Some(best) = root.children.iter().max_by_key(|c| (c.total_reward / c.visits as f64) as u64) {
            Ok(best.action.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| Action {
                action_type: ActionType::Observe,
                target: None,
                parameters: serde_json::json!({}),
                expected_outcome: None,
            }))
        } else {
            Ok(Action { action_type: ActionType::Observe, target: None, parameters: serde_json::json!({}), expected_outcome: None })
        }
    }
    fn select(&self, node: &mut MCTSNode) -> &mut MCTSNode {
        if node.children.is_empty() { return node; }
        let total = node.visits;
        let best = node.children.iter_mut()
            .max_by(|a, b| a.ucb1(total, 1.4).partial_cmp(&b.ucb1(total, 1.4)).unwrap());
        if let Some(child) = best { self.select(child) } else { node }
    }
    fn rollout(&self, state: &ModelState) -> f64 {
        state.expected_utility.unwrap_or(0.0) * 0.8 + (1.0 - state.uncertainty) * 0.2
    }
    fn backpropagate(&self, node: &mut MCTSNode, reward: f64) {
        node.visits += 1;
        node.total_reward += reward;
    }
}
```

## 🔗 SEMANA 5: `src/pipeline.rs` — RAG + TOOLS + ACTIONS

```rust
//! Universal pipeline: RAG retrieval, tool execution, action dispatch.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::neural::NeuralEngine;
use crate::tools::ToolRegistry;
use crate::memory::LongTermMemory;
use crate::types::*;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;
use tracing::debug;

pub struct UniversalPipeline {
    tools: Arc<RwLock<ToolRegistry>>,
    memory: Arc<RwLock<LongTermMemory>>,
    state: Arc<RwLock<ModelState>>,
}

impl UniversalPipeline {
    pub async fn new(
        tools: Arc<RwLock<ToolRegistry>>,
        memory: Arc<RwLock<LongTermMemory>>,
        state: Arc<RwLock<ModelState>>,
    ) -> Self {
        Self { tools, memory, state }
    }

    pub async fn process(&self, input: UniversalInput) -> Result<UniversalOutput> {
        debug!("Processing pipeline input");

        // Build prompt with tools and memory context
        let mut prompt = String::new();
        if let Some(text) = &input.text {
            prompt.push_str(&format!("User: {}\n", text));
        }
        prompt.push_str("\nUse tools: tool:name|arg1|arg2...\n");
        prompt.push_str("Execute actions: action:type|target|params\n");

        let system = "You have tools and can execute actions.";
        let messages = vec![
            Message { role: Role::System, content: system.to_string(), timestamp: chrono::Utc::now() },
            Message { role: Role::User, content: prompt, timestamp: chrono::Utc::now() },
        ];

        let response = NeuralEngine::chat(&messages, None, 0.8, 0.9, 40, 1.1, 0.0, 0.0, 1024, 0, &[]).await?;

        // Parse tool calls and actions
        let tool_calls = self.parse_tool_calls(&response);
        let action = self.parse_action(&response);

        // Execute tools
        let mut tool_results = Vec::new();
        for call in tool_calls {
            if let Ok(result) = self.tools.read().await.execute_call(call).await {
                tool_results.push(result);
            }
        }

        // Update memory
        if let Some(act) = &action {
            self.memory.write().await.store(
                &PerceivedData { text: input.text.clone(), ..Default::default() },
                &ReasoningResult::default(),
                &SimulationResult::default(),
                act,
            ).await?;
        }

        // Update state
        self.state.write().await.uncertainty *= 0.9;

        let mut final_response = response;
        if !tool_results.is_empty() {
            final_response.push_str("\n\nTool results:\n");
            for r in tool_results {
                final_response.push_str(&format!("- {}: {}\n", r.id, r.output));
            }
        }

        Ok(UniversalOutput {
            response: final_response,
            action,
            simulation: None,
            reasoning: None,
        })
    }

    fn parse_tool_calls(&self, text: &str) -> Vec<ToolCall> {
        let mut calls = Vec::new();
        for line in text.lines() {
            if let Some(rest) = line.trim().strip_prefix("tool:") {
                let parts: Vec<&str> = rest.split('|').collect();
                if !parts.is_empty() {
                    let name = parts[0].to_string();
                    let args = if parts.len() > 1 { serde_json::json!({ "args": parts[1..].join("|") }) } else { serde_json::json!({}) };
                    calls.push(ToolCall { id: format!("call_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()), name, arguments: args });
                }
            }
        }
        calls
    }

    fn parse_action(&self, text: &str) -> Option<Action> {
        for line in text.lines() {
            if let Some(rest) = line.trim().strip_prefix("action:") {
                let parts: Vec<&str> = rest.split('|').collect();
                if parts.len() >= 2 {
                    let atype = match parts[0] {
                        "transfer" => ActionType::Act,
                        "learn" => ActionType::Learn,
                        "communicate" => ActionType::Communicate,
                        "plan" => ActionType::Plan,
                        _ => ActionType::Observe,
                    };
                    return Some(Action {
                        action_type: atype,
                        target: Some(parts[1].to_string()),
                        parameters: if parts.len() > 2 { serde_json::json!({ "params": parts[2..].join("|") }) } else { serde_json::json!({}) },
                        expected_outcome: None,
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        None
    }
}
```
