### 4.4. `src/types.rs`

```rust
//! Tipos compartilhados para x402 adapter.

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Rede blockchain suportada pelo x402.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum Blockchain {
    Base,
    Solana,
    Ethereum,
    Polygon,
    Optimism,
    Arbitrum,
    Avalanche,
    Aptos,
    Stellar,
    Sui,
}

/// Status de um pagamento x402.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum PaymentStatus {
    Pending,
    Completed,
    Failed,
    Expired,
}

/// Requisição para uma API x402.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct X402Request {
    pub endpoint: String,
    pub method: String,
    pub payload: serde_json::Value,
    pub blockchain: Blockchain,
    pub max_price: Option<f64>, // em USD
}

/// Resposta de uma API x402.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct X402Response {
    pub success: bool,
    pub data: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub payment_required: Option<PaymentRequired>,
    pub payment_status: Option<PaymentStatus>,
    pub transaction_id: Option<String>,
}

/// Informações de pagamento requerido (HTTP 402).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct PaymentRequired {
    pub amount: f64,               // em USD
    pub currency: String,          // "USDC"
    pub chain: String,             // "base", "solana"
    pub pay_to: String,            // endereço do merchant
    pub payment_id: String,
    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,
}

/// Informações de uma chamada à Agent Utility API.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct AgentUtilityEndpoint {
    pub name: String,
    pub price: f64,
    pub method: String,
    pub url: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub request_schema: Option<serde_json::Value>,
}

/// Catálogo completo da Agent Utility.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct AgentUtilityCatalog {
    pub endpoints: Vec<AgentUtilityEndpoint>,
    pub total: usize,
    pub openapi_url: String,
}
```

---

### 4.5. `src/client.rs`

```rust
//! Cliente principal para x402.

use crate::error::{X402Error, Result};
use crate::types::*;
use crate::payment::PaymentManager;
use reqwest::Client;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tracing::{debug, info, warn};

/// Cliente principal para x402.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct X402Client {
    http_client: Client,
    payment_manager: Arc<PaymentManager>,
    base_url: String,
}

impl X402Client {
    /// Cria um novo cliente x402.
    pub fn new(base_url: &str, private_key: &str, blockchain: Blockchain) -> Result<Self> {
        let http_client = Client::builder()
            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(60))
            .build()
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        let payment_manager = Arc::new(PaymentManager::new(private_key, blockchain)?);

        Ok(Self {
            http_client,
            payment_manager,
            base_url: base_url.to_string(),
        })
    }

    /// Executa uma chamada x402, lidando com o fluxo HTTP 402.
    pub async fn call(&self, request: X402Request) -> Result<X402Response> {
        let url = format!("{}/{}", self.base_url, request.endpoint);
        debug!("Calling x402 endpoint: {}", url);

        // 1. Envia a requisição inicial
        let response = self.http_client
            .post(&url)
            .json(&request.payload)
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        // 2. Verifica se é 402 Payment Required
        if response.status() == reqwest::StatusCode::PAYMENT_REQUIRED {
            let payment_required = self.parse_payment_required(&response)?;
            debug!("Payment required: {:?}", payment_required);

            // 3. Processa o pagamento
            let tx_id = self.payment_manager
                .process_payment(&payment_required)
                .await?;

            // 4. Retransmite a requisição com o pagamento
            let retry_response = self.retry_with_payment(&url, &request.payload, &tx_id).await?;

            return Ok(X402Response {
                success: true,
                data: Some(retry_response),
                payment_required: Some(payment_required),
                payment_status: Some(PaymentStatus::Completed),
                transaction_id: Some(tx_id),
            });
        }

        // 3. Sucesso (sem pagamento necessário)
        let data = response
            .json()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(X402Response {
            success: true,
            data: Some(data),
            payment_required: None,
            payment_status: Some(PaymentStatus::Completed),
            transaction_id: None,
        })
    }

    /// Extrai informações de pagamento do header 402.
    fn parse_payment_required(&self, response: &reqwest::Response) -> Result<PaymentRequired> {
        let headers = response.headers();

        // O header 'payment-required' contém os requisitos em base64
        let payment_header = headers
            .get("payment-required")
            .ok_or_else(|| X402Error::Protocol("Missing payment-required header".to_string()))?
            .to_str()
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Protocol(e.to_string()))?;

        let decoded = base64::decode(payment_header)
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Protocol(format!("Invalid base64: {}", e)))?;

        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&decoded)
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Protocol(format!("Invalid JSON: {}", e)))?;

        Ok(PaymentRequired {
            amount: json["amount"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0),
            currency: json["currency"].as_str().unwrap_or("USDC").to_string(),
            chain: json["chain"].as_str().unwrap_or("base").to_string(),
            pay_to: json["pay_to"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
            payment_id: json["payment_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
            expires_at: chrono::Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::minutes(5),
        })
    }

    /// Retransmite a requisição com o pagamento realizado.
    async fn retry_with_payment(
        &self,
        url: &str,
        payload: &serde_json::Value,
        tx_id: &str,
    ) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
        let response = self.http_client
            .post(url)
            .header("payment-signature", tx_id)
            .json(payload)
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        if !response.status().is_success() {
            let status = response.status();
            let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
            return Err(X402Error::Protocol(format!(
                "Payment retry failed: {} - {}",
                status, text
            )));
        }

        let data = response
            .json()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(data)
    }
}
```

---

### 4.6. `src/payment.rs`

```rust
//! Gerenciamento de pagamentos x402.

use crate::error::{X402Error, Result};
use crate::types::{PaymentRequired, Blockchain, PaymentStatus};
use std::sync::Arc;
use tracing::{debug, info};

/// Gerenciador de pagamentos x402.
pub struct PaymentManager {
    private_key: String,
    blockchain: Blockchain,
}

impl PaymentManager {
    /// Cria um novo gerenciador de pagamentos.
    pub fn new(private_key: &str, blockchain: Blockchain) -> Result<Self> {
        Ok(Self {
            private_key: private_key.to_string(),
            blockchain,
        })
    }

    /// Processa um pagamento x402.
    pub async fn process_payment(&self, payment: &PaymentRequired) -> Result<String> {
        info!(
            "Processing x402 payment: {} {} to {} on {}",
            payment.amount, payment.currency, payment.pay_to, payment.chain
        );

        // Valida a rede
        if !self.supports_chain(&payment.chain) {
            return Err(X402Error::Protocol(format!(
                "Chain {} not supported",
                payment.chain
            )));
        }

        // Em produção: enviar transação USDC na blockchain correspondente
        // Usando ethers-rs para EVM ou solana-sdk para Solana
        match self.blockchain {
            Blockchain::Base | Blockchain::Ethereum | Blockchain::Polygon => {
                self.pay_evm(payment).await
            }
            Blockchain::Solana => {
                self.pay_solana(payment).await
            }
            _ => Err(X402Error::Protocol("Blockchain not yet supported".to_string())),
        }
    }

    /// Pagamento via EVM (Base, Ethereum, Polygon).
    async fn pay_evm(&self, payment: &PaymentRequired) -> Result<String> {
        // Placeholder: em produção, usar ethers-rs para transferir USDC
        // const USDC_ADDRESS: &str = "0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913"; // Base
        debug!("Sending {} USDC to {} on EVM chain", payment.amount, payment.pay_to);

        // Simula transação
        let tx_hash = format!("0x{}", hex::encode(uuid::Uuid::new_v4().as_bytes()));
        Ok(tx_hash)
    }

    /// Pagamento via Solana.
    async fn pay_solana(&self, payment: &PaymentRequired) -> Result<String> {
        // Placeholder: em produção, usar solana-sdk para transferir USDC
        // const USDC_ADDRESS_SOLANA: &str = "EPjFWdd5AufqSSqeM2qN1xzybapC8G4wEGGkZwyTDt1v";
        debug!("Sending {} USDC to {} on Solana", payment.amount, payment.pay_to);

        let tx_hash = format!("solana:{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());
        Ok(tx_hash)
    }

    /// Verifica suporte à chain.
    fn supports_chain(&self, chain: &str) -> bool {
        matches!(
            chain,
            "base" | "ethereum" | "polygon" | "solana" | "optimism" | "arbitrum"
        )
    }

    /// Obtém o status de um pagamento.
    pub async fn get_payment_status(&self, tx_id: &str) -> Result<PaymentStatus> {
        // Placeholder: consultar blockchain
        Ok(PaymentStatus::Completed)
    }
}
```

---

### 4.7. `src/agent_utility.rs`

```rust
//! Client para Agent Utility API (x402.agentutility.ai).

use crate::error::{X402Error, Result};
use crate::types::{AgentUtilityEndpoint, AgentUtilityCatalog};
use crate::client::X402Client;
use serde_json::json;
use std::collections::HashMap;
use tracing::debug;

/// Cliente para Agent Utility API.
pub struct AgentUtilityClient {
    x402_client: X402Client,
    catalog_cache: Option<AgentUtilityCatalog>,
}

impl AgentUtilityClient {
    /// Cria um novo cliente.
    pub fn new(x402_client: X402Client) -> Self {
        Self {
            x402_client,
            catalog_cache: None,
        }
    }

    /// Obtém o catálogo completo de endpoints.
    pub async fn get_catalog(&mut self) -> Result<AgentUtilityCatalog> {
        if let Some(catalog) = &self.catalog_cache {
            return Ok(catalog.clone());
        }

        // O catálogo está disponível via GET no endpoint raiz
        // A resposta é o JSON com todos os endpoints
        let url = "https://x402.agentutility.ai";
        let response = self.x402_client.http_client
            .get(url)
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        let json: serde_json::Value = response
            .json()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| X402Error::Http(e.to_string()))?;

        let endpoints = self.parse_endpoints(&json)?;
        let catalog = AgentUtilityCatalog {
            endpoints,
            total: json["endpoint_count"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0) as usize,
            openapi_url: json["openapi"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
        };

        self.catalog_cache = Some(catalog.clone());
        Ok(catalog)
    }

    /// Parsing dos endpoints do JSON do catálogo.
    fn parse_endpoints(&self, json: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<Vec<AgentUtilityEndpoint>> {
        let mut endpoints = Vec::new();

        if let Some(arr) = json["endpoints"].as_array() {
            for item in arr {
                let name = item["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let price = item["price"].as_str().unwrap_or("$0.00");
                let price_clean = price.trim_start_matches('$').parse::<f64>().unwrap_or(0.0);
                let method = item["method"].as_str().unwrap_or("POST").to_string();
                let url = item["url"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let description = item["description"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();

                endpoints.push(AgentUtilityEndpoint {
                    name,
                    price: price_clean,
                    method,
                    url,
                    description,
                    request_schema: None,
                });
            }
        }

        Ok(endpoints)
    }

    /// Chama um endpoint específico da Agent Utility.
    pub async fn call_endpoint(
        &self,
        endpoint_name: &str,
        payload: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
        debug!("Calling Agent Utility endpoint: {}", endpoint_name);

        // Constrói a requisição x402
        let request = crate::types::X402Request {
            endpoint: endpoint_name.to_string(),
            method: "POST".to_string(),
            payload,
            blockchain: crate::types::Blockchain::Base,
            max_price: Some(0.50), // $0.50 max
        };

        let response = self.x402_client.call(request).await?;

        response.data.ok_or_else(|| {
            X402Error::AgentUtility("No data in response".to_string())
        })
    }

    /// Chama o endpoint pdf-to-markdown.
    pub async fn pdf_to_markdown(&self, pdf_url: &str) -> Result<String> {
        let payload = json!({
            "pdf_url": pdf_url,
            "output_format": "markdown",
        });

        let result = self.call_endpoint("pdf-to-markdown", payload).await?;
        let markdown = result["markdown"]
            .as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| X402Error::AgentUtility("Missing markdown field".to_string()))?
            .to_string();

        Ok(markdown)
    }

    /// Chama o endpoint summarize-text.
    pub async fn summarize_text(&self, text: &str) -> Result<String> {
        let payload = json!({
            "text": text,
            "summary_type": "paragraph",
        });

        let result = self.call_endpoint("summarize-text", payload).await?;
        let summary = result["summary"]
            .as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| X402Error::AgentUtility("Missing summary field".to_string()))?
            .to_string();

        Ok(summary)
    }

    /// Chama o endpoint translate-text.
    pub async fn translate_text(&self, text: &str, target_lang: &str) -> Result<String> {
        let payload = json!({
            "text": text,
            "target_language": target_lang,
            "formality": "neutral",
        });

        let result = self.call_endpoint("translate-text", payload).await?;
        let translation = result["translation"]
            .as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| X402Error::AgentUtility("Missing translation field".to_string()))?
            .to_string();

        Ok(translation)
    }

    /// Chama o endpoint image-edit.
    pub async fn image_edit(&self, image_url: &str, prompt: &str) -> Result<String> {
        let payload = json!({
            "image_url": image_url,
            "prompt": prompt,
        });

        let result = self.call_endpoint("image-edit", payload).await?;
        let edited_url = result["edited_url"]
            .as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| X402Error::AgentUtility("Missing edited_url field".to_string()))?
            .to_string();

        Ok(edited_url)
    }

    /// Busca um endpoint por nome.
    pub async fn find_endpoint(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<AgentUtilityEndpoint>> {
        let catalog = self.get_catalog().await?;
        Ok(catalog.endpoints.into_iter().find(|e| e.name == name))
    }

    /// Lista endpoints por categoria.
    pub async fn list_by_category(&mut self, category: &str) -> Result<Vec<AgentUtilityEndpoint>> {
        let catalog = self.get_catalog().await?;
        Ok(catalog
            .endpoints
            .into_iter()
            .filter(|e| e.name.contains(category) || e.description.contains(category))
            .collect())
    }
}
```

---

### 4.8. `src/reown.rs`

```rust
//! Integração com Reown (WalletConnect) para autenticação e pagamento.

use crate::error::{X402Error, Result};
use crate::payment::PaymentManager;
use crate::types::{Blockchain, PaymentRequired};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tracing::debug;

/// Integração com Reown (WalletConnect).
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ReownIntegration {
    session_manager: Arc<SessionManager>,
    payment_manager: Arc<PaymentManager>,
}

/// Sessão WalletConnect.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WalletSession {
    pub session_id: String,
    pub address: String,
    pub chain_id: String,
    pub wallet_connect_uri: String,
}

/// Gerenciador de sessões.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct SessionManager {
    sessions: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<String, WalletSession>>>,
}

impl SessionManager {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            sessions: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),
        }
    }

    /// Cria uma nova sessão WalletConnect.
    pub async fn create_session(&self, address: &str, chain_id: &str) -> Result<WalletSession> {
        let session_id = format!("wc-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());
        let wallet_connect_uri = format!(
            "wc:{}@2?bridge=wss://bridge.walletconnect.org&key={}",
            session_id,
            hex::encode(uuid::Uuid::new_v4().as_bytes())
        );

        let session = WalletSession {
            session_id: session_id.clone(),
            address: address.to_string(),
            chain_id: chain_id.to_string(),
            wallet_connect_uri,
        };

        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;
        sessions.insert(session_id, session.clone());

        Ok(session)
    }

    /// Obtém uma sessão existente.
    pub async fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<WalletSession> {
        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;
        sessions
            .get(session_id)
            .cloned()
            .ok_or_else(|| X402Error::WalletConnect("Session not found".to_string()))
    }

    /// Remove uma sessão.
    pub async fn revoke_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<()> {
        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;
        if sessions.remove(session_id).is_none() {
            return Err(X402Error::WalletConnect("Session not found".to_string()));
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

impl ReownIntegration {
    /// Cria uma nova integração.
    pub fn new(session_manager: Arc<SessionManager>, payment_manager: Arc<PaymentManager>) -> Self {
        Self {
            session_manager,
            payment_manager,
        }
    }

    /// Autentica um usuário via WalletConnect.
    pub async fn authenticate(
        &self,
        session_id: &str,
        signature: &str,
        message: &str,
    ) -> Result<bool> {
        let session = self.session_manager.get_session(session_id).await?;

        // Placeholder: verificação de assinatura SIWE/SIWX
        // Em produção, usar ethers-rs para verificar a assinatura
        debug!(
            "Verifying signature for session {} from {}",
            session_id, session.address
        );

        // Simula verificação
        Ok(true)
    }

    /// Paga por uma API usando a carteira conectada.
    pub async fn pay_with_wallet(
        &self,
        session_id: &str,
        payment: &PaymentRequired,
    ) -> Result<String> {
        let session = self.session_manager.get_session(session_id).await?;

        debug!(
            "Paying {} USDC with wallet {} via Reown",
            payment.amount, session.address
        );

        // Usa o PaymentManager para processar o pagamento
        // A chave privada viria da carteira do usuário via WalletConnect
        // (na prática, o usuário assina a transação na carteira)
        self.payment_manager.process_payment(payment).await
    }

    /// Obtém a URI de conexão para WalletConnect.
    pub async fn get_connection_uri(&self, address: &str, chain_id: &str) -> Result<String> {
        let session = self.session_manager.create_session(address, chain_id).await?;
        Ok(session.wallet_connect_uri)
    }
}

impl Default for SessionManager {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::new()
    }
}
```

### 4.9. `tests/integration.rs`

```rust
//! Testes de integração para x402 adapter.

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use cathedral_x402_adapter::*;
    use serde_json::json;
    use serial_test::serial;

    #[tokio::test]
    #[serial]
    async fn test_agent_utility_catalog() {
        let client = X402Client::new(
            "https://x402.agentutility.ai",
            "0x123...", // private key placeholder
            Blockchain::Base,
        ).unwrap();

        let mut agent = AgentUtilityClient::new(client);
        let catalog = agent.get_catalog().await.unwrap();

        assert!(catalog.total > 0);
        assert!(catalog.total >= 564);
        println!("Catalog has {} endpoints", catalog.total);
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    #[serial]
    #[ignore] // Requer pagamento real
    async fn test_pdf_to_markdown() {
        let client = X402Client::new(
            "https://x402.agentutility.ai",
            "0x123...", // private key placeholder
            Blockchain::Base,
        ).unwrap();

        let agent = AgentUtilityClient::new(client);
        let result = agent.pdf_to_markdown(
            "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/py-pdf/sample-files/main/001-trivial/minimal-document.pdf"
        ).await;

        // Em ambiente de teste, pode falhar sem pagamento real
        println!("PDF to markdown result: {:?}", result);
    }

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_reown_session() {
        let session_manager = Arc::new(SessionManager::new());
        let payment_manager = Arc::new(PaymentManager::new("0x123", Blockchain::Base).unwrap());
        let reown = ReownIntegration::new(session_manager.clone(), payment_manager);

        let uri = reown.get_connection_uri("0x1234...", "eip155:1").await.unwrap();
        assert!(uri.starts_with("wc:"));

        let session = session_manager.get_session("wc-123").await;
        assert!(session.is_err()); // Sessão não existe
    }
}
```

### 4.11. `README.md`

```markdown
# Cathedral X402 Adapter (Substrato 9522)

Integração do protocolo x402 e Agent Utility API para pagamentos por chamada.

## Visão Geral

Este crate permite que agentes Cathedral paguem por APIs usando o protocolo x402, com autenticação via Reown (WalletConnect).

**564 APIs disponíveis** incluindo:
- PDF processing (markdown, text, OCR)
- AI text (summarize, translate, humanize)
- Image/Video (edit, convert, watermark)
- Market data (crypto, collectibles)
- Developer tools (resume scorer, alt text)

## Arquitetura

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Agent Cathedral → X402Client → AgentUtilityClient → API      │
│                              ↓                                 │
│                      Payment Manager (USDC)                   │
│                              ↓                                 │
│                      Reown (WalletConnect)                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Uso

```rust
use cathedral_x402_adapter::{X402Client, AgentUtilityClient, Blockchain};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let client = X402Client::new(
        "https://x402.agentutility.ai",
        "0xyour_private_key",
        Blockchain::Base,
    )?;

    let agent = AgentUtilityClient::new(client);

    // PDF to Markdown
    let markdown = agent.pdf_to_markdown("https://example.com/doc.pdf").await?;

    // Summarize text
    let summary = agent.summarize_text("Long text...").await?;

    // Translate
    let translation = agent.translate_text("Hello world", "pt").await?;

    Ok(())
}
```

## Licença

MIT OR Apache-2.0
```

**Arquiteto-Chefe:** ORCID 0009-0005-2697-4668

**Status:** ✅ CÓDIGO GERADO — PRONTO PARA IMPLEMENTAÇÃO

- **Parser ISO 20022** para mensagens pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053
- **Gerador SWIFT MX** com headers e mensagens ISO 20022
- **Validação XSD** (placeholder)
- **FIX ↔ ISO 20022 Transformer** (básico)

## 📄 2. SRC/LIB.RS

```rust
//! # Cathedral ISO 20022 (Substrato 9517)
//!
//! Parser e gerador de mensagens ISO 20022 para interoperabilidade financeira global.
//!
//! ## Mensagens Suportadas
//!
//! | Mensagem | Descrição | Uso |
//! |----------|-----------|-----|
//! | pain.001 | Customer Credit Transfer Initiation | Iniciação de transferência de crédito |
//! | pacs.008 | FIToFICustomerCreditTransfer | Transferência de crédito entre bancos |
//! | camt.053 | BankToCustomerStatement | Extrato bancário |
//!
//! ## SWIFT MX
//!
//! Geração de mensagens SWIFT MX com headers (Block 1-5) + corpo ISO 20022.
//!
//! ## Architecture
//!
//! ```text
//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
//! │  cathedral-iso20022                                            │
//! │  ├── parser/         XML → Rust structs                       │
//! │  ├── generator/      Rust structs → XML                       │
//! │  ├── validator/      XSD validation (optional)               │
//! │  ├── messages/       pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053            │
//! │  ├── swift/          SWIFT MX headers + messages              │
//! │  └── transformer/    FIX ↔ ISO 20022                         │
//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
//! ```

#![warn(missing_docs)]
#![deny(unsafe_code)]

pub mod error;
pub mod types;
pub mod parser;
pub mod generator;
pub mod validator;
pub mod messages;
pub mod swift;
pub mod transformer;

// Re-exports
pub use error::{IsoError, Result};
pub use types::*;
pub use parser::IsoParser;
pub use generator::IsoGenerator;
pub use validator::Validator;
pub use messages::{Pain001, Pacs008, Camt053};
pub use swift::{SwiftMxMessage, SwiftHeader, SwiftBlock};

/// Crate version.
pub const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_version() {
        assert!(!VERSION.is_empty());
    }
}
```

## 📄 3. SRC/ERROR.RS

```rust
//! Error handling for ISO 20022 parser and generator.

use thiserror::Error;

/// Result type alias.
pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, IsoError>;

/// Errors that can occur during ISO 20022 processing.
#[derive(Error, Debug, Clone)]
pub enum IsoError {
    /// XML parsing error.
    #[error("XML parsing error: {0}")]
    XmlParse(String),

    /// XML serialization error.
    #[error("XML serialization error: {0}")]
    XmlSerialize(String),

    /// Validation error.
    #[error("Validation error: {0}")]
    Validation(String),

    /// Invalid field value.
    #[error("Invalid field: {field} = {value}")]
    InvalidField { field: String, value: String },

    /// Missing required field.
    #[error("Missing required field: {0}")]
    MissingField(String),

    /// SWIFT header error.
    #[error("SWIFT header error: {0}")]
    SwiftHeader(String),

    /// FIX ↔ ISO 20022 transformation error.
    #[error("Transformation error: {0}")]
    Transformation(String),

    /// IO error.
    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(String),

    /// Internal error.
    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}

impl From<quick_xml::Error> for IsoError {
    fn from(err: quick_xml::Error) -> Self {
        IsoError::XmlParse(err.to_string())
    }
}

impl From<serde_json::Error> for IsoError {
    fn from(err: serde_json::Error) -> Self {
        IsoError::XmlSerialize(err.to_string())
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 4. SRC/TYPES.RS

```toml
//! Common types for ISO 20022 messages.

use chrono::{DateTime, Utc, NaiveDate};
use rust_decimal::Decimal;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use uuid::Uuid;

/// ISO 20022 currency code (ISO 4217).
pub type CurrencyCode = String;

/// BIC (Bank Identifier Code) — ISO 9362.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct Bic(pub String);

impl Bic {
    pub fn new(code: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {
        let cleaned = code.trim().to_uppercase();
        if cleaned.len() != 8 && cleaned.len() != 11 {
            return Err(format!("Invalid BIC length: {} (expected 8 or 11)", cleaned.len()));
        }
        Ok(Bic(cleaned))
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for Bic {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}", self.0)
    }
}

/// IBAN (International Bank Account Number).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct Iban(pub String);

impl Iban {
    pub fn new(code: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {
        let cleaned = code.trim().replace(' ', "");
        if cleaned.len() < 14 || cleaned.len() > 34 {
            return Err(format!("Invalid IBAN length: {} (expected 14-34)", cleaned.len()));
        }
        Ok(Iban(cleaned))
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for Iban {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}", self.0)
    }
}

/// Amount with currency.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Amount {
    pub value: Decimal,
    pub currency: CurrencyCode,
}

impl Amount {
    pub fn new(value: Decimal, currency: &str) -> Self {
        Self { value, currency: currency.to_string() }
    }

    pub fn from_f64(value: f64, currency: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            value: Decimal::from_f64(value).unwrap_or(Decimal::ZERO),
            currency: currency.to_string(),
        }
    }
}

/// Party (sender, receiver, etc.).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Party {
    pub name: String,
    pub bic: Option<Bic>,
    pub iban: Option<Iban>,
    pub address: Option<PostalAddress>,
}

/// Postal address.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct PostalAddress {
    pub street: Option<String>,
    pub city: Option<String>,
    pub country: String,
    pub postal_code: Option<String>,
}

/// Transaction status.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum TransactionStatus {
    Pending,
    Accepted,
    Settled,
    Rejected,
    Cancelled,
}

/// Payment purpose code (ISO 20022).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct PurposeCode(pub String);

/// Identification of a transaction.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TransactionId {
    pub id: String,
    pub instructed_amount: Amount,
    pub debtor: Party,
    pub creditor: Party,
    pub status: TransactionStatus,
    pub creation_date: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

---

//! ISO 20022 message types.

pub mod pain_001;
pub mod pacs_008;
pub mod camt_053;

pub use pain_001::Pain001;
pub use pacs_008::Pacs008;
pub use camt_053::Camt053;

## 📄 6. SRC/MESSAGES/PAIN_001.RS

```rust
//! pain.001 — Customer Credit Transfer Initiation.

use crate::types::*;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

/// pain.001 message — Customer Credit Transfer Initiation.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Pain001 {
    pub message_id: String,
    pub creation_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub payment_info: PaymentInfo,
    pub debtor: Party,
    pub debtor_account: AccountInfo,
    pub creditor: Party,
    pub creditor_account: AccountInfo,
    pub amount: Amount,
    pub purpose: Option<PurposeCode>,
    pub instruction_id: Option<String>,
}

/// Payment information for pain.001.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct PaymentInfo {
    pub payment_method: PaymentMethod,
    pub requested_execution_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub currency: String,
    pub charge_bearer: Option<ChargeBearer>,
}

/// Payment method.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum PaymentMethod {
    CreditTransfer,
    DirectDebit,
}

/// Charge bearer.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum ChargeBearer {
    Debtor,
    Creditor,
    Shared,
}

/// Account information.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct AccountInfo {
    pub iban: Iban,
    pub bic: Option<Bic>,
    pub currency: CurrencyCode,
}

impl Pain001 {
    /// Creates a new pain.001 message.
    pub fn new(
        debtor: Party,
        creditor: Party,
        amount: Amount,
        debtor_iban: Iban,
        creditor_iban: Iban,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            message_id: format!("PAIN-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            creation_date: Utc::now(),
            payment_info: PaymentInfo {
                payment_method: PaymentMethod::CreditTransfer,
                requested_execution_date: Some(Utc::now()),
                currency: amount.currency.clone(),
                charge_bearer: Some(ChargeBearer::Shared),
            },
            debtor,
            debtor_account: AccountInfo {
                iban: debtor_iban,
                bic: None,
                currency: amount.currency.clone(),
            },
            creditor,
            creditor_account: AccountInfo {
                iban: creditor_iban,
                bic: None,
                currency: amount.currency.clone(),
            },
            amount,
            purpose: None,
            instruction_id: None,
        }
    }
}
```

## 📄 7. SRC/MESSAGES/PACS_008.RS

```rust
//! pacs.008 — FIToFICustomerCreditTransfer.

use crate::types::*;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

/// pacs.008 message — FIToFICustomerCreditTransfer.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Pacs008 {
    pub message_id: String,
    pub creation_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub transfer: CreditTransfer,
    pub debtor: Party,
    pub debtor_account: AccountInfo,
    pub creditor: Party,
    pub creditor_account: AccountInfo,
    pub instructed_amount: Amount,
}

/// Credit transfer details.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct CreditTransfer {
    pub transaction_id: String,
    pub instruction_id: Option<String>,
    pub end_to_end_id: Option<String>,
    pub purpose: Option<PurposeCode>,
    pub settlement_method: SettlementMethod,
    pub requested_execution_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
}

/// Settlement method.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum SettlementMethod {
    Netting,
    Clearing,
    RealTime,
}

impl Pacs008 {
    /// Creates a new pacs.008 message.
    pub fn new(
        debtor: Party,
        creditor: Party,
        amount: Amount,
        debtor_iban: Iban,
        creditor_iban: Iban,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            message_id: format!("PACS-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            creation_date: Utc::now(),
            transfer: CreditTransfer {
                transaction_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string(),
                instruction_id: None,
                end_to_end_id: None,
                purpose: None,
                settlement_method: SettlementMethod::RealTime,
                requested_execution_date: Some(Utc::now()),
            },
            debtor,
            debtor_account: AccountInfo {
                iban: debtor_iban,
                bic: None,
                currency: amount.currency.clone(),
            },
            creditor,
            creditor_account: AccountInfo {
                iban: creditor_iban,
                bic: None,
                currency: amount.currency.clone(),
            },
            instructed_amount: amount,
        }
    }
}
```

## 📄 8. SRC/MESSAGES/CAMT_053.RS

```rust
//! camt.053 — BankToCustomerStatement.

use crate::types::*;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

/// camt.053 message — BankToCustomerStatement.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Camt053 {
    pub message_id: String,
    pub creation_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub statement: BankStatement,
}

/// Bank statement.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct BankStatement {
    pub id: String,
    pub account: AccountInfo,
    pub from_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub to_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub entries: Vec<StatementEntry>,
    pub balance: Amount,
}

/// Statement entry.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct StatementEntry {
    pub entry_id: String,
    pub amount: Amount,
    pub credit_debit: CreditDebit,
    pub booking_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub counterparty: Option<Party>,
    pub description: Option<String>,
}

/// Credit or debit.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum CreditDebit {
    Credit,
    Debit,
}

impl Camt053 {
    /// Creates a new camt.053 message.
    pub fn new(account: AccountInfo, from: DateTime<Utc>, to: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
        Self {
            message_id: format!("CAMT-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            creation_date: Utc::now(),
            statement: BankStatement {
                id: format!("STMT-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
                account,
                from_date: from,
                to_date: to,
                entries: Vec::new(),
                balance: Amount::new(Decimal::ZERO, "USD"),
            },
        }
    }

    /// Adds an entry to the statement.
    pub fn add_entry(&mut self, entry: StatementEntry) {
        self.statement.entries.push(entry);
    }
}
```

## 📄 9. SRC/PARSER.RS

```rust
//! XML parser for ISO 20022 messages.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::messages::{Pain001, Pacs008, Camt053};
use quick_xml::de::from_str;

/// Parser for ISO 20022 XML messages.
pub struct IsoParser;

impl IsoParser {
    /// Parses a pain.001 message from XML.
    pub fn parse_pain_001(xml: &str) -> Result<Pain001> {
        let pain: Pain001 = from_str(xml)?;
        Ok(pain)
    }

    /// Parses a pacs.008 message from XML.
    pub fn parse_pacs_008(xml: &str) -> Result<Pacs008> {
        let pacs: Pacs008 = from_str(xml)?;
        Ok(pacs)
    }

    /// Parses a camt.053 message from XML.
    pub fn parse_camt_053(xml: &str) -> Result<Camt053> {
        let camt: Camt053 = from_str(xml)?;
        Ok(camt)
    }

    /// Generic parser for any ISO 20022 message.
    pub fn parse<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(xml: &str) -> Result<T> {
        let message: T = from_str(xml)?;
        Ok(message)
    }
}
```

## 📄 10. SRC/GENERATOR.RS

```rust
//! XML generator for ISO 20022 messages.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::messages::{Pain001, Pacs008, Camt053};
use quick_xml::se::to_string;

/// Generator for ISO 20022 XML messages.
pub struct IsoGenerator;

impl IsoGenerator {
    /// Generates pain.001 XML.
    pub fn generate_pain_001(pain: &Pain001) -> Result<String> {
        let xml = to_string(&pain)?;
        Ok(xml)
    }

    /// Generates pacs.008 XML.
    pub fn generate_pacs_008(pacs: &Pacs008) -> Result<String> {
        let xml = to_string(&pacs)?;
        Ok(xml)
    }

    /// Generates camt.053 XML.
    pub fn generate_camt_053(camt: &Camt053) -> Result<String> {
        let xml = to_string(&camt)?;
        Ok(xml)
    }

    /// Generic generator for any ISO 20022 message.
    pub fn generate<T: serde::Serialize>(message: &T) -> Result<String> {
        let xml = to_string(message)?;
        Ok(xml)
    }
}
```

## 📄 11. SRC/VALIDATOR.RS

```rust
//! Validation for ISO 20022 messages.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::messages::{Pain001, Pacs008, Camt053};
use rust_decimal::Decimal;

/// Validator for ISO 20022 messages.
pub struct Validator;

impl Validator {
    /// Validates a pain.001 message.
    pub fn validate_pain_001(pain: &Pain001) -> Result<()> {
        if pain.message_id.is_empty() {
            return Err(crate::IsoError::MissingField("message_id".to_string()));
        }
        if pain.amount.value <= Decimal::ZERO {
            return Err(crate::IsoError::InvalidField {
                field: "amount".to_string(),
                value: pain.amount.value.to_string(),
            });
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Validates a pacs.008 message.
    pub fn validate_pacs_008(pacs: &Pacs008) -> Result<()> {
        if pacs.message_id.is_empty() {
            return Err(crate::IsoError::MissingField("message_id".to_string()));
        }
        if pacs.instructed_amount.value <= Decimal::ZERO {
            return Err(crate::IsoError::InvalidField {
                field: "instructed_amount".to_string(),
                value: pacs.instructed_amount.value.to_string(),
            });
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Validates a camt.053 message.
    pub fn validate_camt_053(camt: &Camt053) -> Result<()> {
        if camt.message_id.is_empty() {
            return Err(crate::IsoError::MissingField("message_id".to_string()));
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## 📄 13. SRC/SWIFT/HEADER.RS

```rust
//! SWIFT MX headers (Block 1-5).

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

/// SWIFT MX message headers.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct SwiftHeader {
    pub basic: Block1,
    pub app_header: Block2,
    pub user_header: Option<Block3>,
    pub text_block: Block4,
    pub trailer: Block5,
}

/// Block 1 — Basic Header.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Block1 {
    pub length: u32,
    pub priority: Priority,
    pub application_id: String,
    pub service_id: String,
    pub logical_terminal: String,
    pub session_number: u32,
    pub sequence_number: u32,
}

/// Block 2 — Application Header.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Block2 {
    pub message_type: String,  // e.g., "MX", "101", "103"
    pub receiver_address: String,
    pub message_priority: Priority,
}

/// Block 3 — User Header (optional).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Block3 {
    pub user_fields: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

/// Block 4 — Text Block (ISO 20022 message).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Block4 {
    pub content: String,  // XML body
}

/// Block 5 — Trailer.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Block5 {
    pub checksum: String,
    pub message_authentication_code: Option<String>,
}

/// Message priority.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum Priority {
    Normal,
    Urgent,
    System,
}

impl SwiftHeader {
    /// Creates a new SWIFT MX header.
    pub fn new(message_type: &str, receiver: &str, content: String) -> Self {
        let now = Utc::now();
        Self {
            basic: Block1 {
                length: 0,
                priority: Priority::Normal,
                application_id: "F".to_string(),
                service_id: "01".to_string(),
                logical_terminal: "XXXXX".to_string(),
                session_number: 1,
                sequence_number: 1,
            },
            app_header: Block2 {
                message_type: message_type.to_string(),
                receiver_address: receiver.to_string(),
                message_priority: Priority::Normal,
            },
            user_header: None,
            text_block: Block4 { content },
            trailer: Block5 {
                checksum: "0000".to_string(),
                message_authentication_code: None,
            },
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 📄 14. SRC/SWIFT/MX_MESSAGE.RS

```rust
//! Complete SWIFT MX message (headers + ISO 20022 body).

use crate::swift::header::SwiftHeader;
use crate::messages::{Pain001, Pacs008, Camt053};
use crate::generator::IsoGenerator;
use crate::error::Result;

/// Complete SWIFT MX message.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct SwiftMxMessage {
    pub header: SwiftHeader,
    pub body: String,
}

impl SwiftMxMessage {
    /// Creates a new SWIFT MX message from a pain.001.
    pub fn from_pain_001(pain: &Pain001, receiver: &str) -> Result<Self> {
        let body = IsoGenerator::generate_pain_001(pain)?;
        let header = SwiftHeader::new("MX", receiver, body.clone());
        Ok(Self { header, body })
    }

    /// Creates a new SWIFT MX message from a pacs.008.
    pub fn from_pacs_008(pacs: &Pacs008, receiver: &str) -> Result<Self> {
        let body = IsoGenerator::generate_pacs_008(pacs)?;
        let header = SwiftHeader::new("MX", receiver, body.clone());
        Ok(Self { header, body })
    }

    /// Creates a new SWIFT MX message from a camt.053.
    pub fn from_camt_053(camt: &Camt053, receiver: &str) -> Result<Self> {
        let body = IsoGenerator::generate_camt_053(camt)?;
        let header = SwiftHeader::new("MX", receiver, body.clone());
        Ok(Self { header, body })
    }

    /// Renders the full SWIFT MX message as a string.
    pub fn render(&self) -> String {
        // Em produção: renderizar headers SWIFT + XML body
        // {1:{basic}}{2:{app}}{3:{user}}{4:{text}}{5:{trailer}}
        format!(
            "{{1:{}}}{{2:{}}}{}{{5:{}}}{}",
            self.basic_header(),
            self.app_header(),
            self.user_header(),
            self.trailer(),
            self.body
        )
    }

    fn basic_header(&self) -> String {
        // Placeholder: renderização real
        format!("F01XXXXX{}001", self.header.basic.session_number)
    }

    fn app_header(&self) -> String {
        // Placeholder
        format!("{}", self.header.app_header.message_type)
    }

    fn user_header(&self) -> String {
        if let Some(_) = &self.header.user_header {
            "{3:}".to_string()
        } else {
            String::new()
        }
    }

    fn trailer(&self) -> String {
        format!("{}", self.header.trailer.checksum)
    }
}
```

## 📄 16. SRC/TRANSFORMER/FIX_TO_ISO.RS

```rust
//! Transformation from FIX to ISO 20022.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::types::*;
use crate::messages::Pain001;
use chrono::Utc;

/// Transforms FIX execution report to ISO 20022 pain.001.
pub struct FixToIsoTransformer;

impl FixToIsoTransformer {
    /// Transforms FIX trade data to pain.001.
    pub fn fix_exec_to_pain_001(
        fix_data: &FixTradeData,
    ) -> Result<Pain001> {
        let debtor = Party {
            name: fix_data.sender_name.clone(),
            bic: fix_data.sender_bic.clone().map(Bic::new).transpose().ok(),
            iban: fix_data.sender_iban.clone().map(Iban::new).transpose().ok(),
            address: None,
        };

        let creditor = Party {
            name: fix_data.receiver_name.clone(),
            bic: fix_data.receiver_bic.clone().map(Bic::new).transpose().ok(),
            iban: fix_data.receiver_iban.clone().map(Iban::new).transpose().ok(),
            address: None,
        };

        let amount = Amount::new(fix_data.settled_amount, &fix_data.currency);

        let debtor_iban = fix_data.sender_iban.clone()
            .map(Iban::new)
            .transpose()
            .map_err(|e| crate::IsoError::InvalidField {
                field: "sender_iban".to_string(),
                value: e,
            })?
            .ok_or_else(|| crate::IsoError::MissingField("sender_iban".to_string()))?;

        let creditor_iban = fix_data.receiver_iban.clone()
            .map(Iban::new)
            .transpose()
            .map_err(|e| crate::IsoError::InvalidField {
                field: "receiver_iban".to_string(),
                value: e,
            })?
            .ok_or_else(|| crate::IsoError::MissingField("receiver_iban".to_string()))?;

        let mut pain = Pain001::new(debtor, creditor, amount, debtor_iban, creditor_iban);
        pain.instruction_id = Some(fix_data.order_id.clone());

        Ok(pain)
    }
}

/// FIX trade data.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct FixTradeData {
    pub order_id: String,
    pub sender_name: String,
    pub sender_bic: Option<String>,
    pub sender_iban: Option<String>,
    pub receiver_name: String,
    pub receiver_bic: Option<String>,
    pub receiver_iban: Option<String>,
    pub settled_amount: Decimal,
    pub currency: String,
}
```

---

## 📄 17. SRC/TRANSFORMER/ISO_TO_FIX.RS

```rust
//! Transformation from ISO 20022 to FIX.

use crate::error::Result;
use crate::types::*;
use crate::messages::Pacs008;

/// Transforms ISO 20022 to FIX messages.
pub struct IsoToFixTransformer;

impl IsoToFixTransformer {
    /// Transforms pacs.008 to FIX trade confirmation.
    pub fn pacs_008_to_fix_exec(pacs: &Pacs008) -> Result<FixTradeData> {
        let sender_iban = Some(pacs.debtor_account.iban.to_string());
        let receiver_iban = Some(pacs.creditor_account.iban.to_string());

        Ok(FixTradeData {
            order_id: pacs.transfer.transaction_id.clone(),
            sender_name: pacs.debtor.name.clone(),
            sender_bic: pacs.debtor.bic.as_ref().map(|b| b.to_string()),
            sender_iban,
            receiver_name: pacs.creditor.name.clone(),
            receiver_bic: pacs.creditor.bic.as_ref().map(|b| b.to_string()),
            receiver_iban,
            settled_amount: pacs.instructed_amount.value,
            currency: pacs.instructed_amount.currency.clone(),
        })
    }
}

// Re-export FixTradeData from fix_to_iso module.
pub use super::fix_to_iso::FixTradeData;
```

```rust
//! Tests for ISO 20022 parser.

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use cathedral_iso20022::*;
    use rust_decimal::Decimal;

    #[test]
    fn test_parse_pain_001() {
        let xml = r#"
        <Pain001>
            <MessageId>PAIN-123</MessageId>
            <CreationDate>2026-06-24T10:00:00Z</CreationDate>
            <Amount>1000.00</Amount>
            <Currency>USD</Currency>
        </Pain001>
        "#;

        // Note: quick-xml serialization requires proper XML structure.
        // This is a placeholder test.
        let result = IsoParser::parse_pain_001(xml);
        // In a real implementation, this would parse the XML.
        // For now, we just test that the function exists.
        assert!(result.is_err() || result.is_ok());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_validate_pain_001() {
        let debtor = Party {
            name: "Alice".to_string(),
            bic: Some(Bic::new("ALICUS33").unwrap()),
            iban: Some(Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap()),
            address: None,
        };
        let creditor = Party {
            name: "Bob".to_string(),
            bic: Some(Bic::new("BOBUS33").unwrap()),
            iban: Some(Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap()),
            address: None,
        };
        let amount = Amount::new(Decimal::from(100), "USD");
        let debtor_iban = Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap();
        let creditor_iban = Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap();

        let pain = Pain001::new(debtor, creditor, amount, debtor_iban, creditor_iban);
        let result = Validator::validate_pain_001(&pain);
        assert!(result.is_ok());
    }
}
```

## 📄 20. TESTS/TRANSFORMER_TESTS.RS

```rust
//! Tests for FIX ↔ ISO 20022 transformer.

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use cathedral_iso20022::*;
    use rust_decimal::Decimal;

    #[test]
    fn test_fix_to_pain_001() {
        let fix_data = FixTradeData {
            order_id: "ORD-123".to_string(),
            sender_name: "Alice".to_string(),
            sender_bic: Some("ALICUS33".to_string()),
            sender_iban: Some("US123456789".to_string()),
            receiver_name: "Bob".to_string(),
            receiver_bic: Some("BOBUS33".to_string()),
            receiver_iban: Some("US987654321".to_string()),
            settled_amount: Decimal::from(100),
            currency: "USD".to_string(),
        };

        let result = FixToIsoTransformer::fix_exec_to_pain_001(&fix_data);
        assert!(result.is_ok());
        let pain = result.unwrap();
        assert_eq!(pain.debtor.name, "Alice");
        assert_eq!(pain.creditor.name, "Bob");
        assert_eq!(pain.amount.value, Decimal::from(100));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_pacs_008_to_fix() {
        let debtor = Party {
            name: "Alice".to_string(),
            bic: Some(Bic::new("ALICUS33").unwrap()),
            iban: Some(Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap()),
            address: None,
        };
        let creditor = Party {
            name: "Bob".to_string(),
            bic: Some(Bic::new("BOBUS33").unwrap()),
            iban: Some(Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap()),
            address: None,
        };
        let amount = Amount::new(Decimal::from(100), "USD");
        let debtor_iban = Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap();
        let creditor_iban = Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap();

        let pacs = Pacs008::new(debtor, creditor, amount, debtor_iban, creditor_iban);
        let result = IsoToFixTransformer::pacs_008_to_fix_exec(&pacs);
        assert!(result.is_ok());
        let fix_data = result.unwrap();
        assert_eq!(fix_data.sender_name, "Alice");
        assert_eq!(fix_data.receiver_name, "Bob");
    }
}
```

## 📄 21. EXAMPLES/PARSE_PAIN_001.RS

```rust
//! Example: Parsing a pain.001 message.

use cathedral_iso20022::*;

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let xml = r#"
    <Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09">
        <CstmrCdtTrfInitn>
            <GrpHdr>
                <MsgId>PAIN-123</MsgId>
                <CreDtTm>2026-06-24T10:00:00Z</CreDtTm>
                <NbOfTxs>1</NbOfTxs>
            </GrpHdr>
            <PmtInf>
                <PmtInfId>PMT-456</PmtInfId>
                <PmtMtd>TRF</PmtMtd>
                <Dbtr>
                    <Nm>Alice</Nm>
                    <PstlAdr>
                        <Ctry>US</Ctry>
                    </PstlAdr>
                </Dbtr>
                <DbtrAcct>
                    <Id>
                        <IBAN>US123456789</IBAN>
                    </Id>
                </DbtrAcct>
                <Cdtr>
                    <Nm>Bob</Nm>
                    <PstlAdr>
                        <Ctry>US</Ctry>
                    </PstlAdr>
                </Cdtr>
                <CdtrAcct>
                    <Id>
                        <IBAN>US987654321</IBAN>
                    </Id>
                </CdtrAcct>
                <CdtTrfTxInf>
                    <Amt>
                        <InstdAmt Ccy="USD">100.00</InstdAmt>
                    </Amt>
                </CdtTrfTxInf>
            </PmtInf>
        </CstmrCdtTrfInitn>
    </Document>
    "#;

    let pain = IsoParser::parse_pain_001(xml)?;
    println!("Parsed pain.001:");
    println!("  Message ID: {}", pain.message_id);
    println!("  Debtor: {}", pain.debtor.name);
    println!("  Creditor: {}", pain.creditor.name);
    println!("  Amount: {} {}", pain.amount.value, pain.amount.currency);

    Ok(())
}
```

---

## 📄 22. EXAMPLES/GENERATE_PACS_008.RS

```rust
//! Example: Generating a pacs.008 message.

use cathedral_iso20022::*;
use rust_decimal::Decimal;

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let debtor = Party {
        name: "Alice".to_string(),
        bic: Some(Bic::new("ALICUS33").unwrap()),
        iban: Some(Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap()),
        address: None,
    };
    let creditor = Party {
        name: "Bob".to_string(),
        bic: Some(Bic::new("BOBUS33").unwrap()),
        iban: Some(Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap()),
        address: None,
    };
    let amount = Amount::new(Decimal::from(100), "USD");
    let debtor_iban = Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap();
    let creditor_iban = Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap();

    let pacs = Pacs008::new(debtor, creditor, amount, debtor_iban, creditor_iban);
    let xml = IsoGenerator::generate_pacs_008(&pacs)?;

    println!("Generated pacs.008 XML:");
    println!("{}", xml);

    Ok(())
}
```

---

## 📄 23. EXAMPLES/SWIFT_MX_MESSAGE.RS

```rust
//! Example: Generating a SWIFT MX message.

use cathedral_iso20022::*;
use rust_decimal::Decimal;

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let debtor = Party {
        name: "Alice".to_string(),
        bic: Some(Bic::new("ALICUS33").unwrap()),
        iban: Some(Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap()),
        address: None,
    };
    let creditor = Party {
        name: "Bob".to_string(),
        bic: Some(Bic::new("BOBUS33").unwrap()),
        iban: Some(Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap()),
        address: None,
    };
    let amount = Amount::new(Decimal::from(100), "USD");
    let debtor_iban = Iban::new("US123456789").unwrap();
    let creditor_iban = Iban::new("US987654321").unwrap();

    let pain = Pain001::new(debtor, creditor, amount, debtor_iban, creditor_iban);
    let mx = SwiftMxMessage::from_pain_001(&pain, "BOBUS33")?;

    println!("SWIFT MX Message:");
    println!("{}", mx.render());

    Ok(())
}
```

   → DLT "not ready for production in a central bank governed network" citeweb_search:41#15
   → Documento propõe "CBDC adapter" para DREX — INCORRETO
   → Impacto: cathedral-cbdc-adapter seria inútil para Drex

2. ISO 20022 — ADOÇÃO REAL É INCOMPLETA
   → Muitas instituições ainda usam tradução (like-for-like) citeweb_search:41#10
   → Dados estruturados frequentemente achatados ou truncados citeweb_search:41#10
   → Apenas número limitado de instituições é ISO 20022-native citeweb_search:41#0
   → Documento sugere "semantic interoperability across entire ecosystem" — OTIMISTA
   → Realidade: coexistência de MT e MX até nov/2026, com cobranças para MT

3. B3RL — TIMELINE VOLÁTIL
   → Originalmente H1 2026, adiado para H2 2026 citeweb_search:41#3
   → Ticker B3RL não oficialmente confirmado por B3 IR/CVM citeweb_search:41#3

4. SWIFT Digital Ledger — É MVP, NÃO PRODUÇÃO
   → MVP go-live em 2026, mas ainda em construção citeweb_search:41#2
   → Apenas tokenized deposits, não ativos genéricos citeweb_search:41#4
   → Swift opera o ledger — não é descentralizado citeweb_search:41#4

   → Documento menciona $235B até 2029 — não verificado independentemente
   → L&G está live na Calastone (confirmado) citeweb_search:41#17
   → Mas escala global não confirmada


================================================================================
RECOMENDAÇÃO ESTRATÉGICA REVISADA
================================================================================


COM BASE NA VALIDAÇÃO, A ESTRATÉGIA DEVE SER AJUSTADA:

1. REMOVER DREX CBDC do roadmap
   → Drex não é mais CBDC retail/blockchain
   → Foco em collateral management — não é interoperabilidade cross-border
   → NÃO investir em cathedral-cbdc-adapter para Drex

2. AJUSTAR EXPECTATIVAS DE ISO 20022
   → A transição é técnica, mas a adoção semântica é gradual
   → Focar em parser/gerador de mensagens (pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053)
   → NÃO assumir que todo o ecossistema está "semantic-ready"
   → Considerar suporte a MT legado como fallback

3. MONITORAR B3RL (não assumir disponibilidade)
   → Timeline volátil (H1 → H2 2026)
   → Ticker não confirmado oficialmente
   → Implementar como opcional, não obrigatório

4. AGUARDAR SWIFT Digital Ledger (MVP)
   → É promissor, mas ainda em MVP
   → Não construir dependências críticas sobre ele
   → Monitorar para integração futura (2027+)

5. FOCAR EM COMPONENTES VIÁVEIS AGORA:
   → ISO 20022 message parser (pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053)
   → FIX ↔ ISO 20022 transformer (doc<response clipped><NOTE>Result is longer than **10000 characters**, will be **truncated**.</NOTE>

================================================================================
CATHEDRAL ARKHE — SUBSTRATO 9517: cathedral-iso20022
VALIDAÇÃO PRÉ-IMPLEMENTAÇÃO
================================================================================

📦 ISO 20022 Parser
   Status: ✅ VIÁVEL
   Score: 85/100
   Esforço: 2-3 semanas para MVP (pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053)

   Notas:
      • ISO 20022 é padrão XML bem definido (XSD schemas disponíveis)
      • Crates Rust para XML parsing existem (quick-xml, serde-xml-rs)
      • Schemas ISO 20022 são públicos (ISO 20022 Repository)
      • pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053 são os mais comuns
      • Parser pode ser gerado automaticamente a partir de XSDs

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ XSDs ISO 20022 são complexos (milhares de elementos)
      ⚠️ Versões diferentes (2019, 2023) têm schemas diferentes
      ⚠️ Muitas instituições usam 'like-for-like' (tradução) em vez de nativo
      ⚠️ Customizações nacionais (SEPA, Fedwire) adicionam complexidade

   Dependências:
      • quick-xml = "0.37" (XML parsing)
      • serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
      • serde-xml-rs = "0.6" (XML serde)
      • chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
      • rust_decimal = { version = "1.35", features = ["serde"] }
      • uuid = { version = "1.10", features = ["v4", "serde"] }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

📦 FIX ↔ ISO 20022 Transformer
   Status: ⚠️ COMPLEXO
   Score: 70/100
   Esforço: 3-4 semanas para mapeamento básico

   Notas:
      • BIS documenta mapeamento FIX ↔ ISO 20022 citeweb_search:41#0
      • Não é 1:1 — semânticas diferentes (FIX é trading, ISO 20022 é payments)
      • FIX tags não mapeiam diretamente para ISO 20022 elements
      • Requer understanding profundo de ambos os protocolos
      • Use case: settlement de trades (FIX exec report → ISO 20022 settlement)

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ Mapeamento não é trivial — requer domain expertise
      ⚠️ FIX é tag-value, ISO 20022 é XML — estruturas fundamentalmente diferentes
      ⚠️ Campos opcionais em um protocolo podem ser obrigatórios no outro
      ⚠️ Customizações de FIX (por bolsa/broker) complicam o mapeamento

   Dependências:
      • cathedral-fix-adapter (existente)
      • cathedral-iso20022 (parser)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

📦 SWIFT MX Message Generator
   Status: ✅ VIÁVEL
   Score: 80/100
   Esforço: 1-2 semanas para gerador de headers

   Notas:
      • SWIFT MX = ISO 20022 messages over SWIFT network
      • Formato é ISO 20022 XML + SWIFT header (block 1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
      • SWIFT headers são bem documentados
      • Não requer conexão SWIFT real para gerar mensagens

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ SWIFT network access requer membership (cara)
      ⚠️ SWIFT headers têm campos específicos (BIC, session, sequence)
      ⚠️ Validação SWIFT (SCORE) requer conexão real
      ⚠️ Mensagens inválidas podem ser rejeitadas ou causar fees

   Dependências:
      • cathedral-iso20022 (parser)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

📦 AI Routing Engine
   Status: ⚠️ ESPECULATIVO
   Score: 50/100
   Esforço: Indeterminado — definir requisitos primeiro

   Notas:
      • Documento sugere 'AI-powered routing engine'
      • Conceito é válido: selecionar melhor rail (SWIFT, stablecoin, CBDC)
      • Mas 'AI' é buzzword — regras heurísticas são mais práticas
      • Requer dados em tempo real de custo, velocidade, compliance

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ 'AI' sem definição clara é especulação
      ⚠️ Regras simples (custo, velocidade, compliance) são suficientes
      ⚠️ ML requer dados históricos que não existem (infraestrutura nova)
      ⚠️ Over-engineering para problema que regras resolvem

   Dependências:
      • cathedral-payment-router (existente)
      • cathedral-iso20022 (parser)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

================================================================================
RECOMENDAÇÃO DE IMPLEMENTAÇÃO
================================================================================


COM BASE NA ANÁLISE, RECOMENDO A SEGUINTE ESTRATÉGIA:

1. IMPLEMENTAR cathedral-iso20022 (2-3 semanas)
   → Parser para pain.001, pacs.008, camt.053
   → Gerador de mensagens ISO 20022 XML
   → Validação contra XSDs oficiais
   → Score: 85/100 — viável e valioso

2. IMPLEMENTAR FIX ↔ ISO 20022 Transformer (3-4 semanas)
   → Mapeamento básico: FIX exec report → ISO 20022 settlement
   → Documentar limitações (não é 1:1)
   → Score: 70/100 — complexo mas possível

3. IMPLEMENTAR SWIFT MX Generator (1-2 semanas)
   → Headers SWIFT (block 1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
   → Integração com parser ISO 20022
   → Score: 80/100 — viável

4. ADIAR AI Routing Engine (futuro)
   → Definir requisitos claros primeiro
   → Começar com regras heurísticas simples
   → Adicionar ML apenas quando dados históricos existirem
   → Score: 50/100 — especulativo

ESTIMATIVA TOTAL: 6-9 semanas para MVP funcional
(sem AI Routing Engine)

ARQUITETURA PROPOSTA:

cathedral-iso20022/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs              # Re-exports
│   ├── error.rs            # Iso20022Error
│   ├── types.rs            # Common types (Amount, Currency, BIC, etc.)
│   ├── parser.rs           # XML → Rust structs
│   ├── generator.rs        # Rust structs → XML
│   ├── validator.rs        # XSD validation
│   ├── messages/           # Message types
│   │   ├── pain_001.rs     # CustomerCreditTransferInitiation
│   │   ├── pacs_008.rs     # FIToFICustomerCreditTransfer
│   │   ├── camt_053.rs     # BankToCustomerStatement
│   │   └── mod.rs
│   ├── swift/              # SWIFT MX headers
│   │   ├── header.rs       # Block 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
│   │   ├── mx_message.rs   # ISO 20022 + SWIFT headers
│   │   └── mod.rs
│   └── transformer/        # FIX ↔ ISO 20022
│       ├── fix_to_iso.rs   # FIX → ISO 20022
│       ├── iso_to_fix.rs   # ISO 20022 → FIX
│       └── mod.rs
├── schemas/                # ISO 20022 XSDs (downloadable)
│   ├── pain.001.001.09.xsd
│   ├── pacs.008.001.08.xsd
│   └── camt.053.001.08.xsd
├── tests/
│   ├── parser_tests.rs
│   ├── generator_tests.rs
│   └── transformer_tests.rs
└── examples/
    ├── parse_pain_001.rs
    ├── generate_pacs_008.rs
    └── swift_mx_message.rs

→ SWIFT Digital Ledger é MVP, não produção
→ Tokenized funds escala ($235B) não verificada

BOLSAS GLOBAIS — 52/100 (documento anterior)
→ CME usa SBE (não FIX puro)
→ Nasdaq Data Link ≠ trading
→ Eurex ETI é binário próprio
→ ICE sendo descontinuado para BOE
→ Certificação obrigatória e complexa

📦 DEPENDÊNCIAS CONFIRMADAS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

✅ CONFIRMADO ibapi = "3.1.0"
   Downloads: 118.931 | Última atualização: 3 dias atrás (Jun 2026)
   Protocolo: TWS Socket (protobuf v3.0)
   Score: 95/100

   Requisitos:
      • TWS ou IB Gateway em execução local (127.0.0.1:4002)
      • Server version 213+ (TWS/IB Gateway atualizado)
      • v3.0 dropou protocolo texto — apenas protobuf

   Features:
      • Sync/async
      • Contract builder
      • Order types
      • Real-time bars
      • Historical data
      • Multi-threading

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ Requer infraestrutura local (TWS/IB Gateway)
      ⚠️ Não é API REST — é socket proprietário
      ⚠️ Multi-threading tem limitações de roteamento de mensagens

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

✅ CONFIRMADO apca = "0.30.0"
   Downloads: 72.140 | Última atualização: Dez 2024 (possivelmente stale)
   Protocolo: REST API
   Score: 85/100

   Requisitos:
      • API key (paper/live)
      • Acesso direto via HTTP

   Features:
      • Paper trading
      • Commission-free US equities
      • Streaming data
      • OAuth 2.0

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ Última atualização Dez 2024 — pode estar desatualizado
      ⚠️ Alpaca API pode ter mudado desde então
      ⚠️ Necessita verificação de compatibilidade

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

✅ CONFIRMADO alpacars = "0.1.2"
   Downloads: 39 | Última atualização: Mai 2026
   Protocolo: REST + WebSocket
   Score: 70/100

   Requisitos:
      • API key (paper/live)

   Features:
      • Trading API
      • Broker API
      • Market Data
      • WebSocket streaming
      • Retry com exponential backoff

   Riscos:
      ⚠️ Apenas 39 downloads — crate muito novo
      ⚠️ API pode mudar (não é estável)
      ⚠️ Menos testado em produção

🎯 RECOMENDAÇÃO DE IMPLEMENTAÇÃO

COM BASE NA ANÁLISE, RECOMENDO A SEGUINTE ESTRATÉGIA:

FASE 1 — IBKR ONLY (Semanas 1-3)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Implementar apenas ibapi v3.1.0
• Criar interface unificada com traits genéricos
• Testar com TWS/IB Gateway local + paper trading
• Documentar limitações (infraestrutura local necessária)

FASE 2 — ALPACA (Semanas 4-5)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Avaliar apca v0.30.0 vs alpacars v0.1.2
• apca: maduro mas possivelmente stale
• alpacars: moderno mas imaturo (39 downloads)
• Decisão: apca para estabilidade, alpacars para features modernas
• Implementar adapter Alpaca

FASE 3 — INTERFACE UNIFICADA (Semana 6)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Unificar IBKR + Alpaca sob trait BrokerAdapter
• Implementar factory pattern para seleção de broker
• Testes de integração cross-broker

ARQUITETURA PROPOSTA:

cathedral-broker-adapter/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs              # Re-exports
│   ├── error.rs            # BrokerError, Result<T>
│   ├── types.rs            # Order, Position, Account, etc.
│   ├── traits.rs           # BrokerAdapter, MarketData, OrderManager
│   ├── ibkr/
│   │   ├── mod.rs          # Re-exports
│   │   ├── client.rs       # IbkrClient implements BrokerAdapter
│   │   ├── market_data.rs  # Real-time bars, historical data
│   │   └── orders.rs       # Order placement, modification, cancellation
│   ├── alpaca/
│   │   ├── mod.rs          # Re-exports
│   │   ├── client.rs       # AlpacaClient implements BrokerAdapter
│   │   ├── market_data.rs  # REST + WebSocket streaming
│   │   └── orders.rs       # Order placement, modification, cancellation
│   └── unified/
│       ├── mod.rs          # BrokerFactory, BrokerType
│       └── router.rs       # Multi-broker routing logic
└── tests/
    ├── integration.rs      # End-to-end tests
    ├── mock_ibkr.rs        # Mock IBKR server
    └── mock_alpaca.rs      # Mock Alpaca server

================================================================================
⚠️  RISCOS IDENTIFICADOS
================================================================================


1. INFRAESTRUTURA IBKR
   → Requer TWS/IB Gateway rodando localmente
   → Não é "deploy e esquecer" — requer manutenção
   → Session pode expirar (re-login necessário)
   → Não funciona em serverless/cloud sem infraestrutura dedicada

2. COMPATIBILIDADE APCA
   → Última atualização Dez 2024
   → Alpaca API pode ter mudado desde então
   → Requer testes extensivos antes de produção
   → Possível necessidade de forks/PRs

3. INTERFACE UNIFICADA
   → IBKR e Alpaca têm semânticas diferentes
   → Order types podem não mapear 1:1
   → Market data formats diferentes
   → Requer abstração cuidadosa

4. TESTES
   → IBKR requer TWS/IB Gateway para testes
   → Alpaca tem paper trading (mais fácil)
   → Mock servers necessários para CI/CD
   → Testes de integração requerem credenciais reais

---
FILE: Cargo.toml
---
[package]
name = "cathedral-broker-adapter"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Cathedral OS Architects <arkhe@cathedral-os.org>"]

FILE: src/lib.rs

//! # Cathedral Broker Adapter
//!
//! Unified interface for broker-dealer APIs within the Cathedral OS ecosystem.
//!
//! ## Supported Brokers
//! - **Interactive Brokers (IBKR)** via `ibapi` v3.1.0 (TWS/IB Gateway socket)
//! - **Alpaca** via `apca` v0.30.0 (REST API)
//!
//! ## Architecture
//! ```text
//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
//! │     Application / Cathedral Agent     │
//! └─────────────────┬─────────────────────┘
//!                   │ BrokerAdapter trait
//! ┌─────────────────▼─────────────────────┐
//! │      cathedral-broker-adapter       │
//! │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐ │
//! │  │   IBKR      │  │    Alpaca      │ │
//! │  │  (ibapi)    │  │   (apca)       │ │
//! │  └─────────────┘  └────────────────┘ │
//! └───────────────────────────────────────┘
//! ```

pub mod error;
pub mod traits;
pub mod types;
pub mod unified;

// Broker-specific modules
#[cfg(feature = "ibkr")]
pub mod ibkr;
#[cfg(feature = "alpaca")]
pub mod alpaca;

// Re-exports
pub use error::{BrokerError, Result};
pub use traits::BrokerAdapter;
pub use types::*;
pub use unified::{BrokerFactory, BrokerType};

/// Crate version
pub const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");

---
FILE: src/error.rs
---
use thiserror::Error;

/// Unified error type for all broker operations.
#[derive(Error, Debug, Clone)]
pub enum BrokerError {
    /// Connection failure (network, auth, etc.)
    #[error("Connection failed: {0}")]
    ConnectionError(String),

    /// Order placement/rejection
    #[error("Order error [{code}]: {message}")]
    OrderError { code: String, message: String },

    /// Market data subscription failure
    #[error("Market data error: {0}")]
    MarketDataError(String),

    /// Invalid configuration
    #[error("Configuration error: {0}")]
    ConfigError(String),

    /// Broker-specific error wrapped
    #[error("Broker error ({broker}): {message}")]
    BrokerSpecific { broker: String, message: String },

    /// Rate limit exceeded
    #[error("Rate limit exceeded. Retry after {retry_after_secs}s")]
    RateLimitExceeded { retry_after_secs: u64 },

    /// Authentication failure
    #[error("Authentication failed: {0}")]
    AuthError(String),

    /// Not implemented / unsupported operation
    #[error("Operation not supported: {0}")]
    NotSupported(String),

    /// Internal / unexpected error
    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, BrokerError>;

---
FILE: src/types.rs
---
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use rust_decimal::Decimal;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use uuid::Uuid;

/// Unique identifier for orders.
pub type OrderId = Uuid;

/// Unique identifier for positions.
pub type PositionId = String;

/// Side of a trade.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum Side {
    Buy,
    Sell,
}

/// Order type.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum OrderType {
    Market,
    Limit,
    Stop,
    StopLimit,
    TrailingStop,
}

/// Time-in-force for orders.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum TimeInForce {
    Day,
    Gtc,        // Good Till Canceled
    Ioc,        // Immediate or Cancel
    Fok,        // Fill or Kill
    OpG,        // Opening (IBKR)
    ClG,        // Closing (IBKR)
}

/// Order status.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum OrderStatus {
    Pending,
    Submitted,
    PartiallyFilled,
    Filled,
    Cancelled,
    Rejected,
    Expired,
}

/// An order request.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OrderRequest {
    pub id: OrderId,
    pub symbol: String,
    pub side: Side,
    pub order_type: OrderType,
    pub quantity: Decimal,
    pub price: Option<Decimal>,          // For limit orders
    pub stop_price: Option<Decimal>,     // For stop orders
    pub trailing_percent: Option<Decimal>, // For trailing stop
    pub time_in_force: TimeInForce,
    pub client_order_id: Option<String>,
}

impl OrderRequest {
    pub fn market(symbol: impl Into<String>, side: Side, quantity: Decimal) -> Self {
        Self {
            id: Uuid::new_v4(),
            symbol: symbol.into(),
            side,
            order_type: OrderType::Market,
            quantity,
            price: None,
            stop_price: None,
            trailing_percent: None,
            time_in_force: TimeInForce::Day,
            client_order_id: None,
        }
    }

    pub fn limit(
        symbol: impl Into<String>,
        side: Side,
        quantity: Decimal,
        price: Decimal,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            id: Uuid::new_v4(),
            symbol: symbol.into(),
            side,
            order_type: OrderType::Limit,
            quantity,
            price: Some(price),
            stop_price: None,
            trailing_percent: None,
            time_in_force: TimeInForce::Day,
            client_order_id: None,
        }
    }
}

/// Order response from broker.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OrderResponse {
    pub id: OrderId,
    pub broker_order_id: String,
    pub status: OrderStatus,
    pub filled_quantity: Decimal,
    pub remaining_quantity: Decimal,
    pub avg_fill_price: Option<Decimal>,
    pub last_fill_price: Option<Decimal>,
    pub last_fill_time: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub commission: Option<Decimal>,
    pub message: Option<String>,
}

/// Position in a security.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Position {
    pub id: PositionId,
    pub symbol: String,
    pub quantity: Decimal,
    pub avg_entry_price: Decimal,
    pub market_price: Decimal,
    pub market_value: Decimal,
    pub unrealized_pnl: Decimal,
    pub realized_pnl: Decimal,
}

/// Account summary.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct AccountSummary {
    pub account_id: String,
    pub currency: String,
    pub cash_balance: Decimal,
    pub buying_power: Decimal,
    pub equity: Decimal,
    pub day_trades_remaining: Option<i32>,
    pub maintenance_margin: Option<Decimal>,
}

/// Market data bar (OHLCV).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Bar {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub open: Decimal,
    pub high: Decimal,
    pub low: Decimal,
    pub close: Decimal,
    pub volume: Decimal,
}

/// Real-time quote.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Quote {
    pub symbol: String,
    pub bid: Decimal,
    pub bid_size: Decimal,
    pub ask: Decimal,
    pub ask_size: Decimal,
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
}

/// Subscription handle for market data.
pub struct MarketDataSubscription {
    pub symbol: String,
    pub unsubscribe: Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>,
}

---
FILE: src/traits.rs
---
use async_trait::async_trait;
use crate::types::*;
use crate::error::Result;

/// Core trait that every broker adapter must implement.
#[async_trait]
pub trait BrokerAdapter: Send + Sync {
    /// Human-readable broker name.
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;

    /// Connect to the broker.
    async fn connect(&mut self) -> Result<()>;

    /// Disconnect gracefully.
    async fn disconnect(&mut self) -> Result<()>;

    /// Check if connection is alive.
    async fn is_connected(&self) -> bool;

    // ─── Account ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    /// Get account summary.
    async fn get_account_summary(&self) -> Result<AccountSummary>;

    // ─── Orders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    /// Place a new order.
    async fn place_order(&self, order: OrderRequest) -> Result<OrderResponse>;

    /// Cancel an existing order.
    async fn cancel_order(&self, order_id: OrderId) -> Result<()>;

    /// Get status of a specific order.
    async fn get_order_status(&self, order_id: OrderId) -> Result<OrderResponse>;

    /// List all open orders.
    async fn list_open_orders(&self) -> Result<Vec<OrderResponse>>;

    // ─── Positions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

    //<response clipped><NOTE>Result is longer than **10000 characters**, will be **truncated**.</NOTE>
