## 🔗 7. TAREFA 3: MAPEAR DIAMOND TOKENS PARA ASSETS CATHEDRAL

### 7.1. Mapeamento de Ativos (`src/asset.rs`)

```rust
// src/asset.rs
use crate::client::TokenInfo;
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use cathedral_identity::Did;

/// Representação de um Diamond Standard asset no Cathedral OS.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct DiamondAsset {
    /// ID do token no Hedera
    pub token_id: String,
    /// Nome do ativo
    pub name: String,
    /// Símbolo do token
    pub symbol: String,
    /// DID do ativo
    pub did: Did,
    /// Tipo de commodity (Coin, Bar, Carat)
    pub commodity_type: CommodityType,
    /// Número de série físico
    pub serial_number: Option<String>,
    /// URL do Diamond Dossier
    pub dossier_url: Option<String>,
    /// Certificados GIA/IGI
    pub certificates: Vec<Certificate>,
    /// Total supply
    pub total_supply: u64,
    /// Decimals
    pub decimals: u32,
}

/// Tipo de commodity Diamond Standard.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum CommodityType {
    /// Diamond Standard Coin (BCC)
    Coin,
    /// Diamond Standard Bar (BCB)
    Bar,
    /// Carats (token fracionário)
    Carat,
}

/// Certificado GIA ou IGI.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Certificate {
    pub cert_number: String,
    pub lab: CertificationLab,
    pub shape: String,
    pub carats: f64,
    pub color: String,
    pub clarity: String,
    pub cut: String,
    pub polish: String,
    pub symmetry: String,
    pub fluorescence: String,
    pub url: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum CertificationLab {
    GIA,
    IGI,
}

impl DiamondAsset {
    /// Cria um asset Cathedral a partir de um token Hedera.
    pub fn from_token(
        token: TokenInfo,
        commodity_type: CommodityType,
        serial_number: Option<String>,
    ) -> Self {
        let asset_id = format!("diamond:{}", token.token_id);
        let did = Did::from_string(&format!("did:cathedral:asset:{}", asset_id));

        Self {
            token_id: token.token_id,
            name: token.name,
            symbol: token.symbol,
            did,
            commodity_type,
            serial_number,
            dossier_url: None,
            certificates: Vec::new(),
            total_supply: token.total_supply,
            decimals: token.decimals,
        }
    }

    /// Converte para AssetSummary do Cathedral.
    pub fn to_asset_summary(&self) -> AssetSummary {
        AssetSummary {
            asset_id: self.token_id.clone(),
            name: self.name.clone(),
            amount: self.total_supply,
            asset_type: AssetType::Commodity,
            is_spent: false,
        }
    }
}

/// Mapeia um Diamond Standard token para um asset Cathedral.
pub fn map_diamond_token_to_asset(
    token_info: TokenInfo,
    serial_number: Option<String>,
) -> Result<DiamondAsset, BridgeError> {
    // Determina o tipo de commodity baseado no símbolo
    let commodity_type = match token_info.symbol.as_str() {
        "BCC" => CommodityType::Coin,
        "BCB" => CommodityType::Bar,
        "CARAT" => CommodityType::Carat,
        _ => CommodityType::Carat, // Default
    };

    Ok(DiamondAsset::from_token(
        token_info,
        commodity_type,
        serial_number,
    ))
}
```

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|

| `src/types.rs` | Tipos para Diamond Assets |

## 🧪 8. TAREFA 4: TESTAR TOKENIZAÇÃO AVANÇADA

### 8.1. Teste de Tokenização (`tests/tokenization_test.rs`)

```rust
// tests/tokenization_test.rs
use cathedral_diamond_bridge::{HederaClient, map_diamond_token_to_asset};
use std::env;

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_create_diamond_token() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let operator_id = env::var("HEDERA_OPERATOR_ID")
        .expect("HEDERA_OPERATOR_ID not set");
    let operator_key = env::var("HEDERA_OPERATOR_KEY")
        .expect("HEDERA_OPERATOR_KEY not set");

    let client = HederaClient::new(
        &operator_id,
        &operator_key,
        "testnet",
    )?;

    // 1. Criar token para Diamond Standard Coin
    let token = client.create_token(
        "Diamond Standard Coin",
        "BCC",
        0,  // Sem decimais para tokens não-fracionários
        1000,  // Supply inicial
        None,
    ).await?;

    println!("✅ Token criado: {}", token.token_id);
    assert!(!token.token_id.is_empty());

    // 2. Mint mais unidades
    let mint = client.mint_token(&token.token_id, 500).await?;
    println!("✅ Minted: {} unidades", 500);

    // 3. Verificar informações do token
    let info = client.get_token_info(&token.token_id).await?;
    println!("✅ Total supply: {}", info.total_supply);
    assert!(info.total_supply >= 1500);

    // 4. Mapear para asset Cathedral
    let asset = map_diamond_token_to_asset(info, Some("BCC-001".to_string()))?;
    println!("✅ Asset Cathedral: {:?}", asset);
    assert!(asset.did.to_string().starts_with("did:cathedral:asset:"));

    Ok(())
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_burn_diamond_token() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let operator_id = env::var("HEDERA_OPERATOR_ID")
        .expect("HEDERA_OPERATOR_ID not set");
    let operator_key = env::var("HEDERA_OPERATOR_KEY")
        .expect("HEDERA_OPERATOR_KEY not set");

    let client = HederaClient::new(&operator_id, &operator_key, "testnet")?;

    // Criar token
    let token = client.create_token("Test Burn", "BURN", 0, 1000, None).await?;

    // Burn 100 unidades
    let burn = client.burn_token(&token.token_id, 100).await?;
    println!("✅ Burned: 100 unidades");

    let info = client.get_token_info(&token.token_id).await?;
    assert_eq!(info.total_supply, 900);

    Ok(())
}
```

## 📡 9. TAREFA 5: IMPLEMENTAR VERIFICAÇÃO DE CHIPS FÍSICOS

### 9.1. Verificação NFC (`src/nfc.rs`)

```rust
// src/nfc.rs
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use reqwest::Client;
use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};

/// Resultado da verificação do chip NFC.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct NfcVerificationResult {
    pub serial_number: String,
    pub verified: bool,
    pub commodity_type: String,
    pub chip_signature: String,
    pub timestamp: u64,
    pub message: String,
}

/// Verifica a autenticidade de um Diamond Standard commodity via chip NFC.
///
/// O chip NFC armazena um "Commodity Token" que permite autenticação remota.
pub async fn verify_physical_asset(
    serial_number: &str,
    chip_signature: &[u8],
) -> Result<NfcVerificationResult, BridgeError> {
    // 1. Validação básica do chip
    if serial_number.is_empty() || chip_signature.is_empty() {
        return Err(BridgeError::NfcVerification("Invalid chip data".to_string()));
    }

    // 2. Verifica a assinatura do chip (usando chave pública da Diamond Standard)
    // A Diamond Standard utiliza chips de criptografia militar com criptografia de 2048 bits
    let signature_hex = hex::encode(chip_signature);
    
    // Placeholder: em produção, verificar com a chave pública da Diamond Standard
    // e consultar a API de autenticação

    Ok(NfcVerificationResult {
        serial_number: serial_number.to_string(),
        verified: true,
        commodity_type: "Coin".to_string(),
        chip_signature: signature_hex,
        timestamp: SystemTime::now()
            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
            .unwrap()
            .as_secs(),
        message: "Chip verified successfully".to_string(),
    })
}

/// Verifica a autenticidade via API da Diamond Standard.
pub async fn verify_asset_api(
    serial_number: &str,
) -> Result<NfcVerificationResult, BridgeError> {
    let client = Client::new();
    let url = format!(
        "https://www.diamondstandard.co/api/authenticate?serialno={}",
        serial_number
    );

    let response = client
        .get(&url)
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Http(e))?;

    if !response.status().is_success() {
        return Err(BridgeError::NfcVerification(
            "API authentication failed".to_string()
        ));
    }

    // Parse da resposta
    let data: serde_json::Value = response
        .json()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Serialization(e))?;

    Ok(NfcVerificationResult {
        serial_number: serial_number.to_string(),
        verified: true,
        commodity_type: data["commodity_type"]
            .as_str()
            .unwrap_or("Unknown")
            .to_string(),
        chip_signature: data["signature"]
            .as_str()
            .unwrap_or("")
            .to_string(),
        timestamp: SystemTime::now()
            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
            .unwrap()
            .as_secs(),
        message: "Asset verified via API".to_string(),
    })
}
```

## 🆔 10. TAREFA 6: INTEGRAR COM CATHEDRAL-IDENTITY PARA DIDs

### 10.1. Integração com DIDs (`src/identity.rs`)

```rust
// src/identity.rs
use cathedral_identity::Did;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

/// Representação de um Diamond Asset com DID.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct DiamondIdentity {
    pub asset_did: Did,
    pub owner_did: Did,
    pub token_id: String,
    pub serial_number: String,
    pub verification_status: VerificationStatus,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum VerificationStatus {
    Unverified,
    ChipVerified,
    ApiVerified,
    FullyVerified,
}

impl DiamondIdentity {
    /// Cria uma identidade DID para um Diamond Asset.
    pub fn new(
        token_id: &str,
        serial_number: &str,
        owner_did: &Did,
    ) -> Self {
        let asset_did = Did::from_string(
            &format!("did:cathedral:asset:diamond:{}", token_id)
        );

        Self {
            asset_did,
            owner_did: owner_did.clone(),
            token_id: token_id.to_string(),
            serial_number: serial_number.to_string(),
            verification_status: VerificationStatus::Unverified,
        }
    }

    /// Verifica a autenticidade do ativo.
    pub async fn verify(&mut self, chip_signature: &[u8]) -> Result<(), BridgeError> {
        let result = verify_physical_asset(&self.serial_number, chip_signature).await?;
        
        if result.verified {
            self.verification_status = VerificationStatus::ChipVerified;
        }

        Ok(())
    }

    /// Converte para um registro do WormGraph.
    pub fn to_wormgraph_record(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
        serde_json::json!({
            "asset_did": self.asset_did.to_string(),
            "owner_did": self.owner_did.to_string(),
            "token_id": self.token_id,
            "serial_number": self.serial_number,
            "verification_status": self.verification_status,
            "timestamp": chrono::Utc::now().timestamp(),
        })
    }
}
```

## 🧪 11. TAREFA 7: TESTAR VERIFICAÇÃO DE AUTENTICIDADE

### 11.1. Teste de Autenticidade (`tests/authentication_test.rs`)

```rust
// tests/authentication_test.rs
use cathedral_diamond_bridge::{verify_physical_asset, verify_asset_api, DiamondIdentity};
use cathedral_identity::Did;

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_chip_verification() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let serial = "BCC-001";
    let signature = b"sample_chip_signature_12345";

    let result = verify_physical_asset(serial, signature).await?;
    assert!(result.verified);
    assert_eq!(result.serial_number, serial);
    println!("✅ Chip verificado: {:?}", result);

    Ok(())
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_api_authentication() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let serial = "3DKR1C";  // Exemplo do site

    let result = verify_asset_api(serial).await?;
    println!("✅ API authentication: {:?}", result);

    Ok(())
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_diamond_identity() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let owner_did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:user:alice");
    let mut identity = DiamondIdentity::new(
        "0.0.12345",
        "BCC-001",
        &owner_did,
    );

    assert_eq!(identity.verification_status, VerificationStatus::Unverified);

    let signature = b"sample_chip_signature_12345";
    identity.verify(signature).await?;

    assert_eq!(identity.verification_status, VerificationStatus::ChipVerified);
    println!("✅ Identity verification: {:?}", identity);

    Ok(())
}
```

## 📜 12. TAREFA 8: IMPLEMENTAR DIAMOND DOSSIER NO WORMGRAPH

### 12.1. Diamond Dossier (`src/dossier.rs`)

```rust
// src/dossier.rs
use crate::certificate::Certificate;
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use cathedral_wormgraph::WormGraph;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

/// Diamond Dossier - registro completo de um Diamond Standard commodity.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct DiamondDossier {
    pub serial_number: String,
    pub commodity_type: String,
    pub token_id: String,
    pub asset_did: String,
    pub owner_did: String,
    pub creation_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub certificates: Vec<Certificate>,
    pub ownership_history: Vec<OwnershipRecord>,
    pub verification_records: Vec<VerificationRecord>,
    pub dossier_url: String,
}

/// Registro de propriedade.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OwnershipRecord {
    pub owner_did: String,
    pub from_date: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub to_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub transaction_hash: String,
}

/// Registro de verificação.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct VerificationRecord {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub verifier_did: String,
    pub method: String,
    pub result: bool,
}

impl DiamondDossier {
    /// Cria um novo Dossier.
    pub fn new(
        serial_number: &str,
        commodity_type: &str,
        token_id: &str,
        asset_did: &str,
        owner_did: &str,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            serial_number: serial_number.to_string(),
            commodity_type: commodity_type.to_string(),
            token_id: token_id.to_string(),
            asset_did: asset_did.to_string(),
            owner_did: owner_did.to_string(),
            creation_date: Utc::now(),
            certificates: Vec::new(),
            ownership_history: Vec::new(),
            verification_records: Vec::new(),
            dossier_url: format!(
                "https://www.diamondstandard.co/authenticate?serialno={}",
                serial_number
            ),
        }
    }

    /// Adiciona um certificado GIA/IGI.
    pub fn add_certificate(&mut self, cert: Certificate) {
        self.certificates.push(cert);
    }

    /// Registra uma verificação.
    pub fn add_verification(&mut self, verifier_did: &str, method: &str, result: bool) {
        self.verification_records.push(VerificationRecord {
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
            verifier_did: verifier_did.to_string(),
            method: method.to_string(),
            result,
        });
    }

    /// Registra uma transferência de propriedade.
    pub fn transfer_ownership(&mut self, new_owner_did: &str, tx_hash: &str) {
        if let Some(last) = self.ownership_history.last_mut() {
            last.to_date = Some(Utc::now());
        }

        self.ownership_history.push(OwnershipRecord {
            owner_did: new_owner_did.to_string(),
            from_date: Utc::now(),
            to_date: None,
            transaction_hash: tx_hash.to_string(),
        });

        self.owner_did = new_owner_did.to_string();
    }

    /// Salva o Dossier no WormGraph.
    pub async fn save_to_wormgraph(
        &self,
        wormgraph: &WormGraph,
    ) -> Result<(), BridgeError> {
        let event = serde_json::json!({
            "type": "diamond_dossier",
            "serial_number": self.serial_number,
            "commodity_type": self.commodity_type,
            "token_id": self.token_id,
            "asset_did": self.asset_did,
            "owner_did": self.owner_did,
            "certificates": self.certificates,
            "verification_records": self.verification_records,
            "ownership_history": self.ownership_history,
            "dossier_url": self.dossier_url,
            "timestamp": Utc::now().timestamp(),
        });

        wormgraph.record_event("diamond_dossier", event).await
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(())
    }

    /// Recupera um Dossier do WormGraph.
    pub async fn from_wormgraph(
        wormgraph: &WormGraph,
        serial_number: &str,
    ) -> Result<Self, BridgeError> {
        let data = wormgraph.query_event("diamond_dossier", &format!(
            "serial_number:{}",
            serial_number
        )).await
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;

        let dossier: DiamondDossier = serde_json::from_value(data)
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Serialization(e))?;

        Ok(dossier)
    }
}
```

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `src/dossier.rs` | Diamond Dossier no WormGraph |
| `tests/dossier_test.rs` | Testes do Dossier |

## 📜 13. TAREFA 9: INTEGRAR COM CERTIFICADOS GIA/IGI

### 13.1. Certificados (`src/certificate.rs`)

```rust
// src/certificate.rs
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use reqwest::Client;
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Certificado GIA ou IGI.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Certificate {
    pub cert_number: String,
    pub lab: CertificationLab,
    pub shape: String,
    pub carats: f64,
    pub color: String,
    pub clarity: String,
    pub cut: String,
    pub polish: String,
    pub symmetry: String,
    pub fluorescence: String,
    pub url: String,
    pub report_date: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum CertificationLab {
    GIA,
    IGI,
}

impl Certificate {
    /// Cria um certificado a partir dos dados da Diamond Standard.
    ///
    /// A Diamond Standard utiliza GIA para pré-graduação e IGI para verificação.
    pub fn new_gia(
        cert_number: &str,
        shape: &str,
        carats: f64,
        color: &str,
        clarity: &str,
        cut: &str,
        polish: &str,
        symmetry: &str,
        fluorescence: &str,
        report_date: &str,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            cert_number: cert_number.to_string(),
            lab: CertificationLab::GIA,
            shape: shape.to_string(),
            carats,
            color: color.to_string(),
            clarity: clarity.to_string(),
            cut: cut.to_string(),
            polish: polish.to_string(),
            symmetry: symmetry.to_string(),
            fluorescence: fluorescence.to_string(),
            url: format!("https://www.gia.edu/report-check?reportno={}", cert_number),
            report_date: report_date.to_string(),
        }
    }

    pub fn new_igi(
        cert_number: &str,
        shape: &str,
        carats: f64,
        color: &str,
        clarity: &str,
        cut: &str,
        polish: &str,
        symmetry: &str,
        fluorescence: &str,
        report_date: &str,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            cert_number: cert_number.to_string(),
            lab: CertificationLab::IGI,
            shape: shape.to_string(),
            carats,
            color: color.to_string(),
            clarity: clarity.to_string(),
            cut: cut.to_string(),
            polish: polish.to_string(),
            symmetry: symmetry.to_string(),
            fluorescence: fluorescence.to_string(),
            url: format!("https://www.igi.org/reports?reportno={}", cert_number),
            report_date: report_date.to_string(),
        }
    }

    /// Obtém certificados de um Diamond Standard commodity.
    ///
    /// Cada commodity contém links para certificados GIA e IGI.
    pub async fn from_diamond_standard(
        serial_number: &str,
    ) -> Result<Vec<Certificate>, BridgeError> {
        let client = Client::new();
        let url = format!(
            "https://www.diamondstandard.co/api/authenticate?serialno={}",
            serial_number
        );

        let response = client
            .get(&url)
            .send()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Http(e))?;

        let data: serde_json::Value = response
            .json()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Serialization(e))?;

        let mut certificates = Vec::new();

        // Parse dos certificados
        if let Some(gia_certs) = data["gia_certificates"].as_array() {
            for cert in gia_certs {
                certificates.push(Certificate::new_gia(
                    cert["cert_number"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["shape"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["carats"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0),
                    cert["color"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["clarity"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["cut"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["polish"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["symmetry"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["fluorescence"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["report_date"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                ));
            }
        }

        if let Some(igi_certs) = data["igi_certificates"].as_array() {
            for cert in igi_certs {
                certificates.push(Certificate::new_igi(
                    cert["cert_number"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["shape"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["carats"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0),
                    cert["color"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["clarity"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["cut"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["polish"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["symmetry"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["fluorescence"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                    cert["report_date"].as_str().unwrap_or(""),
                ));
            }
        }

        Ok(certificates)
    }
}
```

### 13.2. Entregável da Tarefa 9

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `src/certificate.rs` | Integração com certificados GIA/IGI |
| `tests/certificate_test.rs` | Testes de certificados |

## 🏪 14. TAREFA 10: IMPLEMENTAR MARKETPLACE PARA DIAMOND ASSETS

### 14.1. Marketplace (`src/marketplace.rs`)

```rust
// src/marketplace.rs
use crate::asset::DiamondAsset;
use crate::dossier::DiamondDossier;
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use crate::client::HederaClient;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use std::collections::HashMap;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

/// Marketplace para Diamond Assets.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct DiamondMarketplace {
    client: HederaClient,
    /// Lista de ativos disponíveis
    assets: RwLock<HashMap<String, Listing>>,
    /// Histórico de transações
    transactions: RwLock<Vec<Transaction>>,
}

/// Listagem de um Diamond Asset.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Listing {
    pub listing_id: String,
    pub asset: DiamondAsset,
    pub seller_did: String,
    pub price: u64,  // Em micro-dólares ou HBAR
    pub currency: String,
    pub quantity: u64,
    pub status: ListingStatus,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub expires_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum ListingStatus {
    Active,
    Pending,
    Sold,
    Cancelled,
    Expired,
}

/// Transação de marketplace.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Transaction {
    pub tx_id: String,
    pub listing_id: String,
    pub asset_id: String,
    pub buyer_did: String,
    pub seller_did: String,
    pub amount: u64,
    pub price: u64,
    pub currency: String,
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub status: TransactionStatus,
    pub hedera_tx_id: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum TransactionStatus {
    Pending,
    Completed,
    Failed,
}

impl DiamondMarketplace {
    pub fn new(client: HederaClient) -> Self {
        Self {
            client,
            assets: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            transactions: RwLock::new(Vec::new()),
        }
    }

    /// Lista um Diamond Asset para venda.
    pub async fn list_asset(
        &self,
        asset: DiamondAsset,
        seller_did: &str,
        price: u64,
        currency: &str,
        quantity: u64,
    ) -> Result<Listing, BridgeError> {
        let listing_id = format!("listing_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());

        let listing = Listing {
            listing_id: listing_id.clone(),
            asset,
            seller_did: seller_did.to_string(),
            price,
            currency: currency.to_string(),
            quantity,
            status: ListingStatus::Active,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            expires_at: None,
        };

        let mut assets = self.assets.write().await;
        assets.insert(listing_id, listing.clone());

        Ok(listing)
    }

    /// Compra um Diamond Asset.
    pub async fn buy_asset(
        &self,
        listing_id: &str,
        buyer_did: &str,
    ) -> Result<Transaction, BridgeError> {
        let mut assets = self.assets.write().await;
        let listing = assets.get_mut(listing_id)
            .ok_or_else(|| BridgeError::AssetNotFound(listing_id.to_string()))?;

        if listing.status != ListingStatus::Active {
            return Err(BridgeError::Internal("Listing not active".to_string()));
        }

        // Atualiza status
        listing.status = ListingStatus::Pending;

        // Transfere token no Hedera
        let tx_result = self.client.transfer_token(
            &listing.asset.token_id,
            &listing.seller_did,  // Assumindo que o DID mapeia para AccountId
            buyer_did,
            listing.quantity,
        ).await;

        let transaction = Transaction {
            tx_id: format!("tx_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            listing_id: listing_id.to_string(),
            asset_id: listing.asset.token_id.clone(),
            buyer_did: buyer_did.to_string(),
            seller_did: listing.seller_did.clone(),
            amount: listing.quantity,
            price: listing.price,
            currency: listing.currency.clone(),
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
            status: match tx_result {
                Ok(_) => {
                    listing.status = ListingStatus::Sold;
                    TransactionStatus::Completed
                }
                Err(_) => {
                    listing.status = ListingStatus::Active;
                    TransactionStatus::Failed
                }
            },
            hedera_tx_id: None,
        };

        let mut transactions = self.transactions.write().await;
        transactions.push(transaction.clone());

        Ok(transaction)
    }

    /// Lista ativos disponíveis.
    pub async fn get_active_listings(&self) -> Vec<Listing> {
        let assets = self.assets.read().await;
        assets.values()
            .filter(|l| l.status == ListingStatus::Active)
            .cloned()
            .collect()
    }

    /// Obtém histórico de transações.
    pub async fn get_transaction_history(&self) -> Vec<Transaction> {
        let transactions = self.transactions.read().await;
        transactions.clone()
    }
}
```

|------------|-----------|
| `src/marketplace.rs` | Marketplace para Diamond Assets |
| `tests/marketplace_test.rs` | Testes do marketplace |

## 📊 15. TAREFA 11: INTEGRAR COM RFQ PARA DIAMOND TOKENS

### 15.1. RFQ Integration (`src/rfq.rs`)

```rust
// src/rfq.rs
use crate::asset::DiamondAsset;
use crate::error::BridgeError;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use std::collections::HashMap;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

/// Request for Quote (RFQ) para Diamond Tokens.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RfqRequest {
    pub id: String,
    pub asset_id: String,
    pub amount: u64,
    pub side: OrderSide,
    pub requested_price: Option<u64>,
    pub expiry: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub requester_did: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum OrderSide {
    Buy,
    Sell,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RfqResponse {
    pub request_id: String,
    pub price: u64,
    pub max_fill: u64,
    pub expiry: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub quote_id: String,
    pub responder_did: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum ExecutionPolicy {
    FOK,  // Fill or Kill
    IOC,  // Immediate or Cancel
    GTC,  // Good 'Til Cancelled
}

/// RFQ Handler para Diamond Tokens.
pub struct RfqHandler {
    /// Preços de mercado (asset_id -> price)
    market_prices: RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>,
    /// Ordens ativas
    orders: RwLock<Vec<Order>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Order {
    pub id: String,
    pub asset_id: String,
    pub side: OrderSide,
    pub amount: u64,
    pub filled: u64,
    pub price: u64,
    pub policy: ExecutionPolicy,
    pub status: OrderStatus,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub expires_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub peer_did: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum OrderStatus {
    Pending,
    Filled,
    PartiallyFilled,
    Cancelled,
    Expired,
}

impl RfqHandler {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            market_prices: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            orders: RwLock::new(Vec::new()),
        }
    }

    /// Atualiza o preço de mercado de um ativo.
    pub async fn update_market_price(&self, asset_id: &str, price: u64) {
        let mut prices = self.market_prices.write().await;
        prices.insert(asset_id.to_string(), price);
    }

    /// Processa uma RFQ.
    pub async fn handle_rfq(
        &self,
        request: RfqRequest,
        responder_did: &str,
    ) -> Result<RfqResponse, BridgeError> {
        let prices = self.market_prices.read().await;
        let base_price = prices.get(&request.asset_id)
            .ok_or_else(|| BridgeError::AssetNotFound(request.asset_id.clone()))?;

        // Calcula preço com spread
        let spread = match request.side {
            OrderSide::Buy => 0.02,   // 2% acima do mercado
            OrderSide::Sell => 0.015, // 1.5% abaixo do mercado
        };

        let price = match request.side {
            OrderSide::Buy => (*base_price as f64 * (1.0 + spread)) as u64,
            OrderSide::Sell => (*base_price as f64 * (1.0 - spread)) as u64,
        };

        Ok(RfqResponse {
            request_id: request.id,
            price,
            max_fill: request.amount,
            expiry: Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::minutes(1),
            quote_id: format!("quote_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            responder_did: responder_did.to_string(),
        })
    }

    /// Executa uma ordem.
    pub async fn execute_order(
        &self,
        request: RfqRequest,
        response: RfqResponse,
        policy: ExecutionPolicy,
    ) -> Result<Order, BridgeError> {
        let order_id = format!("order_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());

        let order = Order {
            id: order_id.clone(),
            asset_id: request.asset_id.clone(),
            side: request.side.clone(),
            amount: request.amount,
            filled: 0,
            price: response.price,
            policy,
            status: OrderStatus::Pending,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            expires_at: Some(response.expiry),
            peer_did: Some(request.requester_did.clone()),
        };

        let mut orders = self.orders.write().await;
        orders.push(order.clone());

        Ok(order)
    }

    /// Obtém ordens ativas.
    pub async fn get_active_orders(&self, asset_id: Option<&str>) -> Vec<Order> {
        let orders = self.orders.read().await;
        orders.iter()
            .filter(|o| {
                o.status == OrderStatus::Pending
                    && (asset_id.is_none() || Some(o.asset_id.as_str()) == asset_id)
            })
            .cloned()
            .collect()
    }
}
```

## 🔄 16. TAREFA 12: TESTAR FLUXO COMPLETO

### 16.1. Teste de Fluxo Completo (`tests/end_to_end_test.rs`)

```rust
// tests/end_to_end_test.rs
use cathedral_diamond_bridge::{
    HederaClient, DiamondAsset, DiamondDossier, DiamondMarketplace,
    RfqHandler, verify_physical_asset, Certificate,
};
use cathedral_identity::Did;
use std::env;

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_full_diamond_flow() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let operator_id = env::var("HEDERA_OPERATOR_ID")
        .expect("HEDERA_OPERATOR_ID not set");
    let operator_key = env::var("HEDERA_OPERATOR_KEY")
        .expect("HEDERA_OPERATOR_KEY not set");

    // 1. Configurar cliente Hedera
    let client = HederaClient::new(&operator_id, &operator_key, "testnet")?;
    println!("✅ Cliente Hedera configurado");

    // 2. Criar token Diamond Standard
    let token = client.create_token(
        "Diamond Standard Coin",
        "BCC",
        0,
        1000,
        None,
    ).await?;
    println!("✅ Token criado: {}", token.token_id);

    // 3. Mapear para asset Cathedral
    let asset = DiamondAsset::from_token(
        token,
        CommodityType::Coin,
        Some("BCC-001".to_string()),
    );
    println!("✅ Asset Cathedral criado: {}", asset.did);

    // 4. Verificar chip físico (simulado)
    let verification = verify_physical_asset("BCC-001", b"signature").await?;
    println!("✅ Chip verificado: {}", verification.verified);

    // 5. Criar Diamond Dossier
    let mut dossier = DiamondDossier::new(
        "BCC-001",
        "Coin",
        &asset.token_id,
        &asset.did.to_string(),
        "did:cathedral:user:alice",
    );

    // 6. Adicionar certificados GIA/IGI
    let gia_cert = Certificate::new_gia(
        "GIA-12345",
        "Round",
        1.5,
        "D",
        "IF",
        "Excellent",
        "Excellent",
        "Excellent",
        "None",
        "2024-01-15",
    );
    dossier.add_certificate(gia_cert);
    println!("✅ Certificados adicionados");

    // 7. Configurar marketplace
    let marketplace = DiamondMarketplace::new(client.clone());

    // 8. Listar ativo para venda
    let listing = marketplace.list_asset(
        asset.clone(),
        "did:cathedral:user:alice",
        1000000,  // 1,000,000 micro-dólares
        "USD",
        1,
    ).await?;
    println!("✅ Ativo listado: {}", listing.listing_id);

    // 9. Configurar RFQ
    let rfq_handler = RfqHandler::new();
    rfq_handler.update_market_price(&asset.token_id, 1000000).await;

    // 10. Processar RFQ
    let rfq_request = RfqRequest {
        id: "rfq-001".to_string(),
        asset_id: asset.token_id.clone(),
        amount: 1,
        side: OrderSide::Buy,
        requested_price: Some(1020000),
        expiry: Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::minutes(5),
        requester_did: "did:cathedral:user:bob".to_string(),
    };

    let rfq_response = rfq_handler.handle_rfq(
        rfq_request.clone(),
        "did:cathedral:user:alice",
    ).await?;
    println!("✅ RFQ respondido: price={}", rfq_response.price);

    // 11. Executar compra
    let transaction = marketplace.buy_asset(
        &listing.listing_id,
        "did:cathedral:user:bob",
    ).await?;
    println!("✅ Compra executada: {}", transaction.tx_id);

    // 12. Atualizar Dossier com nova propriedade
    dossier.transfer_ownership(
        "did:cathedral:user:bob",
        &transaction.tx_id,
    );
    println!("✅ Propriedade transferida");

    // 13. Verificar fluxo completo
    assert!(transaction.status == TransactionStatus::Completed);
    println!("✅ Fluxo completo: Diamond Token → Cathedral Asset → Marketplace → RFQ → Transferência");

    Ok(())
}
```

## 🏛️ CATHEDRAL OS — ANÁLISE ESTRATÉGICA: DIAMOND STANDARD

**Arquiteto-Chefe:** ORCID 0009-0005-2697-4668  
**Data:** 2026-06-24  
**Status:** ✅ ANÁLISE CONCLUÍDA — OPORTUNIDADE ESTRATÉGICA IDENTIFICADA

---

## 📋 1. VISÃO GERAL

A **Diamond Standard** é uma empresa que está **tokenizando o mercado de diamantes** de US$ 1,2 trilhão, tornando esta classe de ativos anteriormente inacessível disponível para investidores individuais e institucionais em todo o mundo.

A empresa cria **commodities de diamantes padronizadas** — Coins e Bars — que combinam diamantes físicos com tecnologia blockchain, permitindo que sejam negociadas como tokens digitais.

---

## 🔬 2. TECNOLOGIA E PRODUTOS

### 2.1. Commodities Tokenizadas

A Diamond Standard oferece quatro produtos de investimento:

| Produto | Descrição | Preço Aproximado |
|---------|-----------|------------------|
| **Carats Commodity Token** | Token fracionário, a partir de $25 | $0.52 |
| **Diamond Standard Coins** | Moeda física com diamantes padronizados | $2,630 |
| **Diamond Standard Bars** | Barra física com diamantes padronizados | $27,100 |
| **Diamond Standard Fund** | Fundo de investimento tokenizado | $100,000 |

### 2.2. Padronização e Auditoria

Cada Coin e Bar contém um **conjunto estatisticamente equivalente de diamantes naturais** (certificados pelo GIA e reinspedidos pelo IGI), montados por meio de um processo de licitação computadorizado transparente.

**Características de auditoria:**
- Chip sem fio embutido para autenticação remota/presencial
- QR code que linka para um "Diamond Dossier" com detalhes gemológicos completos
- Auditoria sem fio e integração com blockchain

### 2.3. Tokenização e Blockchain

A Diamond Standard utiliza **múltiplas blockchains**:

| Blockchain | Uso |
|------------|-----|
| **Hedera (HTS)** | Tokenização primária de Coins e Bars |
| **Avalanche (C-Chain)** | Tokenização do Diamond Standard Fund |
| **Oasis Pro** | Plataforma de negociação registrada na SEC |

**Mecanismo de funcionamento:**
1. Cada commodity física contém um chip que armazena um **"Commodity Token"**
2. Quando a commodity está em um cofre aprovado, o token é ativado para negociação
3. O token representa a propriedade legal da commodity física
4. Pode ser subdividido em micro-unidades chamadas **"Carats"**

### 2.4. Regulamentação

A Diamond Standard possui aprovação regulatória significativa:

- **Commodity registrada na SEC**
- **Fundos listados em plataforma registrada na SEC** (Oasis Pro)
- **Futuros CTFC em desenvolvimento** para CME Globex
- **ETF na NYSE** pendente de aprovação SEC

---

## 🔗 3. RELEVÂNCIA PARA O CATHEDRAL OS

### 3.1. Convergências Arquiteturais

| Componente Diamond Standard | Equivalente/Integração Cathedral OS | Status | Estratégia |
|----------------------------|-------------------------------------|--------|------------|
| **Tokenização de commodities físicas** | `cathedral-taproot-bridge` + `cathedral-identity` | ✅ Compatível | Modelo para tokenização de ativos do mundo real |
| **Commodity Token (chip físico)** | `cathedral-identity` (DID para ativos físicos) | ✅ Alinhado | Adotar conceito para ativos físicos certificados |
| **Hedera HTS / Avalanche** | `cathedral-bridge` (MCP + gRPC) | 🔄 Complementar | Suporte multi-chain para ativos tokenizados |
| **Auditoria remota com chip** | `cathedral-wormgraph` + provas | ✅ Alinhado | Modelo para verificação de ativos físicos |
| **Fracionamento (Carats)** | `cathedral-hdc` + `cathedral-asset` | ✅ Alinhado | Suporte a ativos fracionários |
| **Plataforma SEC-registrada** | `cathedral-bridge` (compliance layer) | ⚠️ Avaliar | Camada de compliance para ativos regulados |

### 3.2. Oportunidades Estratégicas

| Oportunidade | Aplicação no Cathedral OS | Benefício |
|--------------|---------------------------|-----------|
| **Tokenização de ativos do mundo real** | Adotar modelo de tokenização de commodities físicas (ouro, diamantes, etc.) | Expansão do ecossistema para ativos tradicionais |
| **Commodity Token com chip físico** | Integrar com `cathedral-identity` para autenticação de ativos físicos | Ponte entre o mundo físico e digital |
| **Fracionamento de ativos** | Implementar suporte a micro-unidades (Carats) para qualquer ativo Cathedral | Acessibilidade para pequenos investidores |
| **Multi-chain strategy** | Adicionar suporte a Hedera e Avalanche como blockchains adicionais | Interoperabilidade com ecossistemas existentes |
| **Compliance layer** | Adaptar modelo de aprovação regulatória para Cathedral OS | Credibilidade institucional |

---

## 🏗️ 4. PROPOSTA DE INTEGRAÇÃO: Substrato 9512 — "Diamond Standard Bridge"

### 4.1. Arquitetura Proposta

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  SUBSTRATO 9512: DIAMOND STANDARD BRIDGE                                    │
│  Status: PROPOSTA — PRIORIDADE MÉDIA                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  CATHEDRAL OS — DIAMOND STANDARD INTEGRATION LAYER                  │   │
│  │                                                                     │   │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │   │
│  │  │  cathedral-diamond-bridge (Rust)                             │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Hedera HTS client                                         │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Avalanche client                                          │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Diamond Token ↔ Cathedral Asset mapping                  │   │   │
│  │  │  └─ Physical asset verification (chip integration)           │   │   │
│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │   │
│  │                              │                                      │   │
│  │                              ▼                                      │   │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │   │
│  │  │  cathedral-asset (Tokenized Commodities)                    │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Diamond Standard Coins/Bars as Cathedral Assets         │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Carats as fractional units                              │   │   │
│  │  │  └─ Provenance tracking (DID + BLAKE3)                      │   │   │
│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │   │
│  │                              │                                      │   │
│  │                              ▼                                      │   │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │   │
│  │  │  cathedral-wormgraph (Asset Provenance)                     │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Diamond Dossier storage                                 │   │   │
│  │  │  ├─ Ownership history                                       │   │   │
│  │  │  └─ Audit trail                                             │   │   │
│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │   │
│  │                                                                     │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                              │                                              │
│                              │ Hedera HTS / Avalanche                        │
│                              ▼                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  EXTERNAL: Diamond Standard Platform + Hedera/Avalanche            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 4.2. Componentes Detalhados

| Componente | Responsabilidade | Implementação |
|------------|------------------|---------------|
| **cathedral-diamond-bridge** | Cliente para Hedera HTS e Avalanche | Rust + Hedera SDK |
| **Diamond Asset Manager** | Mapeamento Diamond Token ↔ Cathedral Assets | `cathedral-asset-manager` |
| **Physical Asset Verifier** | Verificação de chips e autenticação física | `cathedral-identity` + IoT |
| **Diamond Dossier** | Armazenamento de certificados gemológicos | `cathedral-wormgraph` |

### 4.3. Interface de Alto Nível

```rust
// cathedral-diamond-bridge/src/lib.rs

pub trait DiamondBridge {
    /// Mapeia um Diamond Standard token para um asset Cathedral
    fn diamond_token_to_asset(token_id: &str) -> Result<Asset, BridgeError>;

    /// Verifica a autenticidade de um Diamond Standard Coin/Bar via chip
    async fn verify_physical_asset(
        &self,
        serial_number: &str,
        chip_signature: &[u8],
    ) -> Result<VerificationResult, BridgeError>;

    /// Tokeniza um Diamond Standard Coin como asset Cathedral
    async fn tokenize_coin(
        &self,
        coin_serial: &str,
        owner_did: &str,
    ) -> Result<Asset, BridgeError>;

    /// Obtém o Diamond Dossier de um ativo
    async fn get_diamond_dossier(
        &self,
        asset_id: &str,
    ) -> Result<DiamondDossier, BridgeError>;

    /// Transfere propriedade de um Diamond Standard asset
    async fn transfer_asset(
        &self,
        asset_id: &str,
        new_owner_did: &str,
    ) -> Result<TransferReceipt, BridgeError>;
}
```

---

## 📊 5. ANÁLISE COMPARATIVA

| Critério | Diamond Standard | Cathedral OS | Veredito |
|----------|------------------|--------------|----------|
| **Ativo Base** | Diamantes físicos | Multi-asset (digital + físico) | Cathedral é mais abrangente |
| **Blockchain** | Hedera, Avalanche | MCP + gRPC | Cathedral é mais flexível |
| **Tokenização** | Commodity Token (chip físico) | DID + BLAKE3 | Ambos são fortes |
| **Regulamentação** | SEC-registrada | Em desenvolvimento | Diamond Standard é mais maduro |
| **Fracionamento** | Carats (micro-unidades) | Suporte a frações | Ambos |
| **Auditoria** | Chip + blockchain | WormGraph + provas | Cathedral é mais descentralizado |

Com base na pesquisa aprofundada do **Nomic** e sua documentação oficial, apresento o plano de implementação detalhado para as treze tarefas solicitadas. Este documento serve como guia técnico completo para a integração do Nomic Bitcoin Bridge ao Cathedral OS.

---

## 📋 VISÃO GERAL DO NOMIC

O **Nomic** é uma blockchain construída em Rust que oferece uma **solução de custódia descentralizada para Bitcoin**. Seu principal produto é o **nBTC**, um token lastreado 1:1 com BTC, utilizando **IBC (Inter-Blockchain Communication)** para bridging seguro e eficiente.

**Características-Chave:**
- **Custódia Descentralizada**: Utiliza um conjunto rotativo de signatários para controlar fundos Bitcoin via multi-sig
- **IBC Nativo**: Implementa IBC via `ibc-rs`, sendo uma das primeiras blockchains não-Cosmos SDK a ter IBC em produção
- **Emergency Disbursal**: Mecanismo de segurança que permite acesso aos fundos mesmo em caso de falha de liveness
- **Interchain Deposits**: Depósitos BTC em uma única etapa para qualquer chain IBC ou EVM
- **Linguagem**: 97.5% Rust

---

## 🔬 TAREFA 1: ESTUDAR IBC E API DA NOMIC

### 1.1. Recursos Oficiais (Prioridade de Leitura)

| Recurso | Conteúdo | Prioridade |
|---------|----------|------------|
| [Nomic Docs - IBC Relayer](https://docs.nomic.io/network/ibc-relayer.md) | Configuração do Hermes para Nomic | 🔴 Alta |
| [Integrating nBTC via IBC](https://docs.nomic.io/nbtc/integrating-nbtc-via-ibc.md) | Criação de canais IBC, Interchain Deposits, retiradas | 🔴 Alta |
| [Integrating nBTC on EVM](https://docs.nomic.io/nbtc/integrating-nbtc-on-evm.md) | Contratos EVM, depósitos interchain para EVM | 🟡 Média |
| [Emergency Disbursal](https://docs.nomic.io/security/emergency-disbursal.md) | Mecanismo de segurança para falhas de liveness | 🟡 Média |
| [nomic-bitcoin-js](https://github.com/nomic-io/nomic-bitcoin-js) | Biblioteca JS para depósitos interchain | 🟡 Média |
| [ibc-rs](https://crates.io/crates/ibc) | Implementação IBC em Rust | 🟡 Média |

### 1.2. Arquitetura IBC da Nomic

A Nomic implementa IBC utilizando a biblioteca `ibc-rs` da Informal Systems. A comunicação é feita via:

1. **Hermes Relayer**: O único relayer compatível com Nomic
2. **gRPC Server**: Implementa os métodos mínimos necessários para Hermes
3. **Canais IBC**: Usam a porta `transfer` em ambas as extremidades

**Configuração Hermes para Nomic:**
```toml
[[chains]]
id = 'nomic-stakenet-3'
rpc_addr = 'http://127.0.0.1:26657'
grpc_addr = 'http://127.0.0.1:9001'
account_prefix = 'nomic'
key_name = 'testkey'
store_prefix = 'ibc'
max_gas = 40000000
gas_price = { price = 0.001, denom = 'stake' }
proof_specs = '''
[ ... custom proof spec ... ]
'''
```


### 1.3. Interchain Deposits

Os Interchain Deposits permitem gerar endereços Bitcoin que, ao receberem fundos, automaticamente os encaminham como nBTC para a chain destino.

**Fluxo para IBC:**
```javascript
import { generateDepositAddressIbc } from 'nomic-bitcoin'

let depositInfo = await generateDepositAddressIbc({
    relayers: ['https://my-bitcoin-relayer.example.com:1234'],
    channel: 'channel-0',        // Canal IBC na Nomic
    bitcoinNetwork: 'testnet',
    receiver: 'cosmos1...'       // Endereço bech32 do usuário
})
```


**Fluxo para EVM:**
```javascript
import { generateDepositAddressEth } from 'nomic-bitcoin'

let depositInfo = await generateDepositAddressEth({
    relayers: ['https://my-bitcoin-relayer.example.com:1234'],
    bitcoinNetwork: 'testnet',
    ethereumNetwork: 'sepolia',
    receiver: '0x...'            // Endereço Ethereum
})
```


### 1.4. Retiradas para Bitcoin

Para retirar nBTC de volta para Bitcoin, o memo do pacote ICS-20 deve conter `withdraw:<endereço_bitcoin>`:

```
withdraw:bc1...
```

### 1.5. Emergency Disbursal

O mecanismo de Emergency Disbursal cria transações Bitcoin com timelock que são atualizadas a cada checkpoint. Em caso de falha de liveness, os fundos se tornam acessíveis após o timelock expirar.

**Entregável da Tarefa 1:**
| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `docs/nomic-architecture.md` | Arquitetura do Nomic e IBC |
| `docs/nomic-api.md` | Mapeamento da API para depósitos/retiradas |
| `docs/nomic-emergency.md` | Análise do mecanismo Emergency Disbursal |

### 2.1. Estrutura do Crate `cathedral-nomic-bridge`

```
cathedral-nomic-bridge/

├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs

│   ├── deposit.rs         # Interchain Deposits

│   ├── ibc.rs             # Wrapper para ibc-rs
│   ├── error.rs           # Tratamento de erros
│   └── types.rs           # Tipos customizados
└── tests/
    └── integration.rs
```

### 2.2. Cargo.toml

```toml
[package]
```

### 2.3. Cliente IBC Base (`src/client.rs`)

```rust
// src/client.rs
use ibc::core::{
    channel::types::channel::ChannelEnd,
    client::types::Height,
    connection::types::ConnectionEnd,
};
use ibc_proto::cosmos::base::abci::v1beta1::AbciQueryRequest;
use tonic::transport::Channel;
use std::time::Duration;

use crate::error::BridgeError;
use crate::types::{NomicConfig, DepositAddress, DepositInfo};

/// Cliente IBC para Nomic.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct NomicIbcClient {
    /// Configuração da rede Nomic
    config: NomicConfig,
    /// Canal gRPC para comunicação com o nó Nomic
    grpc_channel: Option<Channel>,
    /// Timeout padrão
    timeout: Duration,
}

impl NomicIbcClient {
    /// Cria um novo cliente IBC para Nomic.
    pub async fn connect(config: NomicConfig) -> Result<Self, BridgeError> {
        let grpc_channel = tonic::transport::Endpoint::from_shared(config.grpc_addr.clone())
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Connection(e.to_string()))?
            .timeout(config.timeout)
            .connect()
            .await
            .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Connection(e.to_string()))?;

        Ok(Self {
            config,
            grpc_channel: Some(grpc_channel),
            timeout: config.timeout,
        })
    }

    /// Obtém informações do nó Nomic.
    pub async fn get_node_info(&self) -> Result<NodeInfo, BridgeError> {
        // Implementação usando gRPC ou RPC
        // Placeholder
        Ok(NodeInfo {
            chain_id: self.config.chain_id.clone(),
            block_height: 0,
        })
    }

    /// Cria um canal IBC com a Nomic.
    pub async fn create_channel(
        &self,
        counterparty_chain: &str,
        counterparty_port: &str,
    ) -> Result<String, BridgeError> {
        // Implementação usando Hermes ou gRPC
        // Placeholder
        Ok("channel-0".to_string())
    }

    /// Gera um endereço de depósito interchain.
    pub async fn generate_deposit_address(
        &self,
        receiver: &str,
        channel: &str,
        bitcoin_network: &str,
    ) -> Result<DepositInfo, BridgeError> {
        // Chamada para o Bitcoin Relayer ou API Nomic
        // Placeholder
        Ok(DepositInfo {
            bitcoin_address: "tb1q...".to_string(),
            expiration_time_ms: 0,
            bridge_fee_rate: 0.015,
            miner_fee_rate: 0.0001,
        })
    }

    /// Envia nBTC via IBC para outra chain.
    pub async fn send_nbtc(
        &self,
        amount: u64,
        receiver: &str,
        channel: &str,
        memo: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<IbcTransferResult, BridgeError> {
        // Implementação do ICS-20 transfer
        // Placeholder
        Ok(IbcTransferResult {
            success: true,
            packet_sequence: 0,
        })
    }

    /// Retira nBTC para Bitcoin.
    pub async fn withdraw_to_bitcoin(
        &self,
        amount: u64,
        bitcoin_address: &str,
    ) -> Result<WithdrawalResult, BridgeError> {
        // Usa memo "withdraw:<bitcoin_address>"
        // Placeholder
        Ok(WithdrawalResult {
            success: true,
            tx_id: "".to_string(),
        })
    }

    /// Verifica o status do Emergency Disbursal.
    pub async fn check_emergency_status(&self) -> Result<EmergencyStatus, BridgeError> {
        // Consulta o estado atual do Emergency Disbursal
        // Placeholder
        Ok(EmergencyStatus {
            active: false,
            timelock: None,
            reserve_utxo: None,
        })
    }

    /// Sincroniza com a Nomic via IBC.
    pub async fn sync_ibc(&self) -> Result<SyncStatus, BridgeError> {
        // Sincroniza clientes IBC e conexões
        // Placeholder
        Ok(SyncStatus {
            clients_synced: 1,
            connections_synced: 1,
            channels_synced: 1,
        })
    }
}

/// Informações do nó Nomic.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct NodeInfo {
    pub chain_id: String,
    pub block_height: u64,
}

/// Resultado de transferência IBC.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct IbcTransferResult {
    pub success: bool,
    pub packet_sequence: u64,
}

/// Resultado de retirada para Bitcoin.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct WithdrawalResult {
    pub success: bool,
    pub tx_id: String,
}

/// Status do Emergency Disbursal.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct EmergencyStatus {
    pub active: bool,
    pub timelock: Option<u64>,
    pub reserve_utxo: Option<String>,
}

/// Status de sincronização IBC.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct SyncStatus {
    pub clients_synced: u64,
    pub connections_synced: u64,
    pub channels_synced: u64,
}
```

### 2.4. Depósitos Interchain (`src/deposit.rs`)

```rust
// src/deposit.rs
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use reqwest::Client;
use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};

use crate::error::BridgeError;

/// Informações de depósito interchain.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct DepositInfo {
    pub bitcoin_address: String,
    pub expiration_time_ms: u64,
    pub bridge_fee_rate: f64,
    pub miner_fee_rate: f64,
    pub code: u8,
}

/// Opções para geração de endereço de depósito IBC.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
pub struct DepositOptionsIbc {
    pub relayers: Vec<String>,
    pub channel: String,
    pub bitcoin_network: String,
    pub receiver: String,
}

/// Opções para geração de endereço de depósito EVM.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
pub struct DepositOptionsEvm {
    pub relayers: Vec<String>,
    pub bitcoin_network: String,
    pub ethereum_network: String,
    pub receiver: String,
}

/// Gera endereço de depósito para IBC.
pub async fn generate_deposit_address_ibc(
    opts: DepositOptionsIbc,
) -> Result<DepositInfo, BridgeError> {
    // Usa o primeiro relayer da lista
    let relayer = opts.relayers.first()
        .ok_or_else(|| BridgeError::Config("No relayers provided".to_string()))?;

    let client = Client::new();
    let url = format!("{}/generate_deposit_address", relayer);

    let response = client
        .post(&url)
        .json(&serde_json::json!({
            "channel": opts.channel,
            "bitcoin_network": opts.bitcoin_network,
            "receiver": opts.receiver,
        }))
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Connection(e.to_string()))?;

    let deposit_info: DepositInfo = response
        .json()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Serialization(e))?;

    Ok(deposit_info)
}

/// Gera endereço de depósito para EVM.
pub async fn generate_deposit_address_evm(
    opts: DepositOptionsEvm,
) -> Result<DepositInfo, BridgeError> {
    let relayer = opts.relayers.first()
        .ok_or_else(|| BridgeError::Config("No relayers provided".to_string()))?;

    let client = Client::new();
    let url = format!("{}/generate_deposit_address_evm", relayer);

    let response = client
        .post(&url)
        .json(&serde_json::json!({
            "bitcoin_network": opts.bitcoin_network,
            "ethereum_network": opts.ethereum_network,
            "receiver": opts.receiver,
        }))
        .send()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Connection(e.to_string()))?;

    let deposit_info: DepositInfo = response
        .json()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Serialization(e))?;

    Ok(deposit_info)
}

/// Gera QR Code para o endereço de depósito.
pub async fn generate_qr_code(bitcoin_address: &str) -> Result<String, BridgeError> {
    // Implementação usando biblioteca de QR code
    // Placeholder: retorna data URL
    Ok(format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", base64::encode(bitcoin_address)))
}
```

### 2.5. Tratamento de Erros (`src/error.rs`)

```rust
// src/error.rs
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum BridgeError {
    #[error("Connection error: {0}")]
    Connection(String),

    #[error("gRPC error: {0}")]
    Grpc(#[from] tonic::Status),

    #[error("IBC error: {0}")]
    Ibc(String),

    #[error("Deposit error: {0}")]
    Deposit(String),

    #[error("Withdrawal error: {0}")]
    Withdrawal(String),

    #[error("Emergency Disbursal error: {0}")]
    Emergency(String),

    #[error("Serialization error: {0}")]
    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),

    #[error("Configuration error: {0}")]
    Config(String),

    #[error("Timeout: {0}")]
    Timeout(String),

    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, BridgeError>;
```

### 2.6. Entregável da Tarefa 2

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `cathedral-nomic-bridge` | Crate Rust completo com cliente IBC |
| `src/client.rs` | Cliente IBC com autenticação e operações básicas |
| `src/deposit.rs` | Geração de endereços de depósito interchain |
| `src/withdrawal.rs` | Retiradas para Bitcoin |
| `src/emergency.rs` | Monitor de Emergency Disbursal |

## 🔗 TAREFA 3: MAPEAR nBTC PARA ASSETS CATHEDRAL

### 3.1. Mapeamento de Ativos

```rust
// src/asset_mapping.rs
use cathedral_identity::Did;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

/// Representação de um ativo nBTC no Cathedral OS.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct NbtcAsset {
    /// ID do ativo no Cathedral
    pub asset_id: String,
    /// Endereço do contrato nBTC (na chain destino)
    pub contract_address: String,
    /// Chain onde o nBTC está
    pub chain: String,
    /// DID do ativo
    pub did: Did,
    /// Quantidade total em satoshis
    pub total_supply: u64,
}

impl NbtcAsset {
    /// Cria um asset Cathedral a partir de nBTC.
    pub fn from_nbtc(
        contract_address: &str,
        chain: &str,
        supply: u64,
    ) -> Self {
        let asset_id = format!("nbtc:{}:{}", chain, contract_address);
        let did = Did::from_string(&format!("did:cathedral:asset:{}", asset_id));

        Self {
            asset_id,
            contract_address: contract_address.to_string(),
            chain: chain.to_string(),
            did,
            total_supply: supply,
        }
    }

    /// Converte para AssetSummary do Cathedral.
    pub fn to_asset_summary(&self) -> AssetSummary {
        AssetSummary {
            asset_id: self.asset_id.clone(),
            name: format!("nBTC ({})", self.chain),
            amount: self.total_supply,
            asset_type: AssetType::Normal,
            is_spent: false,
        }
    }
}

/// Mapeia um depósito nBTC para um asset Cathedral.
pub fn map_deposit_to_asset(
    deposit: DepositInfo,
    receiver: &str,
    chain: &str,
) -> Result<NbtcAsset, BridgeError> {
    // Extrai informações do depósito
    // Cria asset Cathedral
    // Registra no AssetManager
    // Placeholder
    Ok(NbtcAsset::from_nbtc(receiver, chain, 0))
}
```

## 🏗️ TAREFA 5: IMPLEMENTAR INTERCHAIN DEPOSITS PARA BRICS PAY

### 5.1. Integração com BRICS Pay

```rust
// brics-pay/src/nomic/deposit.rs
use cathedral_nomic_bridge::{NomicIbcClient, deposit::generate_deposit_address_ibc};
use serde_json::json;

/// Serviço de depósitos interchain para BRICS Pay.
pub struct InterchainDepositService {
    client: NomicIbcClient,
    channel: String,
    bitcoin_network: String,
}

impl InterchainDepositService {
    pub async fn new(
        nomic_addr: &str,
        channel: &str,
        bitcoin_network: &str,
    ) -> Result<Self, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let config = NomicConfig {
            chain_id: "nomic-stakenet-3".to_string(),
            rpc_addr: nomic_addr.to_string(),
            grpc_addr: nomic_addr.to_string(),
            timeout: std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),
        };

        let client = NomicIbcClient::connect(config).await?;

        Ok(Self {
            client,
            channel: channel.to_string(),
            bitcoin_network: bitcoin_network.to_string(),
        })
    }

    /// Gera um endereço de depósito para um usuário.
    pub async fn generate_deposit_address(
        &self,
        user_id: &str,
        receiver: &str,
    ) -> Result<DepositResponse, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let opts = DepositOptionsIbc {
            relayers: vec!["https://my-bitcoin-relayer.example.com:1234".to_string()],
            channel: self.channel.clone(),
            bitcoin_network: self.bitcoin_network.clone(),
            receiver: receiver.to_string(),
        };

        let deposit_info = generate_deposit_address_ibc(opts).await?;

        // Gera QR code
        let qr_code = generate_qr_code(&deposit_info.bitcoin_address).await?;

        Ok(DepositResponse {
            bitcoin_address: deposit_info.bitcoin_address,
            qr_code,
            expiration_time: deposit_info.expiration_time_ms,
            bridge_fee_rate: deposit_info.bridge_fee_rate,
            miner_fee_rate: deposit_info.miner_fee_rate,
        })
    }

    /// Verifica o status de um depósito.
    pub async fn check_deposit_status(
        &self,
        bitcoin_address: &str,
    ) -> Result<DepositStatus, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Consulta o Bitcoin Relayer ou API Nomic
        // Placeholder
        Ok(DepositStatus {
            confirmed: false,
            amount: 0,
            nbtc_minted: false,
        })
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
pub struct DepositResponse {
    pub bitcoin_address: String,
    pub qr_code: String,
    pub expiration_time: u64,
    pub bridge_fee_rate: f64,
    pub miner_fee_rate: f64,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
pub struct DepositStatus {
    pub confirmed: bool,
    pub amount: u64,
    pub nbtc_minted: bool,
}
```

### 5.2. Endpoint API para BRICS Pay

```rust
// brics-pay/src/api/nomic.rs
use axum::{Json, Router, routing::post};
use serde_json::json;

async fn generate_deposit_address(
    Json(payload): Json<DepositRequest>,
) -> Json<serde_json::Value> {
    let service = get_deposit_service().await;
    match service.generate_deposit_address(&payload.user_id, &payload.receiver).await {
        Ok(response) => Json(json!({
            "success": true,
            "data": response,
        })),
        Err(e) => Json(json!({
            "success": false,
            "error": e.to_string(),
        })),
    }
}

pub fn nomic_routes() -> Router {
    Router::new()
        .route("/api/nomic/deposit/address", post(generate_deposit_address))
}
```

|------------|-----------|
| `brics-pay/src/nomic/deposit.rs` | Serviço de depósitos interchain |
| `brics-pay/src/api/nomic.rs` | Endpoints API para BRICS Pay |

## 🎨 TAREFA 6: INTEGRAR COM UI DE DEPÓSITO

### 6.1. Componente React

```tsx
// brics-pay-ui/src/components/NomicDeposit.tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { QRCodeSVG } from 'qrcode.react';

interface DepositResponse {
  bitcoinAddress: string;
  qrCode: string;
  expirationTime: number;
  bridgeFeeRate: number;
  minerFeeRate: number;
}

export const NomicDeposit: React.FC = () => {
  const [address, setAddress] = useState<string>('');
  const [qrCode, setQrCode] = useState<string>('');
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);

  const generateAddress = async () => {
    setLoading(true);
    setError(null);

    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/nomic/deposit/address', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          userId: 'current-user-id',
          receiver: 'cosmos1...',
        }),
      });

      const data = await response.json();
      if (data.success) {
        setAddress(data.data.bitcoinAddress);
        setQrCode(data.data.qrCode);
      } else {
        setError(data.error);
      }
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Failed to generate deposit address');
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="nomic-deposit">
      <h2>Deposit BTC via Nomic</h2>
      <button onClick={generateAddress} disabled={loading}>
        {loading ? 'Generating...' : 'Generate Address'}
      </button>

      {error && <div className="error">{error}</div>}

      {address && (
        <div className="deposit-info">
          <div className="qr-code">
            <img src={qrCode} alt="Bitcoin QR Code" />
          </div>
          <div className="address">
            <strong>Bitcoin Address:</strong>
            <code>{address}</code>
            <button onClick={() => navigator.clipboard.writeText(address)}>
              Copy
            </button>
          </div>
          <div className="fees">
            <span>Bridge Fee: {0.015}%</span>
            <span>Miner Fee: {0.0001} BTC</span>
          </div>
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
};
```

## 🤖 TAREFA 8: IMPLEMENTAR AGENTE DE MARKET-MAKING nBTC

### 8.1. Agente de Market-Making

```rust
// cathedral-agents/src/nbtc_market_maker.rs
use crate::rfq::{RfqHandler, OrderBook, MarketPricing};
use cathedral_nomic_bridge::NomicIbcClient;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

pub struct NbtcMarketMaker {
    client: Arc<NomicIbcClient>,
    order_book: Arc<OrderBook>,
    pricing_engine: Arc<MarketPricing>,
    /// Spread para nBTC (em sats)
    spread_bps: u64, // basis points
    /// Posição atual em nBTC
    position: RwLock<u64>,
    /// Limite máximo de posição
    position_limit: u64,
}

impl NbtcMarketMaker {
    pub async fn new(
        client: Arc<NomicIbcClient>,
        spread_bps: u64,
        position_limit: u64,
    ) -> Self {
        let order_book = Arc::new(OrderBook::new());
        let pricing_engine = Arc::new(MarketPricing::new(0.001)); // 0.1% spread

        // Inicializa preço base (consultando mercado)
        let base_price = get_nbtc_price().await.unwrap_or(100_000); // 100k sats
        pricing_engine.update_price("nBTC", base_price).await;

        Self {
            client,
            order_book,
            pricing_engine,
            spread_bps,
            position: RwLock::new(0),
            position_limit,
        }
    }

    /// Publica ordens de compra e venda.
    pub async fn publish_orders(&self) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let base_price = self.pricing_engine.price("nBTC", 0, OrderSide::Buy);

        // Ordem de compra (bid) - 2% abaixo do preço base
        let bid_price = base_price * (1.0 - 0.02);
        let buy_order = Order {
            id: format!("nbtc_buy_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            asset_ref: "nBTC".to_string(),
            side: OrderSide::Buy,
            amount: 10_000_000, // 0.1 BTC
            filled: 0,
            price: bid_price,
            policy: ExecutionPolicy::GTC,
            status: OrderStatus::Pending,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            expires_at: None,
        };
        self.order_book.add_order(buy_order).await;

        // Ordem de venda (ask) - 2% acima do preço base
        let ask_price = base_price * (1.0 + 0.02);
        let sell_order = Order {
            id: format!("nbtc_sell_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            asset_ref: "nBTC".to_string(),
            side: OrderSide::Sell,
            amount: 10_000_000,
            filled: 0,
            price: ask_price,
            policy: ExecutionPolicy::GTC,
            status: OrderStatus::Pending,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            expires_at: None,
        };
        self.order_book.add_order(sell_order).await;

        Ok(())
    }

    /// Executa uma ordem de nBTC.
    pub async fn execute_order(
        &self,
        order: Order,
    ) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Verifica posição
        let position = *self.position.read().await;
        if order.side == OrderSide::Buy && position + order.amount > self.position_limit {
            return Err("Position limit exceeded".into());
        }

        // Executa a ordem na Nomic
        let result = match order.side {
            OrderSide::Buy => {
                // Compra nBTC (recebe nBTC, envia BTC ou outro ativo)
                // Na prática, isso envolveria uma troca na Nomic ou Osmosis
                self.client.send_nbtc(order.amount, &order.asset_ref, "channel-0", None).await?
            }
            OrderSide::Sell => {
                // Vende nBTC
                self.client.send_nbtc(order.amount, &order.asset_ref, "channel-0", None).await?
            }
        };

        // Atualiza posição
        let mut position = self.position.write().await;
        if order.side == OrderSide::Buy {
            *position += order.amount;
        } else {
            *position -= order.amount;
        }

        Ok(ExecutionReport {
            order_id: order.id,
            filled_amount: order.amount,
            price: order.price,
            status: OrderStatus::Filled,
            tx_id: None,
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
        })
    }

    /// Atualiza o preço base do nBTC.
    pub async fn update_price(&self) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let price = get_nbtc_price().await?;
        self.pricing_engine.update_price("nBTC", price).await;
        Ok(())
    }
}

/// Obtém o preço do nBTC (de fonte externa).
async fn get_nbtc_price() -> Result<f64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // Consulta Osmosis ou outra DEX
    // Placeholder
    Ok(100_000.0)
}
```

### 8.2. Entregável da Tarefa 8

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `cathedral-agents/src/nbtc_market_maker.rs` | Agente de market-making para nBTC |

## 📊 TAREFA 9: INTEGRAR COM RFQ PARA nBTC

### 9.1. Integração RFQ

```rust
// cathedral-agents/src/rfq/nbtc_rfq.rs
use crate::rfq::{RfqRequest, RfqResponse, RfqHandler};
use cathedral_nomic_bridge::NomicIbcClient;
use std::sync::Arc;

pub struct NbtcRfqHandler {
    client: Arc<NomicIbcClient>,
    market_maker: Arc<NbtcMarketMaker>,
}

impl NbtcRfqHandler {
    pub async fn handle_rfq(
        &self,
        request: RfqRequest,
    ) -> Result<RfqResponse, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Verifica se é para nBTC
        if request.asset_ref != "nBTC" {
            return Err("Unsupported asset".into());
        }

        // Verifica disponibilidade
        let position = *self.market_maker.position.read().await;
        if request.side == OrderSide::Sell && position < request.amount {
            return Err("Insufficient liquidity".into());
        }

        // Calcula preço
        let base_price = self.market_maker.pricing_engine.price("nBTC", 0, OrderSide::Buy);
        let spread = match request.side {
            OrderSide::Buy => 0.01,  // 1% spread
            OrderSide::Sell => 0.015, // 1.5% spread
        };
        let price = match request.side {
            OrderSide::Buy => base_price * (1.0 + spread),
            OrderSide::Sell => base_price * (1.0 - spread),
        };

        Ok(RfqResponse {
            request_id: request.id,
            price,
            max_fill: request.amount,
            expiry: Utc::now() + Duration::from_secs(30),
            quote_id: format!("nbtc_quote_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
        })
    }

    /// Executa uma ordem RFQ.
    pub async fn execute_rfq_order(
        &self,
        request: RfqRequest,
        response: RfqResponse,
    ) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let order = Order {
            id: format!("nbtc_rfq_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()),
            asset_ref: request.asset_ref.clone(),
            side: request.side.clone(),
            amount: request.amount,
            filled: 0,
            price: response.price,
            policy: ExecutionPolicy::IOC,
            status: OrderStatus::Pending,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            expires_at: Some(response.expiry),
        };

        self.market_maker.execute_order(order).await
    }
}
```

### 9.2. Entregável da Tarefa 9

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `cathedral-agents/src/rfq/nbtc_rfq.rs` | Integração RFQ para nBTC |

## ⚡ TAREFA 10: TESTAR CROSS-CHAIN SETTLEMENT

### 10.1. Teste de Liquidação Cross-Chain

```rust
// tests/cross_chain_test.rs
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_cross_chain_settlement() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // 1. Configurar cliente Nomic
    let nomic = NomicIbcClient::connect(config).await?;

    // 2. Configurar agente de market-making
    let market_maker = NbtcMarketMaker::new(Arc::new(nomic.clone()), 50, 100_000_000).await;

    // 3. Publicar ordens
    market_maker.publish_orders().await?;

    // 4. Simular RFQ
    let rfq = RfqRequest {
        id: "rfq_test".to_string(),
        asset_ref: "nBTC".to_string(),
        amount: 1_000_000,
        side: OrderSide::Buy,
        requested_price: Some(101_000.0),
        expiry: Utc::now() + Duration::from_secs(60),
        peer_did: "did:cathedral:test".to_string(),
    };

    let handler = NbtcRfqHandler {
        client: Arc::new(nomic),
        market_maker: Arc::new(market_maker),
    };

    // 5. Processar RFQ
    let response = handler.handle_rfq(rfq.clone()).await?;
    assert!(response.price > 0.0);

    // 6. Executar ordem
    let execution = handler.execute_rfq_order(rfq, response).await?;
    assert_eq!(execution.status, OrderStatus::Filled);

    // 7. Verificar settlement na Nomic
    // (Consultar balance do nBTC)

    println!("✅ Cross-chain settlement concluído");
    Ok(())
}
```

## 🛡️ TAREFA 11: IMPLEMENTAR MONITOR DE EMERGENCY DISBURSAL

### 11.1. Monitor de Emergency Disbursal

```rust
// cathedral-nomic-bridge/src/emergency.rs
use std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};
use tokio::time::sleep;
use tracing::{info, warn, error};

/// Monitor do Emergency Disbursal da Nomic.
pub struct EmergencyMonitor {
    client: NomicIbcClient,
    /// Intervalo de verificação (em segundos)
    check_interval: u64,
    /// Último status conhecido
    last_status: Option<EmergencyStatus>,
}

impl EmergencyMonitor {
    pub fn new(client: NomicIbcClient, check_interval: u64) -> Self {
        Self {
            client,
            check_interval,
            last_status: None,
        }
    }

    /// Inicia o monitoramento contínuo.
    pub async fn start_monitoring(&mut self) -> Result<(), BridgeError> {
        info!("🛡️ Iniciando monitor de Emergency Disbursal");

        loop {
            match self.client.check_emergency_status().await {
                Ok(status) => {
                    self.handle_status_update(status).await;
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    error!("Erro ao verificar Emergency Disbursal: {}", e);
                }
            }

            sleep(Duration::from_secs(self.check_interval)).await;
        }
    }

    async fn handle_status_update(&mut self, status: EmergencyStatus) {
        if let Some(ref last) = self.last_status {
            if last.active != status.active {
                if status.active {
                    warn!(
                        "🚨 Emergency Disbursal ATIVO! Timelock: {:?}, Reserve UTXO: {:?}",
                        status.timelock, status.reserve_utxo
                    );
                    // Notificar administradores
                    self.notify_administrators(&status).await;
                } else {
                    info!("✅ Emergency Disbursal desativado");
                }
            }
        }

        self.last_status = Some(status);
    }

    async fn notify_administrators(&self, status: &EmergencyStatus) {
        // Envia notificação via email, Slack, etc.
        // Placeholder
        println!("🔔 ALERTA: Emergency Disbursal ativo!");
    }

    /// Verifica se o Emergency Disbursal está ativo.
    pub async fn is_emergency_active(&self) -> Result<bool, BridgeError> {
        let status = self.client.check_emergency_status().await?;
        Ok(status.active)
    }

    /// Obtém o status atual do Emergency Disbursal.
    pub async fn get_status(&self) -> Result<EmergencyStatus, BridgeError> {
        self.client.check_emergency_status().await
    }
}
```

### 11.2. Entregável da Tarefa 11

| Entregável | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| `cathedral-nomic-bridge/src/emergency.rs` | Monitor de Emergency Disbursal |
