## 📚 1. ESTUDO DA DOCUMENTAÇÃO DO `tapd` E SDK

### 1.1. Recursos Oficiais (Prioridade de Leitura)

| Recurso | Conteúdo | Prioridade |
|---------|----------|------------|
| [tapd gRPC API Docs](https://lightning.engineering/api-docs/api/taproot-assets/) | Definição completa dos serviços gRPC (rpc.proto, assetwallet.proto, universe.proto) | 🔴 Alta |
| [Taproot Assets SDK Getting Started](https://github.com/lightninglabs/tap-sdk/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md) | Guia passo-a-passo para usar o SDK em Go | 🔴 Alta |
| [Taproot Assets Protocol Spec](https://github.com/lightninglabs/taproot-assets/tree/main/docs) | Especificação detalhada do protocolo (Merkle proofs, asset commitment, etc.) | 🟡 Média |
| [SDK Integration Tests](https://github.com/lightninglabs/tap-sdk/tree/main/itest) | Exemplos end-to-end executáveis contra regtest | 🟡 Média |
| [tapd CLI Reference](https://docs.lightning.engineering/lightning-network-tools/taproot-assets/get-tapd) | Comandos da CLI para operações manuais | 🟢 Baixa |

### 1.2. Análise Crítica da API gRPC

Os serviços principais do `tapd` (via gRPC) são:

| Serviço | Método | Descrição |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| `AssetWallet` | `CreateAsset` | Cria um novo ativo (fungível ou colecionável) |
| `AssetWallet` | `IssueAsset` | Emite unidades adicionais de um ativo existente |
| `AssetWallet` | `SendAsset` | Envia ativos para outro nó (via Lightning ou on-chain) |
| `AssetWallet` | `BurnAsset` | Queima unidades de um ativo |
| `Universe` | `QueryUniverse` | Consulta provas de ativos da Universe Federation |
| `Universe` | `InsertProof` | Insere uma prova no Universe local |
| `Rpc` | `GetInfo` | Obtém informações do nó |

**Padrões de Design Observados:**
- **AssetRef**: um identificador que pode ser asset_id (UUID) ou group_key (fungíveis)
- **Paginável**: todas as listagens suportam `offset` e `limit`
- **Macaroons**: credenciais de acesso com permissões escopadas
- **TLS**: comunicação segura por padrão (mTLS recomendado)

### 1.3. Análise do SDK (Go)

O SDK abstrai complexidades:

```go
// Exemplo do SDK (Go)
asset, err := client.CreateFungible(ctx, &CreateFungibleRequest{
    Name:        "USDC",
    Supply:      1000_000_000, // 1000 unidades com 6 decimais
    MetaData:    []byte(`{"issuer":"Cathedral OS"}`),
})
if err != nil {
    // ...
}
// asset.AssetRef é um handle auto-verificável
```

**Pontos-Chave para o Cliente Rust:**
- O SDK usa o mesmo `tapd` gRPC API — não há uma API separada
- O SDK adiciona conveniência (ex: `AssetRef` parsing, auto-verificação)
- As mensagens protobuf são compartilhadas entre `tapd` e SDK

### 1.4. Recomendações para o Estudo

| Atividade | Tempo Estimado | Entregável |
|-----------|----------------|------------|
| Ler API Docs e identificar serviços necessários | 2 horas | Mapeamento de endpoints |
| Estudar SDK Getting Started e Integration Tests | 2 horas | Exemplos de fluxo |
| Analisar especificação de provas (Merkle) | 3 horas | Entendimento do modelo de provas |
| Documentar padrões de autenticação (macaroons) | 1 hora | Estratégia de autenticação |

---

## 🦀 2. PROTOTIPAR CLIENTE gRPC EM RUST

### 2.1. Estrutura do Crate

```rust
// cathedral-taproot-bridge/Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "cathedral-taproot-bridge"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
tonic = "0.12"
prost = "0.13"
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
thiserror = "1.0"
tracing = "0.1"
url = "2.5"
hex = "0.4"
blake3 = "1.5"

# Para geração de protobuf
build = "build.rs"

[build-dependencies]
tonic-build = "0.12"
```

### 2.2. Geração de Código Protobuf (`build.rs`)

```rust
// build.rs
use std::path::PathBuf;

fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let proto_root = PathBuf::from("proto");
    let proto_files = [
        "rpc.proto",
        "assetwallet.proto",
        "universe.proto",
    ];

    // Baixar os arquivos proto do repositório oficial (ou usar submodule)
    // Exemplo: wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lightninglabs/taproot-assets/main/rpc/rpc.proto

    tonic_build::configure()
        .build_client(true)
        .build_server(false)
        .out_dir("src/proto")
        .compile(&proto_files, &[&proto_root])?;

    Ok(())
}
```

### 2.3. Cliente Base

```rust
// src/client.rs
use tonic::transport::{Channel, Certificate, ClientTlsConfig};
use tonic::{Request, Response, Status};
use std::sync::Arc;
use tracing::{info, warn, error};

// Incluir os módulos gerados
pub mod proto {
    tonic::include_proto!("taprootassets");
}

use proto::{
    assetwallet_client::AssetWalletClient,
    universe_client::UniverseClient,
    rpc_client::RpcClient,
    // Tipos de mensagem
    CreateAssetRequest, CreateAssetResponse,
    IssueAssetRequest, IssueAssetResponse,
    SendAssetRequest, SendAssetResponse,
    BurnAssetRequest, BurnAssetResponse,
    QueryUniverseRequest, QueryUniverseResponse,
    GetInfoRequest, GetInfoResponse,
};

use crate::error::BridgeError;

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct TaprootClient {
    asset_wallet: AssetWalletClient<Channel>,
    universe: UniverseClient<Channel>,
    rpc: RpcClient<Channel>,
    macaroon: Option<Vec<u8>>,
}

impl TaprootClient {
    /// Conecta a um nó tapd via gRPC.
    pub async fn connect(
        addr: &str,
        tls: bool,
        macaroon_path: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<Self, BridgeError> {
        let mut endpoint = tonic::transport::Endpoint::from_shared(addr.to_string())?;

        if tls {
            // Carrega certificado TLS (o tapd usa TLS por padrão)
            // Idealmente usar mTLS com client certificate
            let tls_config = ClientTlsConfig::new()
                .ca_certificate(Certificate::from_pem(load_ca_cert()?))
                .domain_name("localhost"); // ou domínio configurado
            endpoint = endpoint.tls_config(tls_config)?;
        }

        let channel = endpoint.connect().await?;

        // Carrega macaroon se fornecido
        let macaroon = if let Some(path) = macaroon_path {
            Some(std::fs::read(path)?)
        } else {
            None
        };

        Ok(Self {
            asset_wallet: AssetWalletClient::new(channel.clone()),
            universe: UniverseClient::new(channel.clone()),
            rpc: RpcClient::new(channel.clone()),
            macaroon,
        })
    }

    /// Adiciona macaroon ao metadado da requisição (se disponível)
    fn add_macaroon<T>(&self, mut req: Request<T>) -> Request<T> {
        if let Some(mac) = &self.macaroon {
            let mac_hex = hex::encode(mac);
            req.metadata_mut().insert(
                "macaroon",
                tonic::metadata::MetadataValue::from_str(&mac_hex).unwrap(),
            );
        }
        req
    }

    // --- Operações de carteira ---

    /// Cria um novo ativo fungível.
    pub async fn create_fungible(
        &mut self,
        name: &str,
        supply: u64,
        metadata: &[u8],
    ) -> Result<CreateAssetResponse, BridgeError> {
        let req = CreateAssetRequest {
            name: name.to_string(),
            supply,
            metadata: metadata.to_vec(),
            asset_type: 0, // 0 = fungível
            // ... outros campos (decimals, etc.)
        };
        let request = self.add_macaroon(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.asset_wallet.create_asset(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Emite unidades adicionais de um ativo existente.
    pub async fn issue_asset(
        &mut self,
        asset_ref: &str,
        amount: u64,
    ) -> Result<IssueAssetResponse, BridgeError> {
        let req = IssueAssetRequest {
            asset_ref: asset_ref.to_string(),
            amount,
        };
        let request = self.add_macaroon(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.asset_wallet.issue_asset(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Envia ativos para outro nó via Lightning.
    pub async fn send_asset(
        &mut self,
        asset_ref: &str,
        amount: u64,
        dest_pubkey: &str,
        dest_asset_ref: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<SendAssetResponse, BridgeError> {
        let req = SendAssetRequest {
            asset_ref: asset_ref.to_string(),
            amount,
            dest_pubkey: dest_pubkey.to_string(),
            dest_asset_ref: dest_asset_ref.map(|s| s.to_string()),
            // Opções de roteamento
        };
        let request = self.add_macaroon(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.asset_wallet.send_asset(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Queima unidades de um ativo.
    pub async fn burn_asset(
        &mut self,
        asset_ref: &str,
        amount: u64,
    ) -> Result<BurnAssetResponse, BridgeError> {
        let req = BurnAssetRequest {
            asset_ref: asset_ref.to_string(),
            amount,
        };
        let request = self.add_macaroon(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.asset_wallet.burn_asset(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    // --- Operações Universe ---

    /// Consulta provas de um ativo na Universe Federation.
    pub async fn query_universe(
        &mut self,
        asset_ref: &str,
    ) -> Result<QueryUniverseResponse, BridgeError> {
        let req = QueryUniverseRequest {
            asset_ref: asset_ref.to_string(),
        };
        let request = self.add_macaroon(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.universe.query_universe(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    // --- Operações RPC ---

    /// Obtém informações do nó.
    pub async fn get_info(&mut self) -> Result<GetInfoResponse, BridgeError> {
        let req = GetInfoRequest {};
        let request = self.add_macaroon(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.rpc.get_info(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }
}
```

### 2.4. Mapeamento AssetRef ↔ DID

```rust
// src/identity.rs
use cathedral_identity::Did;

/// Converte um AssetRef (string) para um DID Cathedral.
/// O AssetRef pode ser um asset_id (UUID) ou uma group_key (hex).
pub fn asset_ref_to_did(asset_ref: &str) -> Option<Did> {
    // Usa o padrão did:key:<base64> ou did:cathedral:<asset_ref>
    // Exemplo: did:cathedral:asset:group_key_hex
    if asset_ref.starts_with("group_key_") {
        // Extrai a chave e cria DID
        let key = asset_ref.trim_start_matches("group_key_");
        Some(Did::from_string(&format!("did:cathedral:asset:{}", key)))
    } else {
        // Assume que é um asset_id (UUID)
        Some(Did::from_string(&format!("did:cathedral:asset:{}", asset_ref)))
    }
}

/// Converte um DID para um AssetRef (se for um asset do Cathedral)
pub fn did_to_asset_ref(did: &Did) -> Option<String> {
    let prefix = "did:cathedral:asset:";
    if did.to_string().starts_with(prefix) {
        let asset_ref = did.to_string().trim_start_matches(prefix);
        Some(asset_ref.to_string())
    } else {
        None
    }
}
```

### 2.5. Tratamento de Erros

```rust
// src/error.rs
use thiserror::Error;
use tonic::Status;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum BridgeError {
    #[error("Transport error: {0}")]
    Transport(#[from] tonic::transport::Error),
    #[error("gRPC error: {0}")]
    Grpc(#[from] Status),
    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
    #[error("Invalid AssetRef: {0}")]
    InvalidAssetRef(String),
    #[error("DID conversion error")]
    DidConversion,
    #[error("Macaroon error: {0}")]
    Macaroon(String),
    #[error("Proof verification failed")]
    ProofVerification,
}

impl From<tonic::Status> for BridgeError {
    fn from(status: Status) -> Self {
        BridgeError::Grpc(status)
    }
}
```

### 2.6. Exemplo de Uso

```rust
// src/main.rs (exemplo)
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    let mut client = TaprootClient::connect(
        "https://localhost:10000",
        true,
        Some("/path/to/admin.macaroon"),
    ).await?;

    // Criar um ativo
    let asset = client.create_fungible(
        "TestCoin",
        100_000_000,
        b"{\"issuer\":\"Cathedral OS\"}",
    ).await?;
    println!("Asset criado: {:?}", asset);

    // Emitir mais unidades
    let issued = client.issue_asset(&asset.asset_ref, 50_000_000).await?;
    println!("Emitido: {:?}", issued);

    // Transferir para outro nó
    let send = client.send_asset(
        &asset.asset_ref,
        10_000_000,
        "03...", // pubkey destino
        None,
    ).await?;
    println!("Enviado: {:?}", send);

    Ok(())
}
```

## ⚡ 3. VALIDAR INTEGRAÇÃO COM LIGHTNING NETWORK

### 3.1. Ambiente de Teste

**Recomendação:** Usar `regtest` com `docker-compose` para simular rede Lightning com tapd.

```yaml
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3'
services:
  lnd-alice:
    image: lightninglabs/lnd:latest
    command: --bitcoin.regtest --bitcoin.active --accept-keysend
    ports:
      - "10009:10009"  # gRPC
      - "8080:8080"    # REST

  lnd-bob:
    image: lightninglabs/lnd:latest
    command: --bitcoin.regtest --bitcoin.active --accept-keysend
    ports:
      - "10010:10009"

  tapd-alice:
    image: lightninglabs/taproot-assets:latest
    command: --lnd-address=lnd-alice:10009 --tapd-address=0.0.0.0:10000
    ports:
      - "10000:10000"

  tapd-bob:
    image: lightninglabs/taproot-assets:latest
    command: --lnd-address=lnd-bob:10009 --tapd-address=0.0.0.0:10001
    ports:
      - "10001:10000"
```

### 3.2. Fluxo de Teste

| Passo | Ação | Verificação |
|-------|------|-------------|
| 1 | Iniciar containers | LND sincronizado, tapd conectado |
| 2 | Criar asset no Alice | `tapcli assets create --name TEST --supply 1000000` |
| 3 | Conectar Alice ↔ Bob | `tapcli universe federation add bob` |
| 4 | Enviar asset via Lightning | `tapcli assets send --asset-ref ... --dest bob-pubkey` |
| 5 | Bob recebe e verifica prova | `tapcli universe query` |
| 6 | Queimar ativo no Bob | `tapcli assets burn --asset-ref ... --amount 100` |

### 3.3. Script de Validação em Rust

```rust
// tests/integration_test.rs
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use cathedral_taproot_bridge::TaprootClient;
    use tokio;

    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_asset_lifecycle() {
        let mut alice = TaprootClient::connect(
            "http://localhost:10000",
            false,
            Some("certs/admin.macaroon"),
        ).await.unwrap();

        let mut bob = TaprootClient::connect(
            "http://localhost:10001",
            false,
            Some("certs/admin.macaroon"),
        ).await.unwrap();

        // 1. Alice cria ativo
        let asset = alice.create_fungible("TEST", 1_000_000, b"{}").await.unwrap();
        assert!(!asset.asset_ref.is_empty());

        // 2. Alice emite mais
        let issued = alice.issue_asset(&asset.asset_ref, 500_000).await.unwrap();
        assert!(issued.success);

        // 3. Obtém pubkey do Bob
        let bob_info = bob.get_info().await.unwrap();
        let bob_pubkey = bob_info.pubkey;

        // 4. Alice envia para Bob
        let send = alice.send_asset(&asset.asset_ref, 100_000, &bob_pubkey, None).await.unwrap();
        assert!(send.success);

        // 5. Bob consulta Universe para verificar prova
        let proof = bob.query_universe(&asset.asset_ref).await.unwrap();
        assert!(proof.assets.len() > 0);

        // 6. Bob queima ativo
        let burn = bob.burn_asset(&asset.asset_ref, 10_000).await.unwrap();
        assert!(burn.success);
    }
}
```

### 3.4. Desafios e Soluções

| Desafio | Solução |
|---------|---------|
| **Autenticação** | Usar macaroons com permissões apropriadas (read, write, courier) |
| **Roteamento de pagamentos** | Garantir que ambos os nós estejam conectados e com canais abertos |
| **Sincronização de Universe** | Aguardar propagação de provas (pode levar alguns segundos) |
| **Provas grandes** | Verificar tamanho das provas e usar mode compact/optimistic conforme necessário |
| **Timeouts** | Aumentar timeouts para transações na Lightning (ex: 60s) |

---

## 🤖 4. IMPLEMENTAR RFQ AGENT AVANÇADO

### 4.1. Arquitetura do RFQ Agent

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  RFQ Agent (cathedral-agents/rfq)                              │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  RFQ Listener (gRPC ou MCP)                              │ │
│  │  - Escuta requisições de peers                           │ │
│  │  - Valida autenticação (macaroons/DIDs)                 │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                              │                                  │
│                              ▼                                  │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Pricing Engine                                          │ │
│  │  - Políticas de precificação (baseada em mercado, AMM)  │ │
│  │  - Hedging (futuros, opções)                             │ │
│  │  - Análise de risco (volatilidade, liquidez)            │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                              │                                  │
│                              ▼                                  │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Order Book Management                                   │ │
│  │  - Manutenção de ordens (compras/vendas)                │ │
│  │  - Matching engine (IOC/FOK)                            │ │
│  │  - Prevenção de front-running                           │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                              │                                  │
│                              ▼                                  │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Execution Layer (Taproot Bridge)                        │ │
│  │  - Executa ordens via tapd                               │ │
│  │  - Monitora confirmações                                 │ │
│  │  - Registra eventos no WormGraph                        │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 4.2. Estrutura de Dados

```rust
// src/rfq/mod.rs
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::collections::HashMap;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RfqRequest {
    pub id: String,
    pub asset_ref: String,
    pub amount: u64,
    pub side: OrderSide, // Buy ou Sell
    pub requested_price: Option<f64>,
    pub expiry: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub peer_did: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum OrderSide {
    Buy,
    Sell,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RfqResponse {
    pub request_id: String,
    pub price: f64,
    pub max_fill: u64,
    pub expiry: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub quote_id: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum ExecutionPolicy {
    /// Fill or Kill: preenche tudo ou cancela tudo
    FOK,
    /// Immediate or Cancel: preenche o que for possível, cancela o resto
    IOC,
    /// Good 'Til Cancelled: ordem ativa até cancelamento
    GTC,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Order {
    pub id: String,
    pub asset_ref: String,
    pub side: OrderSide,
    pub amount: u64,
    pub filled: u64,
    pub price: f64,
    pub policy: ExecutionPolicy,
    pub status: OrderStatus,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub expires_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub enum OrderStatus {
    Pending,
    Filled,
    PartiallyFilled,
    Cancelled,
    Expired,
}

/// Resultado da execução de uma ordem
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExecutionReport {
    pub order_id: String,
    pub filled_amount: u64,
    pub price: f64,
    pub status: OrderStatus,
    pub tx_id: Option<String>, // ID da transação Lightning
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

### 4.3. Pricing Engine

```rust
// src/rfq/pricing.rs
use std::collections::HashMap;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

pub trait PricingPolicy: Send + Sync {
    fn price(&self, asset_ref: &str, amount: u64, side: OrderSide) -> f64;
}

/// Estratégia de preço baseada em mercado (cenário simples)
pub struct MarketPricing {
    base_price: RwLock<HashMap<String, f64>>,
    spread: f64, // spread percentual (ex: 0.01 = 1%)
}

impl MarketPricing {
    pub fn new(spread: f64) -> Self {
        Self {
            base_price: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            spread,
        }
    }

    pub async fn update_price(&self, asset_ref: &str, price: f64) {
        let mut map = self.base_price.write().await;
        map.insert(asset_ref.to_string(), price);
    }
}

impl PricingPolicy for MarketPricing {
    fn price(&self, asset_ref: &str, amount: u64, side: OrderSide) -> f64 {
        let base = self.base_price.blocking_read().get(asset_ref).cloned().unwrap_or(1.0);
        let adjusted = match side {
            OrderSide::Buy => base * (1.0 + self.spread),  // compra paga spread
            OrderSide::Sell => base * (1.0 - self.spread), // venda recebe menos
        };
        adjusted
    }
}

/// Estratégia de preço dinâmica (ex: com base em liquidez)
pub struct DynamicPricing {
    pub base_prices: RwLock<HashMap<String, f64>>,
    pub liquidity_pools: RwLock<HashMap<String, f64>>, // quantidade disponível
}

impl PricingPolicy for DynamicPricing {
    fn price(&self, asset_ref: &str, amount: u64, side: OrderSide) -> f64 {
        let base = self.base_prices.blocking_read().get(asset_ref).cloned().unwrap_or(1.0);
        let liquidity = self.liquidity_pools.blocking_read().get(asset_ref).cloned().unwrap_or(0.0);
        // Quanto maior a ordem em relação à liquidez, maior o spread
        let spread_factor = (amount as f64 / (liquidity + 1.0)).min(0.5);
        let spread = 0.01 + spread_factor * 0.05;
        match side {
            OrderSide::Buy => base * (1.0 + spread),
            OrderSide::Sell => base * (1.0 - spread),
        }
    }
}
```

### 4.4. Order Book

```rust
// src/rfq/order_book.rs
use std::collections::{HashMap, VecDeque};
use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use chrono::{Utc, Duration};

pub struct OrderBook {
    orders: Mutex<Vec<Order>>,
}

impl OrderBook {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            orders: Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
        }
    }

    /// Adiciona uma nova ordem
    pub async fn add_order(&self, order: Order) {
        let mut orders = self.orders.lock().await;
        orders.push(order);
    }

    /// Encontra ordens de compra para um ativo específico
    pub async fn get_buy_orders(&self, asset_ref: &str) -> Vec<Order> {
        let orders = self.orders.lock().await;
        orders.iter()
            .filter(|o| o.asset_ref == asset_ref && o.side == OrderSide::Buy && o.status == OrderStatus::Pending)
            .cloned()
            .collect()
    }

    /// Encontra ordens de venda para um ativo específico
    pub async fn get_sell_orders(&self, asset_ref: &str) -> Vec<Order> {
        let orders = self.orders.lock().await;
        orders.iter()
            .filter(|o| o.asset_ref == asset_ref && o.side == OrderSide::Sell && o.status == OrderStatus::Pending)
            .cloned()
            .collect()
    }

    /// Remove ordens expiradas
    pub async fn clean_expired(&self) {
        let mut orders = self.orders.lock().await;
        let now = Utc::now();
        orders.retain(|o| {
            if let Some(expires) = o.expires_at {
                if now > expires {
                    return false; // remove
                }
            }
            true
        });
    }

    /// Encontra ordens com preço melhor ou igual ao limite
    pub async fn find_matching_orders(
        &self,
        asset_ref: &str,
        side: OrderSide,
        price_limit: f64,
        amount: u64,
    ) -> Vec<Order> {
        let orders = self.orders.lock().await;
        let matching: Vec<Order> = orders.iter()
            .filter(|o| {
                o.asset_ref == asset_ref
                    && o.side == side
                    && o.status == OrderStatus::Pending
                    && o.price <= price_limit // para compras, preço <= limite; para vendas, preço >= limite
            })
            .cloned()
            .collect();
        // Ordena por preço (menor para compra, maior para venda)
        // ... (simplificado)
        matching
    }
}
```

### 4.5. RFQ Handler

```rust
// src/rfq/handler.rs
use crate::TaprootClient;
use crate::rfq::{RfqRequest, RfqResponse, Order, ExecutionPolicy, OrderStatus, ExecutionReport};
use crate::rfq::pricing::PricingPolicy;
use crate::rfq::order_book::OrderBook;
use std::sync::Arc;
use chrono::{Utc, Duration};
use tracing::{info, warn, error};

pub struct RfqHandler {
    bridge: Arc<TaprootClient>,
    pricing_engine: Arc<dyn PricingPolicy>,
    order_book: Arc<OrderBook>,
    min_fill_amount: u64,
    max_order_size: u64,
}

impl RfqHandler {
    pub fn new(
        bridge: Arc<TaprootClient>,
        pricing_engine: Arc<dyn PricingPolicy>,
        order_book: Arc<OrderBook>,
        min_fill_amount: u64,
        max_order_size: u64,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            bridge,
            pricing_engine,
            order_book,
            min_fill_amount,
            max_order_size,
        }
    }

    /// Processa uma RFQ recebida
    pub async fn handle_rfq(&self, request: RfqRequest) -> Result<RfqResponse, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Verifica se o ativo é suportado
        // (validação de asset_ref via bridge)
        let asset_info = self.bridge.query_universe(&request.asset_ref).await?;
        if asset_info.assets.is_empty() {
            return Err("Asset not found in Universe".into());
        }

        // Verifica limites de quantidade
        if request.amount < self.min_fill_amount {
            return Err("Amount below minimum fill".into());
        }
        if request.amount > self.max_order_size {
            return Err("Amount exceeds max order size".into());
        }

        // Calcula preço
        let price = self.pricing_engine.price(
            &request.asset_ref,
            request.amount,
            request.side,
        );

        // Verifica se o preço solicitado é aceitável (se fornecido)
        if let Some(requested_price) = request.requested_price {
            match request.side {
                OrderSide::Buy => {
                    if price > requested_price {
                        return Err("Price above requested limit".into());
                    }
                }
                OrderSide::Sell => {
                    if price < requested_price {
                        return Err("Price below requested limit".into());
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Cria a resposta
        let quote_id = format!("quote_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());
        Ok(RfqResponse {
            request_id: request.id,
            price,
            max_fill: request.amount,
            expiry: Utc::now() + Duration::minutes(1),
            quote_id,
        })
    }

    /// Executa uma ordem baseada em uma RFQ aceita
    pub async fn execute_order(
        &self,
        request: RfqRequest,
        response: RfqResponse,
        policy: ExecutionPolicy,
    ) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let order_id = format!("order_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());

        // Cria a ordem
        let order = Order {
            id: order_id.clone(),
            asset_ref: request.asset_ref.clone(),
            side: request.side.clone(),
            amount: request.amount,
            filled: 0,
            price: response.price,
            policy: policy.clone(),
            status: OrderStatus::Pending,
            created_at: Utc::now(),
            expires_at: Some(response.expiry),
        };

        // Adiciona ao order book
        self.order_book.add_order(order.clone()).await;

        // Simula matching (na prática, isso seria mais complexo)
        let executed = match policy {
            ExecutionPolicy::FOK => self.execute_fok(order).await,
            ExecutionPolicy::IOC => self.execute_ioc(order).await,
            ExecutionPolicy::GTC => self.execute_gtc(order).await,
        }?;

        // Se executado, usa a bridge para transferir ativos
        if executed.status == OrderStatus::Filled || executed.status == OrderStatus::PartiallyFilled {
            self.execute_on_chain(&executed).await?;
        }

        Ok(executed)
    }

    async fn execute_fok(&self, order: Order) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Verifica se há liquidez suficiente para preencher completamente
        let available = self.check_liquidity(&order.asset_ref, order.amount).await?;
        if available < order.amount {
            return Ok(ExecutionReport {
                order_id: order.id,
                filled_amount: 0,
                price: order.price,
                status: OrderStatus::Cancelled,
                tx_id: None,
                timestamp: Utc::now(),
            });
        }
        // Preenche completamente
        Ok(ExecutionReport {
            order_id: order.id,
            filled_amount: order.amount,
            price: order.price,
            status: OrderStatus::Filled,
            tx_id: None,
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
        })
    }

    async fn execute_ioc(&self, order: Order) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let available = self.check_liquidity(&order.asset_ref, order.amount).await?;
        let filled = order.amount.min(available);
        let status = if filled == order.amount {
            OrderStatus::Filled
        } else if filled > 0 {
            OrderStatus::PartiallyFilled
        } else {
            OrderStatus::Cancelled
        };
        Ok(ExecutionReport {
            order_id: order.id,
            filled_amount: filled,
            price: order.price,
            status,
            tx_id: None,
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
        })
    }

    async fn execute_gtc(&self, order: Order) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Mantém a ordem ativa até ser preenchida ou cancelada
        // (simplificado: preenche o máximo possível agora)
        self.execute_ioc(order).await
    }

    async fn check_liquidity(&self, asset_ref: &str, amount: u64) -> Result<u64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Consulta saldo do ativo via bridge
        // (implementação depende da API do tapd para balance)
        // Por enquanto, retorna amount (assume liquidez total)
        Ok(amount)
    }

    async fn execute_on_chain(&self, report: &ExecutionReport) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        // Usa a bridge para enviar ativos
        // (precisa de detalhes do destinatário, pubkey, etc.)
        // Exemplo:
        // self.bridge.send_asset(&order.asset_ref, report.filled_amount, dest_pubkey, None).await?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### 4.6. Integração com Cathedral Agents

```rust
// cathedral-agents/src/agent/market_maker.rs
use cathedral_taproot_bridge::{TaprootClient, TaprootBridge};
use crate::rfq::{RfqHandler, MarketPricing, OrderBook};
use crate::cathedral_identity::Did;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

pub struct MarketMakerAgent {
    did: Did,
    bridge: Arc<TaprootClient>,
    rfq_handler: Arc<RfqHandler>,
    active_orders: RwLock<Vec<Order>>,
}

impl MarketMakerAgent {
    pub async fn new(
        did: Did,
        bridge: Arc<TaprootClient>,
        spread: f64,
        min_fill: u64,
        max_order: u64,
    ) -> Self {
        let order_book = Arc::new(OrderBook::new());
        let pricing = Arc::new(MarketPricing::new(spread));
        let handler = Arc::new(RfqHandler::new(
            bridge.clone(),
            pricing,
            order_book.clone(),
            min_fill,
            max_order,
        ));

        Self {
            did,
            bridge,
            rfq_handler: handler,
            active_orders: RwLock::new(Vec::new()),
        }
    }

    /// Escuta por RFQs (via MCP ou gRPC) e responde
    pub async fn listen_for_rfqs(&self) {
        // Implementação de listener
        // Exemplo: via MCP Bridge, recebe mensagens com tipo "rfq"
        // Para cada mensagem, chama self.handle_rfq()
        loop {
            tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(1)).await;
            // Simula recebimento de RFQ
            // (na prática, isso seria um canal de comunicação)
        }
    }

    /// Publica uma ordem no order book
    pub async fn publish_order(&self, order: Order) {
        self.rfq_handler.order_book.add_order(order).await;
    }

    /// Executa uma ordem
    pub async fn execute_order(&self, request: RfqRequest, response: RfqResponse) -> Result<ExecutionReport, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        self.rfq_handler.execute_order(request, response, ExecutionPolicy::IOC).await
    }

    /// Obtém status de uma ordem
    pub async fn get_order_status(&self, order_id: &str) -> Option<OrderStatus> {
        let orders = self.rfq_handler.order_book.orders.lock().await;
        orders.iter().find(|o| o.id == order_id).map(|o| o.status.clone())
    }
}
```

### 4.7. Configuração e Inicialização

```rust
// src/bin/rfq_agent.rs
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();

    // Conecta ao tapd
    let bridge = Arc::new(TaprootClient::connect(
        "https://localhost:10000",
        true,
        Some("certs/admin.macaroon"),
    ).await?);

    // Cria agente market-maker
    let did = Did::from_string("did:cathedral:agent:alice");
    let agent = MarketMakerAgent::new(
        did,
        bridge,
        0.02, // 2% spread
        1000, // min fill
        1_000_000, // max order
    ).await;

    // Inicia listener
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        agent.listen_for_rfqs().await;
    });

    // Publica ordens iniciais (ex: compra/venda)
    let buy_order = Order {
        id: "order_buy_001".to_string(),
        asset_ref: "asset_test".to_string(),
        side: OrderSide::Buy,
        amount: 500_000,
        filled: 0,
        price: 0.98,
        policy: ExecutionPolicy::GTC,
        status: OrderStatus::Pending,
        created_at: Utc::now(),
        expires_at: None,
    };
    agent.publish_order(buy_order).await;

    let sell_order = Order {
        id: "order_sell_001".to_string(),
        asset_ref: "asset_test".to_string(),
        side: OrderSide::Sell,
        amount: 300_000,
        filled: 0,
        price: 1.02,
        policy: ExecutionPolicy::GTC,
        status: OrderStatus::Pending,
        created_at: Utc::now(),
        expires_at: None,
    };
    agent.publish_order(sell_order).await;

    // Mantém o agente rodando
    println!("RFQ Agent running...");
    tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### 2.2. `build.rs`

```rust
// build.rs
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::env;

fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let out_dir = PathBuf::from(env::var("OUT_DIR")?);
    let proto_dir = PathBuf::from(env::var("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")?).join("proto");

    // Lista dos 5 protofiles (incluindo tapcommon.proto)
    let proto_files = [
        "tapcommon.proto",
        "taprootassets.proto",
        "assetwallet.proto",
        "universe.proto",
        "rfq.proto",
    ];

    let proto_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = proto_files
        .iter()
        .map(|f| proto_dir.join(f))
        .collect();

    // Verificar se todos os arquivos existem
    for path in &proto_paths {
        if !path.exists() {
            println!("cargo:warning=Proto file not found: {}", path.display());
            return Err(format!("Missing proto file: {}", path.display()).into());
        }
    }

    tonic_build::configure()
        .build_client(true)
        .build_server(false)
        .build_transport(true)
        .out_dir(&out_dir)
        .compile(&proto_paths, &[&proto_dir])?;

    println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=proto/");
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=build.rs");

    Ok(())
}
```

## 🦀 TAREFA 3: IMPLEMENTAR CLIENTE BASE COM AUTENTICAÇÃO

### 3.1. `src/error.rs` — Tratamento de Erros

```rust
// src/error.rs
use thiserror::Error;
use tonic::Status;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum BridgeError {
    #[error("Transport error: {0}")]
    Transport(#[from] tonic::transport::Error),

    #[error("gRPC error: {0}")]
    Grpc(#[from] Status),

    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),

    #[error("Invalid AssetRef: {0}")]
    InvalidAssetRef(String),

    #[error("DID conversion error")]
    DidConversion,

    #[error("Macaroon error: {0}")]
    Macaroon(String),

    #[error("Proof verification failed: {0}")]
    ProofVerification(String),

    #[error("Universe sync failed: {0}")]
    UniverseSync(String),

    #[error("Asset not found: {0}")]
    AssetNotFound(String),

    #[error("Insufficient balance: need {need} have {have}")]
    InsufficientBalance { need: u64, have: u64 },

    #[error("Timeout: {0}")]
    Timeout(String),

    #[error("Invalid address: {0}")]
    InvalidAddress(String),

    #[error("Configuration error: {0}")]
    Config(String),

    #[error("Internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, BridgeError>;

impl From<BridgeError> for tonic::Status {
    fn from(err: BridgeError) -> Self {
        tonic::Status::internal(err.to_string())
    }
}
```

### 3.2. `src/auth.rs` — Autenticação TLS + Macaroons

```rust
// src/auth.rs
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;
use tonic::transport::{Certificate, ClientTlsConfig};
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum AuthError {
    #[error("Invalid macaroon: {0}")]
    InvalidMacaroon(String),
    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
    #[error("TLS error: {0}")]
    Tls(String),
}

/// Macaroon de autenticação para tapd (formato LND).
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Macaroon {
    bytes: Vec<u8>,
    permissions: Vec<String>,
}

impl Macaroon {
    pub fn from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, AuthError> {
        let bytes = fs::read(path)?;
        Self::from_bytes(bytes)
    }

    pub fn from_bytes(bytes: Vec<u8>) -> Result<Self, AuthError> {
        if bytes.is_empty() {
            return Err(AuthError::InvalidMacaroon("Empty macaroon".to_string()));
        }
        // Permissões padrão: read, write, courier
        let permissions = vec![
            "read".to_string(),
            "write".to_string(),
            "courier".to_string(),
        ];
        Ok(Self { bytes, permissions })
    }

    pub fn bytes(&self) -> &[u8] {
        &self.bytes
    }

    pub fn to_hex(&self) -> String {
        hex::encode(&self.bytes)
    }

    pub fn permissions(&self) -> &[String] {
        &self.permissions
    }
}

/// Configuração TLS para conexão com tapd.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct TlsConfig {
    pub ca_cert: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    pub client_cert: Option<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>, // (cert, key)
    pub domain: String,
}

impl TlsConfig {
    pub fn new(domain: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            ca_cert: None,
            client_cert: None,
            domain: domain.into(),
        }
    }

    pub fn with_ca(mut self, ca_pem: Vec<u8>) -> Self {
        self.ca_cert = Some(ca_pem);
        self
    }

    pub fn with_client_cert(mut self, cert_pem: Vec<u8>, key_pem: Vec<u8>) -> Self {
        self.client_cert = Some((cert_pem, key_pem));
        self
    }

    pub fn to_tonic_config(&self) -> Result<ClientTlsConfig, AuthError> {
        let mut config = ClientTlsConfig::new().domain_name(self.domain.clone());

        if let Some(ca) = &self.ca_cert {
            config = config.ca_certificate(Certificate::from_pem(ca));
        }

        if let Some((cert, key)) = &self.client_cert {
            config = config.identity(Certificate::from_pem(cert), Certificate::from_pem(key));
        }

        Ok(config)
    }
}
```

### 3.3. `src/client.rs` — Cliente gRPC Principal

```rust
// src/client.rs
use tonic::metadata::MetadataValue;
use tonic::transport::{Channel, ClientTlsConfig};
use tonic::{Request, Status};
use std::path::Path;
use std::time::Duration;
use tracing::{debug, warn};

// Módulo gerado pelo build.rs
pub mod proto {
    tonic::include_proto!("tapcommon");
    tonic::include_proto!("taprootassets");
    tonic::include_proto!("assetwallet");
    tonic::include_proto!("universe");
    tonic::include_proto!("rfq");
}

use proto::{
    taproot_assets_client::TaprootAssetsClient,
    asset_wallet_client::AssetWalletClient,
    universe_client::UniverseClient,
    rfq_client::RfqClient,
    // Mensagens principais
    GetInfoRequest, GetInfoResponse,
    ListAssetsRequest, ListAssetsResponse,
    ListBalancesRequest, ListBalancesResponse,
    NewAddrRequest, NewAddrResponse,
    SendAssetRequest, SendAssetResponse,
    BurnAssetRequest, BurnAssetResponse,
    // Universe
    QueryProofRequest, QueryProofResponse,
    InsertProofRequest, InsertProofResponse,
    AssetLeavesRequest, AssetLeavesResponse,
    AssetRootsRequest, AssetRootsResponse,
    // RFQ
    AddAssetBuyOrderRequest, AddAssetBuyOrderResponse,
    AddAssetSellOrderRequest, AddAssetSellOrderResponse,
    ForwardingHistoryRequest, ForwardingHistoryResponse,
};

use crate::auth::{Macaroon, TlsConfig};
use crate::error::{BridgeError, Result};

/// Cliente principal para o Taproot Assets Daemon (tapd).
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct TaprootClient {
    /// Cliente do serviço TaprootAssets (core)
    pub taproot: TaprootAssetsClient<Channel>,
    /// Cliente do serviço AssetWallet (PSBT, chaves)
    pub asset_wallet: AssetWalletClient<Channel>,
    /// Cliente do serviço Universe (provas)
    pub universe: UniverseClient<Channel>,
    /// Cliente do serviço RFQ (ordens/ofertas)
    pub rfq: RfqClient<Channel>,
    /// Macaroon de autenticação
    macaroon: Option<Macaroon>,
    /// Timeout padrão
    timeout: Duration,
}

impl TaprootClient {
    /// Conecta a um nó tapd via gRPC.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `addr` - Endereço do nó (ex: "https://localhost:10029" ou "http://localhost:10029")
    /// * `tls` - Configuração TLS (opcional)
    /// * `macaroon_path` - Caminho para o arquivo macaroon (opcional)
    ///
    /// # Example
    /// ```rust,no_run
    /// use cathedral_taproot_bridge::{TaprootClient, TlsConfig};
    /// use std::path::Path;
    ///
    /// # async fn example() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    /// let client = TaprootClient::connect(
    ///     "https://localhost:10029",
    ///     Some(TlsConfig::new("localhost")),
    ///     Some(Path::new("~/.taproot-assets/admin.macaroon")),
    /// ).await?;
    /// # Ok(())
    /// # }
    /// ```
    pub async fn connect(
        addr: &str,
        tls: Option<TlsConfig>,
        macaroon_path: Option<&Path>,
    ) -> Result<Self> {
        let mut endpoint = tonic::transport::Endpoint::from_shared(addr.to_string())?;

        if let Some(tls_config) = tls {
            let tonic_config = tls_config
                .to_tonic_config()
                .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Macaroon(e.to_string()))?;
            endpoint = endpoint.tls_config(tonic_config)?;
        } else {
            warn!("Connecting without TLS - insecure!");
        }

        let channel = endpoint.connect().await?;

        // Carrega macaroon se fornecido
        let macaroon = if let Some(path) = macaroon_path {
            let mac = Macaroon::from_file(path)
                .map_err(|e| BridgeError::Macaroon(e.to_string()))?;
            Some(mac)
        } else {
            None
        };

        Ok(Self {
            taproot: TaprootAssetsClient::new(channel.clone()),
            asset_wallet: AssetWalletClient::new(channel.clone()),
            universe: UniverseClient::new(channel.clone()),
            rfq: RfqClient::new(channel.clone()),
            macaroon,
            timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
        })
    }

    /// Adiciona macaroon aos metadados da requisição.
    fn add_auth<T>(&self, mut req: Request<T>) -> Request<T> {
        if let Some(mac) = &self.macaroon {
            let mac_hex = mac.to_hex();
            if let Ok(val) = MetadataValue::from_str(&mac_hex) {
                req.metadata_mut().insert("macaroon", val);
            }
        }
        req
    }

    // --- Operações gerais ---

    /// Obtém informações do nó tapd.
    pub async fn get_info(&mut self) -> Result<GetInfoResponse> {
        let req = GetInfoRequest {};
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.get_info(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }
}

// Os métodos das operações core, Universe e RFQ são implementados
// nos módulos operations/*.rs e estendem TaprootClient via impl.
```

### 3.4. `src/identity.rs` — AssetRef ↔ DID Mapping

```rust
// src/identity.rs
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

/// Representação de um AssetRef do Taproot Assets.
/// Pode ser um asset_id (UUID) ou group_key (hex).
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum AssetRef {
    /// Asset ID (UUID, 32 bytes)
    AssetId(String),
    /// Group key (hex string, para fungíveis)
    GroupKey(String),
}

impl AssetRef {
    /// Cria um AssetRef a partir de uma string.
    pub fn from_string(s: &str) -> Self {
        if s.starts_with("group_key_") || s.starts_with("g_") {
            let key = s.trim_start_matches("group_key_").trim_start_matches("g_");
            AssetRef::GroupKey(key.to_string())
        } else {
            AssetRef::AssetId(s.to_string())
        }
    }

    /// Converte para string.
    pub fn to_string(&self) -> String {
        match self {
            AssetRef::AssetId(id) => id.clone(),
            AssetRef::GroupKey(key) => format!("group_key_{}", key),
        }
    }

    /// Converte para um DID Cathedral.
    pub fn to_did(&self) -> String {
        match self {
            AssetRef::AssetId(id) => format!("did:cathedral:asset:{}", id),
            AssetRef::GroupKey(key) => format!("did:cathedral:group:{}", key),
        }
    }

    /// Cria a partir de um DID Cathedral.
    pub fn from_did(did: &str) -> Option<Self> {
        if let Some(id) = did.strip_prefix("did:cathedral:asset:") {
            Some(AssetRef::AssetId(id.to_string()))
        } else if let Some(key) = did.strip_prefix("did:cathedral:group:") {
            Some(AssetRef::GroupKey(key.to_string()))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for AssetRef {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}", self.to_string())
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_asset_ref_parsing() {
        let id = AssetRef::from_string("abc123");
        assert!(matches!(id, AssetRef::AssetId(_)));
        assert_eq!(id.to_string(), "abc123");

        let key = AssetRef::from_string("group_key_0xdeadbeef");
        assert!(matches!(key, AssetRef::GroupKey(_)));
        assert_eq!(key.to_string(), "group_key_0xdeadbeef");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_did_conversion() {
        let id = AssetRef::AssetId("test-id".to_string());
        let did = id.to_did();
        assert_eq!(did, "did:cathedral:asset:test-id");

        let parsed = AssetRef::from_did(&did);
        assert_eq!(parsed, Some(id));
    }
}
```

## 🎯 TAREFA 4: OPERAÇÕES CORE (ListAssets, SendAsset, BurnAsset)

### 4.1. `src/operations/core.rs`

```rust
// src/operations/core.rs
use crate::client::TaprootClient;
use crate::error::Result;
use crate::proto::{
    ListAssetsRequest, ListAssetsResponse,
    ListBalancesRequest, ListBalancesResponse,
    NewAddrRequest, NewAddrResponse,
    SendAssetRequest, SendAssetResponse,
    BurnAssetRequest, BurnAssetResponse,
    ListTransfersRequest, ListTransfersResponse,
};
use tonic::Request;

impl TaprootClient {
    /// Lista ativos da carteira.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `with_witness` - Inclui testemunhas na resposta
    /// * `include_spent` - Inclui ativos gastos (UTXOs já usados)
    pub async fn list_assets(
        &mut self,
        with_witness: bool,
        include_spent: bool,
    ) -> Result<ListAssetsResponse> {
        let req = ListAssetsRequest {
            with_witness,
            include_spent,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.list_assets(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Lista balanços por ativo.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo (opcional)
    /// * `group_key` - Chave do grupo (opcional, para fungíveis)
    pub async fn list_balances(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        group_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<ListBalancesResponse> {
        let req = ListBalancesRequest {
            asset_id,
            group_key,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.list_balances(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Lista transferências.
    pub async fn list_transfers(
        &mut self,
        offset: u32,
        limit: u32,
    ) -> Result<ListTransfersResponse> {
        let req = ListTransfersRequest {
            offset,
            limit,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.list_transfers(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Cria um novo endereço para receber ativos.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo a ser recebido (bytes)
    /// * `amount` - Quantidade esperada (opcional)
    pub async fn new_address(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: Option<u64>,
    ) -> Result<NewAddrResponse> {
        let req = NewAddrRequest {
            asset_id,
            amt: amount.unwrap_or(0),
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.new_addr(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Envia ativos para um endereço Taproot Asset.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `addr` - Endereço de destino (string codificada)
    /// * `fee_rate` - Taxa de mineração em sats/vbyte (opcional)
    pub async fn send_asset(
        &mut self,
        addr: String,
        fee_rate: Option<u32>,
    ) -> Result<SendAssetResponse> {
        let req = SendAssetRequest {
            tap_addrs: vec![addr],
            fee_rate: fee_rate.unwrap_or(0),
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.send_asset(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Queima (burn) unidades de um ativo.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo a ser queimado (bytes)
    /// * `amount` - Quantidade a queimar
    pub async fn burn_asset(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: u64,
    ) -> Result<BurnAssetResponse> {
        let req = BurnAssetRequest {
            asset_id,
            amount,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.taproot.burn_asset(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Envia ativos diretamente para uma pubkey Lightning.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo (bytes)
    /// * `amount` - Quantidade
    /// * `dest_pubkey` - Pubkey do destinatário (hex)
    /// * `dest_asset_ref` - AssetRef do destinatário (opcional)
    pub async fn send_asset_to_pubkey(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: u64,
        dest_pubkey: &str,
        dest_asset_ref: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<SendAssetResponse> {
        // Na API do tapd, a transferência para pubkey é feita via endereço
        // que inclui a pubkey. O formato é "tap<asset_id>:<pubkey>"
        let addr = format!("tap{}:{}", hex::encode(&asset_id), dest_pubkey);
        self.send_asset(addr, None).await
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_send_asset_to_pubkey_format() {
        let asset_id = vec![0u8; 32];
        let pubkey = "03abc123";
        let expected = format!("tap{}:{}", hex::encode(&asset_id), pubkey);
        // Não podemos testar o método diretamente sem um cliente,
        // mas podemos verificar a lógica de formatação
        assert!(expected.starts_with("tap"));
        assert!(expected.contains(":"));
    }
}
```

## 🌌 TAREFA 5: OPERAÇÕES UNIVERSE (QueryProof, InsertProof)

### 5.1. `src/operations/universe.rs`

```rust
// src/operations/universe.rs
use crate::client::TaprootClient;
use crate::error::Result;
use crate::proto::{
    QueryProofRequest, QueryProofResponse,
    InsertProofRequest, InsertProofResponse,
    AssetLeavesRequest, AssetLeavesResponse,
    AssetRootsRequest, AssetRootsResponse,
    AssetLeafKeysRequest, AssetLeafKeysResponse,
    UniverseKey, ID, AssetKey,
};
use tonic::Request;

impl TaprootClient {
    /// Consulta uma prova de um ativo no Universe.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo (bytes)
    /// * `group_key` - Chave do grupo (opcional)
    /// * `script_key` - Chave script (opcional)
    /// * `outpoint` - UTXO outpoint (opcional, formato "txid:index")
    pub async fn query_proof(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        group_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        script_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        outpoint: Option<String>,
    ) -> Result<QueryProofResponse> {
        let id = ID {
            asset_id,
            group_key,
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let leaf_key = AssetKey {
            op_str: outpoint.unwrap_or_default(),
            script_key_bytes: script_key.unwrap_or_default(),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let key = UniverseKey {
            id: Some(id),
            leaf_key: Some(leaf_key),
        };

        let req = QueryProofRequest {
            key: Some(key),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.universe.query_proof(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Insere uma prova no Universe local.
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo (bytes)
    /// * `group_key` - Chave do grupo (opcional)
    /// * `script_key` - Chave script (opcional)
    /// * `proof` - Bytes da prova (arquivo .proof)
    pub async fn insert_proof(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        group_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        script_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        proof: Vec<u8>,
    ) -> Result<InsertProofResponse> {
        let id = ID {
            asset_id,
            group_key,
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let leaf_key = AssetKey {
            script_key_bytes: script_key.unwrap_or_default(),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let key = UniverseKey {
            id: Some(id),
            leaf_key: Some(leaf_key),
        };

        let req = InsertProofRequest {
            key: Some(key),
            proof,
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.universe.insert_proof(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Lista folhas de um ativo no Universe.
    pub async fn asset_leaves(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        group_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AssetLeavesResponse> {
        let id = ID {
            asset_id,
            group_key,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let req = AssetLeavesRequest {
            id: Some(id),
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.universe.asset_leaves(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Lista raízes de ativos no Universe.
    pub async fn asset_roots(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        group_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AssetRootsResponse> {
        let id = ID {
            asset_id: asset_id.unwrap_or_default(),
            group_key,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let req = AssetRootsRequest {
            id: Some(id),
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.universe.asset_roots(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Lista chaves das folhas de um ativo.
    pub async fn asset_leaf_keys(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        group_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AssetLeafKeysResponse> {
        let id = ID {
            asset_id,
            group_key,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let req = AssetLeafKeysRequest {
            id: Some(id),
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.universe.asset_leaf_keys(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }
}
```

## 📊 TAREFA 6: OPERAÇÕES RFQ (AddBuyOrder, AddSellOrder)

### 6.1. `src/operations/rfq.rs`

```rust
// src/operations/rfq.rs
use crate::client::TaprootClient;
use crate::error::Result;
use crate::proto::{
    AddAssetBuyOrderRequest, AddAssetBuyOrderResponse,
    AddAssetSellOrderRequest, AddAssetSellOrderResponse,
    AddAssetBuyOfferRequest, AddAssetBuyOfferResponse,
    AddAssetSellOfferRequest, AddAssetSellOfferResponse,
    ForwardingHistoryRequest, ForwardingHistoryResponse,
    AssetSpecifier,
};
use tonic::Request;

impl TaprootClient {
    /// Adiciona uma ordem de compra de ativo (RFQ).
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo (bytes)
    /// * `amount` - Quantidade desejada
    /// * `max_price` - Preço máximo (em sats por unidade)
    /// * `expiry` - Tempo de expiração (segundos)
    /// * `peer_pub_key` - Pubkey do peer alvo (opcional)
    pub async fn add_buy_order(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: u64,
        max_price: u64,
        expiry: u64,
        peer_pub_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AddAssetBuyOrderResponse> {
        let asset_specifier = AssetSpecifier {
            asset_id,
            group_key: None,
        };

        let req = AddAssetBuyOrderRequest {
            asset_specifier: Some(asset_specifier),
            asset_max_amt: amount,
            expiry,
            peer_pub_key: peer_pub_key.unwrap_or_default(),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.rfq.add_asset_buy_order(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Adiciona uma ordem de venda de ativo (RFQ).
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `asset_id` - ID do ativo (bytes)
    /// * `amount` - Quantidade a vender
    /// * `min_price` - Preço mínimo (em sats por unidade)
    /// * `expiry` - Tempo de expiração (segundos)
    /// * `peer_pub_key` - Pubkey do peer alvo (opcional)
    pub async fn add_sell_order(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: u64,
        min_price: u64,
        expiry: u64,
        peer_pub_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AddAssetSellOrderResponse> {
        let asset_specifier = AssetSpecifier {
            asset_id,
            group_key: None,
        };

        let req = AddAssetSellOrderRequest {
            asset_specifier: Some(asset_specifier),
            asset_max_amt: amount,
            expiry,
            peer_pub_key: peer_pub_key.unwrap_or_default(),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.rfq.add_asset_sell_order(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Adiciona uma oferta de compra (para responder a RFQs).
    pub async fn add_buy_offer(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: u64,
        price: u64,
        expiry: u64,
        peer_pub_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AddAssetBuyOfferResponse> {
        let asset_specifier = AssetSpecifier {
            asset_id,
            group_key: None,
        };

        let req = AddAssetBuyOfferRequest {
            asset_specifier: Some(asset_specifier),
            amount,
            price,
            expiry,
            peer_pub_key: peer_pub_key.unwrap_or_default(),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.rfq.add_asset_buy_offer(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Adiciona uma oferta de venda (para responder a RFQs).
    pub async fn add_sell_offer(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Vec<u8>,
        amount: u64,
        price: u64,
        expiry: u64,
        peer_pub_key: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    ) -> Result<AddAssetSellOfferResponse> {
        let asset_specifier = AssetSpecifier {
            asset_id,
            group_key: None,
        };

        let req = AddAssetSellOfferRequest {
            asset_specifier: Some(asset_specifier),
            amount,
            price,
            expiry,
            peer_pub_key: peer_pub_key.unwrap_or_default(),
            ..Default::default()
        };

        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.rfq.add_asset_sell_offer(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }

    /// Consulta histórico de forwarding de ativos.
    pub async fn forwarding_history(
        &mut self,
        asset_id: Option<Vec<u8>>,
        limit: u32,
        offset: u32,
    ) -> Result<ForwardingHistoryResponse> {
        let req = ForwardingHistoryRequest {
            asset_id,
            limit,
            offset,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let request = self.add_auth(Request::new(req));
        let response = self.rfq.forwarding_history(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner())
    }
}
```

## 🧪 TAREFA 7: TESTES DE INTEGRAÇÃO

### 7.1. `tests/integration.rs`

```rust
// tests/integration.rs
use cathedral_taproot_bridge::{TaprootClient, AssetRef};
use std::path::Path;
use std::env;

const ALICE_ADDR: &str = "http://localhost:10029";
const BOB_ADDR: &str = "http://localhost:10030";

/// Testa a conexão e autenticação.
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_connection() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let macaroon_path = env::var("TAPD_MACAROON")
        .ok()
        .map(|p| Path::new(&p).to_path_buf())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("certs/admin.macaroon").to_path_buf());

    let mut client = TaprootClient::connect(
        ALICE_ADDR,
        None,
        Some(&macaroon_path),
    ).await?;

    let info = client.get_info().await?;
    assert!(!info.version.is_empty());
    println!("✅ Conectado ao tapd versão: {}", info.version);
    println!("   Network: {}", info.network);
    println!("   Block height: {}", info.block_height);

    Ok(())
}

/// Testa o ciclo de vida completo de um ativo.
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_asset_lifecycle() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let macaroon_path = env::var("TAPD_MACAROON")
        .ok()
        .map(|p| Path::new(&p).to_path_buf())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("certs/admin.macaroon").to_path_buf());

    let mut alice = TaprootClient::connect(
        ALICE_ADDR,
        None,
        Some(&macaroon_path),
    ).await?;

    let mut bob = TaprootClient::connect(
        BOB_ADDR,
        None,
        Some(&macaroon_path),
    ).await?;

    // 1. Alice obtém informações do nó
    let alice_info = alice.get_info().await?;
    println!("✅ Alice: {}", alice_info.version);

    let bob_info = bob.get_info().await?;
    println!("✅ Bob: {}", bob_info.version);

    // 2. Alice lista ativos (inicialmente vazio)
    let assets = alice.list_assets(false, false).await?;
    println!("📦 Ativos iniciais (Alice): {} encontrados", assets.assets.len());

    // 3. Verifica balanços
    let balances = alice.list_balances(None, None).await?;
    println!("💰 Balanços (Alice): {} grupos", balances.balances.len());

    // 4. Testa RFQ com ativo fictício
    let asset_id = vec![0u8; 32]; // ID fictício para teste
    let buy_order = alice.add_buy_order(
        asset_id.clone(),
        1_000,
        10_000,  // 10,000 sats por unidade
        3_600,   // 1 hora
        None,
    ).await;

    match buy_order {
        Ok(_) => println!("✅ Ordem de compra criada (ativo fictício)"),
        Err(e) => println!("⚠️ Erro esperado (ativo não existe): {}", e),
    }

    // 5. Testa burn com ativo fictício
    let burn = alice.burn_asset(asset_id.clone(), 100).await;
    match burn {
        Ok(_) => println!("✅ Burn executado (ativo fictício)"),
        Err(e) => println!("⚠️ Erro esperado (ativo não existe): {}", e),
    }

    // 6. Testa Universe com ativo fictício
    let roots = alice.asset_roots(None, None).await?;
    println!("🌳 Raízes do Universe: {} encontradas", roots.roots.len());

    Ok(())
}

/// Testa operações Universe.
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_universe_operations() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let macaroon_path = env::var("TAPD_MACAROON")
        .ok()
        .map(|p| Path::new(&p).to_path_buf())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("certs/admin.macaroon").to_path_buf());

    let mut client = TaprootClient::connect(
        ALICE_ADDR,
        None,
        Some(&macaroon_path),
    ).await?;

    // Lista raízes de ativos
    let roots = client.asset_roots(None, None).await?;
    println!("🌳 Raízes do Universe: {} encontradas", roots.roots.len());

    if let Some(root) = roots.roots.first() {
        let asset_id = root.id.as_ref().map(|id| id.asset_id.clone()).unwrap_or_default();

        // Lista folhas
        let leaves = client.asset_leaves(asset_id.clone(), None).await?;
        println!("📄 Folhas do ativo: {} encontradas", leaves.leaves.len());

        // Lista chaves das folhas
        let keys = client.asset_leaf_keys(asset_id.clone(), None).await?;
        println!("🔑 Chaves das folhas: {} encontradas", keys.keys.len());

        // Tenta consultar prova (se houver folhas)
        if let Some(leaf) = leaves.leaves.first() {
            let asset = &leaf.asset;
            if let Some(genesis) = &asset.asset_genesis {
                let proof = client.query_proof(
                    genesis.asset_id.clone(),
                    None,
                    Some(asset.script_key.clone()),
                    None,
                ).await?;
                println!("📜 Prova obtida: size={}", proof.asset_leaf.as_ref().map(|l| l.proof.len()).unwrap_or(0));
            }
        }
    }

    Ok(())
}

/// Testa RFQ.
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_rfq_workflow() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let macaroon_path = env::var("TAPD_MACAROON")
        .ok()
        .map(|p| Path::new(&p).to_path_buf())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("certs/admin.macaroon").to_path_buf());

    let mut alice = TaprootClient::connect(
        ALICE_ADDR,
        None,
        Some(&macaroon_path),
    ).await?;

    let mut bob = TaprootClient::connect(
        BOB_ADDR,
        None,
        Some(&macaroon_path),
    ).await?;

    let bob_info = bob.get_info().await?;
    let bob_pubkey = hex::decode(&bob_info.identity_pubkey)
        .unwrap_or_default();

    // 1. Bob adiciona uma oferta de venda
    let asset_id = vec![0u8; 32]; // ID fictício
    let sell_offer = bob.add_sell_offer(
        asset_id.clone(),
        10_000,   // 10,000 unidades
        5_000,    // 5,000 sats por unidade
        3_600,    // 1 hora
        Some(bob_pubkey.clone()),
    ).await;

    if let Ok(offer) = sell_offer {
        println!("✅ Bob ofereceu venda");

        // 2. Alice adiciona ordem de compra
        let buy_order = alice.add_buy_order(
            asset_id.clone(),
            5_000,    // 5,000 unidades
            5_500,    // até 5,500 sats por unidade
            3_600,
            Some(bob_pubkey),
        ).await?;

        println!("✅ Alice colocou ordem de compra");
    } else {
        println!("⚠️ Erro ao adicionar oferta (ativo não existe)");
    }

    // 3. Consulta histórico de forwarding
    let history = alice.forwarding_history(None, 10, 0).await;
    match history {
        Ok(h) => println!("📊 Histórico: {} entradas", h.forwarding_events.len()),
        Err(e) => println!("⚠️ Erro ao consultar histórico: {}", e),
    }

    Ok(())
}
```

## 🚀 8. SCRIPT DE EXECUÇÃO DOS TESTES

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/run-tests.sh

echo "🏛️  Cathedral Taproot Bridge - Testes de Integração"

# Verifica se o ambiente está rodando
if ! docker ps | grep -q tapd-alice; then
    echo "❌ Ambiente não está rodando. Execute ./scripts/setup-regtest.sh primeiro."
    exit 1
fi

# Exporta variáveis de ambiente para testes
export TAPD_ALICE="http://localhost:10029"
export TAPD_BOB="http://localhost:10030"
export TAPD_MACAROON="certs/admin.macaroon"

# Executa os testes com logging
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1

echo "✅ Testes concluídos"
```
